# META
source_lines=13875
stages=DESUGAR,MARK
# SOURCE
-- Self-hosted typecheck stage — port of lib/typecheck.ml's HM core.  SLICE 1:
-- the Hindley-Milner engine — monotypes with Ref-based union-find tyvars,
-- level-based generalization, unification (occurs-check + level adjust),
-- instantiation, and inference for literals, variables, application, lambdas,
-- let (let-polymorphism), if, tuples, lists, annotations, ADT constructors, and
-- match/pattern inference. Validated WITHOUT the prelude (engine only) against
-- dev/tc_probe.exe — so it needs only the HM core, not the typeclass /
-- dict-passing layer (the === TYPES === rendering drops constraints).
--
-- Deferred to later slices: operators (EBinOp/EInfix route through Num/Eq from
-- the prelude), effect rows, records, interfaces/impls + constraint solving,
-- and explicit type signatures.

import frontend.ast.{
  Lit(..),
  Ty(..),
  Constraint(..),
  Route(..),
  Pat(..),
  RecPatField(..),
  Guard(..),
  Arm(..),
  DoStmt(..),
  FunClause(..),
  FieldAssign(..),
  LetBind(..),
  Expr(..),
  Loc(..),
  ConPayload(..),
  Field(..),
  Variant(..),
  IfaceMethod(..),
  MethodDefault(..),
  Require(..),
  Super(..),
  ImplMethod(..),
  DataVis(..),
  Decl(..),
  PropParam(..),
  Section(..),
  InterpPart(..),
  GuardArm(..),
  UsePath(..),
  UseMember(..),
  useMemberOrigin,
  useMemberLocal,
  useMemberAlias,
  qualifiedLocal,
}
import frontend.desugar.{mapProg}
import frontend.marker.{localBoundNames}
-- The type-aware non-exhaustive-`match` check (`check_match` in lib/exhaust.ml)
-- reuses the Maranget matrix machinery already ported in exhaust.mdk — the only
-- difference from the guard-coverage pass is the ctor oracle (here it comes from
-- the typechecked program's data decls + the inferred scrutinee type).
import frontend.exhaust.{
  Oracle,
  buildOracle,
  useful,
  patUnreachable,
  patHasRange,
  usefulWitness,
  renderWitness,
  desugarPat,
}
import support.char.{isUpper}
import backend.private_mangle.{mangledName}
import support.ordmap.{
  OrdMap,
  omEmpty,
  omInsert,
  omLookup,
  omHasKey,
  omFromPairs,
}
import list.{replicate}
import support.util.{
  listLen,
  lookupAssoc,
  contains,
  endsWith,
  reverseL,
  joinWith,
  joinNl,
  joinDot,
  filterList,
  anyList,
  allList,
  initList,
  isEmptyL,
  isNonEmptyL,
  minI,
  isSome,
  zipL,
  dedup,
  sortUniqS,
  startsWith,
  escStr,
  editDistance,
}

-- ── monotypes & schemes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
public export data Mono =
  | TVar (Ref Tyvar)
  | TCon String
  | TApp Mono Mono
  | TFun Mono EffRow Mono
  -- arg -> <effect row> result
  | TEff EffRow
-- An effect row occupying a type-CONSTRUCTOR ARGUMENT slot, for a data type
-- with an effect-row parameter (`data Async e a = … <e> …`).  Produced ONLY
-- by kind-directed elaboration at a Row-kinded param position; consumed only
-- by unifyN's TApp~TApp recursion.  Never appears in code lacking an
-- effect-poly data type, so its addition is purely additive.

public export data Tyvar =
  | Unbound Int Int  -- Unbound id level | Link
  | Link Mono

-- An effect row (Phase 79/146): a set of concrete effect labels plus an optional
-- tail variable.
--   tail = None    ⇒ closed row, exactly [labels].
--   tail = Some v  ⇒ open row <labels | v>: v can absorb further effects.
-- Inference-synthesized arrows use open rows so effect-subsumption survives
-- equality unification; user annotations are closed unless they name a tail.
public export data EffRow = EffRow (List Atom) (Option (Ref Effvar))
public export data Effvar =
  | EUnbound Int Int  -- EUnbound id level | link
  | ELink EffRow

-- ── Effect refinement domains (capability-effects v2, Stage 1) ─────────────
-- An effect label may carry a PARAMETER drawn from its declared refinement
-- DOMAIN — a small lattice of data-shaped values with a top ⊤ (unconstrained).
-- Stage 1 is the REPRESENTATION only: every param is built at ⊤, so the row is
-- byte-identical to the v1 set-of-bare-labels.  Stage 2 adds the Prefix domain's
-- analysis with NO change to this row code.  (Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml.)
-- WS-4: a PRODUCT refinement domain — a label refined on several named AXES at
-- once, each axis a value in an existing sub-domain (e.g. structured `Net` =
-- `Host(Prefix) × Method(Set)`).  Represented as a sorted assoc-list keyed by
-- axis name; an axis ABSENT from the list is ⊤ on that axis, and `PProduct []`
-- is the product ⊤ (no axis constrained).  The lattice ops are POINTWISE
-- (`dsubN`/`djoinN`/`drenderN` below).  No `dmeetN`/⊥ arm: the escape check and
-- policy compare only ever call `dsub`/`djoin` — ⊓ is unreachable today and is
-- deliberately omitted (model ⊥ as `Option Param`→`None` if ever needed).
public export data Param =
  | PUnit
  | PPrefix (Option String)
  | PSet (Option (List String))
  | PProduct (List (String, Param))

-- v2 Stage 2b inferred-hole marker (mirror of lib/typecheck.ml).  A leaf
-- extern's `<Net _>` parses to the param string "_" (`effHoleSrc`), which
-- `atomOfWritten` keeps as `PPrefix (Some "_")` — the hole the call-site α
-- overwrites.  "_" is unambiguous: it can never be a valid written pattern
-- (fails `prefixPatternOk`).  An un-filled hole degrades to ⊤ for all domain
-- ops (sound over-approximation), so a surviving hole never leaks.
effHoleSrc : String
effHoleSrc = "_"

isHoleStr : String -> Bool
isHoleStr s = s == effHoleSrc

isHoleParam : Param -> Bool
isHoleParam (PPrefix (Some s)) = isHoleStr s
isHoleParam _ = False

normHole : Param -> Param
normHole (PPrefix (Some s)) =
  if isHoleStr s then
    PPrefix None
  else
    PPrefix (Some s)
normHole p = p
-- WS-4: per-axis holes are out of scope (the hole marker stays at the whole-atom
-- `Param` level, not per axis), so `normHole (PProduct …)` is the identity — the
-- explicit clause above (`normHole p = p`) already covers it.  Documented so a
-- reader doesn't mistake the absence for an oversight.
-- an atom = a label applied to a domain parameter.  A v1 atomic label `Foo` is
-- exactly `Atom "Foo" (dtopFor "Foo")` (= PUnit in Stage 1).
public export data Atom = Atom String Param

export atomLabel : Atom -> String
atomLabel (Atom l _) = l

export atomParam : Atom -> Param
atomParam (Atom _ p) = p

-- ── effect-label DOMAIN registry (v2 Stage 2a) ─────────────────────────────
-- Maps a label to the ⊤-param of its declared domain.  A `PPrefix None` entry
-- means "Prefix-domain (carries a pattern)"; absence ⇒ atomic (Unit).  Seeded
-- with the builtin Prefix labels (Net/FileRead/FileWrite) and extended per
-- program from `effect Net Prefix` decls.  An assoc-list Ref (the label set is
-- tiny).  (Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's effect_domains Hashtbl.)
effectDomains : Ref (List (String, Param))
effectDomains = Ref []

seedEffectDomains : Unit -> Unit
seedEffectDomains _ = setRef
  effectDomains
  [
    ("Net", PPrefix None),
    ("FileRead", PPrefix None),
    ("FileWrite", PPrefix None),
    ("Env", PSet None),
    ("Exec", PPrefix None),
  ]

-- register a label's domain from its `effect` decl: Some "Prefix" ⇒ Prefix;
-- None ⇒ atomic.
registerEffectDomain : String -> Option String -> Unit
registerEffectDomain name domain =
  setRef effectDomains ((name, domainParam domain)::effectDomains.value)

-- the ⊤-param a declared label carries: atomic ⇒ PUnit; Prefix ⇒ PPrefix None.
domainParam : Option String -> Param
domainParam (Some "Prefix") = PPrefix None
domainParam (Some "Set") = PSet None
domainParam (Some "Product") = PProduct []  -- WS-4: structured (multi-axis) domain ⊤
domainParam _ = PUnit

-- re-seed to the builtins, then register every `effect …` decl in the program.
populateEffectDomains : List Decl -> Unit
populateEffectDomains decls =
  let _ = seedEffectDomains ()
  populateGo decls

populateGo : List Decl -> Unit
populateGo [] = ()
populateGo ((DEffect _ name domain)::rest) =
  let _ = registerEffectDomain name domain
  populateGo rest
populateGo (_::rest) = populateGo rest

lookupDomain : String -> List (String, Param) -> Param
lookupDomain _ [] = PUnit
lookupDomain l ((k, p)::rest) = if l == k then p else lookupDomain l rest

-- ── RefinementDomain interface (design §2.1): dtop/dsub/djoin/dmeet/drender ──
-- Stage 1: only ⊤ params (PUnit / PPrefix None) ever occur; the STRUCTURE is
-- what Stage 2's Prefix analysis plugs into.  A label's domain is fixed by its
-- declaration; dtopFor picks the ⊤ of the label's domain (all PUnit in Stage 1).
-- ⊤ parameter for a label's domain (design §2.1): consults the registry;
-- labels with no Prefix entry are atomic ⇒ PUnit.
dtopFor : String -> Param
dtopFor label = match lookupDomain label effectDomains.value
  PPrefix None => PPrefix None
  PSet None => PSet None
  PProduct _ => PProduct []     -- WS-4: product domain ⊤ (no axis constrained)
  _ => PUnit

-- the concrete (literal) part of a Prefix pattern: drop a single trailing `*`
-- wildcard ("a.com/*" → "a.com/"); a pattern with no `*` is concrete as-is.
prefixConcrete : String -> String
prefixConcrete s =
  let n = stringLength s
  if n > 0 && stringSlice (n - 1) n s == "*" then stringSlice 0 (n - 1) s else s

-- p1 ⊑ p2 : is p1 at least as constrained (more specific) as p2?  In the Prefix
-- domain, p1 ⊑ p2 iff p1's concrete part starts with p2's concrete part.
export dsub : Param -> Param -> Bool
dsub p1 p2 = dsubN (normHole p1) (normHole p2)

dsubN : Param -> Param -> Bool
dsubN PUnit PUnit = True
dsubN _ (PPrefix None) = True  -- anything ⊑ ⊤
dsubN (PPrefix (Some a)) (PPrefix (Some b)) =
  startsWith (prefixConcrete b) (prefixConcrete a)
dsubN (PPrefix None) (PPrefix (Some _)) = False
dsubN _ (PSet None) = True  -- anything ⊑ ⊤ (Set domain)
dsubN (PSet (Some a)) (PSet (Some b)) = subsetStr a b  -- a ⊆ b
dsubN (PSet None) (PSet (Some _)) = False
-- WS-4: PRODUCT subsumption is POINTWISE.  `a ⊑ b` iff for EVERY axis CONSTRAINED
-- in `b`, a's value on that axis ⊑ b's value.  SOUNDNESS-CRITICAL: an axis absent
-- from `a` means a is UNCONSTRAINED (⊤) on that axis, which is NOT ⊑ a constrained
-- b-axis — so a missing a-axis must be looked up against the SUB-DOMAIN ⊤ of the
-- b-axis (`subTopOf bp`), not silently skipped.  (Skipping it would under-
-- approximate the escape check ⇒ unsound: an `a` silent on Method would falsely
-- subsume a `b` that confines Method.)  Axes constrained in `a` but ⊤ in `b` need
-- no check (anything ⊑ ⊤), so we iterate b's axes only.
dsubN _ (PProduct []) = True  -- anything ⊑ product-⊤
dsubN (PProduct ax) (PProduct bx) = allList (axisSub ax) bx
dsubN _ _ = False  -- domain mismatch — never happens (one domain per label)

-- one b-axis ⊑ check: a's value on `name` (its sub-domain ⊤ when a is silent) ⊑ bp.
axisSub : List (String, Param) -> (String, Param) -> Bool
axisSub ax (name, bp) = dsubN (lookupAxisOrTop name ax bp) bp

-- a's value for axis `name`, or the SUB-DOMAIN ⊤ of `bp` when `a` is silent (the
-- soundness default — a missing axis is ⊤ on that axis, NOT ⊑ a constrained bound).
lookupAxisOrTop : String -> List (String, Param) -> Param -> Param
lookupAxisOrTop name ax bp = match lookupAxis name ax
  Some v => v
  None => subTopOf bp

-- the ⊤ of a sub-domain param's domain.
subTopOf : Param -> Param
subTopOf (PPrefix _) = PPrefix None
subTopOf (PSet _) = PSet None
subTopOf (PProduct _) = PProduct []
subTopOf _ = PUnit

lookupAxis : String -> List (String, Param) -> Option Param
lookupAxis _ [] = None
lookupAxis name ((k, v)::rest) =
  if name == k then
    Some v
  else
    lookupAxis name rest

-- total `all` over a list (avoids relying on a stdlib import in compiler).
allList : (a -> Bool) -> List a -> Bool
allList _ [] = True
allList f (x::xs) = if f x then allList f xs else False

-- a ⊆ b for string sets (lists treated as sets)
subsetStr : List String -> List String -> Bool
subsetStr [] _ = True
subsetStr (x::xs) b = if contains x b then subsetStr xs b else False

-- least upper bound (over-approximation)
djoin : Param -> Param -> Param
djoin p1 p2 = djoinN (normHole p1) (normHole p2)

djoinN : Param -> Param -> Param
djoinN PUnit PUnit = PUnit
djoinN (PPrefix None) _ = PPrefix None
djoinN _ (PPrefix None) = PPrefix None
djoinN (PPrefix (Some a)) (PPrefix (Some b)) =
  let k = commonPrefixLen a b 0
  if k == 0 then PPrefix None else PPrefix (Some (stringSlice 0 k a))
djoinN (PSet None) _ = PSet None
djoinN _ (PSet None) = PSet None
djoinN (PSet (Some a)) (PSet (Some b)) =
  -- ∪, saturating to ⊤ (PSet None) past the cardinality cap (setCardCap = 16)
  let u = sortUniqS (a ++ b)
  if listLen u > setCardCap then PSet None else PSet (Some u)
-- WS-4: PRODUCT join is POINTWISE — for every axis present in EITHER side, join
-- the two sub-params (a side silent on an axis = sub-⊤ there, which makes the join
-- ⊤ on that axis — correct over-approximation).  `productNorm` then drops axes
-- that joined to sub-⊤ and re-sorts; all-⊤ collapses to `PProduct []`.  Component
-- saturation (prefix→LCP→⊤, set→card-cap→⊤) is inherited from the recursive calls.
djoinN (PProduct ax) (PProduct bx) =
  productNorm (map (joinAxis ax bx) (axisUnion ax bx))
djoinN p _ = p  -- domain mismatch — never happens

-- join the two sides' values on axis `name` (missing side = sub-⊤).  The sub-⊤
-- to use is taken from whichever side HAS the axis (both sides' axes are sub-⊤
-- compatible since one label has one product shape).
joinAxis : List (String, Param) -> List (String, Param) -> String -> (String, Param)
joinAxis ax bx name = match (lookupAxis name ax, lookupAxis name bx)
  (Some pa, Some pb) => (name, djoinN pa pb)
  (Some pa, None) => (name, djoinN pa (subTopOf pa))
  (None, Some pb) => (name, djoinN (subTopOf pb) pb)
  (None, None) => (name, PUnit)  -- unreachable (name ∈ union)

-- the sorted union of axis NAMES present in either side.
axisUnion : List (String, Param) -> List (String, Param) -> List String
axisUnion ax bx = sortUniqS (map fst ax ++ map fst bx)

-- canonicalize a product: drop axes whose value is its sub-domain ⊤ (unconstrained
-- ⇒ omit), sort by axis name; all-⊤ ⇒ `PProduct []` (the product ⊤).
productNorm : List (String, Param) -> Param
productNorm axes =
  PProduct (sortAxes (filterList (p => not (isSubTop (snd p))) axes))

-- is a sub-param the ⊤ of its sub-domain?
isSubTop : Param -> Bool
isSubTop (PPrefix None) = True
isSubTop (PSet None) = True
isSubTop (PProduct []) = True
isSubTop PUnit = True
isSubTop _ = False

-- sort an axis assoc-list by axis name (insertion sort — the axis count is tiny).
sortAxes : List (String, Param) -> List (String, Param)
sortAxes [] = []
sortAxes (x::xs) = insertAxis x (sortAxes xs)

insertAxis : (String, Param) -> List (String, Param) -> List (String, Param)
insertAxis x [] = [x]
insertAxis x (y::ys) = match stringCompare (fst x) (fst y)
  Gt => y :: insertAxis x ys
  _ => x :: y::ys

-- Set-domain cardinality cap: a join whose union exceeds this many distinct
-- authority elements saturates to ⊤ (PSet None), bounding the lattice height.
setCardCap : Int
setCardCap = 16

-- length of the longest common prefix of two strings (Stage-2 djoin support)
commonPrefixLen : String -> String -> Int -> Int
commonPrefixLen a b i =
  if i >= stringLength a || i >= stringLength b then
    i
  else if stringSlice i (i + 1) a == stringSlice i (i + 1) b then
    commonPrefixLen a b (i + 1)
  else
    i

-- manifest / error text for a param.  ⊤ renders as empty so a ⊤-param atom
-- renders as JUST its label — byte-identical to v1.
export drender : Param -> String
drender p = drenderN (normHole p)

drenderN : Param -> String
drenderN PUnit = ""
drenderN (PPrefix None) = ""
drenderN (PPrefix (Some s)) = " \"" ++ s ++ "\""
drenderN (PSet None) = ""  -- ⊤ renders empty (like the others)
drenderN (PSet (Some xs)) = " {" ++ joinWith ", " (map quoteStr xs) ++ "}"
-- WS-4: render a product as the chosen USER SURFACE (keyword-axes, capitalized:
-- `Host="…" Method={…}`) so error/manifest text round-trips back through the
-- parser.  Product-⊤ (`PProduct []`) renders empty, like every other ⊤.
drenderN (PProduct []) = ""
drenderN (PProduct ax) = " " ++ renderProductLit ax

quoteStr : String -> String
quoteStr s = "\"" ++ s ++ "\""

-- WS-4: render a product's constrained axes as `Host="…" Method={…}` (space-
-- separated, axis-sorted) — the Option-A keyword-axes user surface.  Each axis
-- value renders via its sub-domain (`drenderN` drops the leading space, which we
-- supply per axis as `Name=`).  Only constrained axes appear (⊤ axes are absent
-- by `productNorm`), so the surface is minimal.
renderProductLit : List (String, Param) -> String
renderProductLit ax = joinWith " " (map renderAxis ax)

renderAxis : (String, Param) -> String
renderAxis (name, p) = "\{name}=\{renderAxisVal p}"

-- an axis value WITHOUT the leading-space that `drenderN` prefixes (we reuse the
-- sub-domain rendering but strip the space, so Host="a/*" / Method={"GET"}).
renderAxisVal : Param -> String
renderAxisVal (PPrefix (Some s)) = "\"" ++ s ++ "\""
renderAxisVal (PSet (Some xs)) = "{" ++ joinWith ", " (map quoteStr xs) ++ "}"
renderAxisVal _ = ""  -- a ⊤ axis never reaches here (productNorm drops it)

-- ── atom-set helpers (replace the v1 String-set ops) ───────────────────────
-- render one atom: label + domain refinement text (⊤ ⇒ just the label)
renderAtom : Atom -> String
renderAtom a = atomLabel a ++ drender (atomParam a)

-- render a set of atoms as the comma-separated body of a `<…>`, label-sorted —
-- byte-identical to v1 when all params are ⊤.
renderAtoms : List Atom -> String
renderAtoms atoms = joinWith ", " (map renderAtom (atomsNorm atoms))

-- insert an atom into a label-sorted set, joining same-label params (djoin).
-- With all-⊤ params this is exactly v1's sortInsertS over labels.
atomInsert : Atom -> List Atom -> List Atom
atomInsert x [] = [x]
atomInsert x (y::ys) = match stringCompare (atomLabel x) (atomLabel y)
  Lt => x :: y::ys
  Eq => Atom (atomLabel y) (djoin (atomParam x) (atomParam y)) :: ys
  Gt => y :: atomInsert x ys

-- canonical form: ≤ one atom per label (same-label params joined), label-sorted.
atomsNorm : List Atom -> List Atom
atomsNorm xs = atomsNormGo xs []

atomsNormGo : List Atom -> List Atom -> List Atom
atomsNormGo [] acc = acc
atomsNormGo (x::xs) acc = atomsNormGo xs (atomInsert x acc)

-- union of two atom sets, joining same-label params
atomsUnion : List Atom -> List Atom -> List Atom
atomsUnion a b = atomsNorm (a ++ b)

-- set difference: atoms of [a] whose label is absent from [b], OR present but
-- NOT subsumed (`not (dsub pa pb)`) — the Stage-2 escape direction.  In Stage 1
-- (all ⊤) "present" always means subsumed ⇒ pure label-set diff.
atomsDiff : List Atom -> List Atom -> List Atom
atomsDiff [] _ = []
atomsDiff (x::xs) ys = match findAtom (atomLabel x) ys
  None => x :: atomsDiff xs ys
  Some y =>
    if dsub (atomParam x) (atomParam y) then
      atomsDiff xs ys
    else
      x :: atomsDiff xs ys

findAtom : String -> List Atom -> Option Atom
findAtom _ [] = None
findAtom l (y::ys) = if l == atomLabel y then Some y else findAtom l ys

-- lift a bare AST label to a ⊤-param atom (Stage 1: every annotation atomic)
atomOfLabel : String -> Atom
atomOfLabel l = Atom l (dtopFor l)

-- ── v2 Stage 3: the `IO` widening union alias (design §3.2) ─────────────────
-- `IO` is the coarse security alias: in a BOUND/annotation row it stands for
-- the join of every narrow security label at ⊤.  ioAliasLabels lists them
-- (every minted security label EXCEPT IO itself); internal labels (Mut/Panic)
-- are NOT members, so they are tracked independently of IO.  (Mirror of
-- lib/typecheck.ml's io_alias_labels.)
ioAliasLabels : List String
ioAliasLabels = [
  "Stdout",
  "Stderr",
  "Stdin",
  "Clock",
  "Env",
  "Exec",
  "Rand",
  "Net",
  "FileRead",
  "FileWrite",
]

-- expand a bound `IO` atom into the security-label alias before an escape
-- check, so a NARROW inferred row is subsumed by an `<IO>` bound (narrow ⊑ IO
-- accepts).  Sound in reverse: an inferred raw `IO` against a NARROW bound is
-- never expanded (the bound has no IO) ⇒ escapes (IO ⊑ narrow rejects).  `IO`
-- stays in the expansion (atomsNorm keeps it) so inferred IO-vs-IO matches.
expandIoInBound : List Atom -> List Atom
expandIoInBound b = match findAtom "IO" b
  None => b
  Some _ => atomsNorm (b ++ map atomOfLabel ioAliasLabels)

-- escape-direction difference: inferred [a] not permitted by bound [b], with
-- the bound's `IO` widened to its security-label alias first.
atomsEscape : List Atom -> List Atom -> List Atom
atomsEscape a b = atomsDiff a (expandIoInBound b)

-- build an atom from a WRITTEN annotation label, threading its optional param.
-- A bare label takes the domain ⊤; a written param becomes PPrefix (Some pat)
-- when the label's domain is Prefix, else (atomic label) stays ⊤ (the param is
-- diagnosed + dropped by checkEffectParams).  Pure: validation is a separate pass.
atomOfWritten : (String, Option String) -> Atom
atomOfWritten (l, None) = Atom l (dtopFor l)
atomOfWritten (l, Some pat) =
  -- the universal inferred-hole marker `_`: recognized REGARDLESS of the label's
  -- domain (a Set-domain `<Env _>` must stay a hole, not become `PSet ["_"]`).
  -- The domain-directed fillHoleAtom replaces it with the correct domain param.
  if isHoleStr pat then Atom l (PPrefix (Some pat))
  else match dtopFor l
    PPrefix None => Atom l (PPrefix (Some pat))
    PSet None => Atom l (PSet (Some (decodeSetParam pat)))
    PProduct _ => Atom l (decodeProductParam pat)
    _ => Atom l (dtopFor l)

-- A Set-domain written param is carried in the `Option String` carrier as a
-- brace-delimited, comma-separated literal `{a,b,c}` (the parser's `effParamFor`
-- TLBrace clause builds exactly this).  Decode it back to the element list.
-- (Keeps the carrier type `Option String`, so no AST `TyEffect` ripple.)
export decodeSetParam : String -> List String
decodeSetParam s =
  let n = stringLength s
  if n >= 2 && stringSlice 0 1 s == "{" && stringSlice (n - 1) n s == "}" then
    sortUniqS (filterList (!= "") (splitOnComma (stringSlice 1 (n - 1) s)))
  else
    sortUniqS (filterList (!= "") (splitOnComma s))

-- WS-4: a PRODUCT written param is carried in the `Option String` carrier as a
-- structured SENTINEL string the parser's `effParamFor` product clause builds:
--
--   @P{Host="idp.example.com/*";Method={GET,POST}}
--
-- Grammar (internal wire form, NOT user syntax — that is §4 of WS-4-DESIGN.md):
--   @P{                         -- product sentinel prefix
--     <Axis>=<val>              -- one or more axes, ';'-separated
--   }                           -- closing brace
-- where <val> is either `"…"` (a Prefix sub-param) or `{a,b}` (a Set sub-param).
-- A PLAIN string with NO `@P{` sentinel is the §3 "host-axis lift": a bare
-- `<Net "a.com/*">` under a Product-registered Net means Host=a.com/*, Method=⊤.
-- (A *third* nested level would make this encoding fiddly — WS-4 is the natural
-- stopping point for the carrier hack; widen the AST carrier THEN, not piecemeal.)
export decodeProductParam : String -> Param
decodeProductParam s =
  let n = stringLength s
  if n >= 4 && stringSlice 0 3 s == "@P{" && stringSlice (n - 1) n s == "}" then
    productNorm (map decodeAxis (splitOnSemi (stringSlice 3 (n - 1) s)))
  else
    productNorm [("Host", PPrefix (Some s))]

-- decode one `Axis=val` axis spec.  val = `"…"` ⇒ Prefix; `{a,b}` ⇒ Set.
decodeAxis : String -> (String, Param)
decodeAxis spec = match splitOnFirstEqStr spec
  None => (spec, PUnit)          -- malformed (no '='): treated as ⊤ ⇒ dropped by productNorm
  Some i =>
    let name = stringSlice 0 i spec
    let rhs = stringSlice (i + 1) (stringLength spec) spec
    (name, decodeAxisVal rhs)

decodeAxisVal : String -> Param
decodeAxisVal rhs =
  let n = stringLength rhs
  if n >= 2 && stringSlice 0 1 rhs == "{" && stringSlice (n - 1) n rhs == "}" then
    PSet (Some (decodeSetParam rhs))
  else if n >= 2 && stringSlice 0 1 rhs == "\"" && stringSlice (n - 1) n rhs == "\"" then
    PPrefix (Some (stringSlice 1 (n - 1) rhs))
  else
    PPrefix (Some rhs)

-- index of the first '=' in a string, or None.
splitOnFirstEqStr : String -> Option Int
splitOnFirstEqStr s = splitOnFirstEqGo s 0 (stringLength s)

splitOnFirstEqGo : String -> Int -> Int -> Option Int
splitOnFirstEqGo s i n =
  if i >= n then
    None
  else if stringSlice i (i + 1) s == "=" then
    Some i
  else
    splitOnFirstEqGo s (i + 1) n

-- split a string on ';' (product-axis separator).
splitOnSemi : String -> List String
splitOnSemi s = splitOnSemiGo s 0 0 []

splitOnSemiGo : String -> Int -> Int -> List String -> List String
splitOnSemiGo s start i acc =
  let n = stringLength s
  if i >= n then
    reverseL (stringSlice start n s :: acc)
  else if stringSlice i (i + 1) s == ";" then
    splitOnSemiGo s (i + 1) (i + 1) (stringSlice start i s :: acc)
  else
    splitOnSemiGo s start (i + 1) acc

-- split a string on ',' (Set-element separator; authority tokens contain none)
splitOnComma : String -> List String
splitOnComma s = splitOnCommaGo s 0 0 []

splitOnCommaGo : String -> Int -> Int -> List String -> List String
splitOnCommaGo s start i acc =
  let n = stringLength s
  if i >= n then
    reverseL (stringSlice start n s :: acc)
  else if stringSlice i (i + 1) s == "," then
    splitOnCommaGo s (i + 1) (i + 1) (stringSlice start i s :: acc)
  else
    splitOnCommaGo s start (i + 1) acc

atomsOfWritten : List (String, Option String) -> List Atom
atomsOfWritten ls = atomsNorm (map atomOfWritten ls)

-- ── v2 Stage 2b: known-literal-prefix abstract analysis α (design §2.4) ─────
-- Abstract a DESUGARED core string-producing expr to a known literal prefix or
-- Unknown.  Intraprocedural (fork (c)): EApp results, fn-parameter EVars, field
-- access ⇒ Unknown ⇒ ⊤.  `lets` carries let-bound RHSs in scope for the EVar
-- rule.  (Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's `alpha`.)
data KP = Known String | Unknown

-- longest common prefix as a KP (Unknown if empty)
kpLcp : String -> String -> KP
kpLcp a b =
  let k = commonPrefixLen a b 0
  if k == 0 then Unknown else Known (stringSlice 0 k a)

-- `++`: the left operand's known prefix is fixed regardless of the right (§2.4)
alphaLeftPrefix : KP -> KP
alphaLeftPrefix (Known pa) = Known pa
alphaLeftPrefix Unknown = Unknown

alpha : List (String, Expr) -> Expr -> KP
alpha lets (ELoc _ e) = alpha lets e
alpha lets (EDoOrigin _ e) = alpha lets e
alpha lets (EAnnot e _) = alpha lets e
alpha lets (EHeadAnnot e _) = alpha lets e
alpha _ (ELit (LString s)) = Known s
alpha lets (EBinOp op a _ _)
  | op == "++" = alphaLeftPrefix (alpha lets a)
alpha _ (EBinOp _ _ _ _) = Unknown
alpha lets (EVar x) = match lookupAssoc x lets
  Some rhs => alpha lets rhs
  None => Unknown
alpha lets (ELet _ _ (PVar x) e1 e2) = alpha ((x, e1)::lets) e2
alpha lets (ELet _ _ _ _ e2) = alpha lets e2
alpha lets (ELetGroup binds body) = alpha (alphaCollectBinds binds lets) body
alpha lets (EIf _ t f) = match alpha lets t
  Known a => match alpha lets f
    Known b => kpLcp a b
    Unknown => Unknown
  Unknown => Unknown
alpha lets (EMatch _ arms) = alphaArms lets None arms
alpha _ _ = Unknown

-- propagate single-clause zero-param where-bindings (`x = e`) into scope
alphaCollectBinds : List LetBind -> List (String, Expr) -> List (String, Expr)
alphaCollectBinds [] acc = acc
alphaCollectBinds ((LetBind n clauses)::rest) acc = match clauses
  [FunClause [] rhs] => alphaCollectBinds rest ((n, rhs)::acc)
  _ => alphaCollectBinds rest acc

-- LCP of all arm results if every arm is Known; else Unknown
alphaArms : List (String, Expr) -> Option String -> List Arm -> KP
alphaArms _ (Some s) [] = Known s
alphaArms _ None [] = Unknown
alphaArms lets acc ((Arm _ _ rhs)::rest) = match alpha lets rhs
  Unknown => Unknown
  Known s => match acc
    None => alphaArms lets (Some s) rest
    Some a => match kpLcp a s
      Known c => alphaArms lets (Some c) rest
      Unknown => Unknown

-- the FIRST argument of an application spine: `f a b c` ⇒ Some a (locked dec 1)
spineFirstArg : Expr -> Option Expr
spineFirstArg (ELoc _ e) = spineFirstArg e
spineFirstArg (EDoOrigin _ e) = spineFirstArg e
spineFirstArg (EApp f x) = match spineHeadIsApp f
  True => spineFirstArg f
  False => Some x
spineFirstArg _ = None

-- is the application head itself a (curried) application?  `EApp (EApp ..) _`
-- ⇒ recurse left; `EApp (EVar/leaf) x` ⇒ x is the first arg.
spineHeadIsApp : Expr -> Bool
spineHeadIsApp (ELoc _ e) = spineHeadIsApp e
spineHeadIsApp (EDoOrigin _ e) = spineHeadIsApp e
spineHeadIsApp (EApp _ _) = True
spineHeadIsApp _ = False

-- the delimiter footgun guard (design §2.5 / fork (b)): a written Prefix pattern
-- must end in `*` or contain a `/` delimiter; a bare prefix is ambiguous.
prefixPatternOk : String -> Bool
prefixPatternOk s =
  let n = stringLength s
  n > 0 && (stringSlice (n - 1) n s == "*" || strHasSlash s)

strHasSlash : String -> Bool
strHasSlash s = strHasSlashGo s 0 (stringLength s)

strHasSlashGo : String -> Int -> Int -> Bool
strHasSlashGo s i n =
  if i >= n then
    False
  else if stringSlice i (i + 1) s == "/" then
    True
  else
    strHasSlashGo s (i + 1) n

-- forall <quantified tyvar ids> <quantified effvar ids>. mono
public export data Scheme = Forall (List Int) (List Int) Mono

-- ── global inference state ────────────────────────────────────────────────
tyvarCounter : Ref Int
tyvarCounter = Ref 0

currentLevel : Ref Int
currentLevel = Ref 0

newTyvarId : Unit -> Int
newTyvarId _ =
  let n = tyvarCounter.value
  let _ = setRef tyvarCounter (n + 1)
  n

freshVar : Unit -> Mono
freshVar _ = TVar (Ref (Unbound (newTyvarId ()) currentLevel.value))

-- ── effect variables + rows (Phase 79/146) ────────────────────────────────
effvarCounter : Ref Int
effvarCounter = Ref 0

newEffvarId : Unit -> Int
newEffvarId _ =
  let n = effvarCounter.value
  let _ = setRef effvarCounter (n + 1)
  n

-- the empty closed row — a pure arrow
pureRow : EffRow
pureRow = EffRow [] None

freshEffvar : Unit -> Ref Effvar
freshEffvar _ = Ref (EUnbound (newEffvarId ()) currentLevel.value)

freshEffvarAt : Int -> Ref Effvar
freshEffvarAt lvl = Ref (EUnbound (newEffvarId ()) lvl)

-- an open row with a fresh tail variable
openRow : Unit -> EffRow
openRow _ = EffRow [] (Some (freshEffvar ()))

-- follow ELink tails (merging labels) to a canonical row whose tail, if present,
-- is an unbound effvar
effrowNorm : EffRow -> EffRow
effrowNorm (EffRow labels tail) = match tail
  Some cell => match cell.value
    ELink r2 =>
      let nr = effrowNorm r2
      match nr
        EffRow ls2 t2 => EffRow (atomsUnion labels ls2) t2
    EUnbound _ _ => EffRow labels tail
  None => EffRow labels tail

-- the concrete labels (atoms) currently known for a row (ignores the open tail)
export effrowLabels : EffRow -> List Atom
effrowLabels r = match effrowNorm r
  EffRow labels _ => labels

-- v2 Stage 2b: fill inferred-hole atoms in a freshly-incurred call effect row.
-- Returns a row whose head labels have every hole atom replaced by α of the
-- call's first spine argument (Unknown / no-arg ⇒ ⊤); non-hole atoms and the
-- open tail are preserved.  When no hole is present the row is unchanged.
-- `lets` is the enclosing function body's α-let scope (EFFECTS WS-2): seeded into
-- α so an outer-body `let dest = "…"; fetch dest` recovers the literal prefix.
-- (Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's fill_holes_in_row.)
fillHolesInRow : List (String, Expr) -> EffRow -> Option Expr -> EffRow
fillHolesInRow lets eff firstArg = match effrowNorm eff
  EffRow labels tail =>
    if anyHoleAtom labels then
      EffRow (map (fillHoleAtom lets firstArg) labels) tail
    else
      eff

anyHoleAtom : List Atom -> Bool
anyHoleAtom [] = False
anyHoleAtom (a::rest) =
  if isHoleParam (atomParam a) then
    True
  else
    anyHoleAtom rest

fillHoleAtom : List (String, Expr) -> Option Expr -> Atom -> Atom
fillHoleAtom lets firstArg a =
  if isHoleParam (atomParam a) then
    Atom (atomLabel a) (holeFillParam (atomLabel a) lets firstArg)
  else
    a

-- DOMAIN-DIRECTED hole fill (WS-3b): the recovered literal becomes the right
-- shape for the LABEL's domain.  Prefix-domain (Net/Exec) ⇒ `PPrefix (Some s)`;
-- Set-domain (Env) ⇒ a singleton authority set `PSet (Some [s])`.  No recovery
-- (no arg / Unknown) ⇒ the domain's ⊤ (`dtopFor label`), NOT a hardcoded
-- `PPrefix None` — an unfilled Env hole must degrade to `PSet None`, not Prefix-⊤.
-- WS-4: Product-domain (Net) ⇒ Host=recovered-literal, Method=⊤ (α has no method
-- info — a sound over-approximation).
holeFillParam : String -> List (String, Expr) -> Option Expr -> Param
holeFillParam label lets (Some e) = match alpha lets e
  Known s => match dtopFor label
    PSet None => PSet (Some (sortUniqS [s]))
    PPrefix None => PPrefix (Some s)
    PProduct _ => productNorm [("Host", PPrefix (Some s))]
    _ => dtopFor label
  Unknown => dtopFor label
holeFillParam label _ None = dtopFor label

effvarLevel : Ref Effvar -> Int
effvarLevel cell = match cell.value
  EUnbound _ l => l
  ELink _ => 0

-- sorted + deduplicated string set (canonical effect-label order, matching the
-- OCaml `List.sort_uniq String.compare` so multi-label rows render identically)
-- sortUniqS / sortUniqSGo / sortInsertS moved to support/util.mdk (imported above).

enterLevel : Unit -> Unit
enterLevel _ = setRef currentLevel (currentLevel.value + 1)

exitLevel : Unit -> Unit
exitLevel _ = setRef currentLevel (currentLevel.value - 1)

-- ── effect propagation (Phase 79) ─────────────────────────────────────────
-- the latent effect of the function body currently being inferred: application
-- arms widen it; entering a lambda saves+reopens it, and the accumulated row
-- becomes the lambda's arrow effect.  A closed (tail=None) ambient is a SINK that
-- discards effects — the top-level state where a value binding's effects drop.
curEffect : Ref EffRow
curEffect = Ref (EffRow [] None)

-- the unbound-tail id of an effvar cell (-1 once linked); unique like tyvar ids,
-- so id-equality stands in for the reference's physical `==` on effvars
effvarId : Ref Effvar -> Int
effvarId cell = match cell.value
  EUnbound id _ => id
  ELink _ => 0 - 1

-- record that the current context performs row [eff]'s effects, widening ambient
-- [cur] WITHOUT closing its tail (so the next performed effect can still be
-- absorbed).  A closed ambient is a sink and discards.  An open [eff] is tied to
-- the ambient through a shared fresh tail.
performEffect : Ref EffRow -> EffRow -> Unit
performEffect cur eff = match effrowNorm cur.value
  EffRow _ None => ()
  EffRow curLabels (Some ct) => match effrowNorm eff
    EffRow effLabels effTail =>
      let _ = setRef cur (EffRow (atomsUnion curLabels effLabels) (Some ct))
      match effTail
        Some et => if effvarId et == effvarId ct then () else performLink ct et
        None => ()

performLink : Ref Effvar -> Ref Effvar -> Unit
performLink ct et =
  let v3 = freshEffvarAt (minI (effvarLevel ct) (effvarLevel et))
  let _ = setRef ct (ELink (EffRow [] (Some v3)))
  setRef et (ELink (EffRow [] (Some v3)))

-- unify two effect rows.  PERMISSIVE (Phase 79): an open row absorbs the other
-- side's labels by binding its tail; two open rows route through one shared fresh
-- tail.  Differing closed rows / an open row whose labels exceed a closed bound
-- are left as-is (the laundering subset check lands in Phase 146, Stage D).
unifyRow : EffRow -> EffRow -> Unit
unifyRow r1 r2 = unifyRowN (effrowNorm r1) (effrowNorm r2)

unifyRowN : EffRow -> EffRow -> Unit
unifyRowN (EffRow l1 (Some v1)) (EffRow l2 (Some v2))
  | effvarId v1 == effvarId v2 = ()
  | otherwise =
    let v3 = freshEffvarAt (minI (effvarLevel v1) (effvarLevel v2))
    let _ = setRef v1 (ELink (EffRow (atomsDiff l2 l1) (Some v3)))
    setRef v2 (ELink (EffRow (atomsDiff l1 l2) (Some v3)))
unifyRowN (EffRow l1 (Some v1)) (EffRow l2 None) =
  let _ = effectLeakCheck (atomsEscape l1 l2) l2
  setRef v1 (ELink (EffRow (atomsDiff l2 l1) None))
unifyRowN (EffRow l1 None) (EffRow l2 (Some v2)) =
  let _ = effectLeakCheck (atomsEscape l2 l1) l1
  setRef v2 (ELink (EffRow (atomsDiff l1 l2) None))
unifyRowN (EffRow _ None) (EffRow _ None) = ()

-- SOUNDNESS (Phase 146): an open row (an inference-synthesized arrow, i.e. a
-- concrete value's latent effect) meeting a closed bound — its own labels must
-- not exceed the bound, else they escape.  [escaping] = open labels \ bound.
effectLeakCheck : List Atom -> List Atom -> Unit
effectLeakCheck [] bound = ()
effectLeakCheck escaping bound =
  pushTypeError "T-EFFECT-LEAK" (effectLeakMsg bound escaping)

effectLeakMsg : List Atom -> List Atom -> String
effectLeakMsg bound extras = "Effectful value used where <\{renderAtoms bound}> is allowed, but it performs <\{renderAtoms extras}>"

-- v2 Stage 2a: diagnostic for a rejected written effect param (mirror of
-- lib/typecheck.ml's EffectParam message).
effectParamMsg : String -> String -> String
effectParamMsg label why = "Invalid effect parameter on <\{label}>: \{why}"

-- Runtime B1 dedup: the shared "must end in '*' or contain a '/'…" rationale for
-- a bare prefix param, parameterized by the noun (`pattern` for a top-level
-- Prefix param, `Host pattern` for a product's Host axis).
prefixPatternErrMsg : String -> String -> String
prefixPatternErrMsg noun pat = "\{noun} \"\{pat}\" must end in '*' or contain a '/' delimiter (a bare prefix would admit a sibling host/path)"

-- validate every written effect param in a program's type annotations against
-- the registry: a Prefix label's pattern must pass prefixPatternOk; an atomic
-- label rejects a param outright.  Runs as a <Mut> pass (errors via
-- pushTypeError) — the OCaml side does this inline in atom_of_written.
checkEffectParams : List Decl -> Unit
checkEffectParams [] = ()
checkEffectParams (d::rest) =
  let _ = checkEffectParamsDecl d
  checkEffectParams rest

checkEffectParamsDecl : Decl -> Unit
checkEffectParamsDecl (DTypeSig _ _ t) = checkEffectParamsTy t
checkEffectParamsDecl (DExtern _ _ t) = checkEffectParamsTy t
checkEffectParamsDecl (DAttrib _ d) = checkEffectParamsDecl d
checkEffectParamsDecl _ = ()

checkEffectParamsTy : Ty -> Unit
checkEffectParamsTy (TyFun a b) =
  let _ = checkEffectParamsTy a
  checkEffectParamsTy b
checkEffectParamsTy (TyApp a b) =
  let _ = checkEffectParamsTy a
  checkEffectParamsTy b
checkEffectParamsTy (TyTuple ts) = checkEffectParamsTys ts
checkEffectParamsTy (TyEffect labels _ t) =
  let _ = checkEffectAtoms labels
  checkEffectParamsTy t
checkEffectParamsTy (TyConstrained _ t) = checkEffectParamsTy t
checkEffectParamsTy _ = ()

checkEffectParamsTys : List Ty -> Unit
checkEffectParamsTys [] = ()
checkEffectParamsTys (t::ts) =
  let _ = checkEffectParamsTy t
  checkEffectParamsTys ts

checkEffectAtoms : List (String, Option String) -> Unit
checkEffectAtoms [] = ()
checkEffectAtoms ((_, None)::rest) = checkEffectAtoms rest
checkEffectAtoms ((l, Some pat)::rest) =
  let _ = checkOneEffectParam l pat
  checkEffectAtoms rest

checkOneEffectParam : String -> String -> Unit
checkOneEffectParam l pat = match dtopFor l
  PPrefix None =>
    -- v2 Stage 2b: the inferred hole "_" carries no written pattern to validate.
    if isHoleStr pat then ()
    else if prefixPatternOk pat then ()
    else pushTypeError "T-EFFECT-PARAM" (effectParamMsg l (prefixPatternErrMsg "pattern" pat))
  PSet None =>
    -- Set-domain param is a brace-set literal `{a,b}` (parser-encoded); always well-formed.
    ()
  PProduct _ =>
    checkProductAxes l (decodeProductParam pat)
  _ => pushTypeError "T-EFFECT-PARAM" (effectParamMsg l
    "label '\{l}' is atomic and takes no parameter (declare it `effect \{l} Prefix` to parameterize)")

-- WS-4: validate a decoded product's axes: a Host (Prefix) axis must pass
-- prefixPatternOk; a Method (Set) axis is always well-formed; any other axis name
-- is rejected (only Host/Method are known product axes for Net).
checkProductAxes : String -> Param -> Unit
checkProductAxes l (PProduct axes) = checkProductAxesGo l axes
checkProductAxes _ _ = ()

checkProductAxesGo : String -> List (String, Param) -> Unit
checkProductAxesGo _ [] = ()
checkProductAxesGo l ((name, p)::rest) =
  let _ = checkOneAxis l name p
  checkProductAxesGo l rest

checkOneAxis : String -> String -> Param -> Unit
checkOneAxis l "Host" (PPrefix (Some pat)) =
  if prefixPatternOk pat then
    ()
  else
    pushTypeError "T-EFFECT-PARAM" (effectParamMsg l (prefixPatternErrMsg "Host pattern" pat))
checkOneAxis _ "Method" _ = ()
checkOneAxis l name _ =
  pushTypeError
    "T-EFFECT-PARAM"
    (effectParamMsg
      l
      ("unknown product axis '" ++ name ++ "' (known axes: Host, Method)"))

-- replace a row's bound tail effvar with its fresh instance from [esub], so each
-- call site of a polymorphic HOF gets its own effect variable
substRow : List (Int, Ref Effvar) -> EffRow -> EffRow
substRow esub r = match effrowNorm r
  EffRow labels (Some v) => match v.value
    EUnbound id _ => match lookupAssocI id esub
      Some v2 => EffRow labels (Some v2)
      None => EffRow labels (Some v)
    ELink _ => EffRow labels (Some v)
  EffRow labels None => EffRow labels None

-- lower a row's tail effvar level to <= [level] alongside the tyvar being bound
lowerRowLevels : Int -> EffRow -> Unit
lowerRowLevels level r = match effrowNorm r
  EffRow _ (Some v) => match v.value
    EUnbound id lvl => if lvl > level then setRef v (EUnbound id level) else ()
    ELink _ => ()
  EffRow _ None => ()

-- build a curried arrow carrying the body's latent effect [eff] on the LAST arrow
-- only; intermediate arrows stay pure-open (partial application performs nothing)
arrowsWithLastEffect : List Mono -> EffRow -> Mono -> Mono
arrowsWithLastEffect [] _ ret = ret
arrowsWithLastEffect [pt] eff ret = TFun pt eff ret
arrowsWithLastEffect (pt::rest) eff ret =
  TFun pt (openRow ()) (arrowsWithLastEffect rest eff ret)

-- free generalizable effect variables: a row tail effvar deeper than the current
-- scope (deduped by the caller, mirroring freeGenVars)
freeEffvars : Mono -> List Int
freeEffvars t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => match cell.value
    Link t2 => freeEffvars t2
    Unbound _ _ => []
  TCon _ => []
  TApp a b => freeEffvars a ++ freeEffvars b
  TFun a r b => freeEffvars a ++ rowFreeEffvars r ++ freeEffvars b
  TEff r => rowFreeEffvars r

rowFreeEffvars : EffRow -> List Int
rowFreeEffvars r = match effrowNorm r
  EffRow _ (Some v) => match v.value
    EUnbound id lvl => if lvl > currentLevel.value then [id] else []
    ELink _ => []
  EffRow _ None => []

-- the declared concrete effect set of a signature: labels on the first `<…>`
-- reached walking down the return side (Phase 79e escape check)
declaredEffects : Ty -> List Atom
declaredEffects (TyFun _ ret) = declaredEffects ret
declaredEffects (TyEffect effs _ _) = atomsOfWritten effs
declaredEffects (TyConstrained _ t) = declaredEffects t
declaredEffects _ = []

-- record metadata: name → (quantified param ids, result mono, field types).  A
-- global Ref (like the reference's record table) so field access / create /
-- update / patterns can look records up without threading an env field.
public export data RecordInfo = RecordInfo (List Int) Mono (List (String, Mono))

recordsRef : Ref (List (String, RecordInfo))
recordsRef = Ref []

-- Keyed indices over `recordsRef`, maintained in lockstep at every registration
-- (registerRecordInfoKeyed) and cleared with it (resetState) — so the hot
-- per-field-access read paths never walk the full registry (issue #149).
--   recordByNameRef : registry KEY → RecordInfo.  omInsert is last-write-wins and
--     the register site inserts in registration order, so it reproduces the old
--     prepend-list first-match (latest-registered wins) for lookupRecordByName.
--   fieldOwnersRef  : field name → the registry keys whose RecordInfo declares it
--     (a multimap).  fieldOwnerNames still `sortUniqS`es this so the owner-list
--     order that feeds ambiguousFieldMsg/headL stays byte-identical.
recordByNameRef : Ref (OrdMap RecordInfo)
recordByNameRef = Ref omEmpty

fieldOwnersRef : Ref (OrdMap (List String))
fieldOwnersRef = Ref omEmpty

-- Kind of a data-type parameter, inferred from usage: KRow iff the param name
-- appears in a field's effect-tail position (`<e>`), else KType.  Drives
-- kind-aware type-app elaboration (a KRow arg becomes a TEff row, not a TApp
-- tyvar) and ctor-result-type construction.  Per-type, positional.
public export data Kind = KType | KRow

dataParamKindsRef : Ref (List (String, List Kind))
dataParamKindsRef = Ref []

-- Transparent type aliases (`type Foo = Int`, `type Pair a = (a, a)`).  Keyed by
-- alias name → (params, RHS surface type).  Populated in a pre-pass by
-- `registerData`'s DTypeAlias arm (mirrors `dataParamKindsRef`); consulted
-- on-demand at the `Ty→Mono` seam (`fromAstTypeE`) to expand an alias to its RHS.
-- The param list is stored even though stage 2 only expands the zero-param case,
-- so parameterized expansion (stage 3) can extend this without reshaping the table.
aliasTableRef : Ref (List (String, (List String, Ty)))
aliasTableRef = Ref []

-- Names of record/named-field types exported ABSTRACTLY (`export data`/`export
-- record` without `public`).  Seeded ONCE (over EVERY module's decls, prelude
-- included) by the multi-module check drivers, BEFORE per-module checking, and
-- deliberately NOT cleared by resetState — it is whole-program metadata, not a
-- per-module accumulator.  An abstract export brings the type NAME into scope but
-- never registers its fields (publicDataDecls drops VisAbstract decls, so they
-- reach neither recordsRef nor dataParamKindsRef in an importer).  A dot-access on
-- such a type therefore lands at resolveFieldRecord's `head known but no record
-- info` branch with the type name still recoverable as the receiver's head tycon;
-- membership here distinguishes that case (clear abstract-export diagnostic) from a
-- genuinely-unknown field on an unbound receiver (the `<unknown>` message).  This
-- mirrors resolve's destructure-side AbstractFieldAccess signal (env.types ∋ owner
-- ∧ owner owns no field) without threading anything new through the import seed.
abstractRecordTypesRef : Ref (List String)
abstractRecordTypesRef = Ref []

-- collect the names of all VisAbstract record-shaped (a DData whose variants
-- carry named fields) decls across the given decl list.  Only
-- record-shaped abstract types can be a dot-access receiver, but harvesting any
-- abstract DData name too is harmless (a positional ADT can't reach the abstract
-- branch with a field access — its fields never exist).
collectAbstractRecordTypes : List Decl -> List String
collectAbstractRecordTypes [] = []
collectAbstractRecordTypes ((DData VisAbstract n _ _ _)::rest) =
  n :: collectAbstractRecordTypes rest
collectAbstractRecordTypes (_::rest) = collectAbstractRecordTypes rest

-- seed abstractRecordTypesRef from every module's decls (the per-module progs plus
-- the prelude/core decls), once, before checking begins.
seedAbstractRecordTypes : List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> Unit
seedAbstractRecordTypes coreDecls modules =
  setRef
    abstractRecordTypesRef
    (collectAbstractRecordTypes coreDecls ++ flatMap (m => collectAbstractRecordTypes (snd m)) modules)

-- return-position method sites discovered during inference: (method name, the
-- EMethodAt route ref to fill, the EMethodAt impl-dicts ref to fill, the method's
-- result mono).  After the whole program is inferred, each result mono's head
-- tycon is the concrete impl tag the site dispatches to (RKey) — or, when it is a
-- constrained function's constraint variable, that function's dict parameter
-- (RDict).  The impl-dicts ref is filled (single-module elaborate path only) when
-- the route is a parametric impl with a `requires` — one route per requires.
pendingSites : Ref (List (String, Ref Route, Ref (List Route), Mono, Mono, String))
pendingSites = Ref []

-- Phase 151 / Gap G: comparison/equality OPERATOR sites discovered during
-- inference: (method name, the EBinOp route ref to fill, the operand mono).
-- After inference resolveBinopSites stamps each ref with the resolved Eq/Ord impl
-- route (RKey) — but ONLY when the operand grounds to a NON-primitive type; the
-- structural-builtin EBinOp eval path handles primitive / ungroundable operands
-- (so `eq a b = a == b` on Int never recurses).  dictPass then rewrites the
-- stamped node into the method application.
-- (method, route ref, operand mono, inImpl-flag, enclosing-fn name).  The 5th
-- field is currentFn at record time: for a TOP-LEVEL constrained fn an operator on
-- its own constraint var routes RDict $dict_<encl>_<slot> (via enclDictVarOf), the
-- operator analogue of the direct `eq x y` arg-stamp path.
pendingBinopSites : Ref (List (String, Ref Route, Mono, Bool, String))
pendingBinopSites = Ref []

-- Roadmap #18c: the EUnOp analogue of pendingBinopSites — `-` (Num's `negate`)
-- sites discovered during inference.  Same tuple shape/fields, same resolve/
-- rewrite seam (resolveUnopSites / rewriteUnopExpr), one operand instead of two.
pendingUnopSites : Ref (List (String, Ref Route, Mono, Bool, String))
pendingUnopSites = Ref []

-- SHARED-FLOAT-RESIDUAL §3(C): arithmetic EBinOp sites whose operand type may
-- ground to a concrete primitive.  Recorded during inference (operand Mono + the
-- node's route ref); resolveArithSites zonks each Mono after the whole module is
-- inferred and, ONLY when it grounds to concrete Float, stamps RScalar "Float" so
-- lowering carries the scalar type into CBinPrim and the native emitter picks the
-- Float primitive without re-deriving the operand LTy structurally.  A still-
-- polymorphic (Num a) operand never grounds → stays RNone → today's dict path
-- (mirrors resolveBinopSite's grounded-only guard, so dict-routed arithmetic is
-- untouched).  Int operands are left unstamped (RNone ⇒ the Int primitive is the
-- default anyway) to avoid perturbing every integer binop's IR.
pendingArithSites : Ref (List (Ref Route, Mono))
pendingArithSites = Ref []

-- Error-path BLOCKER B4 (audit finding D3): missing-impl dispatch obligations.
-- methodIfaceParamsRef maps each interface method NAME → (iface name, the iface's
-- typarams, the method's declared AST type).  Built once per checkProgramSeeded
-- from the program's DInterface decls.  At each EVar/EMethodAt occurrence of a
-- registered method, recordImplObligation captures the OCCURRENCE mono so that,
-- after inference, checkImplObligations can read what the interface params
-- resolved to (parallel-walking the declared type against the normalized
-- occurrence mono) and reject the call when those concrete head types have NO
-- matching impl in the program — mirroring lib/typecheck.ml's check_method_usages
-- (`No impl of … for …`, lib/typecheck.ml:3845).  Skips while a param is still a
-- polymorphic tyvar (deferred to the caller, exactly like the oracle).
-- #147: keyed by method NAME (was a program-sized assoc List scanned linearly on
-- hot per-node paths).  omInsert is last-write-wins and the register site inserts
-- in the SAME order the old List prepended, so on a method-name collision the
-- latest-registered entry wins — identical to the old `lookupAssoc` first-match.
methodIfaceParamsRef : Ref (OrdMap (String, List String, Ty))
methodIfaceParamsRef = Ref omEmpty

-- #147: cached SET of iface names with ≥1 method registered above, so
-- `ifaceRegistered` is an O(log n) membership test instead of a full linear scan
-- of the method table per operator/numeric-literal.  A separate key (iface name,
-- not method name) and NOT derived from the method map: two interfaces sharing a
-- method name collapse to one entry in the (name-keyed) map, but BOTH must stay
-- registered here — matching the old full-List scan.  Maintained in lockstep at
-- the two mutation sites (registerMethodIfaceParamsMethods and resetState).
registeredIfacesRef : Ref (OrdMap Unit)
registeredIfacesRef = Ref omEmpty

-- C5 (TYPECHECK-AUDIT / Phase 112): names that are BOTH an interface method AND a
-- cross-module-imported standalone top-level function (e.g. box's `toList`/`isEmpty`
-- shadowing Foldable's).  Populated per module by elabModuleStamp from the full graph
-- (a top-level funDef name that is also a method name and is NOT defined in the
-- current module).  resolveSite consults it: a method site at a concrete receiver
-- with NO impl, whose name is here, routes RLocal (eval reads the standalone) instead
-- of RKey<head> (which would panic with no impl).  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
-- env.standalone_values + the EApp RLocal stamp (typecheck.ml:1603-1640,4585).
-- NOT cleared by resetState — elabModuleStamp rebuilds it per module.
standaloneValuesRef : Ref (List String)
standaloneValuesRef = Ref []

-- DEFINER-shadow names (facet 2): the names THIS module declares as a top-level
-- funDef that ALSO name an interface method (buildDefinerShadows) — e.g. a user
-- `eq : List Int -> List Int -> Bool`.  A DIRECT call `eq [1] [2]` in user code
-- must resolve to the user's OWN definition (RLocal), NOT to a method impl, even
-- when the receiver's head HAS an impl of the interface (`List` has `Eq`).  The
-- arg-stamp pass would otherwise stamp `RKey:List` (the impl), making the eval path
-- dispatch to the prelude `Eq (List a)` impl (`[1] ≠ [2]` → False) instead of the
-- user's fn (True) — a run≠build divergence (the EMIT path already routes to the
-- user's def).  resolveRLocalSite stamps these RLocal unconditionally.  Populated
-- per-module in checkModuleFullImpl from buildDefinerShadows; reset by resetState.
definerShadowNamesRef : Ref (List String)
definerShadowNamesRef = Ref []

-- P0-19: the declared top-level SIGNATURES of definer-shadow standalones
-- (`size : Int -> Int`), keyed by name.  When a definer-shadow occurrence resolves
-- to the STANDALONE (no visible impl for the grounded receiver, or an ungrounded
-- receiver), inferDefinerShadowApp enforces this declared domain against the actual
-- argument — closing the S2/S5 "must then type against the standalone's domain" hole
-- that let `size "hi"` / a poly wrapper `useIt x = size x; useIt (Box 3)` type against
-- the permissive method scheme and reach codegen as garbage.  Set per-path from
-- `sigsOf prog`; empty for an unsignatured standalone (no domain to enforce → the old
-- permissive behavior, unchanged).
definerShadowSigsRef : Ref (List (String, Ty))
definerShadowSigsRef = Ref []

-- P0-19 d4b (S4): True while inferring the HEAD of a definer-shadow APPLICATION
-- (`size (Box 3)`) — set around the head-infer call in inferDefinerShadowVarApp /
-- inferDefinerShadowApp.  A shadow occurrence reaches inferVar / inferMethodAt in
-- two shapes: as an application head (dispatch-vs-standalone decided per-receiver by
-- the surrounding shadow-app machinery, so the head must keep the permissive METHOD
-- scheme `a -> Int`) OR in bare VALUE position (`map size xs`, `let f = size`), which
-- S4 says ALWAYS denotes the standalone.  This flag distinguishes the two so only the
-- value-position occurrence is pinned to the standalone scheme.  Default/reset False.
shadowHeadCtxRef : Ref Bool
shadowHeadCtxRef = Ref False

-- P0-18 (EMIT path only): the mangler (`mangleUnits`) renames a definer-shadow
-- standalone def + its call sites to `<mid>__name` BEFORE marking, but (correctly)
-- leaves the interface method name bare — so the mark pass's NAME-based shadow
-- detector (buildStandaloneShadowsGraph / buildDefinerShadows, keyed on
-- funDef-name ∩ iface-method-name) no longer sees a shadow and the occurrence never
-- becomes an `EMethodAt` dispatch site (→ a silent direct call to the standalone =
-- the build miscompile).  elaborateModules recovers the shadow post-mangle by
-- forward-constructing `mangledName mid method` for every (module, iface-method)
-- pair and checking it against the module's actual funDef names; each hit is a
-- `(mangledSymbol ↦ bareMethod)` entry recorded here.  The mark pass then (a) marks
-- the mangled occurrence `EMethodAt` with the BARE dispatch name (so `implFor` finds
-- the impl for a receiver that HAS one) and (b) carries the mangled symbol in the
-- route's `RLocal <symbol>` fallback for a receiver that does NOT.  EMPTY on the
-- un-mangled run/check path (forward-construction never matches a bare funDef), so
-- every non-emit path is byte-identical.  Set once at the top of elaborateModules;
-- deliberately NOT cleared by resetState (checkModuleFullImpl's per-module resetState
-- must not wipe it mid-graph) — the forward-construction membership check makes a
-- stale value inert (no funDef matches → no entry).
mangledShadowMapRef : Ref (List (String, String))
mangledShadowMapRef = Ref []

-- the MANGLED standalone symbol a route's RLocal fallback should call, or "" for
-- the bare-name (un-mangled) case.  Read off the marked EMethodAt route at record
-- time (before resolveArgStamps may overwrite the ref with an RKey) so the mangled
-- symbol survives into the pendingRLocalSites entry.
routeLocalSym : Route -> String
routeLocalSym (RLocal s _) = s
routeLocalSym _ = ""

-- Bug C: importer-shadow method names → their IMPORTED STANDALONE scheme (e.g.
-- `toList` → `Map k v -> List (k, v)`).  env1 rebinds these names to the polymorphic
-- INTERFACE-METHOD scheme (so a genuine method call types polymorphically), which
-- loses the standalone's concrete result element type — typing `toList m : List a`
-- (a free) instead of `List (String, Int)`, so a downstream `debug` dispatches on the
-- ungrounded element and renders garbage.  inferAppExpr's standalone-shadow arm reads
-- THIS to recover the standalone result type when the receiver has no impl, mirroring
-- lib/typecheck.ml:2312 (`instantiate_raw (lookup_var env mname)`, where the oracle's
-- env.standalone_values keeps the var bound to the standalone).  Populated per module
-- in checkModuleFullImpl from seedVars (the import seed, pre-rebind).
shadowStandaloneSchemesRef : Ref (List (String, Scheme))
shadowStandaloneSchemesRef = Ref []

-- Bug C: the impl key table for the CURRENT module's full impl universe, stashed at
-- module start so inferAppExpr's standalone-shadow arm can ask, DURING inference,
-- whether a concrete receiver head has an impl of the method's interface (the static
-- analogue of resolveRLocalSite's post-inference check, but needed eagerly so the
-- call's RESULT type unifies against the standalone — not the method scheme — before
-- a downstream `debug` dispatches on it).  Set in checkModuleFullImpl from implDecls.
shadowKeyTableRef : Ref KeyBuckets
shadowKeyTableRef = Ref omEmpty

-- P0-19 (constrained-receiver arm): the `=>`-constraint variables of the top-level
-- group CURRENTLY being inferred, as (interface name, the constraint's instantiated
-- mono).  Set by processSCC between preunifySigsEx (which instantiates the sigs) and
-- inferMembers (which infers the bodies), cleared right after — funConstraintsRef /
-- activeDictVars are only registered AFTER inference (they need the surviving
-- union-find root), so the bodies themselves cannot ask those which of their tyvars
-- are dict-bound.  Read by definerReceiverIsDictVar: a definer-shadow occurrence whose
-- receiver is one of these vars DISPATCHES through the dict and is NOT a standalone.
groupConstraintMonosRef : Ref (List (String, Mono))
groupConstraintMonosRef = Ref []

-- C5: method name → dispatch-arg index, built graph-wide from ALL interface decls
-- (so a PRELUDE method like Foldable's `toList` — absent from a user module's own
-- decls — still resolves its index during that module's check).  Set per module by
-- checkModuleFullImpl from [implDecls].  Independent of methodIfaceParamsRef (which
-- the module path populates only for the module's OWN interfaces).
methodDispatchIdxRef : Ref (List (String, Int))
methodDispatchIdxRef = Ref []

-- FLAT-PATH SHADOW FIX (facet 1): interface-method names that a USER top-level fn
-- shadows on the single-file flat check path.  processSCC drops a scheme with one
-- of these names from the env it THREADS FORWARD, so a later prelude binding
-- (`neq x y = not (eq x y)`) resolves the name to the METHOD scheme (kept in the
-- base env / globalS) rather than the user's shadowing fn.  Set by checkProgramSeeded
-- right before processTopGroups; empty everywhere else (multi-module path checks
-- core in isolation and never needs this).  See checkProgramSeeded for rationale.
methodShadowNamesRef : Ref (List String)
methodShadowNamesRef = Ref []

-- FLAT-PATH SHADOW SCOPING (SHADOW-SEMANTICS S1: shadow-hood is per-module, per-name).
-- The flat path hands checkProgramSeededSplit ONE program, `core ++ user`, and then
-- applied the definer-shadow name set to EVERY occurrence in it — INCLUDING the
-- prelude's own bodies.  So a user file that merely DEFINES (and never uses)
-- `map : Int -> Int` made core's `map2`/`map3`/`replaceWith`/`discard` type their own
-- `map` calls against the USER's `map`, yielding 14 errors — all inside core, all
-- reported at a fabricated `1:0` on the user's signature.  (Once SHADOW-SEMANTICS S2 is
-- inverted so a standalone WINS over a same-named method, the same leak stops merely
-- mistyping and starts ROUTING the prelude's calls into user code — a silent miscompile
-- of the prelude.)  The multi-module path never had this: checkModuleFullImpl seeds the
-- shadow set PER MODULE and checks core in ISOLATION.
--
-- These three refs reproduce that isolation on the flat path WITHOUT splitting the
-- inference: definerShadowNamesRef is toggled OFF while a CORE-owned letrec group (and
-- core's impl/default/prop/test bodies) is inferred and back ON for the user's, so every
-- consumer of that ref (definerShadowVarHead / definerShadowArgHead /
-- maybeStandaloneValueMono / recordImplObligation / resolveRLocalSite) becomes
-- per-module for free.  Empty/False on every other path — resetState clears them and
-- ONLY a checkProgramSeededSplit with a NON-EMPTY prelude sets them — so the
-- multi-module path and the prelude-free probe paths are byte-identical.
flatShadowScopingRef : Ref Bool
flatShadowScopingRef = Ref False

-- the prelude's own top-level fn names (a set: this is consulted once per letrec group).
flatCoreFnNamesRef : Ref (OrdMap Unit)
flatCoreFnNamesRef = Ref omEmpty

-- the definer-shadow names of the USER module only — what definerShadowNamesRef holds
-- whenever a user-owned group/body is being inferred.
flatUserShadowNamesRef : Ref (List String)
flatUserShadowNamesRef = Ref []

-- C5: arg-position method occurrences whose name is a standalone-shadow, recorded
-- UNGATED (the ordinary arg-stamp machinery is emit-path-only, but eval also
-- arg-tag-dispatches and panics on a receiver with no impl — box's `toList b`).
-- Each entry is (name, route ref, the discriminating-argument mono CELL);
-- resolveRLocalSites reads the cell post-inference and stamps RLocal when the
-- receiver grounds to a head with no impl.
-- Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's EApp-receiver RLocal stamp.  Reset by resetState.
-- (name, routeRef, receiverMono, mangledSym, forceLocal) — mangledSym (P0-18) is the
-- mangled standalone symbol read off the marked route at record time; resolveRLocalSite
-- stamps `RLocal mangledSym` for a no-impl receiver.  "" on the un-mangled path.
-- P0-20: forceLocal — the definer-shadow APP path (inferDefinerShadowApp) has ALREADY
-- committed the site's TYPE to the standalone's scheme, so its route is not open to
-- re-derivation: post-inference the receiver can ground to a head that HAS an impl (Num
-- defaulting on an unsignatured standalone) and resolveRLocalSite would dispatch a site
-- typed as the standalone — check says one thing, the binary does another.  True pins
-- the route to RLocal.  recordRLocalSite (value position / importer shadows) pushes
-- False and keeps the old per-receiver re-derivation.
-- S-1: `dictMonos`/`dictIfaces` are the SHADOWING STANDALONE's own `=>`-constraint
-- slots (slot-parallel, expanded-supers, from shadowStandaloneDicts), carried here so
-- the stamp can turn them into dict Routes.  They are read INSIDE resolveRLocalSites
-- via routesOfMonosTop — a PURE function of the union-find-solved monos — rather than
-- pushed through pendingDictApps, because resolveRLocalSites runs BEFORE
-- resolveDictApps in elabModuleStamp: routing them through pendingDictApps would read
-- `[]` and reproduce the S-1 bug with more code.  This way the fix is ordering-immune
-- and the route has a single writer.  Empty for an unconstrained standalone.
-- (A record, not a 6/7-tuple: Medaka tuples top out at arity 5.)
public export data RLocalSite =
  | RLocalSite String (Ref Route) Mono String Bool (List Mono) (List String)

pendingRLocalSites : Ref (List RLocalSite)
pendingRLocalSites = Ref []

-- P0-18: while typing a definer-shadow method occurrence that heads a single-arg
-- application (`size (Box 3)`), suppress recordRLocalSite's default push — the
-- standalone scheme's declared domain (`Int`) is NOT the actual receiver type.
-- inferDefinerShadowApp instead records the ACTUAL argument mono so per-receiver
-- routing sees `Box` (which has a `Sizeable` impl → method) vs `Int` (no impl →
-- standalone).  Set only around that occurrence's `infer env f`; false everywhere else.
suppressRLocalRecord : Ref Bool
suppressRLocalRecord = Ref False

-- P0-18: same idea as suppressRLocalRecord for the EMIT path — while typing a
-- definer-shadow method occurrence heading an application, suppress recordArgStamp's
-- default push (standalone declared domain) so inferDefinerShadowApp can record the
-- ACTUAL argument mono into pendingArgStamps instead.
suppressArgStamp : Ref Bool
suppressArgStamp = Ref False

-- one entry per occurrence of a registered interface method: (iface, typarams,
-- declared method Ty, the occurrence's instantiated mono).  Resolved post-inference.
-- B.10.2c: the 5th element is the `currentLoc` span snapshotted at the RECORD
-- site (during inference, when the ELoc context is live), so the post-HM
-- obligation check (checkImplObligations) can pair a `No impl of …` reject with
-- the offending expression's range — mirror of lib/typecheck.ml capturing
-- `!current_loc` into method_usages / constraint_obligations at record time
-- rather than reading the stale global at the post-HM raise.
pendingImplObligations : Ref (List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc))
pendingImplObligations = Ref []

-- PERF: the obligation list's length, in O(1).  That list grows with every
-- constrained occurrence in the program and is NEVER trimmed, yet each top-level
-- group brackets its inference with a length-before / length-after pair to recover
-- the obligations IT added (they are consed on the front, so `takeFirst` recovers
-- them).  Measuring that length by walking the list was O(N) over an O(N) list once
-- per group — O(N²), and the single hottest symbol in the typecheck stage on a large
-- file.  This counter mirrors the list's length exactly; the ONLY mutators of the
-- list are `resetState` (clears both), `pushPendingObl` (extends both), and
-- inferDefaultMethodBody's error rollback (restores both from a snapshot).
pendingImplObligationsN : Ref Int
pendingImplObligationsN = Ref 0

-- The one and only way to add an obligation: keeps the length counter in step.
pushPendingObl : (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
pushPendingObl obl =
  let _ = setRef pendingImplObligations (obl::pendingImplObligations.value)
  setRef pendingImplObligationsN (pendingImplObligationsN.value + 1)

-- PLAN.md #11: one entry per source integer literal (ENumLit):
-- (litVar, fref, n, dref).  [litVar] is the literal's fresh Num-obligated type var;
-- [fref] is the node's own Option Float cell; [n] the int value; [dref] is the
-- node's `fromInt`-route cell (filled by resolveSites — the literal is modelled as a
-- `fromInt n` method occurrence).  After defaulting + grounding, the post-HM pass
-- setNumlitFloats decides the runtime rep (mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's
-- set_numlit_floats): litVar ground Float ⇒ fref Some, dref cleared ⇒ ELit (LFloat f);
-- litVar ground Int ⇒ both cleared ⇒ ELit (LInt n); litVar still polymorphic `Num a`
-- (the soundness case — `1` in `inc x = x + 1`) ⇒ dref KEPT (RDictFwd onto the
-- enclosing Num dict) ⇒ dictPass emits `EApp (EMethodAt "fromInt" …) (ELit (LInt n))`.
numlitRefs : Ref (List (Mono, Ref (Option Float), Int, Ref Route))
numlitRefs = Ref []

-- Chunk A (error framing): the union-find root ids of every type var that took
-- part in a unification failure (`typeMismatch`).  Consulted ONLY by
-- `checkUndeterminedObligation` to SUPPRESS a secondary `ambiguous instance`
-- cascade on a var the primary mismatch already explained — never read on the
-- inference path, so a well-typed program (no mismatch) never poisons anything
-- and behaves identically.
poisonedVars : Ref (List Int)
poisonedVars = Ref []

-- Chunk D (Num mis-framing): (litVar, loc) for every numeric literal whose var
-- carried a Num obligation — captured at inferNumLit/inferNumLitBare record time
-- while the var is still unbound.  Lets checkOneImplObligation reframe a purely
-- literal Num obligation forced to a concrete non-Num type (`Type mismatch: Int vs
-- T`) while an OPERATOR-sourced one (`x + 1`) keeps `No impl of Num for T`.  Storing
-- the VAR (not just the loc) lets the operator-taint pass below share a root through
-- `normalize`, so `1 + x` and `x + 1` are both recognised as operator-sourced
-- regardless of unify direction.  Error-path only; reset per run.
numlitVarLocs : Ref (List (Mono, Loc))
numlitVarLocs = Ref []

-- PERF: the literals that are NOT YET operator-tainted.  `markNumlitOpTaint` scans
-- THIS instead of the full `numlitVarLocs` (which is never trimmed).  Sound because
-- tainting is idempotent: it appends a loc to `numlitOpLocs`, whose only reader
-- (`numlitReframeLoc`) does a membership test — so re-tainting an already-tainted
-- literal cannot change any outcome, and a tainted literal may leave the worklist.
-- Scanning EVERY literal ever recorded on EVERY arithmetic operand was O(N²), and
-- after the allocation fix (see `rootIdOf`) it was still the typecheck stage's
-- dominant remaining cost on a large file.
--
-- `numlitVarLocs` itself keeps every literal — `numlitReframeLoc` and
-- `tagLitLocsWithRoot` both still need the complete list.
numlitUntainted : Ref (List (Mono, Loc))
numlitUntainted = Ref []

-- Chunk D: locs of literals whose var was ALSO an operand of an arithmetic op
-- (recordIfaceObligation).  Such a literal keeps the Num message — the operator is the
-- real culprit — so the reframe skips it (it reframes `litLocs \ opLocs`).
numlitOpLocs : Ref (List Loc)
numlitOpLocs = Ref []

-- Chunk D: (literalLoc, contextKind) recorded when inferIf / inferListLit / consOp
-- is about to force a still-unbound literal var against a concrete sibling type —
-- lets the reframe pick site-specific wording (if-branches / list elements / cons
-- head-vs-element) over the bare `Type mismatch: Int vs T`.
numlitCtxTags : Ref (List (Loc, String))
numlitCtxTags = Ref []

-- LSP hover: every local binding (let-bound name, lambda param, function-clause
-- param, match-arm binder) flows through inferPat's PVar/PAs arms.  Capture
-- (name, mono var) here so hover can render a *local* variable's inferred type:
-- `checkProgramSchemes` only returns top-level + global schemes, so without this
-- a hover over e.g. a `let input = …` name finds nothing and returns null.  The
-- mono var is union-find-solved by the time inference ends; checkProgramSeeded
-- then generalizes each into `localSchemesOut`, which the LSP reads as a hover
-- fallback.  This side-channel does NOT enter the returned env, so completion and
-- inlay-hint output (which consume the env directly) are unaffected.
localBindRefs : Ref (List (String, Mono))
localBindRefs = Ref []

localSchemesOut : Ref (List (String, Scheme))
localSchemesOut = Ref []

-- Hover side-channel for the SEED (runtime.mdk externs: putStrLn, readLine, …).
-- checkProgramSeeded keeps the seed in scope for inference but excludes it from
-- the returned env (see its comment), so externs aren't otherwise hoverable.
seedSchemesOut : Ref (List (String, Scheme))
seedSchemesOut = Ref []

-- Getters for the LSP (cross-module).  The raw Refs are nullary value globals
-- (@mdk_g_), which the import-aware mangling map doesn't carry, so expose them
-- through functions — the form every other tools→typecheck import already uses.
export currentLocalSchemes : Unit -> List (String, Scheme)
currentLocalSchemes _ = localSchemesOut.value

export currentSeedSchemes : Unit -> List (String, Scheme)
currentSeedSchemes _ = seedSchemesOut.value

recordLocalBind : String -> Mono -> Unit
recordLocalBind name v = setRef localBindRefs ((name, v)::localBindRefs.value)

-- dict-passing state (the typed pipeline's `=>`-constraint layer):
--   activeDictVars   : constraint tyvar id → the dict parameter name a method
--                      occurrence at that variable's type reads (RDict).
--   funConstraintsRef: constrained fn name → its constraints' quantified tyvar
--                      ids (one per `=>` constraint, in slot order) — a call site
--                      maps each to its fresh instantiation var to pick a route.
--   pendingDictApps  : a constrained-fn occurrence's routes ref + the instantiated
--                      constraint monos, resolved to RKey/RDict after inference.
activeDictVars : Ref (List (Int, String))
activeDictVars = Ref []

funConstraintsRef : Ref (List (String, List Int))
funConstraintsRef = Ref []

-- WS-1b: the program decls (interfaces) the supers-expansion reads.  Set by each
-- driver (elaborateDict / the module path) BEFORE inference; read by inferDictAtFound
-- to expand a call site's constraint slots with their transitive supers.  Read-only
-- during inference, so it needs no reset-lifecycle care.
superDeclsRef : Ref (List Decl)
superDeclsRef = Ref []

-- WS-1b: the USER-module interface names (NOT core/prelude).  Super-expansion is
-- gated to user interfaces only: a prelude super chain (Ord requires Eq) is already
-- serviced by arg-tag dispatch and is NOT dict-passed on the eval path, so adding an
-- Eq slot to `clamp : Ord a =>` would desync its def arity from its arg-tag call sites
-- (the prior-attempt clamp regression).  User interfaces (Sub requires Sup) ARE
-- dict-passed and need the super slot.  Set by elaborateModules.
userIfaceNamesRef : Ref (List String)
userIfaceNamesRef = Ref []

-- #11 soundness (native obligation hole): the INTERFACE names of a constrained
-- fn's `=>` constraints, slot-parallel to funConstraintsRef's ids.  funConstraintsRef
-- keeps only the tyvar ids (enough to pick a dict route); but to record a real impl
-- obligation when a constrained fn is INSTANTIATED at a concrete type (`g = f "hello"`
-- where `f : Num a => a -> a`), the call site needs the iface ("Num") so it can check
-- `Num String` against the impl table.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's fun_constraints
-- carrying the iface name into check_constraint_obligations.  Populated at the SAME
-- three sites as funConstraintsRef (registerMember / registerInferredFor /
-- seedDictAritiesFromSigs), with the SAME cross-module accumulate+reseed lifecycle.
funConstraintIfacesRef : Ref (List (String, List String))
funConstraintIfacesRef = Ref []

-- a constrained-fn CALL site's instantiated constraint obligations: (iface, the
-- call's instantiated constraint mono).  Resolved after inference (when the mono is
-- ground) by checkCallObligations — a CONCRETE-head mono with no matching impl is an
-- ERROR; a still-polymorphic head is DEFERRED (left to a further-out caller).  This
-- is the compiler analog of the oracle's constraint_obligations + check_constraint_
-- obligations: pendingDictApps routes the dict, this list checks the impl exists.
pendingCallObligations : Ref (List (String, Mono, Option Loc))
pendingCallObligations = Ref []

-- PERF: `pendingCallObligations`' length in O(1) — see `pendingImplObligationsN`.
-- processSCC brackets each top-level group with a length-before / length-after pair
-- (three reads per group), and this list is never trimmed, so walking it to measure
-- its length was O(N) per group — O(N²) over a large file.  Mutated ONLY by
-- `resetState` and `pushCallObl`.
pendingCallObligationsN : Ref Int
pendingCallObligationsN = Ref 0

pushCallObl : (String, Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
pushCallObl obl =
  let _ = setRef pendingCallObligations (obl::pendingCallObligations.value)
  setRef pendingCallObligationsN (pendingCallObligationsN.value + 1)

-- #11 soundness (native obligation hole, ALWAYS-ON, independent of the dict-
-- elaboration machinery): per top-level binding, the deferred impl obligations it
-- generalizes over — (iface, the quantified-var id the obligation discriminates on).
-- Populated UNCONDITIONALLY at generalization in processSCC/processTopGroups (unlike
-- funConstraintsRef, which the check path leaves empty because its inferred-constraint
-- registration is gated on dictEligibleSetRef).  At a USE site (inferVar) the binding's
-- entry is freshened through the call's instantiation subst and checked against the
-- impl table — the compiler analog of lib/typecheck.ml's inferred_constraints carried
-- on a scheme and re-checked at every instantiation.
schemeObligationsRef : Ref (List (String, List (String, Int)))
schemeObligationsRef = Ref []

-- E6 (module emit path only): funConstraintsRef is wiped by resetState at the start
-- of EACH module's typecheck, but a cross-module `=>`-constrained CALL site (entry
-- calling an imported `clampU`) must still find the callee's generalized constraint
-- ids so inferDictAt records a real pendingDictApp (→ the call gets its dict ARG).
-- This ref ACCUMULATES every module's registered fn-constraints across the graph and
-- is NOT cleared by resetState; checkModuleFullImpl re-seeds funConstraintsRef from
-- it after the per-module reset (gated emitArgStampPasses — empty + unused when OFF, so
-- the golden module drivers are byte-identical).  The DEFINING module's entry (real
-- ids, registered first) wins the lookupAssocSL2 first-match.
crossModuleFunConstraintsRef : Ref (List (String, List Int))
crossModuleFunConstraintsRef = Ref []

-- WS-2/D2: module-attributed mirror of crossModuleFunConstraintsRef.  Keyed by
-- (owning module id, fn name) so the FINAL dict-pass (scopeArities) can keep only
-- the inferred-arity entry whose DEFINING module is in the dict-passed module's
-- scope.  Without this, two distinct top-level fns sharing a bare name but with
-- different `=>`-constraint arity (e.g. `wrap : Tag a => …` in module A vs
-- `wrap : (P a, Q a) => …` in module B) both land in the bare cross-module table;
-- scopeArities keeps both (both pass the bare scopeNames membership), and
-- dictArityOf's first-match returns the WRONG module's arity → a call site in A's
-- importer is sized to B's 2 dict params (over-application) → run-path crash while
-- the emit path's arg-stamp recovers (the §1 D2 repro).  Populated per-module in
-- checkModuleFullImpl (each fn DEFINED in this module's `prog` is attributed to mid);
-- consumed by scopeAritiesQual.  The bare crossModuleFunConstraintsRef is KEPT — the
-- per-module reseed at checkModuleFullImpl:7546 needs the bare form for the current
-- module's E6 cross-module call-site discovery.
crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef : Ref (List ((String, String), List Int))
crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef = Ref []

-- cross-module accumulator for funConstraintIfacesRef, mirroring
-- crossModuleFunConstraintsRef exactly (accumulate across the graph, NOT cleared by
-- resetState, re-seeded after the per-module reset).
crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef : Ref (List (String, List String))
crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef = Ref []
