    let _ = pushTypeError "T-UNKNOWN-RECORD" (unknownRecordMsg name)
    (freshVar (), bindRecPatFieldsFresh env fields)
  Some ri => inferPatRecWith env name ri fields
-- bind the field pattern vars with fresh types (no field-type info) so the
-- arm body still typechecks; the oracle fails fast on the unknown record, we
-- accumulate, so dropping the bindings would raise spurious unbound-var errors

-- bind a record pattern's fields against fresh types, NO unknown-field check
-- (the record itself is already-reported unknown; see inferPatRec error path)
bindRecPatFieldsFresh : TcEnv -> List RecPatField -> List (String, Scheme)
bindRecPatFieldsFresh _ [] = []
bindRecPatFieldsFresh env ((RecPatField fname mp)::rest) = inferRecPatFieldWith env (freshVar ()) fname mp
  ++ bindRecPatFieldsFresh env rest

inferPatRecWith : TcEnv -> String -> RecordInfo -> List RecPatField -> (Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPatRecWith env rname ri fields =
  let ir = instantiateRecord ri
  (fst ir, inferRecPatFields env rname (snd ir) fields)

inferRecPatFields : TcEnv -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List RecPatField -> List (String, Scheme)
inferRecPatFields _ _ _ [] = []
inferRecPatFields env rname fieldTypes ((RecPatField fname mp)::rest) = inferRecPatField env rname fieldTypes fname mp
  ++ inferRecPatFields env rname fieldTypes rest

inferRecPatField : TcEnv -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> String -> Option Pat -> List (String, Scheme)
inferRecPatField env rname fieldTypes fname mp = match lookupAssoc fname fieldTypes
  None =>
    let _ = pushTypeError "T-UNKNOWN-FIELD" (unknownFieldMsg fname rname)
    inferRecPatFieldWith env (freshVar ()) fname mp
  Some ft => inferRecPatFieldWith env ft fname mp
-- bind the field's pattern vars against a fresh type so the arm body still
-- typechecks (the oracle fails fast on the unknown field; we accumulate, so
-- skipping the binding would raise spurious downstream unbound-variable errors)

inferRecPatFieldWith : TcEnv -> Mono -> String -> Option Pat -> List (String, Scheme)
inferRecPatFieldWith _ ft fname None = [(fname, monoScheme ft)]
inferRecPatFieldWith env ft _ (Some p) =
  let pr = inferPat env p
  let _ = unify (fst pr) ft
  snd pr

litType : Lit -> Mono
litType (LInt _) = TCon "Int"
litType (LFloat _) = TCon "Float"
litType (LString _) = TCon "String"
litType (LChar _) = TCon "Char"
litType (LBool _) = TCon "Bool"
litType LUnit = TCon "Unit"

inferPatCon : TcEnv -> String -> List Pat -> (Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPatCon env name pats = match lookupCtor env name
  None =>
    let _ = pushTypeError "T-UNKNOWN-CTOR" (unknownCtorMsg name)
    let pr = inferPats env pats
    (freshVar (), snd pr)
  Some scheme => inferPatConWith env scheme pats
-- still infer the sub-patterns so their bindings reach the arm body
-- (else the continued path raises spurious unbound-variable errors the
-- oracle never reaches — it fails fast on the unknown constructor)

inferPatConWith : TcEnv -> Scheme -> List Pat -> (Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPatConWith env scheme pats =
  let ctorType = instantiate scheme
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  let resultVar = freshVar ()
  let _ = unify ctorType (foldrFun (fst pr) resultVar)
  (resultVar, snd pr)

inferPats : TcEnv -> List Pat -> (List Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPats _ [] = ([], [])
inferPats env (p::ps) =
  let pr = inferPat env p
  let rest = inferPats env ps
  (fst pr :: fst rest, snd pr ++ snd rest)

foldrFun : List Mono -> Mono -> Mono
foldrFun [] result = result
foldrFun (a::rest) result = TFun a pureRow (foldrFun rest result)

inferPatTuple : TcEnv -> List Pat -> (Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPatTuple env pats =
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  (tupleMono (fst pr), snd pr)

inferPatCons : TcEnv -> Pat -> Pat -> (Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPatCons env h t =
  let hr = inferPat env h
  let tr = inferPat env t
  let listTy = TApp (TCon "List") (fst hr)
  let _ = unify (fst tr) listTy
  (listTy, snd hr ++ snd tr)

inferPatList : TcEnv -> List Pat -> (Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPatList env pats =
  let elem = freshVar ()
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  let _ = unifyAll elem (fst pr)
  (TApp (TCon "List") elem, snd pr)

inferPatAs : TcEnv -> String -> Pat -> (Mono, List (String, Scheme))
inferPatAs env x p =
  let pr = inferPat env p
  let _ = recordLocalBind x (fst pr)
  (fst pr, (x, monoScheme (fst pr)) :: snd pr)

unifyAll : Mono -> List Mono -> Unit
unifyAll _ [] = ()
unifyAll t (x::xs) =
  let _ = unify t x
  unifyAll t xs

-- ── expression inference ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
infer : TcEnv -> Expr -> Mono
infer _ (ELit l) = litType l
-- PLAN.md #11: a source integer literal is `Num a` for a fresh `a`.  With the
-- prelude loaded (Num registered + `fromInt` in scope), model the literal exactly
-- as `fromInt n` — mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's e7031e6 ENumLit arm.  inferMethodAt
-- "fromInt" records the Num impl obligation AND a pendingSite keyed on [dref] (the
-- node's route cell), so resolveSites later stamps [dref]: RKey when the literal's
-- type grounds to a concrete Num head, RDictFwd onto the enclosing `Num a` dict when
-- it stays polymorphic (the soundness case, `1` in `inc x = x + 1` — exactly the
-- route `fromInt 0` in `sum` gets).  Apply the `Int -> a` method type to an Int arg
-- (mirror OCaml's `unify from_int_ty (TFun t_int … a)`) to recover the result var
-- [a], and stash (a, fref, n, dref) in numlitRefs so the post-HM setNumlitFloats can
-- decide concrete-Float / concrete-Int / still-poly.  Recording the obligation here
-- AND (when under `+`) in numArithOp is harmless: both unify the same var, both
-- demand `Num`.  Without the prelude (bare-HM oracle, `ifaceRegistered` False)
-- `fromInt` is unbound: fall back to a plain fresh var, dref stays RNone (⇒ Int).
infer env (ENumLit n fref dref) = inferNumLit env n fref dref
infer env (EVar x) = inferVar env x
infer env (EApp f x) = inferAppExpr env f x
infer env (ELam pats body) = inferLam env pats body
infer env (ELet isMut isFun pat e1 e2) = inferLet env isMut isFun pat e1 e2
infer env (ELetGroup binds body) = inferLetGroup env binds body
infer env (EIf c t e) = inferIf env c t e
infer env (EMatch scrut arms) = inferMatch env (infer env scrut) arms
infer env (ETuple es) = tupleMono (inferEach env es)
infer env (EListLit es) = inferListLit env es
infer env (EAnnot e ty) = inferAnnot env e ty
infer env (EBinOp op l r dref) = inferBinopE env op l r dref
infer env (EUnOp op e dref) = inferUnopE env op e dref
infer env (EInfix op l r) = inferInfix env op l r
infer env (ERecordCreate name fields) = inferRecordCreate env name fields
infer env (EFieldAccess e fname r) = inferFieldAccess env e fname r
infer env (ERecordUpdate base fields r) = inferRecordUpdate env base fields r
infer env (EVariantUpdate cname base fields) =
  inferVariantUpdate env cname base fields
infer env (EBlock stmts) = inferBlock env stmts
infer env (EMethodAt name tagRef implRef methodRef) =
  inferMethodAt env name tagRef implRef methodRef
infer env (EDictAt name routesRef) = inferDictAt env name routesRef
infer env (EArrayLit es) = inferArrayLit env es
infer env (ERangeList lo hi _) = inferIntRange env lo hi (TCon "List")
infer env (ERangeArray lo hi _) = inferIntRange env lo hi (TCon "Array")
-- EIndex is desugared to an `index` method call before typecheck (Phase #16b);
-- the built-in index-inference path is retired, so no `EIndex` arm here.
infer env (ESlice arr lo hi _ r) = inferSlice env arr lo hi r
infer env (EHeadAnnot e ty) = inferHeadAnnot env e ty
-- ELoc is transparent: capture the span in `currentLoc` (so any type error
-- recorded while inferring the wrapped expr is attributable to this position,
-- mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's ELoc arm), then infer the inner expr.
infer env (ELoc l e) =
  let _ = setRef currentLoc (Some l)
  infer env e
-- Phase 150: a do-lowered chain.  Record the do-block loc so a monad-shape
-- mismatch during inference is retargeted to the tailored "do requires a monad"
-- error (see typeMismatch); clear it afterwards so sibling code isn't mis-blamed.
infer env (EDoOrigin l e) =
  let _ = setRef currentDoOrigin (Some l)
  let t = infer env e
  let _ = setRef currentDoOrigin None
  t
infer _ _ = panic "typecheck: unsupported expression (slice 1)"

inferArrayLit : TcEnv -> List Expr -> Mono
inferArrayLit env es =
  let elem = freshVar ()
  let _ = unifyAll elem (inferEach env es)
  TApp (TCon "Array") elem

-- [lo..hi] : List Int, [|lo..hi|] : Array Int
inferIntRange : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono -> Mono
inferIntRange env lo hi container =
  let _ = unify (infer env lo) (TCon "Int")
  let _ = unify (infer env hi) (TCon "Int")
  TApp container (TCon "Int")

-- receiver-kind discriminator stamped onto the ESlice Ref: lowering routes
-- "String" to CStringSlice; "List"/"Array" stay on the array path.
indexKind : Mono -> String
indexKind (TCon "String") = "String"
indexKind (TApp (TCon "List") _) = "List"
indexKind _ = "Array"

-- a.[lo..hi] : same container type as a
inferSlice : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Expr -> Ref String -> Mono
inferSlice env arr lo hi r =
  let _ = unify (infer env lo) (TCon "Int")
  let _ = unify (infer env hi) (TCon "Int")
  let at = infer env arr
  let _ = setRef r (indexKind (normalize at))
  at

-- a method occurrence.  Return-position: record (routeRef, result mono) so
-- resolveSites can stamp RKey/RDict.  D3a arg-position (emitArgStampPasses + name in
-- argDispatchIdxRef): the dispatch is decided by the DISCRIMINATING-ARGUMENT type,
-- not the result, so queue recordArgStamp on the arg-position mono instead — it is
-- NOT a pendingSites/methodSiteFns return-position site.
inferMethodAt : TcEnv -> String -> Ref Route -> Ref (List Route) -> Ref (List Route) -> Mono
inferMethodAt env name tagRef implRef methodRef = match lookupVar env name
  None => panic ("unbound method: " ++ name)
  Some s =>
    let inst = instantiateTracked s
    let _ = recordMethodDicts name methodRef (snd inst)
    let _ = recordImplObligation name (fst inst)
    let _ = recordRLocalSite env name tagRef (fst inst)
    let ret = match argDispatchOf name
      Some idx =>
        let _ = recordArgStamp name tagRef implRef idx (fst inst)
        let _ = recordArgSiteFn name idx (fst inst)
        fst inst
      None => recordSite name tagRef implRef (fst inst)
    -- P0-19 d4b (S4): a bare value-position definer shadow (`map size xs`, marked to
    -- `EMethodAt`) denotes the STANDALONE — return its declared scheme type so the type
    -- mismatches live-impl elements (Box vs Int) instead of dispatching, while the route
    -- side-effects above still stamp RLocal (ungrounded receiver) so `map size [1,2,3]`
    -- runs the standalone.  Inert for an application head (shadowHeadCtxRef True).
    match maybeStandaloneValueMono name
      Some m => m
      None => ret
-- C5: if [name] is a standalone-shadow method, record its discriminating-arg
-- mono cell UNGATED so resolveRLocalSites can route RLocal when the receiver
-- grounds to a no-impl head (the arg-stamp path would otherwise leave it RNone
-- → arg-tag panic on a receiver with no impl).

-- Cause-A prerequisite: ALSO record the discriminating-arg mono into
-- methodSiteFns keyed by currentFn (mirroring recordSite, which only fires
-- for return-position).  Without this, an unsignatured fn whose body
-- dispatches a method in ARGUMENT position (`f s = display s`) never
-- surfaces its inferred constraint to inferredConstraintIds → never gets
-- promoted → no leading dict param on the build path → dict word 0 (SIGSEGV/
-- silent).  Inert unless promotion (discoverPromotedModules) consumes it.

-- C5: queue a standalone-shadow method occurrence's discriminating-arg mono cell so
-- resolveRLocalSites can stamp RLocal once inference grounds the receiver.  Only
-- shadows (standaloneValuesRef) are recorded — every other method occurrence is
-- untouched (no entry → byte-identical).  The dispatch index comes from the method's
-- declared interface type (methodDispatchIdx), independent of emitArgStampPasses.
-- S-1: this is the GENERAL shadow-occurrence recorder, and it is the path that a
-- VALUE-position shadow (`map size [1,2,3]`) and an IMPORTER shadow on an UNGROUNDED
-- receiver (`size 3`, where the Num literal is not yet grounded so inferShadowApp's
-- standalone arm is never reached) both take.  Both must carry the standalone's dicts or
-- they under-apply exactly like the headline bug.  Slots only — NOT obligations; see
-- shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt.
recordRLocalSite : TcEnv -> String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
recordRLocalSite env name tagRef inst
  | suppressRLocalRecord.value = ()
  | not (contains name standaloneValuesRef.value) = ()
  | otherwise = match methodDispatchIdx name
    None => ()
    Some idx => match nthArgMono idx inst
      Some am =>
        let sym = routeLocalSym tagRef.value
        let dicts = shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt (standaloneSchemeFor env name sym) (if sym == "" then name else sym) am
        setRef
          pendingRLocalSites
          (RLocalSite name tagRef am sym False (fst dicts) (snd dicts) :: pendingRLocalSites.value)
      None => ()

-- the SHADOWING STANDALONE's scheme.  An IMPORTER shadow's bare name is rebound by env1
-- to the interface-METHOD scheme, so it must come from shadowStandaloneSchemesRef; a
-- DEFINER shadow's comes from the env (under the mangled symbol on the emit path).
standaloneSchemeFor : TcEnv -> String -> String -> Option Scheme
standaloneSchemeFor env name sym = match lookupAssoc name shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value
  Some s => Some s
  None => lookupVar env (if sym == "" then name else sym)

-- C5: the dispatch-arg index of an interface method, from the graph-wide map
-- methodDispatchIdxRef (built by checkModuleFullImpl from every interface decl).
-- Ungated, unlike argDispatchOf — RLocal detection must run on the eval path too.
methodDispatchIdx : String -> Option Int
methodDispatchIdx name = lookupAssoc name methodDispatchIdxRef.value

-- the dispatch-arg index of an arg-position method, but only when stamping is
-- enabled (the emit path) — off, every EMethodAt is a return-position site.
argDispatchOf : String -> Option Int
argDispatchOf name = lookupAssoc name argDispatchIdxRef.value

-- Phase 69.x-e: if [name] is a method with method-level constraints (foldMap's
-- `Monoid m`), map each constraint's scheme id through this occurrence's
-- instantiation subst to its concrete mono and record the (methodRef, monos) so
-- resolveMethodDicts can route them after inference.  A method with no method-level
-- constraint records nothing — methodRef stays [], eval's fold is a no-op.
recordMethodDicts : String -> Ref (List Route) -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
recordMethodDicts name methodRef subst = match alignedMethodConstraintIds name subst
  None => ()
  Some ids => setRef pendingMethodDicts ((methodRef, constraintMonosOf ids subst)::pendingMethodDicts.value)

-- D2: the constraint-slot ids of method [name] that line up with THIS occurrence's
-- instantiation [subst].  methodConstraintsRef can hold MULTIPLE entries for one
-- (uniquely-named) method, each registered with its own FRESH ids by a different
-- elaborate sweep (discoverPromotedModules' discovery sweep, then the real
-- elabWorker sweep) — crossModuleMethodConstraintsRef accumulates them across
-- sweeps and is not re-keyed before recordMethodDicts reads it.  A plain bare-name
-- first-match (lookupAssocSL2) returns whichever entry is frontmost, which after the
-- discovery sweep is a STALE entry whose ids belong to a dead id range, disjoint from
-- the live [subst] keys → constraintMonosOf drops them all → empty route → the
-- cross-module method-level dict is never threaded ("no matching impl" at run /
-- SIGSEGV at build).  The LIVE entry is the one the call's instantiateTracked actually
-- quantified — i.e. whose ids are keys of [subst].  Pick the entry with the most ids
-- present in [subst] (>0); fall back to bare first-match when none overlap (preserves
-- the single-entry / no-instantiation goldens byte-for-byte).  Diamond-safe: a method
-- re-exported through an intermediate keeps the ORIGINAL definer's scheme ids (seedVars
-- threads the scheme object verbatim), so its [subst] keys match the original entry,
-- which alignment selects regardless of intermediate sweeps.
alignedMethodConstraintIds : String -> List (Int, Mono) -> Option (List Int)
alignedMethodConstraintIds name subst = match positionMatch name subst methodConstraintPositionsRef.value
  Some ids => Some ids
  None =>
    let entries = filterList (e => fst e == name) methodConstraintsRef.value
    match entries
      [] => None
      _ => match bestAlignedEntry subst entries (0 - 1) None
        Some ids => Some ids
        None => lookupAssocSL2 name methodConstraintsRef.value

-- The DEFINITIVE slot ids for THIS occurrence: index the occurrence's ORDERED subst
-- (instantiateTracked keys subst by the scheme's own ids via freshSubst, preserving
-- var order) at the method's stable constraint-slot POSITIONS.  Allocation-
-- independent, so it picks the applicative `m` slot regardless of cross-sweep id
-- overlap (where a stale id maps to the container `t` — both Thenable).  None when
-- the method has no recorded positions, or a position is out of range (e.g. an empty
-- subst from a monomorphic recursive call) ⇒ caller falls back to countInSubst.
positionMatch : String -> List (Int, Mono) -> List (String, List Int) -> Option (List Int)
positionMatch name subst positionsTbl = match lookupAssocSL2 name positionsTbl
  None => None
  Some positions =>
    let keys = map fst subst
    let n = listLen keys
    if allInRange positions n then
      Some (map (p => nthInt p keys) positions)
    else
      None

allInRange : List Int -> Int -> Bool
allInRange [] _ = True
allInRange (p::rest) n = p >= 0 && p < n && allInRange rest n

nthInt : Int -> List Int -> Int
nthInt _ [] = 0 - 1
nthInt 0 (x::_) = x
nthInt i (_::xs) = nthInt (i - 1) xs

-- among [entries] (all the same-named method-constraint records), the ids of the one
-- with the most slots present in [subst]; None until a positive-overlap entry is seen.
bestAlignedEntry : List (Int, Mono) -> List (String, List Int) -> Int -> Option (List Int) -> Option (List Int)
bestAlignedEntry _ [] _ best = best
bestAlignedEntry subst ((_, ids)::rest) bestN best =
  let n = countInSubst ids subst
  if n > 0 && n > bestN then
    bestAlignedEntry subst rest n (Some ids)
  else
    bestAlignedEntry subst rest bestN best

countInSubst : List Int -> List (Int, Mono) -> Int
countInSubst [] _ = 0
countInSubst (id::rest) subst =
  (match lookupAssocI id subst
      Some _ => 1
      None => 0) +
    countInSubst rest subst

-- record a return-position method occurrence: resolveSite later stamps its route
-- (RKey/RDict) once inference grounds the result mono.
recordSite : String -> Ref Route -> Ref (List Route) -> Mono -> Mono
recordSite name tagRef implRef methodType =
  let stripped = stripArrows methodType
  let _ = setRef pendingSites ((name, tagRef, implRef, stripped, methodType, currentFn.value)::pendingSites.value)
  let _ = setRef methodSiteFns (consSiteFn currentFn.value stripped methodSiteFns.value)
  methodType

-- a constrained-function occurrence (`f` where `f : C a => …`): instantiate f's
-- scheme (the call's type), and record the routes ref + the instantiated
-- constraint monos so resolveDictApps can pick a dict route once the call's args
-- pin those monos.  funConstraintsRef[f] gives f's constraint tyvar ids, mapped
-- through the instantiation subst to this call's fresh vars.
inferDictAt : TcEnv -> String -> Ref (List Route) -> Mono
inferDictAt env name routesRef = match lookupVar env name
  None => panic ("unbound constrained fn: " ++ name)
  Some scheme => inferDictAtFound name routesRef (instantiateTracked scheme)

-- The callee's constraints are registered (funConstraintsRef has it) ⇒ a normal
-- call: map each constraint id through the instantiation subst to this call's
-- monos.  Absent ⇒ a self/mutually-recursive call (the callee is still inferring
-- in the same group); defer it for realizeRecDictApps to forward the enclosing
-- fn's dict.
-- A constrained callee in funConstraintsRef is reached two ways: (1) a NORMAL call
-- to an already-generalized fn — instantiateTracked pins each of its constraint ids
-- to a fresh occurrence var, so `snd inst` carries them; route via pendingDictApps.
-- (2) a SELF/MUTUALLY-RECURSIVE call — the callee is still the group's monomorphic
-- PLACEHOLDER (Forall [] [] v), so `snd inst` is empty (none of [ids] appear).  Here
-- the constraint var lives in the ENCLOSING fn's own type and the callee's REGISTERED
-- ids only become valid after this pass's registerInferredConstraints re-registers
-- them; defer to realizeRecDictApps (which forwards the enclosing dict).  We MUST NOT
-- take the normal branch here (as a stale cross-module funConstraintsRef seed makes us
-- do): constraintMonosOf maps the registered ids through the empty subst → [] → the
-- recursive call's route ref stays empty while dictPass still prepends the param → the
-- call under-applies → dispatch fails.  Detect (2) as "no registered id is pinned by
-- the instantiation subst" (mirrors the oracle's empty-`sub` self-recursion case).
-- WS-2/D2: a cross-module constrained callee's (dict ids, slot-parallel ifaces) by
-- MODULE IDENTITY.  When [name] is an EXPLICITLY-imported value (currentImportDefinersRef)
-- whose import SOURCE module owns a (source,name) entry in the qualified arity tables,
-- return that entry — the definer-keyed arity, immune to the bare-name most-recent-prepend
-- collision in the jointly-seeded funConstraintsRef.  None ⇒ local fn / wildcard import /
-- re-export / check path (mid ""): inferDictAtFound falls back to the bare funConstraintsRef
-- + funConstraintIfacesRef path (today's behavior; byte-identical when no collision, since
-- the qual entry is the same module's same ids the bare first-match returns sans collision).
qualConstraintFor : String -> Option (List Int, List String)
qualConstraintFor name = match lookupAssoc name currentImportDefinersRef.value
  None => None
  Some src => map (ids => (
    ids,
    fromOption [] (lookupQualIfaces src name crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesQualRef.value),
  )) (lookupQualArity src name crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef.value)

lookupQualArity : String -> String -> List ((String, String), List Int) -> Option (List Int)
lookupQualArity _ _ [] = None
lookupQualArity src name (((m, n), v)::rest)
  | m == src && n == name = Some v
  | otherwise = lookupQualArity src name rest

lookupQualIfaces : String -> String -> List ((String, String), List String) -> Option (List String)
lookupQualIfaces _ _ [] = None
lookupQualIfaces src name (((m, n), v)::rest)
  | m == src && n == name = Some v
  | otherwise = lookupQualIfaces src name rest

-- WS-2/D2: a constrained callee's declared (constraint tyvar ids, iface names).  Prefer
-- the (import-source, name)-keyed QUAL table for a cross-module callee; fall back to the
-- bare-name funConstraintsRef first-match for local / wildcard / re-export.
-- S-1: shared with shadowStandaloneDictSlots — an IMPORTED constrained standalone that
-- shadows a method is exactly a cross-module callee, so it needs the SAME resolution.
-- Reading only the bare-name table there left the importer shadow's dicts EMPTY on `run`
-- (funConstraintsRef is reset per module and only re-seeded from the cross-module table
-- on the EMIT path), so `build` was right and `run` was wrong — the inverse skew.
declaredConstraintSlots : String -> (List Int, List String)
declaredConstraintSlots name = match qualConstraintFor name
  Some (ids, ifs) => (ids, ifs)
  None => (
    fromOption [] (lookupAssocSL2 name funConstraintsRef.value),
    fromOption [] (lookupAssocS name funConstraintIfacesRef.value),
  )

inferDictAtFound : String -> Ref (List Route) -> (Mono, List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono
inferDictAtFound name routesRef inst =
  let q = qualConstraintFor name
  let slots = declaredConstraintSlots name
  let declaredIds = fst slots
  let declaredIfaces = snd slots
  let known = match q
        Some _ => True
        None => hasKeySL2 name funConstraintsRef.value
  if known && anyIdPinned declaredIds (snd inst) then
    -- WS-1b: expand the callee's constraint slots with their transitive supers HERE
    -- (the global table isn't expanded until expandSupersTable runs after inference),
    -- so this call site applies one dict per EXPANDED slot — matching the def params
    -- dictArityOf/dictPass will prepend from the expanded table.  Each super slot's id
    -- equals its sub slot's id (shared type param), still pinned in [snd inst].
    let expanded = expandSupersPairs superDeclsRef.value (zipL declaredIfaces declaredIds)
    let ids = map snd expanded
    let monos = constraintMonosOf ids (snd inst)
    let ifaces = map fst expanded
    let _ = pushDictApp (routesRef, monos, ifaces)
    let _ = setRef dictAppFns (consSiteFn currentFn.value monos dictAppFns.value)
    -- #11 soundness: record this call's instantiated impl obligations (iface paired
    -- with the call's constraint mono), checked after inference by checkCallObligations.
    -- The iface list is slot-parallel to [monos]; a concrete-head mono with no impl
    -- (`Num String` from `f "hello"` where `f : Num a => a -> a`) becomes an error.
    let _ = recordCallObligations ifaces monos
    fst inst
  else
    let _ = setRef pendingRecDictApps (RecDictApp routesRef name currentFn.value (fst inst) :: pendingRecDictApps.value)
    fst inst

-- True iff at least one of [ids] (the callee's registered constraint tyvar ids) is
-- a key of the instantiation subst — i.e. instantiateTracked actually quantified it,
-- so this is a normal generalized call.  False ⇒ the callee is a monomorphic
-- placeholder (self/mutual recursion); subst can't carry its constraint occurrence.
anyIdPinned : List Int -> List (Int, Mono) -> Bool
anyIdPinned [] _ = False
anyIdPinned (id::rest) subst = match lookupAssocI id subst
  Some _ => True
  None => anyIdPinned rest subst

-- pair each iface with its slot-parallel instantiated constraint mono, pushing onto
-- pendingCallObligations.  Length-zips defensively (an iface list shorter than the
-- mono list — e.g. a multi-param constraint whose ifaces weren't recorded — just
-- stops, never over-rejecting).
recordCallObligations : List String -> List Mono -> Unit
recordCallObligations [] _ = ()
recordCallObligations _ [] = ()
recordCallObligations (iface::ifs) (mono::ms)
  | iface == "" = recordCallObligations ifs ms
  | otherwise =
    let _ = pushCallObl (iface, mono, currentLoc.value)
    recordCallObligations ifs ms

-- String-keyed assoc lookup over funConstraintIfacesRef (returns the iface name list).
lookupAssocS : String -> List (String, List String) -> Option (List String)
lookupAssocS _ [] = None
lookupAssocS k ((k2, v)::rest)
  | k == k2 = Some v
  | otherwise = lookupAssocS k rest

hasKeySL2 : String -> List (String, List Int) -> Bool
hasKeySL2 _ [] = False
hasKeySL2 k ((k2, _)::rest)
  | k == k2 = True
  | otherwise = hasKeySL2 k rest

-- instantiate a scheme, returning both the mono and the (quantified id → fresh
-- var) substitution, so a caller can recover specific quantified variables
instantiateTracked : Scheme -> (Mono, List (Int, Mono))
instantiateTracked (Forall ids evars t) =
  let subst = freshSubst ids
  (substMonoP True subst (freshEffSubst evars) t, subst)

constraintMonosOf : List Int -> List (Int, Mono) -> List Mono
constraintMonosOf [] _ = []
constraintMonosOf (id::rest) subst = match lookupAssocI id subst
  Some m => m :: constraintMonosOf rest subst
  None => constraintMonosOf rest subst

lookupAssocSL2 : String -> List (String, List Int) -> Option (List Int)
lookupAssocSL2 _ [] = None
lookupAssocSL2 k ((k2, v)::rest)
  | k == k2 = Some v
  | otherwise = lookupAssocSL2 k rest

-- ── WS-1b: superclass-evidence flatten (port of lib/typecheck.ml expand_supers) ──
-- A `=>`-constrained fn whose body calls a SUPERCLASS method (e.g. `build : Sub a =>`
-- calls `mk`, a Sup method, where `Sub a requires Sup a`) needs an honest super-dict
-- slot threaded through.  Inference registers only the DECLARED constraints in
-- funConstraintsRef/funConstraintIfacesRef; this pass APPENDS, per entry, one extra
-- slot for each (transitive) superinterface, mapping the super's decl param-names to
-- the constraint's instantiated tyvar ids positionally (`Sub a`/`Sup a` share `a`, so
-- the super id == the sub id).  Appended AFTER the declared slots so existing slot
-- indices are unchanged; deduped by (iface, id) within an entry.  Because the dict
-- VALUE is just a type tag (VDict "Widget"), the super slot's route is identical to
-- the sub slot's — no separate projection is needed; the slot just has to EXIST so the
-- call site applies a matching dict and the def binds a matching param.
--
-- Mutates the TABLE refs themselves at ONE finalization point (after inference, before
-- realizeRecDictApps/dictPass) so EVERY reader — define-side dictArityOf and call-side
-- recRoutes/recRoute/inferDictAtFound — sees identical expanded entries automatically.
-- Transitive: a fixpoint over the constraint set (the WS-4a acyclic-supers guard makes
-- it terminate), so `Top` reaches `Base` through `Mid` (chain3b).
expandSupersTable : List Decl -> Unit
expandSupersTable allDecls =
  let _ = setRef funConstraintsRef (map (expandSupersEntry allDecls funConstraintIfacesRef.value) funConstraintsRef.value)
  let _ = setRef funConstraintIfacesRef (map (expandSupersIfaceEntry allDecls) funConstraintIfacesRef.value)
  ()

-- as expandSupersTable but operates on the cross-module snapshot pair (so a
-- cross-module constrained fn gets the same expansion — else it under-fills).
expandSupersCross : List Decl -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List String) -> (List (String, List Int), List (String, List String))
expandSupersCross allDecls idsTbl ifacesTbl = (
  map (expandSupersEntry allDecls ifacesTbl) idsTbl,
  map (expandSupersIfaceEntry allDecls) ifacesTbl,
)

-- expand one (fn, ids) entry: the parallel iface list is looked up by fn name.
expandSupersEntry : List Decl -> List (String, List String) -> (String, List Int) -> (String, List Int)
expandSupersEntry allDecls ifacesTbl (fn, ids) =
  let ifaces = fromOption [] (lookupAssocS fn ifacesTbl)
  let pairs = expandSupersPairs allDecls (zipL ifaces ids)
  (fn, map snd pairs)

-- expand one (fn, ifaces) entry the SAME way, returning the parallel iface names.
expandSupersIfaceEntry : List Decl -> (String, List String) -> (String, List String)
expandSupersIfaceEntry allDecls (fn, ifaces) =
  let ids = idsForIfaceSlots ifaces
  let pairs = expandSupersPairs allDecls (zipL ifaces ids)
  (fn, map fst pairs)

-- a synthetic id list parallel to the iface list when only the iface entry is in
-- hand (the iface-entry expansion must produce the same iface SEQUENCE as the id
-- entry; positions, not values, must agree).  Uses slot index as the id so the
-- super-name substitution + dedup walk identically.  NOTE: this works because in a
-- `requires` chain every slot in one fn's expansion shares its declared tyvar — but
-- DISTINCT declared constraints get distinct synthetic ids, so two unrelated
-- constraints sharing a super still dedup correctly per chain.
idsForIfaceSlots : List String -> List Int
idsForIfaceSlots ifaces = idsForIfaceSlotsGo 0 ifaces

idsForIfaceSlotsGo : Int -> List String -> List Int
idsForIfaceSlotsGo _ [] = []
idsForIfaceSlotsGo i (_::rest) = i :: idsForIfaceSlotsGo (i + 1) rest

-- transitive super-closure over the declared (iface, id) slots: start from the
-- declared slots, repeatedly append direct supers of every current slot until no
-- new (iface, id) pair appears.  Result is the declared slots first (order/indices
-- preserved) then the appended supers, deduped by (iface, id).
expandSupersPairs : List Decl -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
expandSupersPairs allDecls declared = expandSupersFix allDecls declared declared

expandSupersFix : List Decl -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
expandSupersFix allDecls acc frontier =
  let next = dedupPairs (acc ++ flatMap (superSlotsOf allDecls) frontier)
  if listLen next == listLen acc then
    acc
  else
    expandSupersFix allDecls next (dropPairs (listLen acc) next)

-- the direct super slots of one (iface, id) slot: look up iface's (typarams, supers);
-- the super-param NAMES index into typarams, which map positionally to [id] (a single
-- shared id since constraints in a `requires` chain are over the same type param).
superSlotsOf : List Decl -> (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
superSlotsOf allDecls (iface, id)
  | not (contains iface userIfaceNamesRef.value) = []   -- WS-1b: only expand USER interface supers (prelude chains stay arg-tag)
  | otherwise = match ifaceSupersOf allDecls iface
    None => []
    Some (typarams, supers) =>
      let subst = zipL typarams (replicate (listLen typarams) id)
      flatMap (superSlotOf subst) supers

-- one `Super superName params`: map each param name through subst → its id; emit the
-- super slot only if ALL params resolved (matches the oracle's length guard).
superSlotOf : List (String, Int) -> Super -> List (String, Int)
superSlotOf subst (Super superName params) =
  let mapped = filterMaybeIds (map (p => lookupAssoc p subst) params)
  if listLen mapped == listLen params then
    (superName, firstOrZero mapped)::[]
  else
    []

-- every super slot in a `requires` chain carries the SAME id; represent the slot by it.
firstOrZero : List Int -> Int
firstOrZero [] = 0
firstOrZero (x::_) = x

filterMaybeIds : List (Option Int) -> List Int
filterMaybeIds [] = []
filterMaybeIds ((Some x)::rest) = x :: filterMaybeIds rest
filterMaybeIds (None::rest) = filterMaybeIds rest

dropPairs : Int -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
dropPairs n xs
  | n <= 0 = xs
  | otherwise = match xs
    [] => []
    _::rest => dropPairs (n - 1) rest

-- dedup (iface, id) pairs, keeping first occurrence (so declared slots stay leading).
dedupPairs : List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
dedupPairs xs = dedupPairsGo xs []

dedupPairsGo : List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
dedupPairsGo [] _ = []
dedupPairsGo (p::rest) seen
  | anyList (q => fst q == fst p && snd q == snd p) seen =
    dedupPairsGo rest seen
  | otherwise = p :: dedupPairsGo rest (p::seen)

-- the type a method ultimately returns (strip its argument arrows); the impl is
-- chosen by this type's head tycon (pure : a -> f a → f a; empty : f a → f a)
stripArrows : Mono -> Mono
stripArrows (TFun _ _ b) = stripArrows b
stripArrows t = t

-- bare sequential block: value of the last statement; earlier statements typed
-- for effect; let/assign extend the env
inferBlock : TcEnv -> List DoStmt -> Mono
inferBlock _ [] = TCon "Unit"
inferBlock env [s] = inferLastStmt env s
inferBlock env (s::rest) = inferBlock (inferStmt env s) rest

inferLastStmt : TcEnv -> DoStmt -> Mono
inferLastStmt env (DoExpr e) = infer env e
inferLastStmt env s =
  let _ = inferStmt env s
  TCon "Unit"

inferStmt : TcEnv -> DoStmt -> TcEnv
inferStmt env (DoExpr e) =
  let _ = infer env e
  env
inferStmt env (DoLet _ True (PVar x) e) = blockRecLet env x e
inferStmt env (DoLet _ _ pat e) = blockLet env pat e
inferStmt env (DoAssign x e) = extendVar env x (monoScheme (infer env e))
-- A `<-` bind (DoBind) reaching a bare block is a user error: `<-` is
-- monadic-`do`-only, and a monadic `do` lowers to EDo (desugared before
-- typecheck), so only a bare-block `<-` arrives here.  Emit a clean LOCATED
-- error (symmetric with doRequiresMonadMsg) instead of panicking; infer the RHS
-- for effect (surfacing its own errors + snapshotting its ELoc span for the
-- caret) and bind the pattern's vars into env so downstream statements don't
-- cascade spurious `Unbound variable` errors (mirrors inferRecPatField).
inferStmt env (DoBind pat e) =
  let t = infer env e
  let _ = pushTypeError "T-BIND-OUTSIDE-DO" bindOutsideDoMsg
  let pr = inferPat env pat
  let _ = unify (fst pr) t
  extendVars env (snd pr)
inferStmt _ _ = panic "typecheck: unsupported block statement"

blockRecLet : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> TcEnv
blockRecLet env x e =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let placeholder = freshVar ()
  let envSelf = extendVar env x (monoScheme placeholder)
  let t1 = infer envSelf e
  let _ = unify placeholder t1
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  let addedObls = takeFirst (pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum addedObls t1  -- PLAN.md #11
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls [t1]  -- RETPOS soundness
  let _ = recordLocalBind x t1  -- LSP hover (block rec-let bypasses inferPat)
  seedAlphaLets (extendVar env x (generalize t1)) x e  -- WS-2: α scope-seed

blockLet : TcEnv -> Pat -> Expr -> TcEnv
blockLet env (PVar x) e =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let t = infer env e
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  let addedObls = takeFirst (pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum addedObls t   -- PLAN.md #11
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls [t]   -- RETPOS soundness
  let _ = recordLocalBind x t   -- LSP hover (block let bypasses inferPat)
  -- WS-2: α scope-seed — bindings are immutable, so the prefix is stable.
  let env1 = extendVar env x (genRestricted (isNonexpansive e) t)
  seedAlphaLets env1 x e
blockLet env pat e =
  let t = infer env e
  let pr = inferPat env pat
  let _ = unify (fst pr) t
  extendVars env (snd pr)

inferRecordCreate : TcEnv -> String -> List FieldAssign -> Mono
inferRecordCreate env name fields = match lookupRecordByName name
  None => unknownRecordFresh name
  Some ri => inferRecordCreateWith env name ri fields

inferRecordCreateWith : TcEnv -> String -> RecordInfo -> List FieldAssign -> Mono
inferRecordCreateWith env rname ri fields =
  let ir = instantiateRecord ri
  let _ = checkMissingFields rname (snd ir) fields
  let _ = unifyFieldAssigns env rname (snd ir) fields
  fst ir

unifyFieldAssigns : TcEnv -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List FieldAssign -> Unit
unifyFieldAssigns _ _ _ [] = ()
unifyFieldAssigns env rname fieldTypes ((FieldAssign fn val)::rest) =
  let _ = unifyFieldAssign env rname fieldTypes fn val
  unifyFieldAssigns env rname fieldTypes rest

unifyFieldAssign : TcEnv -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> String -> Expr -> Unit
unifyFieldAssign env rname fieldTypes fn val = match lookupAssoc fn fieldTypes
  None => pushTypeError "T-UNKNOWN-FIELD" (unknownFieldMsg fn rname)
  Some ft => unify (infer env val) ft

-- TYPECHECK-AUDIT OBS4: every declared field must be supplied in a record
-- construction (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml ERecordCreate "Every declared field
-- must be supplied" loop).  Walk `declaredFields`, check each name is present
-- among the supplied `FieldAssign` list; push MissingField for any absent one.
checkMissingFields : String -> List (String, Mono) -> List FieldAssign -> Unit
checkMissingFields _ [] _ = ()
checkMissingFields rname ((fname, _)::rest) supplied
  | fieldAssignContains fname supplied = checkMissingFields rname rest supplied
  | otherwise =
    let _ = pushTypeError "T-MISSING-FIELD" (missingFieldMsg fname rname)
    checkMissingFields rname rest supplied

fieldAssignContains : String -> List FieldAssign -> Bool
fieldAssignContains _ [] = False
fieldAssignContains fname ((FieldAssign fn _)::rest)
  | fname == fn = True
  | otherwise = fieldAssignContains fname rest

-- Phase 72 / TYPECHECK-AUDIT C2: receiver-directed field resolution.  Infer the
-- receiver FIRST, then pick the owning record by its head tycon when known; if
-- the receiver is still an unbound var and the field is owned by several
-- records, the access is ambiguous (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's EFieldAccess +
-- field_owners multimap).  `value` keeps the Ref-projection special case (no
-- record in compiler/stdlib declares a `value` field, so this matches the
-- oracle's structural `(Ref a).value → a`).
inferFieldAccess : TcEnv -> Expr -> String -> Ref String -> Mono
inferFieldAccess env e fname r
  | fname == "value" = inferValueField env e
  | otherwise =
    let te = normalize (infer env e)
    match resolveFieldRecord te fname
      None => freshVar ()
      Some (rname, ri) =>
        let _ = setRef r rname
        inferFieldOfRecord te rname ri fname

-- `cell.value` projects a Ref's contents.  Unify the receiver with `Ref a`
-- (forcing it, like the reference's `(Ref a).value → a` special case) and return
-- `a` — done by unification, not a structural match, so it also works when the
-- receiver's type is still an unsolved tyvar at this point (the self-hosted
-- typecheck infers function params as fresh vars, not yet pinned to their sig).
inferValueField : TcEnv -> Expr -> Mono
inferValueField env e =
  let et = infer env e
  let inner = freshVar ()
  let _ = unify et (TApp (TCon "Ref") inner)
  inner

-- the field's type from a resolved record `rname`, given the (already-inferred)
-- receiver type `te` to unify against the record's instantiated result type.  A
-- field absent from a KNOWN record names it (mirrors the oracle's `resolve_in`,
-- which raises UnknownField with the resolved record name).
inferFieldOfRecord : Mono -> String -> RecordInfo -> String -> Mono
inferFieldOfRecord te rname ri fname =
  let ir = instantiateRecord ri
  let _ = unify te (fst ir)
  match lookupAssoc fname (snd ir)
    Some ft => ft
    None => unknownFieldFresh fname rname (map fst (snd ir))

-- field_owners (Phase 72): every registry KEY whose RecordInfo declares `fname`,
-- sorted + deduped (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's `field_candidates` +
-- `List.sort_uniq compare`).  For a plain record the key is its type name; for a
-- named-field variant it's the constructor name — matching the oracle, whose
-- field_owners maps to the record's type name and the variant's con_name.
-- The field→owners multimap (fieldOwnersRef) already holds exactly this set of
-- keys, maintained at registration; `sortUniqS` canonicalizes it so the rendered
-- owner-list order (ambiguousFieldMsg / headL) is byte-identical to the old walk.
fieldOwnerNames : String -> List String
fieldOwnerNames fname =
  sortUniqS (fromOption [] (omLookup fname fieldOwnersRef.value))

-- Resolve the record (name + info) that owns `fname` for a receiver of type `te`
-- (normalized).
-- (a) receiver head tycon is a known record → that record;
-- (b) receiver is an undetermined var → 0 owners = unknown field, 1 = that one,
--     >1 = ambiguous (push AmbiguousField, return None);
-- (c) receiver is a concrete non-record → first owner (so the later unify raises
--     the usual mismatch), or unknown field if none.
-- On error it pushes the matching type error and returns None.
resolveFieldRecord : Mono -> String -> Option (String, RecordInfo)
resolveFieldRecord te fname = match headTyconMono te
  Some r => match lookupRecordByName r
    Some ri => Some (r, ri)
    None => match lookupRecordByMangledHead r fname
      Some pair => Some pair
      None => if contains r abstractRecordTypesRef.value then
        let _ = pushTypeError "T-ABSTRACT-FIELD" (abstractFieldMsg r fname)
        None
      else resolveFieldByOwners te fname
  None => resolveFieldByOwners te fname

-- Mangle-aware record lookup for the emit path.  `backend/private_mangle.mdk`
-- renames a record's DECL name (a record's name IS its constructor) to
-- `<mid>__<name>`, so `recordsRef` is keyed by the mangled name — but a TYPE
-- reference like `r : Rule` is NOT mangled, so the receiver's head tycon stays
-- `Rule` and the exact `lookupRecordByName` above misses.  Recover by finding the
-- registry entry whose key suffix-matches `__<head>` AND owns `fname`; only when
-- it is UNIQUE (else ambiguous → None, so the caller's owners path keeps today's
-- behavior).  No-op off the emit path (unmangled key hits the exact lookup).
lookupRecordByMangledHead : String -> String -> Option (String, RecordInfo)
lookupRecordByMangledHead head fname = match mangledHeadCandidates head fname
  [pair] => Some pair
  _ => None

-- Candidates are the keys that already OWN `fname` (from the field→owners
-- multimap, which is the "declares this field" filter precomputed) whose key
-- suffix matches `__<head>`; each paired with its RecordInfo from
-- recordByNameRef.  The singleton gate in the caller is unchanged, so the order
-- here is irrelevant.
mangledHeadCandidates : String -> String -> List (String, RecordInfo)
mangledHeadCandidates head fname =
  mangledHeadCandidatesGo
    head
    (fromOption [] (omLookup fname fieldOwnersRef.value))

mangledHeadCandidatesGo : String -> List String -> List (String, RecordInfo)
mangledHeadCandidatesGo _ [] = []
mangledHeadCandidatesGo head (key::rest)
  | endsWith ("__" ++ head) key = match lookupRecordByName key
    Some ri => (key, ri) :: mangledHeadCandidatesGo head rest
    None => mangledHeadCandidatesGo head rest
  | otherwise = mangledHeadCandidatesGo head rest

resolveFieldByOwners : Mono -> String -> Option (String, RecordInfo)
resolveFieldByOwners te fname = match fieldOwnerNames fname
  [] =>
    let _ = pushUnknownField fname "<unknown>"
    None
  [r] => pairRecordByName r
  r::rest => resolveFieldAmbiguous te fname (r::rest)

-- multiple owners: receiver still an unbound var ⇒ genuinely ambiguous; a
-- concrete (non-record) receiver ⇒ pick the first owner so the later unify
-- surfaces the type mismatch (mirrors the oracle's two `_` sub-branches).
resolveFieldAmbiguous : Mono -> String -> List String -> Option (String, RecordInfo)
resolveFieldAmbiguous te fname owners = match normalize te
  TVar _ =>
    let _ = pushTypeError "T-AMBIGUOUS-FIELD" (ambiguousFieldMsg fname owners)
    None
  _ => pairRecordByName (headL owners)

pairRecordByName : String -> Option (String, RecordInfo)
pairRecordByName r = map (ri => (r, ri)) (lookupRecordByName r)

headL : List String -> String
headL [] = ""
headL (x::_) = x

pushUnknownField : String -> String -> Unit
pushUnknownField fname rname =
  pushTypeError "T-UNKNOWN-FIELD" (unknownFieldMsg fname rname)

-- `declared`: the record's own field names, so a genuine typo (`aeg` for
-- `age`) can be suggested (ERROR-QUALITY dim F).  When a close-enough
-- candidate exists the error gets a structured `help` + a machine `fix` that
-- replaces the bad field name's span with the suggestion; otherwise unchanged.
unknownFieldFresh : String -> String -> List String -> Mono
unknownFieldFresh fname rname declared =
  let _ = pushUnknownFieldMaybeSuggest fname rname declared
  let v = freshVar ()
  -- Chunk A: the field error already explained this access; poison its result
  -- var so a downstream `debug`/method use doesn't pile on a secondary
  -- `ambiguous instance` cascade (record_wrong_field).
  let _ = poisonMismatchVars v v
  v

pushUnknownFieldMaybeSuggest : String -> String -> List String -> Unit
pushUnknownFieldMaybeSuggest fname rname declared = match suggestField fname declared
  None => pushTypeError "T-UNKNOWN-FIELD" (unknownFieldMsg fname rname)
  Some sug => pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-UNKNOWN-FIELD" currentLoc.value (unknownFieldMsg fname rname ++ ". Did you mean '\{sug}'?") "did you mean '\{sug}'?" (fieldFixFrom currentLoc.value sug)

-- "did you mean" for a record field: nearest declared field name by
-- Levenshtein distance, within a fixed distance of 2.  Skip names < 3 chars
-- (too noisy at that length, same floor as resolve.mdk's `suggestName`).
-- Unlike `suggestName` — which searches EVERY in-scope name and so scales its
-- threshold down with length to avoid noise in a large candidate pool — the
-- candidate pool here is one record's OWN declared fields (typically a
-- handful), so a flat distance-2 budget is safe: it's what's needed to catch
-- a two-letter transposition (`aeg` vs `age`, distance 2) without the
-- length-scaled cap resolve.mdk uses (which would reject that exact case:
-- min(2, max(1, 3/3)) = 1).  Ties favor the lexicographically-first candidate.
-- A small local copy rather than importing resolve.mdk's `suggestName`, which
-- would cycle (nothing here imports resolve.mdk today, and this file is a
-- dependency of the pipeline stage that runs after it).
suggestField : String -> List String -> Option String
suggestField fname declared
  | stringLength fname < 3 = None
  | otherwise = map fst (bestFieldCandidate fname 2 declared None)

bestFieldCandidate : String -> Int -> List String -> Option (String, Int) -> Option (String, Int)
bestFieldCandidate _ _ [] acc = acc
bestFieldCandidate fname lim (c::cs) acc
  | c == fname = bestFieldCandidate fname lim cs acc
  | editDistance fname c > lim = bestFieldCandidate fname lim cs acc
  | otherwise = bestFieldCandidate fname lim cs (keepBetterField c (editDistance fname c) acc)

keepBetterField : String -> Int -> Option (String, Int) -> Option (String, Int)
keepBetterField c d None = Some (c, d)
keepBetterField c d (Some (bc, bd)) =
  if d < bd || d == bd && c < bc then
    Some (c, d)
  else
    Some (bc, bd)

-- The field name itself carries no Loc in the AST (`EFieldAccess Expr String
-- (Ref String)` — no per-field span).  `currentLoc` at the unknown-field site
-- is instead the RECEIVER's own span (infer walks into the receiver first,
-- and nothing re-enters an ELoc between there and here) — verified empirically:
-- `p.aeg` reports receiver span (sc, sc+1) for `p`, `somebody.aeg` reports
-- (sc, sc+8) for `somebody`.  The field name immediately follows the
-- receiver's end column + the `.`, so its span is computable from the
-- receiver's end column.  Valid for the common single-level `recv.field`
-- access; a chained `a.b.cty` receiver is itself a field access with no own
-- ELoc, so this would misplace the fix there — accepted for now (the
-- common-case gain is worth the narrow miss).
fieldFixFrom : Option Loc -> String -> Option (Loc, String)
fieldFixFrom (Some (Loc f _ _ el ec)) sug =
  Some (Loc f el (ec + 1) el (ec + 1 + stringLength sug), sug)
fieldFixFrom None _ = None

-- byte-for-byte with lib/typecheck.ml UnknownField / AmbiguousField / MissingField messages.
unknownFieldMsg : String -> String -> String
unknownFieldMsg fname rname =
  "Field '\{fname}' does not belong to record '\{rname}'"

-- Dot-access on a type exported abstractly: byte-identical to resolve.mdk's
-- AbstractFieldAccess ppResError, so a destructure (`Point { x }`, caught in
-- resolve) and a dot-access (`p.x`, caught here in typecheck) read the same.
abstractFieldMsg : String -> String -> String
abstractFieldMsg tname fname = "'\{tname}' is exported abstractly. Field '\{fname}' is not accessible; declare it `public export` to expose its fields"

-- TYPECHECK-AUDIT OBS4: mirrors lib/typecheck.ml MissingField message byte-for-byte.
missingFieldMsg : String -> String -> String
missingFieldMsg fname rname =
  "Missing field '\{fname}' in construction of record '\{rname}'"

ambiguousFieldMsg : String -> List String -> String
ambiguousFieldMsg fname owners = "Ambiguous field access: '.\{fname}' is declared by \{joinWith ", " owners}; the record type is undetermined. Add a type annotation (e.g. '(r : \{headL owners}).\{fname}')"

-- TYPECHECK-AUDIT D1: convert resolve-not-pre-screened error shapes (unknown
-- record / constructor, unbound variable) from uncatchable interpreter panics
-- into accumulated typeErrors + a fresh-var placeholder, so inference continues
-- (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml, which `fail`s with these byte-identical messages).
unknownRecordMsg : String -> String
unknownRecordMsg name = "Unknown record type: " ++ name

unknownCtorMsg : String -> String
unknownCtorMsg name = "Unknown constructor: " ++ name

unboundVarMsg : String -> String
unboundVarMsg name = "Unbound variable: " ++ name

-- push the message + return a fresh placeholder type so the continued path stays
-- in a valid state (the oracle accumulate-and-continue analog for these shapes).
unknownRecordFresh : String -> Mono
unknownRecordFresh name =
  let _ = pushTypeError "T-UNKNOWN-RECORD" (unknownRecordMsg name)
  freshVar ()

unboundVarFresh : String -> Mono
unboundVarFresh name =
  let _ = pushTypeError "T-UNBOUND" (unboundVarMsg name)
  freshVar ()

inferRecordUpdate : TcEnv -> Expr -> List FieldAssign -> Ref String -> Mono
inferRecordUpdate env base fields r = match firstFieldName fields
  None => panic "empty record update"
  Some fn => inferRecordUpdateField env base fields fn r

-- `Con { base | f = v, … }` — a named-field-variant update (e.g. desugar's
-- `DInterface { d | methods = … }`).  Resolve the variant's field layout by the
-- CONSTRUCTOR name (registered via registerNamedFieldVariants); both `base` and
-- the result have the variant's DATA type, only the provided fields are typed.
inferVariantUpdate : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> List FieldAssign -> Mono
inferVariantUpdate env cname base fields = match lookupRecordByName cname
  None => unknownRecordFresh cname
  Some ri => inferRecordUpdateWith env cname base fields ri

firstFieldName : List FieldAssign -> Option String
firstFieldName [] = None
firstFieldName ((FieldAssign fn _)::_) = Some fn

-- receiver-directed (Phase 72), like inferFieldAccess: infer base first, then
-- pick the owning record by the base's head tycon, falling back to the first
-- update field's owners.
inferRecordUpdateField : TcEnv -> Expr -> List FieldAssign -> String -> Ref String -> Mono
inferRecordUpdateField env base fields fn r =
  let bt = normalize (infer env base)
  match resolveFieldRecord bt fn
    None => freshVar ()
    Some (rname, ri) =>
      -- stamp the RESOLVED receiver record onto the node (bug #38): both emitters
      -- read it to pick the record by name, instead of guessing from the first
      -- update label (which picks the wrong record — and so the wrong slot — when
      -- two records share a field name at different indices).
      let _ = setRef r rname
      inferRecordUpdatePicked rname bt env fields ri

inferRecordUpdatePicked : String -> Mono -> TcEnv -> List FieldAssign -> RecordInfo -> Mono
inferRecordUpdatePicked rname bt env fields ri =
  let ir = instantiateRecord ri
  let _ = unify bt (fst ir)
  let _ = unifyFieldAssigns env rname (snd ir) fields
  fst ir

inferRecordUpdateWith : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> List FieldAssign -> RecordInfo -> Mono
inferRecordUpdateWith env rname base fields ri =
  let ir = instantiateRecord ri
  let _ = unify (infer env base) (fst ir)
  let _ = unifyFieldAssigns env rname (snd ir) fields
  fst ir

-- operators infer by SHAPE (the typeclass constraint they carry — Num/Eq/… — is
-- tracked at the value level but never rendered in a scheme, so it's irrelevant
-- to the === TYPES === output; numeric literals are Int, not polymorphic)
inferBinopE : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Ref Route -> Mono
inferBinopE env op l r dref =
  let lt = infer env l
  let rt = infer env r
  -- Type-error span (Bite 2, fork 1 = WHOLE BINOP): after inferring both
  -- operands, the live `currentLoc` is the LAST operand atom (ELoc wraps
  -- leaves, not the binop level).  Point mismatches at the whole `l <op> r`
  -- span (l's start .. r's end) so both the Num-obligation errors and the
  -- comparison/Eq/Ord `typeMismatch` binop errors squiggle the operator
  -- expression rather than a lone operand.
  let _ = match binopSpanLoc l r
    Some sp => setRef currentLoc (Some sp)
    None => ()
  let _ = recordBinopSite op dref lt
  let _ = recordArithSite op dref lt
  inferBinop op lt rt

-- Synthesize the whole-binop span from its operand Exprs: file + start of the
-- left operand, end of the right operand.  Falls back to whichever single
-- operand loc is available (None only when neither operand is ELoc-wrapped).
binopSpanLoc : Expr -> Expr -> Option Loc
binopSpanLoc l r = match (exprLoc l, exprLoc r)
  (Some (Loc f sl sc _ _), Some (Loc _ _ _ el ec)) => Some (Loc f sl sc el ec)
  (Some ll, None) => Some ll
  (None, Some rr) => Some rr
  (None, None) => None
-- Phase 151 / Gap G: record a binop site for the comparison/equality operators
-- so resolveBinopSites can stamp [dref] once the operand type grounds.  The
-- operand mono is [lt] (unified with [rt] by eqCompareOp/ordCompareOp below).

-- Map a comparison/equality operator to its Eq/Ord method and stash a pending
-- binop site; non-comparison operators record nothing.  G4: snapshot
-- currentImplBody so resolveBinopSite gates the RDict element-dict route to genuine
-- in-impl-body sites (a top-level operand may collide with stale activeDictVars ids).
recordBinopSite : String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
recordBinopSite op dref m = match binopMethod op
  Some method => setRef pendingBinopSites ((method, dref, m, currentImplBody.value, currentFn.value)::pendingBinopSites.value)
  None => ()

-- SHARED-FLOAT-RESIDUAL §3(C): stash an arithmetic binop's operand mono + route
-- ref for the deferred scalar-type stamp.  Non-arithmetic operators record
-- nothing (their route is handled by recordBinopSite or left RNone).  The operand
-- mono is [lt] (unified with [rt] by inferBinop).
isArithBinop : String -> Bool
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in wasm_emit.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
isArithBinop op = contains op ["+", "-", "*", "/", "%"]

recordArithSite : String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
recordArithSite op dref m
  | isArithBinop op =
    setRef pendingArithSites ((dref, m)::pendingArithSites.value)
  | otherwise = ()

-- Roadmap #18a: `++` is the surface operator for `Semigroup`'s `append`, so it
-- joins the comparison operators on the recordBinopSite/resolveBinopSites seam —
-- a ground non-builtin operand (a user `impl Semigroup MyRope`) gets its route
-- stamped and rewriteBinopExpr turns the EBinOp into an `EMethodAt "append"`
-- application.  The builtin String/List appends never route (binopBuiltinHead) —
-- see the recursion note there.
binopMethod : String -> Option String
binopMethod "==" = Some "eq"
binopMethod "!=" = Some "eq"
binopMethod "<" = Some "lt"
binopMethod ">" = Some "gt"
binopMethod "<=" = Some "lte"
binopMethod ">=" = Some "gte"
binopMethod "++" = Some "append"
binopMethod _ = None

-- Roadmap #18c: `-` (unary minus) is the surface operator for `Num`'s `negate`,
-- joining `++`/comparisons on the record*Site/resolve*Sites/rewrite*Expr seam.
-- `!` never routes — Bool negation is not a typeclass method.
unopMethod : String -> Option String
unopMethod "-" = Some "negate"
unopMethod _ = None

-- The EUnOp analogue of recordBinopSite: stash a pending unop site for `-`
-- (nothing for `!`).  Same currentImplBody/currentFn snapshot, same reason.
recordUnopSite : String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
recordUnopSite op dref m = match unopMethod op
  Some method => setRef pendingUnopSites ((method, dref, m, currentImplBody.value, currentFn.value)::pendingUnopSites.value)
  None => ()

-- The EUnOp analogue of inferBinopE: infer the single operand, record its
-- pending unop site (for `-`), then dispatch to inferUnop for the actual
-- obligation/unification work (negateOp for `-`, Bool-unify for `!`).
inferUnopE : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Ref Route -> Mono
inferUnopE env op e dref =
  let t = infer env e
  let _ = recordUnopSite op dref t
  inferUnop op t

inferBinop : String -> Mono -> Mono -> Mono
inferBinop "+" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "-" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "*" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "/" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "%" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "++" lt rt = appendOp lt rt
inferBinop "==" lt rt = eqCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "!=" lt rt = eqCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "<" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop ">" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "<=" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop ">=" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "&&" lt rt = boolOp lt rt
inferBinop "||" lt rt = boolOp lt rt
inferBinop "::" lt rt = consOp lt rt
inferBinop "|>" lt rt = pipeOp lt rt
inferBinop ">>" lt rt = composeOp lt rt False
inferBinop "<<" lt rt = composeOp lt rt True
inferBinop op _ _ = panic ("typecheck: unsupported operator " ++ op)

-- Roadmap #18a: `++` (append) carries a `Semigroup` obligation on its (unified)
-- operand type — a verbatim mirror of `eqCompareOp`/`recordIfaceObligation` for `==`,
-- and the reason `"x" ++ Box 1` is now a located `No impl of Semigroup for Box`
-- reject at CHECK time rather than an eval-time `E-PANIC`.  Reuses the same
-- pendingImplObligations / checkImplObligations path: a synthetic Semigroup
-- obligation with a bare iface-param declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram ["a"]) so
-- dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly the operand mono.  `Monoid a requires Semigroup
-- a`, so a declared `Monoid a =>` constraint superclass-closes to satisfy it (this
-- is what keeps core.mdk's `foldMap f = fold (acc x => acc ++ f x) empty` typing).
-- A still-polymorphic operand defers to the caller, exactly like Num/Eq/Ord.
--
-- Gated on the `Semigroup` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded), via
-- ifaceRegistered: the bare-HM no-prelude oracle (tc_probe /
-- typecheck_main.mdk) has no `Semigroup` class, so recording the obligation only
-- when it exists keeps those drivers byte-identical while `medaka check` rejects.
appendOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
appendOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Semigroup" lt
  lt

-- #146: unified operator-obligation recorder.  The arithmetic (`+ - * / %`, unary
-- `-`), comparison (`== != < > <= >=`) and append (`++`) operators each carry an
-- interface obligation on their (unified) operand type via pushPendingObl — a
-- synthetic obligation with a bare iface-param declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram
-- ["a"]) so dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly the operand mono.  Gated on that
-- interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded): the bare-HM no-prelude oracle
-- (tc_probe / typecheck_main.mdk) registers none of Num/Eq/Ord/Semigroup, so
-- recording only when the interface exists keeps those drivers byte-identical while
-- full check_main (prelude) rejects, matching `medaka check`.  Callers:
-- numArithOp/negateOp ("Num"), eqCompareOp ("Eq"), ordCompareOp ("Ord"), appendOp
-- ("Semigroup").
recordIfaceObligation : String -> Mono -> Unit
recordIfaceObligation iface lt
  | ifaceRegistered iface =
    pushPendingObl (iface, ["a"], TyVar "a", lt, currentLoc.value)
  | otherwise = ()

-- True iff `iface` is registered (has ≥1 method registered from the DInterface
-- decls — present once core.mdk is loaded).  #147: an O(log n) membership test on
-- the cached registeredIfacesRef set, replacing the old full linear scan of the
-- method table per operator/numeric-literal.
ifaceRegistered : String -> Bool
ifaceRegistered iface = omHasKey iface registeredIfacesRef.value

-- G2: arithmetic `+ - * / %` carry a `Num` obligation on their (unified) operand
-- type — mirrors the oracle's `No impl of Num for <T>` reject (lib/typecheck.ml).
-- `++` (append) is NOT Num — it carries a `Semigroup` obligation instead (appendOp
-- above).  We reuse the
-- existing pendingImplObligations / checkImplObligations path: a synthetic Num
-- obligation with a bare iface-param declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram ["a"]) so
-- dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly the operand mono.  When it grounds to a concrete
-- non-Num head (String/Bool) with no `impl Num`, the post-inference check rejects;
-- while still a polymorphic var (a `Num a =>` site) allConcreteHeads defers it to
-- the caller — exactly the oracle's behaviour.
--
-- Gated on the `Num` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded): the bare-HM,
-- no-prelude oracle (tc_probe / typecheck_main.mdk) imposes NO class constraint on
-- `+`, so `1 + 2 : Int` and `"a" - "b" : String` both type there.  Recording the
-- obligation only when Num exists keeps the no-prelude drivers byte-identical to
-- that oracle while the full check_main (prelude) path rejects, matching `medaka
-- check`.
numArithOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
numArithOp lt rt =
  -- Chunk D: taint any literal operand BEFORE the unify grounds it — so a literal
  -- fed to an arithmetic op (`1 + "x"`, `x + 1`, `1 + x`) keeps `No impl of Num for
  -- T` (the `+` is the culprit) rather than being reframed as a bare mismatch.
  let _ = markNumlitOpTaint lt
  let _ = markNumlitOpTaint rt
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Num" lt
  lt

-- PLAN.md #11 soundness arm: model an integer literal as a `fromInt n` method
-- occurrence so its route cell [dref] gets stamped (RKey ground / RDictFwd poly) by
-- resolveSites — exactly like core.mdk's `fromInt 0` in `sum`.  Direct mirror of
-- lib/typecheck.ml's ENumLit infer arm.  Gated on the prelude (`Num` registered AND
-- `fromInt` bound): with no prelude (bare-HM oracle) fall back to a plain fresh
-- Num-obligated var, leaving [dref] RNone (⇒ a static Int literal at rewrite time).
inferNumLit : TcEnv -> Int -> Ref (Option Float) -> Ref Route -> Mono
inferNumLit env n fref dref
  | ifaceRegistered "Num" = match lookupVar env "fromInt"
    None => inferNumLitBare n fref
    Some _ =>
      -- inferMethodAt records the Num impl obligation + a pendingSite on [dref].
      let fromIntTy = inferMethodAt env "fromInt" dref (Ref []) (Ref [])
      let a = freshVar ()
      let _ = unify fromIntTy (TFun (TCon "Int") (openRow ()) a)
      let _ = setRef numlitRefs ((a, fref, n, dref) :: numlitRefs.value)
      let _ = recordNumlitLoc a  -- Chunk D: literal provenance for Num mis-framing
      a
  | otherwise = inferNumLitBare n fref

-- no-prelude fallback: a fresh var, no route (dref untouched ⇒ stays RNone ⇒ Int).
-- PLAN.md #11 (locked §0.2): a numeric literal is ALWAYS a `fromInt n` occurrence,
-- so it carries a `Num` obligation on its var unconditionally — exactly the OCaml
-- oracle, whose ENumLit infer arm `infer env (EVar "fromInt")` records the Num
-- usage even no-prelude (`initial_env` seeds `fromInt`/`Num`).  Recording it here
-- (vs. the prelude-gated `recordIfaceObligation`) lets value-level defaulting ground
-- an AMBIGUOUS literal var to Int (`nums = [1,2,3] : List Int`) so the no-prelude
-- `typecheck_main` matches the prelude-aware oracle golden.  The matching reject is
-- SUPPRESSED no-prelude in checkOneImplObligation (no-prelude imposes no Num impl
-- constraint — see its `ifaceRegistered` guard), so a literal still types Int
-- without a spurious `No impl of Num for Int`.
inferNumLitBare : Int -> Ref (Option Float) -> Mono
inferNumLitBare n fref =
  let a = freshVar ()
  let _ = pushPendingObl ("Num", ["a"], TyVar "a", a, currentLoc.value)
  let _ = setRef numlitRefs ((a, fref, n, Ref RNone)::numlitRefs.value)
  let _ = recordNumlitLoc a  -- Chunk D: literal provenance for Num mis-framing
  a

-- ── Chunk D: Num mis-framing provenance (error-path only) ──────────────────
-- Record a literal's (var, loc) so a later Num-obligation reframe can tell a bare
-- literal from an arithmetic-operator operand.  [currentLoc] is the ENumLit span.
recordNumlitLoc : Mono -> Unit
recordNumlitLoc a = match currentLoc.value
  Some l =>
    let _ = setRef numlitVarLocs ((a, l)::numlitVarLocs.value)
    setRef numlitUntainted ((a, l)::numlitUntainted.value)
  None => ()

-- Called from recordIfaceObligation (arithmetic `+`/`-`/`*`/`/`/`%`): any recorded
-- literal whose var shares a union-find root with [m] was an operand of an op, so
-- mark its loc — the reframe then keeps `No impl of Num for T` for it.  Runs during
-- inference (vars still unbound), so `normalize` reveals the shared root regardless
-- of which operand became the root at unify time.
markNumlitOpTaint : Mono -> Unit
markNumlitOpTaint m =
  let id = rootIdOf m
  if id < 0 || not (anyRootMatch id numlitUntainted.value) then
    ()
  else
    setRef numlitUntainted (taintAndDrop id numlitUntainted.value)

-- True iff SOME worklist literal shares the union-find root [id].  Short-circuits via
-- `||` and allocates nothing, so the common case (this operand taints no literal)
-- leaves the worklist alone — rebuilding it unconditionally would put back an O(N)
-- allocation per operand, which is the very thing `rootIdOf` removed.
anyRootMatch : Int -> List (Mono, Loc) -> Bool
anyRootMatch _ [] = False
anyRootMatch id ((v, _)::rest) = sameRootId id v || anyRootMatch id rest

-- Taint every worklist literal sharing the root [id] (record its loc as
-- operator-tainted) and DROP it from the worklist; keep the others.
taintAndDrop : Int -> List (Mono, Loc) -> List (Mono, Loc)
taintAndDrop _ [] = []
taintAndDrop id ((v, l)::rest)
  | sameRootId id v =
    let _ = setRef numlitOpLocs (l::numlitOpLocs.value)
    taintAndDrop id rest
  | otherwise = (v, l) :: taintAndDrop id rest

-- The union-find ROOT id of [v]; -1 if it does not normalize to a type variable.
-- NON-ALLOCATING (it reads the root's id rather than rebuilding a `TVar` for it) and
-- PATH-COMPRESSING (it goes through `normalizeLink`, so a walked chain is flattened).
-- PERF: `sameRootId` is applied to EVERY recorded literal on EVERY arithmetic /
-- comparison operand (markNumlitOpTaint and tagLitLocsWithRoot each scan the whole,
-- never-trimmed `numlitVarLocs` list), so an uncompressed chain made this the single
-- hottest symbol in the compiler — 57% of a large-declaration typecheck (issue #115).
-- Tyvar ids come from a counter that starts at 0 (`newTyvarId`), so -1 can never
-- collide with a real id.
rootIdOf : Mono -> Int
rootIdOf (TVar cell) = match cell.value
  Unbound id _ => id
  Link t => rootVarId (normalizeLink cell t)
rootIdOf _ = 0 - 1

-- The id of an ALREADY-NORMALIZED mono: its root tyvar's id, or -1 if it is not a
-- type variable at all.  (`normalizeLink` never returns a `Link`, so that arm is
-- unreachable; it folds into the not-a-tyvar answer.)
rootVarId : Mono -> Int
rootVarId (TVar cell) = match cell.value
  Unbound id _ => id
  Link _ => 0 - 1
rootVarId _ = 0 - 1

-- True iff [v] normalizes to the unbound var [id] (same union-find root).
sameRootId : Int -> Mono -> Bool
sameRootId id v = rootIdOf v == id

-- Chunk D: if one of [a]/[b] is a still-unbound literal var and the other is
-- concrete-headed, tag that literal's loc with [kind] so the reframe can use
-- site-specific wording.  Pure state capture — never alters unification.
noteNumlitCtx : Mono -> Mono -> String -> Unit
noteNumlitCtx a b kind =
  let _ = tagNumlitCtxIf a b kind
  tagNumlitCtxIf b a kind

-- Tag EVERY literal whose var shares [v]'s root (not just the first) when [other]
-- has a concrete head — so a heterogeneous list forcing several literals to one
-- type tags them all with the same [kind], letting their reframed messages dedup to
-- a single "list elements have different types" line.
tagNumlitCtxIf : Mono -> Mono -> String -> Unit
tagNumlitCtxIf v other kind
  | isSome (monoHeadCon other) = tagAllLitLocs v kind
  | otherwise = ()

tagAllLitLocs : Mono -> String -> Unit
tagAllLitLocs v kind = match normalize v
  TVar cell => tagLitLocsWithRoot (tyvarId cell) kind numlitVarLocs.value
  _ => ()

tagLitLocsWithRoot : Int -> String -> List (Mono, Loc) -> Unit
tagLitLocsWithRoot _ _ [] = ()
tagLitLocsWithRoot id kind ((mv, l)::rest) =
  let _ = if sameRootId id mv then
    setRef numlitCtxTags ((l, kind)::numlitCtxTags.value)
  else
    ()
  tagLitLocsWithRoot id kind rest

-- Stage-1 Eq dispatch (EQ-DISPATCH-DESIGN.md §2.1): `==`/`!=` carry an `Eq`
-- obligation on their (unified) operand type — a verbatim mirror of
-- `recordIfaceObligation` for `+`.  We reuse the existing pendingImplObligations /
-- checkImplObligations path: a synthetic Eq obligation with a bare iface-param
-- declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram ["a"]) so dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly
-- the operand mono.  When it grounds to a concrete non-Eq head (a function, an
-- un-derived ADT) with no `impl Eq`, the post-inference check rejects; while still
-- a polymorphic var (an `Eq a =>` site) allConcreteHeads defers it to the caller
-- — exactly the Num behaviour.  `<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=` carry the sibling `Ord`
-- obligation via `ordCompareOp` below.  `!=` shares this seam and lowers to
-- `not (eq …)` at the dict layer (binopMethodApp), so it does not double-report.
--
-- Gated on the `Eq` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded), mirroring
-- `ifaceRegistered`: the bare-HM no-prelude oracle (tc_probe / typecheck_main)
-- has no `Eq` class, so recording the obligation only when Eq exists keeps those
-- drivers byte-identical while full check_main (prelude) rejects.
eqCompareOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
eqCompareOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Eq" lt
  TCon "Bool"

-- Stage-1 Ord dispatch (EQ-DISPATCH-DESIGN.md §"sibling Ord follow-up"): the four
-- ordering comparisons `<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=` carry an `Ord` obligation on their
-- (unified) operand type — a verbatim mirror of `eqCompareOp` for `==`.  Reuses the
-- same pendingImplObligations / checkImplObligations path.  `Ord` supersets `Eq`
-- (`interface Ord a requires Eq a`), so a declared `Ord a =>` constraint already
-- superclass-closes to satisfy BOTH the `<`→Ord and a sibling `==`→Eq obligation;
-- an `Eq a =>` declaration does NOT close upward, so a bare `<` under `Eq a =>` is
-- correctly rejected as needing `Ord a`.  When the operand grounds to a concrete
-- non-Ord head (a function, an un-derived ADT) with no `impl Ord`, the
-- post-inference check rejects; while still a polymorphic var it defers to the
-- caller — exactly the Eq/Num behaviour.  NO change to eval/emit: `<` still lowers
-- to `EMethodAt "lt"` at the dict layer, so runtime/emit dispatch is unchanged.
--
-- Gated on the `Ord` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded), mirroring
-- `ifaceRegistered`.
ordCompareOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
ordCompareOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Ord" lt
  TCon "Bool"

boolOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
boolOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt (TCon "Bool")
  let _ = unify rt (TCon "Bool")
  TCon "Bool"

consOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
consOp lt rt =
  let listTy = TApp (TCon "List") lt
  let _ = noteNumlitCtx lt rt "cons"  -- Chunk D: tag cons head-vs-list Num mismatch
  let _ = unify rt listTy
  listTy

pipeOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
pipeOp lt rt =
  let res = freshVar ()
  let eff = openRow ()
  let _ = unify rt (TFun lt eff res)
  let _ = performEffect curEffect eff
  res

-- g >> h : (a->b) -> (b->c) -> (a->c)  [flipped=False]
-- g << h : (a->b) -> (c->a) -> (c->b)  [flipped=True]
composeOp : Mono -> Mono -> Bool -> Mono
composeOp gt ht False =
  let a = freshVar ()
  let b = freshVar ()
  let c = freshVar ()
  let _ = unify gt (TFun a (openRow ()) b)
  let _ = unify ht (TFun b (openRow ()) c)
  TFun a (openRow ()) c
composeOp gt ht True =
  let a = freshVar ()
  let b = freshVar ()
  let c = freshVar ()
  let _ = unify gt (TFun a (openRow ()) b)
  let _ = unify ht (TFun c (openRow ()) a)
  TFun c (openRow ()) b

inferUnop : String -> Mono -> Mono
inferUnop "-" t = negateOp t
inferUnop "!" t =
  let _ = unify t (TCon "Bool")
  TCon "Bool"
inferUnop op _ = panic ("typecheck: unsupported unary op " ++ op)

-- Roadmap #18c: unary minus `-x` carries a `Num` obligation on its operand type —
-- a verbatim mirror of `numArithOp`/`recordIfaceObligation` for binary `+`/`-`/…,
-- and the reason `-"x"` is now a located `No impl of Num for String` reject at
-- CHECK time rather than an eval-time `panic "unary minus on non-number"`.
-- markNumlitOpTaint keeps the same Chunk-D literal-provenance error framing as
-- the binary arithmetic operators (`-"x"` blames the operator, not a bare
-- literal-vs-String mismatch).
negateOp : Mono -> Mono
negateOp t =
  let _ = markNumlitOpTaint t
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Num" t
  t

-- backtick infix `x `f` y` is just f applied to x then y
inferInfix : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferInfix env op l r =
  let ft = inferVar env op
  let lt = infer env l
  let rt = infer env r
  -- reconstruct the equivalent app spine so a hole-bearing extern fills from
  -- its FIRST arg (l) — mirror of the EApp path (v2 Stage 2b).
  let inner = EApp (EVar op) l
  inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp inner r) (inferApp (envAlphaLets env) inner ft lt) rt

inferVar : TcEnv -> String -> Mono
inferVar env x = match maybeStandaloneValueMono x
  -- P0-19 d4b (S4): bare value-position definer shadow → the standalone scheme.
  Some m => m
  None => inferVarPlain env x

inferVarPlain : TcEnv -> String -> Mono
inferVarPlain env x = match lookupVar env x
  Some s =>
    let mono = instantiateVarTracked x s
    recordImplObligation x mono
  None => unboundVarFresh x

-- #11 soundness (native obligation hole, plain check path): a constrained binding used
-- as a plain EVar (`g = f "hello"`) instantiates its DEFERRED obligations at the call.
-- schemeObligationsRef (always-on, unlike funConstraintsRef) records each binding's
-- (iface, quantified-var-id); here we instantiate TRACKED so each obligation var maps
-- to this call's fresh mono, recorded as a call obligation checked after inference.
-- A binding with no recorded obligations takes the plain instantiate path (identical
-- mono).  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml re-checking inferred_constraints at every use.
instantiateVarTracked : String -> Scheme -> Mono
instantiateVarTracked x s = match lookupAssocS2 x schemeObligationsRef.value
  None => instantiate s
  Some obs =>
    let inst = instantiateTracked s
    let _ = recordSchemeCallObligations obs (snd inst)
    fst inst

-- map each (iface, scheme-var-id) through the instantiation subst to this call's mono,
-- pushing onto pendingCallObligations.  An id absent from the subst (shouldn't happen
-- for a quantified var) is skipped.
recordSchemeCallObligations : List (String, Int) -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
recordSchemeCallObligations [] _ = ()
recordSchemeCallObligations ((iface, id)::rest) subst = match lookupAssocI id subst
  Some mono =>
    let _ = pushCallObl (iface, mono, currentLoc.value)
    recordSchemeCallObligations rest subst
  None => recordSchemeCallObligations rest subst

-- String-keyed lookup over schemeObligationsRef.
lookupAssocS2 : String -> List (String, List (String, Int)) -> Option (List (String, Int))
lookupAssocS2 _ [] = None
lookupAssocS2 k ((k2, v)::rest)
  | k == k2 = Some v
  | otherwise = lookupAssocS2 k rest

-- Error-path B4: if [x] is a registered interface method, record this occurrence's
-- (post-instantiation) mono so checkImplObligations can verify a matching impl
-- exists once the dispatch type is resolved.  Returns the mono unchanged.  A
-- non-method name records nothing (the common case).  The marked path records the
-- same via inferMethodAt; the bare typecheck_main path records here.
recordImplObligation : String -> Mono -> Mono
recordImplObligation x mono
  -- A standalone shadow (a top-level funDef that reuses an interface-method name)
  -- resolves in-scope to that standalone, not the method — the scope-blind marker
  -- still rewrites its occurrences to EMethodAt, but a NO-IMPL receiver legitimately
  -- falls back to the standalone (resolveRLocalSite stamps RLocal), so there is no
  -- impl obligation.  Recording one surfaces a spurious `No impl of <Iface> for <T>`
  -- under checkImplObligations (the build path skips that check, so it only bites
  -- `check`).  Skip for EVERY standalone shadow — definer (`definerShadowNamesRef`)
  -- AND importer (an imported standalone; `standaloneValuesRef` holds both).  P0-18:
  -- this closes the `check`-side of the importer-shadow-on-a-no-impl-receiver residual
  -- (`size 3` where `size` is imported) which was REJECTED while `run` correctly
  -- returned the standalone.  Sound: the name genuinely has a standalone fallback, so
