  -- a no-impl receiver is not an error — the route decides impl-vs-standalone per
  -- receiver; a receiver WITH an impl still dispatches (and that impl exists).
  -- Check BOTH refs: the single-file check path (checkProgram) sets only
  -- `definerShadowNamesRef`, while the multi-module path sets `standaloneValuesRef`
  -- (importer ∪ definer) — so either alone would miss one path's shadows.
  | contains x definerShadowNamesRef.value || contains x standaloneValuesRef.value =
    mono
  | otherwise = match omLookup x methodIfaceParamsRef.value
    None => mono
    Some (iface, typarams, mty) =>
      let _ = pushPendingObl (iface, typarams, mty, mono, currentLoc.value)
      mono

-- Expected-type propagation for an application whose argument is a lambda.
-- Inferring `andThen e (stream => Ok [JArray stream])` naively infers the lambda
-- BODY first with `stream : freshVar`, so a downstream conflicting use inside the
-- body (`JArray stream` wants `Array Json`) pins `stream` to the WRONG type before
-- the function's declared param type (`List Json`) is ever reconciled — the later
-- reconciliation then fails at the application with no good site, and the hover
-- side-channel records the wrong `stream` type.  Fix (bidirectional "checking"
-- mode, the do-bind/higher-order case): infer `f` first, peel its parameter type,
-- and unify it onto the lambda's parameter BEFORE inferring the body.  Then the
-- body sees `stream : List Json` and the type error localizes to the `JArray`
-- USE, and recordLocalBind captures the right hover type.  Purely additive: the
-- final `unify ft (TFun xt …)` in inferApp imposes the same equality regardless,
-- so the solution is unchanged — we only make it available earlier.
-- ── Batch B #5: swapped-argument detection (2-arg transposition) ───────────
-- A 2-arg application whose in-order arg/param types clash but whose
-- TRANSPOSITION type-checks is almost always a caller who wrote the arguments
-- in the wrong order (`greet 3 "Alice"` for `greet : String -> Int -> …`).
-- Detect that shape UPSTREAM in inferAppExpr — where the callee name + BOTH
-- args are still in scope — and emit ONE actionable diagnostic naming the
-- swapped call, instead of letting the two per-arg `inferApp` mismatches leak
-- (`Int vs String` + `Int literal vs String`) with no hint that a swap fixes it.
--
-- SOUNDNESS (a wrong "swap" suggestion is worse than the status quo): fire ONLY
-- when the transposition GENUINELY type-checks.  Two guards make the structural
-- test exact — never a wildcard-var approximation:
--   (1) both callee params must be fully CONCRETE (no free tyvars).  A
--       polymorphic param accepts any argument, so a "swap" past it is
--       meaningless AND its fit can't be decided structurally.
--   (2) both arguments must be LITERALS.  A literal's type is known exactly
--       from its syntax (WITHOUT re-inference side effects), so `argLitFits`
--       decides the transposition's type-fit structurally.  A numeric literal
--       (`ENumLit`/`LInt`) fits any `Int`/`Float` param (it is `Num`-poly); a
--       float literal fits only `Float`; other literals fit their exact type.
-- Under both guards, `(not in-order) && swapped` holds only for a real
-- transposition, so the suggested swap always type-checks.  We further require
-- the two params to DIFFER (same-type params ⇒ order is irrelevant, no swap).
-- Anything outside this scope (>2 args, non-literal args, polymorphic params,
-- same-type params) falls through to the normal per-arg path unchanged.

unlocExpr : Expr -> Expr
unlocExpr (ELoc _ e) = unlocExpr e
unlocExpr (EDoOrigin _ e) = unlocExpr e
unlocExpr e = e

exprLoc : Expr -> Option Loc
exprLoc (ELoc l _) = Some l
exprLoc (EDoOrigin l _) = Some l
exprLoc _ = None

-- The span of an application's outermost argument (Bite 3): peel any ELoc
-- wrapper on the `EApp` head, then read the argument Expr's loc.  Non-EApp
-- shapes (never passed as `appExpr`) return None.
appArgLoc : Expr -> Option Loc
appArgLoc (ELoc _ e) = appArgLoc e
appArgLoc (EApp _ x) = exprLoc x
appArgLoc _ = None

-- True iff [m] has NO unbound tyvar anywhere (a fully concrete type).
monoConcrete : Mono -> Bool
monoConcrete m = match normalize m
  TVar _ => False
  TCon _ => True
  TApp a b => monoConcrete a && monoConcrete b
  TFun a _ b => monoConcrete a && monoConcrete b
  TEff _ => True

-- structural equality of two concrete monos (both guaranteed var-free here)
monoSameShape : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
monoSameShape a b = match normalize a
  TCon x => match normalize b
    TCon y => x == y
    _ => False
  TApp a1 b1 => match normalize b
    TApp a2 b2 => monoSameShape a1 a2 && monoSameShape b1 b2
    _ => False
  TFun a1 _ b1 => match normalize b
    TFun a2 _ b2 => monoSameShape a1 a2 && monoSameShape b1 b2
    _ => False
  _ => False

monoIsNumeric : Mono -> Bool
monoIsNumeric m = match normalize m
  TCon "Int" => True
  TCon "Float" => True
  _ => False

monoIsFloat : Mono -> Bool
monoIsFloat m = match normalize m
  TCon "Float" => True
  _ => False

isLitArg : Expr -> Bool
isLitArg e = match unlocExpr e
  ENumLit _ _ _ => True
  ELit _ => True
  _ => False

-- does the LITERAL argument [e] fit the CONCRETE param type [pt]?
argLitFits : Expr -> Mono -> Bool
argLitFits e pt = match unlocExpr e
  ENumLit _ _ _ => monoIsNumeric pt
  ELit (LInt _) => monoIsNumeric pt
  ELit (LFloat _) => monoIsFloat pt
  ELit l => monoSameShape (litType l) pt
  _ => False

-- render a literal argument back to its source text (for the swapped-call hint)
renderLitArg : Expr -> Option String
renderLitArg e = match unlocExpr e
  ENumLit n _ _ => Some (intToString n)
  ELit (LInt n) => Some (intToString n)
  ELit (LFloat f) => Some (floatToString f)
  ELit (LString s) => Some ("\"" ++ s ++ "\"")
  ELit (LChar c) => Some ("'" ++ c ++ "'")
  ELit (LBool b) => Some (if b then "True" else "False")
  ELit LUnit => Some "()"
  _ => None

-- the two param types of a function of arity EXACTLY 2 (result NOT an arrow).
-- Requiring exact arity 2 keeps the detector off a >2-arg call's inner 2-arg
-- SUB-spine: currying makes `g 3 "Alice"` a real inferAppExpr node inside
-- `g 3 "Alice" True`, and firing there would suggest `g "Alice" 3`, silently
-- dropping the third argument.  A 3-param `g` has a `Bool -> String` result
-- after two params, so it bails here; only a genuinely 2-param callee matches.
funParams2 : Mono -> Option (Mono, Mono)
funParams2 m = match normalize m
  TFun p1 _ rest => match normalize rest
    TFun p2 _ result => match normalize result
      TFun _ _ _ => None
      _ => Some (p1, p2)
    _ => None
  _ => None

-- Machine fix: replace the span from arg1's start to arg2's end with the
-- swapped source text.  Requires both args to carry real source spans.
swapArgFix : Expr -> Expr -> String -> Option (Loc, String)
swapArgFix a1 a2 repl = match (exprLoc a1, exprLoc a2)
  (Some (Loc f sl sc _ _), Some (Loc _ _ _ el ec)) =>
    Some (Loc f sl sc el ec, repl)
  _ => None

-- If [f] is exactly `<callee> arg1` (a 2-arg call's function part — the callee
-- applied to just its first argument, NOT itself a nested application), resolve
-- the callee to its NAME and its two concrete param types.  `None` for any
-- other shape (nested spine, unbound callee, non-arity-2 type).
twoArgCallee : TcEnv -> Expr -> Option (String, Expr, Mono, Mono)
twoArgCallee env f = match unlocExpr f
  EApp g a1 => match unlocExpr g
    EApp _ _ => None                           -- 3+ args: out of scope
    gHead =>
      do
        name <- appSpineName gHead
        scheme <- lookupVar env name
        (p1, p2) <- funParams2 (instantiate scheme)
        Some (name, a1, p1, p2)
  _ => None

-- Detect a 2-argument transposition on `f x` (`f` = callee applied to arg1, `x`
-- = arg2).  Returns `Some resultVar` (poisoned, so no cascade) when it fires and
-- has emitted the diagnostic; `None` to fall through to the normal path.
detectSwappedArgs2 : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Option Mono
detectSwappedArgs2 env f a2
  | not (isLitArg a2) = None
  | otherwise = match twoArgCallee env f
    None => None
    Some (name, a1, p1, p2) =>
      if not (isLitArg a1) then
        None
      else
        trySwapReport name a1 a2 p1 p2

trySwapReport : String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono -> Mono -> Option Mono
trySwapReport name a1 a2 p1 p2
  | not (monoConcrete p1 && monoConcrete p2) = None
  | monoSameShape p1 p2 = None                 -- same-type params: order is moot
  | otherwise =
    let inOrder = argLitFits a1 p1 && argLitFits a2 p2
    let swapped = argLitFits a2 p1 && argLitFits a1 p2
    if (not inOrder) && swapped then emitSwapDiag name a1 a2 else None

emitSwapDiag : String -> Expr -> Expr -> Option Mono
emitSwapDiag name a1 a2 = match (renderLitArg a1, renderLitArg a2)
  (Some s1, Some s2) =>
    let swappedCall = "\{name} \{s2} \{s1}"
    let msg = "Arguments to '\{name}' are in the wrong order. Try '\{swappedCall}'"
    let help = "swap the arguments: \{swappedCall}"
    let fix = swapArgFix a1 a2 "\{s2} \{s1}"
    let _ = pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-TYPE-MISMATCH" (orElseLoc (exprLoc a1) currentLoc.value) msg help fix
    let v = freshVar ()
    let _ = poisonMismatchVars v v
    Some v
  _ => None

inferAppExpr : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferAppExpr env f x = match lamUnderLoc x
    Some (loc, pats, body) =>
      let ft = infer env f
      let _ = setLocIfSome loc
      -- ft is `(lamType) -> result`; its FIRST domain is the lambda's expected
      -- type.  Peel that, then peel the lambda type itself for its per-param types.
      let lamExpected = funDomain (normalize ft)
      let xt = inferLamChecked env (expectedParamTypes lamExpected (listLen pats)) pats body
      inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt
    None => match shadowStandaloneHead env f
      Some (mname, tagRefs) => inferShadowApp env mname tagRefs f x
      None => match definerShadowArgHead f
       Some (dname, dtagRef, dimplRef) => inferDefinerShadowApp env dname dtagRef dimplRef f x
       None => match definerShadowVarHead f
        Some dname => inferDefinerShadowVarApp env dname f x
        None => match detectSwappedArgs2 env f x
         Some swapResult => swapResult
         None =>
          let ft = infer env f
          let xt = infer env x
          -- P0-16: detect `f(1, 2)` on the PRISTINE ft (before inferApp unifies
          -- its domain with the tuple), then record the residual result so a
          -- downstream no-impl / mismatch can surface the tailored hint.
          let pend = tupleCallShapeHint f x ft
          let res = inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt
          let _ = match pend
            Some (help, fix) => setRef tupleCallCandidates ((res, help, fix)::tupleCallCandidates.value)
            None => ()
          res

-- @Impl: if [x] is an `@Name` disambiguation hint (a bare `EVar "@Name"`, possibly
-- under an ELoc wrapper), return the name with the leading `@` stripped.  Mirrors
-- the two oracle arms `EApp(f, EVar h)` and `EApp(f, ELoc(_, EVar h))` with
-- `h.[0]='@'`.  Anything else → None → the ordinary application path.

-- Bug C (mirror lib/typecheck.ml:2293-2329): peel a single-argument application's
-- head to a standalone-shadow interface-method reference (`toList m` where `toList`
-- is BOTH a Foldable method AND an imported `Map` standalone).  Handles BOTH the
-- MARKED form (`EMethodAt`, the eval/build path — carries a route ref to stamp RLocal)
-- and the UNMARKED form (`EVar`, the `check` path — no route ref; the empty list).
-- Only fires for a SINGLE-param interface method whose name is a registered importer
-- shadow with a stashed standalone scheme — exactly the oracle's guard.  Everything
-- else (no shadow, multi-param iface, non-method head) returns None → the ordinary path.
shadowStandaloneHead : TcEnv -> Expr -> Option (String, List (Ref Route))
shadowStandaloneHead env (ELoc _ f) = shadowStandaloneHead env f
shadowStandaloneHead env (EMethodAt name tagRef _ _)
  | isShadowStandalone name = Some (name, [tagRef])
  | otherwise = None
shadowStandaloneHead env (EVar name)
  | isShadowStandalone name = Some (name, [])
  | otherwise = None
shadowStandaloneHead _ _ = None

-- a name that is BOTH a registered importer shadow (standaloneValuesRef), has a
-- stashed standalone scheme, and names a SINGLE-param interface method.
isShadowStandalone : String -> Bool
isShadowStandalone name = contains name standaloneValuesRef.value
  && isSome (lookupAssoc name shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value)
  && singleParamIfaceMethod name

-- True iff [name]'s interface (in methodIfaceParamsRef) takes exactly ONE type
-- parameter (the receiver-directed single-arg case the oracle scopes the fallback to).
singleParamIfaceMethod : String -> Bool
singleParamIfaceMethod name = match omLookup name methodIfaceParamsRef.value
  Some (_, typarams, _) => listLen typarams == 1
  None => False

-- Bug C: type `toList m` for a no-impl receiver against the imported STANDALONE
-- scheme (giving the concrete result element type, e.g. `List (String, Int)`) and
-- stamp the route RLocal (when marked); if the receiver DOES have an impl (or its head
-- is not yet concrete) fall through to ordinary dispatch (typed against the method
-- scheme the env rebound the name to).  Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml:2308-2329.
--
-- ✅ S1-RESIDUAL-B CLOSED (2026-07-14).  This function used to have NO groundShadowReceiver
-- call, unlike its definer peer inferDefinerShadowApp (which P0-20 gave one).  An UNGROUNDED
-- receiver — a bare `Num` LITERAL is the everyday case, it is `Num a => a`, not `Int` — so
-- never reached the standalone arm below (headTyconMono is None) and fell to the
-- ordinary-app arm, which types against the METHOD scheme the env rebound the name to.
--
--   -- lib/prov.mdk:  export isEmpty : Int -> Bool
--   import lib.prov.{isEmpty}
--   main = println (debug (isEmpty 0))     -- Type mismatch: Int literal vs Int Int
--
-- The `Int Int` in that message is the tell: the PRELUDE's `Foldable.isEmpty : t a -> Bool`
-- is higher-kinded, so unifying `t a` with the literal's tyvar solved `t := Int, a := Int`.
-- The user's imported `isEmpty : Int -> Bool` was never consulted.  (Filed as "under-applies
-- on run" for a CONSTRAINED standalone; the root is broader — it breaks an UNCONSTRAINED one
-- at `check` too, whenever the shadowed method is higher-kinded.)
--
-- Fix, exactly as filed: ground an ungrounded receiver to the IMPORTED standalone's declared
-- domain BEFORE asking the S2 impl question, so typecheck and the post-inference route
-- resolver ask it about the SAME head.  That symmetry is the whole P0-20 lesson — a decision
-- derived twice, at two different times, over a receiver that changed in between.
--
-- ⚠️ NOT affected by the S2 inversion: an IMPORTER shadow keeps the per-receiver rule
-- (Fork 1).  Grounding only decides WHICH head the rule is applied to; a grounded head with
-- a live impl still dispatches (`isEmpty [1, 2]` → `Foldable.isEmpty`, fixture i4).
inferShadowApp : TcEnv -> String -> List (Ref Route) -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferShadowApp env mname tagRefs f x =
  -- capture the application's loc BEFORE inferring the argument (which moves currentLoc),
  -- so a domain mismatch localizes to the call site, not the argument.
  let dloc = currentLoc.value
  let tx = infer env x
  -- The dict-var carve-out (S5/Fork 2) applies to importer shadows too: a receiver that is
  -- the enclosing fn's `=>`-bound constraint var DISPATCHES through that dict, so it must
  -- NOT be ground to the standalone's domain (that would monomorphise the written signature).
  -- groundShadowReceiver is also inert on an already-grounded receiver, so i1/i3/i4 (all
  -- concrete receivers) are byte-identical.
  let _ = groundShadowReceiver (importerShadowDomain mname) tx (definerReceiverIsDictVar mname tx) dloc
  match headTyconMono tx
    Some head => if implExistsForHead shadowKeyTableRef.value mname head then inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (infer env f) tx
    else match lookupAssoc mname shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value
      Some sscheme =>
        let smono = instantiate sscheme
        let r = freshVar ()
        let eff = openRow ()
        let _ = unify smono (TFun tx eff r)
        let _ = performEffect curEffect eff
        let _ = stampRoutes tagRefs
        -- S-1 (Fork D — the importer half): an IMPORTED constrained standalone shadowing
        -- a method breaks identically to a local one (its def is dict-passed in its own
        -- module; this call must supply the dicts).  Compute them off the head type we
        -- just unified, and defer the ROUTE stamp to resolveRLocalSites — stampRoutes
        -- above ran mid-inference, when the receiver is not yet solved, so it could only
        -- write `RLocal sym []`.  forceLocal=True: this arm has already committed the
        -- site's TYPE to the standalone, so its route is not open to re-derivation.
        let _ = recordImporterShadowDicts env mname tagRefs tx
        r
      None => inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (infer env f) tx
    None => inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (infer env f) tx

-- S1-RESIDUAL-B: the IMPORTED standalone's declared domain — the first arrow domain of the
-- scheme stashed in shadowStandaloneSchemesRef (the concrete-head pick made per module by
-- checkModuleFullImpl).  The importer peer of shadowDomainFor's mangled arm, and it must NOT
-- be `lookupVar env mname`: env1 rebound the bare name to the interface METHOD, which is the
-- very scheme whose higher-kinded domain caused the bug.
--
-- None (⇒ grounding is a no-op) when the name has no stashed standalone scheme, or when that
-- standalone's own first parameter is itself polymorphic (`f : a -> Bool`) — unifying a bare
-- tyvar domain with an ungrounded receiver leaves it ungrounded anyway, so the caller's
-- headTyconMono still says None and the old ordinary-app arm runs, byte-identically.
importerShadowDomain : String -> Option Mono
importerShadowDomain mname = match lookupAssoc mname shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value
  None => None
  Some s => match normalize (instantiate s)
    TFun a _ _ => Some a
    _ => None

-- S-1 (importer half): re-record each of the importer shadow's route refs as an
-- RLocalSite carrying the imported standalone's own constraint dicts, so
-- resolveRLocalSites re-stamps the ref post-inference with the dicts filled in.
-- forceLocal=True — inferShadowApp's standalone arm already committed the site's type.
-- Inert (no dicts, same RLocal stamp) for an unconstrained imported standalone.
recordImporterShadowDicts : TcEnv -> String -> List (Ref Route) -> Mono -> Unit
recordImporterShadowDicts _ _ [] _ = ()
recordImporterShadowDicts env mname (r::rest) tx =
  -- the IMPORTED standalone's scheme, NOT `lookupVar env mname` (which env1 rebound to
  -- the interface method) — see shadowStandaloneDictsWith.
  let dicts = shadowStandaloneDictsWith
    (lookupAssoc mname shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value)
    mname
    (routeLocalSym r.value)
    tx
  let _ = setRef pendingRLocalSites
    (RLocalSite mname r tx (routeLocalSym r.value) True (fst dicts) (snd dicts)
      ::pendingRLocalSites.value)
  recordImporterShadowDicts env mname rest tx

-- P0-18: peel a single-argument application's head to a DEFINER-shadow interface-
-- method occurrence (the user's OWN top-level fn reuses a single-param interface
-- method name, e.g. this module's `size : Int -> Int` shadowing `Sizeable`'s
-- `size`).  Unlike importer shadows (shadowStandaloneHead / inferShadowApp), the
-- user's definition SHADOWS the method in scope, so the occurrence is typed against
-- the standalone scheme whose declared domain (`Int`) is NOT the actual receiver.
-- Returning the route ref lets inferDefinerShadowApp record the REAL argument mono
-- for per-receiver routing (`Box` has a `Sizeable` impl → method; `Int` → standalone).
-- P0-18 (importer residual): on the EMIT path a definer shadow's bare name is NOT
-- in definerShadowNamesRef when the standalone is IMPORTED from another module (that
-- ref is keyed on module-local funDefs).  But the mark pass ALREADY seeded such an
-- occurrence's route with the mangled standalone symbol (`RLocal <sym>`), so a
-- non-empty route symbol is an equally-good "this is a shadow" signal that works
-- cross-module.  Firing here routes the occurrence through inferDefinerShadowApp,
-- which types it against the standalone scheme and records the per-receiver mono —
-- so an importer shadow on a live-impl receiver dispatches to the impl, and one on a
-- no-impl receiver falls back to the standalone (RLocal <sym>), exactly like a
-- definer shadow.  On the un-mangled run/check path the route is RNone (sym ""), so
-- this branch is inert there and the ordinary definerShadowNames branch governs.
definerShadowArgHead : Expr -> Option (String, Ref Route, Ref (List Route))
definerShadowArgHead (ELoc _ f) = definerShadowArgHead f
definerShadowArgHead (EMethodAt name tagRef implRef _)
  | singleParamIfaceMethod name && (contains name definerShadowNamesRef.value || routeLocalSym tagRef.value != "") = Some (name, tagRef, implRef)
  | otherwise = None
definerShadowArgHead _ = None

-- P0-18: type `size (Box 3)` exactly as the ordinary path (against the standalone
-- scheme the env rebound `size` to, preserving program types), but record the
-- ACTUAL argument mono (`Box`) as this site's receiver so resolveRLocalSite routes
-- per-receiver: a receiver head WITH an impl of the shadowed interface dispatches to
-- the method (route left untouched → eval arg-tag / emit RKey), one WITHOUT an impl
-- stays the standalone (RLocal).  suppressRLocalRecord stops recordRLocalSite from
-- ALSO pushing the standalone's declared domain (which would mis-route `Box`→`Int`).
inferDefinerShadowApp : TcEnv -> String -> Ref Route -> Ref (List Route) -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferDefinerShadowApp env name tagRef implRef f x =
  let sym = routeLocalSym tagRef.value
  -- P0-19: capture the application's location BEFORE inferring the argument (which
  -- moves currentLoc), so a standalone-domain mismatch reports at the call site.
  let dloc = currentLoc.value
  -- P0-19 d8 (S2/S6): infer the argument FIRST so the head scheme is chosen PER
  -- RECEIVER.  A receiver with a VISIBLE impl of the shadowed interface (impl universe
  -- is GLOBAL — shadowKeyTableRef spans local ∪ imported ∪ prelude) DISPATCHES → type
  -- the head against the METHOD scheme; else it is the STANDALONE.  Without this the
  -- mangled emit path (sym != "") ALWAYS typed against the standalone scheme
  -- (`Int -> Int`), so a definer shadow whose impl is IMPORTED (`size (Box 3)`, d8)
  -- rejected `Int vs Box` at build/run instead of dispatching to the imported impl.
  let xt = infer env x
  -- P0-19 (constrained-receiver arm): an ungrounded receiver that is a dict-bound
  -- constraint var dispatches through that dict — it must neither be typed against the
  -- standalone scheme nor stamped RLocal (which would send eval/emit to the standalone
  -- and, e.g., apply `sz n = n + 1` to a Bool).
  let isDictVar = definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt
  -- P0-20: ground an ungrounded receiver to the standalone's declared domain BEFORE the
  -- dispatch decision, so typecheck asks the impl question about the SAME head the
  -- post-inference route resolver will.  See groundShadowReceiver.
  let _ = groundShadowReceiver (shadowDomainFor env name sym) xt isDictVar dloc
  let dispatches = definerReceiverDispatches name xt
  let _ = setRef suppressRLocalRecord True
  let _ = setRef suppressArgStamp True
  -- P0-19 d4b: this is an application head (marked EMethodAt on run/build) → keep the
  -- method scheme for per-receiver dispatch; suppress the value-position standalone pin.
  let _ = setRef shadowHeadCtxRef True
  -- P0-18: for the STANDALONE arm, type the head against the STANDALONE scheme.  On the
  -- un-mangled run/check path `infer env f` already resolves the bare name to the
  -- standalone (which shadows the method in scope).  On the EMIT path the bare name
  -- resolves to the interface METHOD scheme instead (the standalone was renamed to
  -- `<mid>__name`), whose result loses the concrete element type — e.g. Map's `toList`
  -- would type `List a` not `List (k, v)`, so a downstream `debug (toList m)` resolves
  -- the WRONG element dict and SIGSEGVs.  When we carry the mangled symbol, instantiate
  -- its (standalone) scheme directly so the impl-body types exactly as the run path
  -- does.  For the DISPATCH arm, type against the METHOD scheme (`definerShadowHeadType
  -- env ""`) so a live-impl receiver unifies and the result comes from the method.
  -- DISPATCH arm: the interface METHOD scheme, fetched EXPLICITLY from
  -- methodIfaceParamsRef (which spans imported interfaces) — NOT `infer env f`, because
  -- on the multi-module path the env rebinds the bare method name to the LOCAL
  -- standalone (`Int -> Int`), so inferMethodAt's lookupVar would hand back `Int -> Int`
  -- and reject a live-impl receiver (`Box`).  STANDALONE arm: the standalone scheme
  -- (mangled sym on the emit path preserves the concrete result type).
  let ft = if dispatches then shadowVarHeadMethodScheme env name f else definerShadowHeadType env sym f
  let _ = setRef shadowHeadCtxRef False
  let _ = setRef suppressRLocalRecord False
  let _ = setRef suppressArgStamp False
  -- S-1: on the STANDALONE arm ONLY, compute that standalone's own `=>`-constraint
  -- dicts (and record its call obligations).  The DISPATCH arm typed `ft` against the
  -- interface METHOD scheme, whose dicts are the method's, not the standalone's — asking
  -- there would be meaningless.  A dict-var receiver dispatches through its dict and is
  -- likewise not the standalone.  ([], []) for an unconstrained standalone.
  let dicts = if dispatches || isDictVar then ([], [])
    else shadowStandaloneDicts env name sym xt
  -- Route by the ACTUAL argument type on BOTH the eval path (pendingRLocalSites,
  -- read by resolveRLocalSites) and the emit path (pendingArgStamps, read by
  -- resolveArgStamps — only recorded when this method arg-dispatches, i.e. on the
  -- build path).  xt grounds to the real receiver post-inference; the two default
  -- pushes (recordRLocalSite / recordArgStamp) that would carry the standalone's
  -- declared domain are suppressed above.
  -- ...EXCEPT for a dict-bound constraint-var receiver: resolveRLocalSite's ungrounded
  -- arm stamps RLocal unconditionally, which would route a genuine dict dispatch to the
  -- shadowing standalone.  Not recording the site leaves the route for ordinary dispatch
  -- (RNone → eval arg-tag / emit RKey via resolveArgStamps).
  -- P0-20: the route FOLLOWS THE ARM (`not dispatches` ⇒ forceLocal).  `ft` above has
  -- already committed this site's TYPE, so re-deriving its route from a receiver mono
  -- that may ground LATER (Num defaulting on an unsignatured standalone) is exactly how
  -- `check` and the binary came to disagree.  A dispatching receiver still re-derives
  -- (its head is already ground; RKey comes from resolveArgStamps on the emit path).
  let _ = if isDictVar then ()
    else setRef pendingRLocalSites
      (RLocalSite name tagRef xt (routeLocalSym tagRef.value) (not dispatches) (fst dicts) (snd dicts)
        ::pendingRLocalSites.value)
  let _ = match argDispatchOf name
    Some _ => setRef pendingArgStamps ((name, tagRef, implRef, xt, currentFn.value)::pendingArgStamps.value)
    None => ()
  -- P0-19: on the UN-MANGLED path (sym == "") `ft` is the permissive METHOD scheme
  -- (`a -> Int`), so `inferApp ft xt` would accept ANY receiver.  When this
  -- occurrence resolves to the STANDALONE — a grounded receiver head with no visible
  -- impl of the shadowed interface, or an ungrounded receiver (S5) — impose the
  -- standalone's DECLARED domain on the argument, so a domain mismatch (`size "hi"`,
  -- or a wrapper generalized over the shadow receiver then called at a live-impl type)
  -- becomes a located reject instead of build-time garbage.  A grounded receiver WITH
  -- an impl still dispatches (no enforcement) — that arm is untouched.  On the mangled
  -- emit path (sym != "") `ft` is already the standalone scheme (definerShadowHeadType),
  -- which enforces the domain itself, so this stays inert there.
  let _ = if sym == "" then enforceStandaloneDomain env name xt dloc else ()
  inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt

-- S1/S2 (THE INVERSION, 2026-07-14): is [name] a DEFINER shadow — a top-level fn defined in
-- THIS module that reuses a single-param interface method's name?  This is the exact
-- predicate that decides whether the inversion applies (Fork 1: definer-only).
--
-- ⚠️ It is NOT the same question as "did we reach inferDefinerShadowApp".  That entry point
-- (definerShadowArgHead) ALSO fires for an IMPORTER shadow on the mangled emit path, where
-- the mark pass seeded the route with the provider's mangled symbol (`routeLocalSym != ""`)
-- — see its header.  Inverting on that signal would invert importer shadows too, which
-- Fork 1 forbids: it breaks the everyday `import map` pattern (`isEmpty [1,2]` must still
-- reach `Foldable.isEmpty`).  definerShadowNamesRef holds bare names on EVERY path — the
-- emit path recovers a mangled local funDef through the mangle map (buildDefinerShadows) —
-- and never holds an imported standalone, so it is the reliable definer/importer test.
--
-- The singleParamIfaceMethod conjunct mirrors definerShadowVarHead / definerShadowArgHead
-- verbatim, so every entry point agrees on which occurrences the machinery owns.  A
-- multi-TYPARAM interface (`interface Ix a i`) is deliberately still OUT of scope — that is
-- the pre-existing S-3 / row-26 bug (SHADOW-SEMANTICS.md §2), untouched by this change.
isDefinerShadow : String -> Bool
isDefinerShadow name = singleParamIfaceMethod name
  && contains name definerShadowNamesRef.value

-- S2 (THE INVERSION, 2026-07-14; SHADOW-INVERSION-DESIGN.md): True iff the shadow [name]
-- applied to a receiver of type [xt] should DISPATCH to the interface method rather than
-- call the standalone.
--
-- ⚠️ FOR A DEFINER SHADOW THE IMPL UNIVERSE IS NO LONGER CONSULTED.  It used to be: a
-- receiver whose head tycon had a visible impl of the shadowed interface dispatched, and the
-- standalone was only a FALLBACK for a no-impl head.  That rule silently ERASED the user's
-- function —
--
--   eq : List Int -> List Int -> Bool
--   eq a b = True
--   main = println (debug (eq [1] [2]))   -- printed False: `impl Eq List` won
--
-- — and `check --types` did not even report the user's signature.  Since the prelude declares
-- ~49 method names over every common type (`map`, `filter`, `length`, `compare`, `eq`, `min`,
-- `empty`, `toList`, `display`, …), that made the most natural names in the language into
-- landmines.  A top-level name written in a module is now THAT MODULE'S NAME: a definer
-- shadow is the standalone, unconditionally, and the argument must type against the
-- standalone's declared domain (a mismatch is a located reject — S2/S3/S6).
--
-- The ONE carve-out (S5, Fork 2): a receiver that is a dict-bound `=>` constraint var of the
-- enclosing function still DISPATCHES.  Writing `Sz a => …` is an explicit, written-down
-- request to resolve through the interface, and there is currently no other way to name the
-- method inside a shadowing module (SHADOW-SEMANTICS.md §1.1).  A grounded head never
-- normalizes to a TVar, so it correctly answers False for every concrete receiver.
--
-- An IMPORTER shadow reaching here (via definerShadowArgHead's mangled-sym arm) keeps the
-- OLD per-receiver rule — Fork 1.
definerReceiverDispatches : String -> Mono -> Bool
definerReceiverDispatches name xt
  | isDefinerShadow name = definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt
  | otherwise = match headTyconMono xt
    Some head => implExistsForHead shadowKeyTableRef.value name head
    None => definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt

-- P0-19 (constrained-receiver arm): True iff an UNGROUNDED definer-shadow receiver is
-- one of the ENCLOSING group's `=>`-constraint variables for the SHADOWED interface (or
-- a superinterface of it).  Such an occurrence dispatches through that dict — it is a
-- METHOD call, not the standalone — so it must be typed against the method scheme and
-- must NOT have the standalone's declared domain imposed on it.
--
-- Without this, ANY constrained function that calls the method on its own dict-bound
-- var, in a program where some top-level fn shadows that method's name, is silently
-- monomorphised to the shadow's domain.  That includes the PRELUDE's own bodies: on the
-- flat single-file path `prog` is core ++ user, so a user `eq : Int -> Int -> Int`
-- made core's `neq : Eq a => a -> a -> Bool` (`neq x y = not (eq x y)`) infer `eq x y :
-- Int` and reject with `Type mismatch: Bool vs Int` at the top of the user's file.
definerReceiverIsDictVar : String -> Mono -> Bool
definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt = match normalize xt
  TVar cell => match omLookup name methodIfaceParamsRef.value
    Some (mIface, _, _) => dictVarSatisfiesIface mIface (tyvarId cell) groupConstraintMonosRef.value
    None => False
  _ => False

-- does any of the group's constraint vars BOTH normalize to tyvar [id] AND carry an
-- interface whose (self + transitive supers) closure includes [mIface]?
dictVarSatisfiesIface : String -> Int -> List (String, Mono) -> Bool
dictVarSatisfiesIface _ _ [] = False
dictVarSatisfiesIface mIface id ((cIface, cm)::rest)
  | monoIsTyvarId cm id && contains mIface (ifaceSelfAndSupers cIface) = True
  | otherwise = dictVarSatisfiesIface mIface id rest

monoIsTyvarId : Mono -> Int -> Bool
monoIsTyvarId m id = match normalize m
  TVar cell => tyvarId cell == id
  _ => False

-- [iface] plus its transitive `requires` supers (a `Ord a =>` constraint also supplies
-- the `Eq` dict).  Reads superDeclsRef, the program's interface decls; NOT gated on
-- userIfaceNamesRef (unlike superSlotsOf, whose gate is about dict SLOTS, not about
-- which interfaces a constraint satisfies) — a PRELUDE super chain satisfies the
-- superinterface just as much, it is simply serviced by arg-tag dispatch.
ifaceSelfAndSupers : String -> List String
ifaceSelfAndSupers iface = supersFix [iface] [iface]

supersFix : List String -> List String -> List String
supersFix acc [] = acc
supersFix acc frontier =
  let next = removeAllS acc (dedup (flatMap directSupers frontier))
  supersFix (acc ++ next) next

directSupers : String -> List String
directSupers iface = match ifaceSupersOf superDeclsRef.value iface
  None => []
  Some (_, supers) => map superIfaceName supers

superIfaceName : Super -> String
superIfaceName (Super n _) = n

-- P0-19: impose a definer-shadow standalone's declared domain on the actual argument
-- [xt] WHEN the occurrence resolves to the standalone (RLocal).  No declared signature ⇒
-- nothing to enforce (permissive fallback, unchanged).  [dloc] re-points currentLoc at the
-- application so a mismatch localizes to the call, not the argument.
--
-- S2 (THE INVERSION): a definer shadow now ALWAYS resolves to the standalone, so its domain
-- is ALWAYS enforced.  This used to skip enforcement whenever the receiver's grounded head
-- had a visible impl of the shadowed interface — that head dispatched, so no domain applied.
-- That skip is precisely what let `eq [1] [2]` sail through `check` against the PRELUDE's
-- `Eq.eq` while the user's `eq : List Int -> List Int -> Bool` was discarded.  The only
-- surviving exemption is the Fork-2 dict-var carve-out: imposing a concrete domain on the
-- enclosing group's `=>`-constraint var would monomorphise its written signature.
enforceStandaloneDomain : TcEnv -> String -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
enforceStandaloneDomain _ name xt dloc = match standaloneShadowDomain name
  None => ()
  Some dom =>
    if definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt then
      ()
    else
      unifyStandaloneDomain dom xt dloc

-- P0-20 (SILENT WRONG ANSWER, `eq 1 2` → the binary printed 0): typecheck's
-- dispatch-vs-standalone decision and the POST-inference route resolver
-- (resolveRLocalSite) must ask the S2 impl question about the SAME receiver head.
--
-- They did not.  A definer-shadow receiver that is not yet grounded AT INFERENCE TIME —
-- a NUMERIC LITERAL is the everyday case, it is `Num a => a`, not `Int` — took the
-- ungrounded arm (S5 ⇒ standalone) and was typed against the standalone's scheme.  That
-- typing then UNIFIES the standalone's declared domain into the receiver, so by the time
-- resolveRLocalSite runs the receiver IS grounded — to `Int` — and `Int` has `impl Eq
-- Int`, so the route resolver left the site to DISPATCH.  Result: `check` typed
-- `eq 1 2 : Int` (the user's `eq : Int -> Int -> Int`) while eval/emit called the
-- interface method and produced a `Bool` — `run` panicked (`intToString: not an Int`)
-- and `build` SILENTLY PRINTED 0.  Only literal receivers were affected: `f k = eq k 1`
-- (k : Int, already grounded) dispatched consistently on all three paths.
--
-- Fix: perform S5's monomorphisation FIRST (it is the same unification the standalone
-- arm would have done, only earlier), then decide.  With the receiver grounded to `Int`,
-- S2 sees the impl and DISPATCHES on every path — check == run == build.  A grounded
-- receiver, a dict-bound one, and an unsignatured standalone (no domain to ground to)
-- are all untouched; so is a standalone whose domain head has NO impl of the shadowed
-- interface (map's `toList : Map k v -> …` — there is deliberately no `impl Foldable
-- Map`), which is every definer shadow in the tree.
--
-- [dom] MUST come from shadowDomainFor, not standaloneShadowDomain: on the MANGLED emit
-- path the standalone was renamed `<mid>__eq`, so the bare-name sig table is empty there
-- and grounding would be silently inert — which is not a no-op, it flips the emit path to
-- the STANDALONE arm while check/run dispatch.  (Observed: `build` printed 3, `run`
-- printed False.)  Same bug, other direction.
groundShadowReceiver : Option Mono -> Mono -> Bool -> Option Loc -> Unit
groundShadowReceiver dom xt isDictVar dloc
  | isDictVar = ()
  | isSome (headTyconMono xt) = ()
  | otherwise = match dom
    None => ()
    Some d => unifyStandaloneDomain d xt dloc

-- P0-20: the definer-shadow standalone's declared domain, resolved on EITHER path.
-- Un-mangled run/check (sym == ""): the module's own sig table, keyed by the bare name.
-- Mangled emit (sym != ""): private_mangle renamed the standalone to `<mid>__name`, so
-- definerShadowSigsRef has no `name` entry — take the domain off the env binding for the
-- mangled symbol, which is the very scheme definerShadowHeadType types this site against.
shadowDomainFor : TcEnv -> String -> String -> Option Mono
shadowDomainFor _ name "" = standaloneShadowDomain name
shadowDomainFor env _ sym = match lookupVar env sym
  None => None
  Some s => match normalize (instantiate s)
    TFun a _ _ => Some a
    _ => None

-- unify the standalone domain against the argument at the call's location.
unifyStandaloneDomain : Mono -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
unifyStandaloneDomain dom xt dloc =
  let _ = setRef currentLoc dloc
  unify dom xt

-- a definer-shadow standalone's declared parameter type, freshly instantiated (so a
-- polymorphic standalone domain does not leak vars across sites).  None when there is
-- no top-level signature, or the signature is not a function type.
standaloneShadowDomain : String -> Option Mono
standaloneShadowDomain name = match lookupAssoc name definerShadowSigsRef.value
  None => None
  Some ty => match normalize (instantiate (sigToScheme ty))
    TFun a _ _ => Some a
    _ => None

-- P0-19 d4b (S4): a definer-shadow occurrence in bare VALUE position (NOT the head of
-- its own application — shadowHeadCtxRef is False) denotes the STANDALONE.  Returns the
-- standalone's declared scheme freshly instantiated so a HOF sees `Int -> Int`, not the
-- permissive method scheme `a -> Int` — making `map size [Box …]` a located reject
-- (Box vs Int) while `map size [1,2,3]` still accepts.  Only fires for a single-param
-- interface-method name that is a module-local standalone WITH a declared signature;
-- everything else (app head, non-shadow, unsignatured standalone) returns None → the
-- ordinary env lookup / method scheme, unchanged.
maybeStandaloneValueMono : String -> Option Mono
maybeStandaloneValueMono name
  | shadowHeadCtxRef.value = None
  | not (singleParamIfaceMethod name) = None
  | not (contains name definerShadowNamesRef.value) = None
  | otherwise = standaloneShadowScheme name

-- P0-19: a definer-shadow standalone's full declared scheme (`Int -> Int`), freshly
-- instantiated.  None when the standalone has no top-level signature (nothing to pin —
-- the permissive fallback stays).  Used both for value-position pinning (d4b) and to
-- type a standalone-fallback application against the standalone's result type (d8).
standaloneShadowScheme : String -> Option Mono
standaloneShadowScheme name = map
  (ty => instantiate (sigToScheme ty))
  (lookupAssoc name definerShadowSigsRef.value)

-- P0-19: peel an application head to a definer-shadow interface-method occurrence on
-- the UN-MARKED path (the `check` pipeline runs no marker, so the head is a bare
-- `EVar`, not the `EMethodAt` that definerShadowArgHead matches on run/build).  Same
-- guard as definerShadowArgHead: a single-param interface method whose name is a
-- module-local standalone (definerShadowNamesRef).
definerShadowVarHead : Expr -> Option String
definerShadowVarHead (ELoc _ f) = definerShadowVarHead f
definerShadowVarHead (EVar name)
  | singleParamIfaceMethod name && contains name definerShadowNamesRef.value =
    Some name
  | otherwise = None
definerShadowVarHead _ = None

-- S2 (THE INVERSION): type an un-marked (check-path) definer-shadow application against
-- the module's OWN standalone, unconditionally.  Peer of inferDefinerShadowApp (the marked
-- run/build path); the two must agree cell-for-cell or `check` and the binary diverge (S7),
-- which is the bug class this whole arc keeps producing.
--
-- The receiver's impl universe is no longer consulted (see definerReceiverDispatches).  The
-- sole dispatch arm left is the Fork-2 carve-out: a dict-bound `=>` constraint var, which is
-- an explicit written request to resolve through the interface.  Everything else — grounded
-- live-impl head included — types against the LOCAL standalone scheme, so a domain mismatch
-- (`size (Box 3)` against `size : Int -> Int`) is a located reject at the call, not a silent
-- dispatch that erases the user's function.
inferDefinerShadowVarApp : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferDefinerShadowVarApp env name f x =
  let dloc = currentLoc.value
  let xt = infer env x
  let isDictVar = definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt
  -- P0-20: ground an ungrounded receiver to the standalone's declared domain BEFORE the
  -- dispatch decision (see groundShadowReceiver).  Still load-bearing under the inversion:
  -- it is what gives a bare `Num` literal receiver (`eq 1 2`) the standalone's domain
  -- rather than leaving it open for Num-defaulting to ground LATER, behind typecheck's back.
  let _ = groundShadowReceiver (standaloneShadowDomain name) xt isDictVar dloc
  if isDictVar then
    inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (shadowVarHeadMethodScheme env name f) xt
  else
    inferDefinerStandaloneVarApp env name f x xt dloc

-- P0-19 (d8 dispatch arm): the interface METHOD scheme for [name], from
-- methodIfaceParamsRef (which spans imported interfaces) so per-receiver dispatch works
-- even when the multi-module env has rebound the bare name to the local standalone.
-- Falls back to `infer env f` (flagged as an app head so maybeStandaloneValueMono does
-- not pin it) when the method type is unavailable.
shadowVarHeadMethodScheme : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Mono
shadowVarHeadMethodScheme env name f = match omLookup name methodIfaceParamsRef.value
  Some (_, _, ty) => instantiate (sigToScheme ty)
  None =>
    let _ = setRef shadowHeadCtxRef True
    let ft = infer env f
    let _ = setRef shadowHeadCtxRef False
    ft

-- P0-19 (standalone arm): type the application against the LOCAL standalone scheme so
-- the result is the standalone's (S2/S5); a domain mismatch (`size "hi"`) or a wrapper
-- generalized over the shadow receiver then called at a foreign type localizes to the
-- call [dloc].  No declared signature ⇒ fall back to the permissive method-scheme head
-- + enforceStandaloneDomain (the pre-P0-19 permissive behavior, unchanged).
inferDefinerStandaloneVarApp : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Mono
inferDefinerStandaloneVarApp env name f x xt dloc = match standaloneShadowScheme name
  Some smono =>
    let r = freshVar ()
    let eff = openRow ()
    let _ = setRef currentLoc dloc
    let _ = unify smono (TFun xt eff r)
    let _ = performEffect curEffect eff
    -- S-1 (REJECT direction).  standaloneShadowScheme instantiates the DECLARED SIG via
    -- sigToScheme, which DROPS the `=>` constraints entirely — so `Num String` was never
    -- asked and `check` ACCEPTED `size "hi"` against `size : Num a => a -> a`.  Re-read
    -- the constraints off the signature and record them as call obligations at the
    -- receiver, which makes it a located reject.  (This arm has a signature by
    -- construction; the no-signature arm below goes through `infer env f` → inferVarPlain
    -- → instantiateVarTracked, the ordinary obligation path.)
    let _ = recordStandaloneSigObligations name "" xt
    r
  None =>
    let _ = setRef shadowHeadCtxRef True
    let ft = infer env f
    let _ = setRef shadowHeadCtxRef False
    let _ = enforceStandaloneDomain env name xt dloc
    inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt

-- P0-18: the type of a definer-shadow application HEAD.  With a mangled symbol (the
-- emit path), instantiate the standalone's own scheme (bound under `<mid>__name`) so
-- the concrete result type survives; else infer the head node (the un-mangled path,
-- where the bare name already resolves to the standalone).
definerShadowHeadType : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Mono
definerShadowHeadType env "" f = infer env f
definerShadowHeadType env sym f = match lookupVar env sym
  Some s => instantiate s
  None => infer env f

-- stamp every route ref (one for the marked EMethodAt form, none for the unmarked
-- check-path EVar form) RLocal.  S-1: the standalone's own constraint dicts are NOT
-- resolvable here (inference has not solved the receiver yet) — the caller ALSO pushes
-- an RLocalSite so resolveRLocalSites re-stamps the same ref with the filled dicts
-- post-inference.  An unconstrained standalone never gets a second stamp that differs.
stampRoutes : List (Ref Route) -> Unit
stampRoutes [] = ()
stampRoutes (r::rest) =
  let _ = setRef r (RLocal (routeLocalSym r.value) [])
  stampRoutes rest

-- ── S-1: the shadowing standalone's OWN constraint dicts ──────────────────────
-- A `=>`-constrained top-level fn whose name collides with a visible interface method
-- (`eq : Num a => a -> a` vs the prelude's `Eq.eq`) sits in BOTH the shadow name set
-- and the dict name set.  The marking prePass is a first-match guard chain whose shadow
-- arm is tested FIRST, so the occurrence becomes `EMethodAt`, never `EDictAt` — and no
-- dict is ever computed for it.  But `dictPassDecl` (keyed on the same dict name set)
-- still prepends the dict PARAM to the definition.  Def arity 2, call arity 1 ⇒ the
-- call silently UNDER-APPLIES and the first real argument lands in the dict slot.
--
-- So: when a shadow occurrence resolves to the STANDALONE arm, compute that
-- standalone's constraint slots here — the shadowed interface decides WHICH function,
-- the standalone's own constraints decide WHICH DICTS (clause S9).
--
-- [xt] is the RECEIVER's mono.  We take a tracked instantiation of the standalone's own
-- scheme and unify it with `xt -> <fresh>`, which pins the standalone's constraint
-- variable to the receiver exactly as inferApp will pin the site's real head — so the
-- subst maps each constraint id to whatever that constraint variable becomes at THIS
-- call.  The fresh result var is a throwaway: it does NOT re-type the site (the caller's
-- own `ft`/`inferApp` still owns the site's type), it only ties the dict to the receiver.
--
-- ⚠️ Unify against the RECEIVER, not against the caller's head type `ft`.  On the
-- UN-MANGLED path `ft` is the permissive interface-METHOD scheme, not the standalone's
-- (the two doc-comments in inferDefinerShadowApp contradict each other on this; `:5050`
-- is the correct one).  Unifying the standalone's `a -> a` against the method's
-- `a -> a -> Bool` is an OCCURS-CHECK failure — "Cannot construct infinite type" on the
-- perfectly legal `eq : Num a => a -> a`.  The receiver is common to both.
--
-- Returns the slot-ordered (monos, ifaces) that routesOfMonosTop turns into Routes, and
-- records the call's impl obligations so `size "hi"` (⇒ `Num String`, which has no
-- impl) becomes a LOCATED REJECT at `check` instead of build-time garbage — the second,
-- independent hole in this same arm.  ([] , []) for an unconstrained standalone, which
-- SHORT-CIRCUITS BEFORE THE UNIFY, so an unconstrained shadow (all 5 of the compiler's
-- own) does not even perturb the substitution — the fixpoint provably cannot move.
shadowStandaloneDicts : TcEnv -> String -> String -> Mono -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDicts env name sym xt =
  -- P0-20's sym-awareness lesson, mirrored: on the mangled emit path the standalone is
  -- bound under `<mid>__name`, so a BARE-name lookup here would be silently inert and
  -- `build` would emit the un-dicted call while run/check dicted correctly — the same
  -- bug, mirrored.  shadowDomainFor exists for exactly this reason.
  shadowStandaloneDictsWith (lookupVar env (if sym == "" then name else sym)) name sym xt

-- as shadowStandaloneDicts, but with the standalone's scheme supplied explicitly.
-- The IMPORTER shadow needs this: in the importing module `env1` deliberately rebinds the
-- bare name to the polymorphic INTERFACE-METHOD scheme (that rebinding is the entire
-- reason shadowStandaloneSchemesRef exists), so `lookupVar env name` hands back the
-- METHOD — whose quantified ids are not the ones funConstraintsRef recorded for the
-- imported standalone, so every dict slot silently resolves to nothing.  That is why the
-- importer shadow was correct on `build`/`wasm` (which reach the standalone through the
-- MANGLED symbol) and still under-applied on `run`.
shadowStandaloneDictsWith : Option Scheme -> String -> String -> Mono -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDictsWith mscheme name sym xt =
  let _ = recordStandaloneSigObligations name sym xt
  shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt mscheme (if sym == "" then name else sym) xt

-- the dict slots ONLY — no call obligations.
-- recordRLocalSite needs this: it fires for EVERY shadow method occurrence, at a point
-- where the standalone-vs-dispatch choice is NOT yet made (resolveRLocalSite decides it
-- post-inference from the grounded receiver).  Recording an obligation here would fire on
-- the DISPATCH arm too and spuriously reject `size (Box 3)` with `Num Box`.  When the
-- receiver does turn out to have an impl, stampRLocalOrFallback simply discards these
-- slots and the route dispatches as before.
shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt : Option Scheme -> String -> Mono -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt mscheme key xt =
  let slots = declaredConstraintSlots key
  -- REJECT direction — from the SIGNATURE, NOT from schemeObligationsRef.
  -- ⚠️ For a SIGNATURED binding the two obligation tables live in DIFFERENT ID SPACES:
  -- funConstraintsRef's ids (and the env scheme's quantified ids) come from
  -- sigToSchemeTvs over the declared signature, while schemeObligationsRef's come from
  -- GENERALIZATION.  Mapping the latter's ids through the former's substitution silently
  -- finds nothing — which is why the dict ROUTES resolved correctly (they use
  -- funConstraintsRef) while the obligation stayed unrecorded and `size "hi"` was
  -- ACCEPTED.  The signature is the one source both halves can agree on.
  -- the unconstrained standalone: nothing to solve, no unify, no perturbation at all.
  -- This short-circuit is what makes the change provably fixpoint-safe — all 5 of the
  -- compiler's own definer shadows are unconstrained and never reach the unify below.
  if isEmptyL (fst slots) then ([], [])
  else match mscheme
    None => ([], [])
    Some s =>
      let inst = instantiateTracked s
      let r = freshVar ()
      let eff = openRow ()
      let _ = unify (fst inst) (TFun xt eff r)
      shadowStandaloneDictSlots slots (snd inst)

-- the standalone's dict ROUTE slots for this call: its declared constraint ids mapped
-- through the call's instantiation subst.  Expanded with transitive supers EXACTLY as
-- inferDictAtFound does (WS-1b) — dictArityOf sizes the DEFINITION's leading dict params
-- from the same expanded table, so call and def must agree slot-for-slot or we have
-- merely moved the arity disagreement.  Empty on the plain `check` path
-- (funConstraintsRef is empty there) — check stamps no routes, so that is correct;
-- recordStandaloneSigObligations is what gives `check` its reject direction.
shadowStandaloneDictSlots : (List Int, List String) -> List (Int, Mono) -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDictSlots slots subst =
  if isEmptyL (fst slots) then ([], [])
  else
    let expanded = expandSupersPairs superDeclsRef.value (zipL (snd slots) (fst slots))
    (constraintMonosOf (map snd expanded) subst, map fst expanded)

-- ── S-1, REJECT direction on the plain `check` path ───────────────────────────
-- Record the standalone's declared `=>` constraints as CALL obligations, resolved from
-- its own SIGNATURE.
--
-- ⚠️ Why the SIGNATURE and not schemeObligationsRef: for a signatured binding those are
-- two different ID SPACES (sigToSchemeTvs's fresh quantification vs generalization's), so
-- schemeObligationsRef's ids are not keys of the env scheme's instantiation subst and
-- recordSchemeCallObligations silently records nothing.  That is exactly why the dict
-- ROUTES came out right (they key off funConstraintsRef, which IS the signature's id
-- space) while `check` still ACCEPTED `size "hi"` (⇒ `Num String`, which has no impl).
--
-- ⚠️ sigToSchemeTvs's `tvs` are the scheme's BOUND tyvars, and `instantiate` RE-freshens
-- them — so unifying the *instantiated* type against the receiver does not touch `tvs` at
-- all.  We must instantiateTracked and map each constraint tyvar's ID through the subst,
-- which is precisely what that function's own doc-comment prescribes ("so a caller can
-- recover the id of a specific constraint variable … a call-site instantiation's subst
-- maps them").  Getting this wrong records nothing and reads as "the fix does not work".
--
-- Keyed by the BARE name on the un-mangled run/check path and by the MANGLED symbol on
-- the emit path (sigsOf runs over the already-renamed decls there) — try both, exactly as
-- shadowDomainFor has to.
recordStandaloneSigObligations : String -> String -> Mono -> Unit
recordStandaloneSigObligations name sym xt = match standaloneShadowSigTy name sym
  None => ()
  Some ty => match sigConstraintsOf ty
    [] => ()
    cs =>
      let pr = sigToSchemeTvs ty
      let inst = instantiateTracked (fst pr)
      let r = freshVar ()
      let eff = openRow ()
      let _ = unify (fst inst) (TFun xt eff r)
      recordSigConstraintObls cs (snd pr) (snd inst)

-- the standalone's declared signature Ty, under whichever key this path stores it.
standaloneShadowSigTy : String -> String -> Option Ty
standaloneShadowSigTy name sym = match lookupAssoc name definerShadowSigsRef.value
  Some ty => Some ty
  None => if sym == "" then None else lookupAssoc sym definerShadowSigsRef.value

-- every `=>` constraint in the signature.  Recurses the spine like constraintVarNamesTy
-- rather than matching only the top node, so an effect-annotated sig still yields them.
sigConstraintsOf : Ty -> List Constraint
sigConstraintsOf (TyConstrained cs t) = cs ++ sigConstraintsOf t
sigConstraintsOf (TyEffect _ _ t) = sigConstraintsOf t
sigConstraintsOf _ = []

-- one obligation per (iface, constraint tyvar) in the signature, at that tyvar's mono in
-- THIS call's instantiation.  A constraint over something other than a plain tyvar, or a
-- tyvar absent from either map, records nothing (never over-rejects).
recordSigConstraintObls : List Constraint -> List (String, Mono) -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
recordSigConstraintObls [] _ _ = ()
recordSigConstraintObls ((Constraint iface args)::rest) tvs subst =
  let _ = recordSigConstraintArgs iface args tvs subst
  recordSigConstraintObls rest tvs subst

recordSigConstraintArgs : String -> List Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
recordSigConstraintArgs _ [] _ _ = ()
recordSigConstraintArgs iface (a::rest) tvs subst =
  let _ = match constraintArgVarName a
    Some v => match lookupAssoc v tvs
      Some tv => match lookupAssocI (monoTyvarId tv) subst
        Some m => pushCallObl (iface, m, currentLoc.value)
        None => ()
      None => ()
    None => ()
  recordSigConstraintArgs iface rest tvs subst

-- the tyvar NAME a constraint argument mentions (`Num a` → "a"); None for a non-tyvar
-- argument (a concrete head), which carries no per-call obligation here.
constraintArgVarName : Ty -> Option String
constraintArgVarName (TyVar v) = Some v
constraintArgVarName _ = None

-- set currentLoc only when the lambda carried a span (mirror the ELoc arm), so
-- a bare unlocated lambda doesn't blank out an enclosing position.
setLocIfSome : Option Loc -> Unit
setLocIfSome None = ()
setLocIfSome (Some l) = setRef currentLoc (Some l)

-- peek through an ELoc wrapper to a lambda; returns its loc, params and body.
-- Non-lambda arguments (the common case) take the ordinary inference path.
lamUnderLoc : Expr -> Option (Option Loc, List Pat, Expr)
lamUnderLoc (ELam pats body) = Some (None, pats, body)
lamUnderLoc (ELoc l (ELam pats body)) = Some (Some l, pats, body)
lamUnderLoc _ = None

-- the function's first parameter type (the lambda's expected type), or a fresh
-- var when `ft` is not yet a concrete arrow (then nothing is pushed; the trailing
-- `unify ft (TFun xt …)` reconciles).
funDomain : Mono -> Mono
funDomain (TFun a _ _) = a
funDomain _ = freshVar ()

-- peel up to `n` arrow domains off the (normalized) lambda's expected type; those
-- domains are the lambda's expected PARAM types.  Stops early if it is not (yet)
-- an arrow — those params fall back to a fresh var in inferLamChecked, and the
-- trailing `unify ft (TFun xt …)` reconciles them.
expectedParamTypes : Mono -> Int -> List Mono
expectedParamTypes _ 0 = []
expectedParamTypes (TFun a _ b) n =
  a :: expectedParamTypes (normalize b) (n - 1)
expectedParamTypes _ _ = []

-- like inferLam, but each lambda param is unified with the corresponding expected
-- param type (from the function being applied) right after inferPat — so the body
-- and the hover side-channel see the expected type, not a bare fresh var.
inferLamChecked : TcEnv -> List Mono -> List Pat -> Expr -> Mono
inferLamChecked env expected pats body =
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  let _ = unifyParamsExpected (fst pr) expected
  let saved = curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef curEffect (openRow ())
  let bodyTy = infer (extendVars env (snd pr)) body
  let bodyEff = curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef curEffect saved
  arrowsWithLastEffect (fst pr) bodyEff bodyTy

-- unify each param type with its expected type, for the prefix both lists cover.
unifyParamsExpected : List Mono -> List Mono -> Unit
unifyParamsExpected (p::ps) (e::es) =
  let _ = unify p e
  unifyParamsExpected ps es
unifyParamsExpected _ _ = ()

-- Chunk C: recover the ultimate callee name of an application spine (the
-- leftmost head), stripping transparent wrappers.  `None` when the head is not
-- a plain variable (so no name to blame in an over-application message).
appSpineName : Expr -> Option String
appSpineName (ELoc _ e) = appSpineName e
appSpineName (EDoOrigin _ e) = appSpineName e
appSpineName (EAnnot e _) = appSpineName e
appSpineName (EApp f _) = appSpineName f
appSpineName (EVar n) = Some n
appSpineName (EVarAt n _) = Some n
appSpineName _ = None

-- Chunk C: number of arguments applied in this spine (`f a b c` ⇒ 3).
appSpineArgCount : Expr -> Int
appSpineArgCount (ELoc _ e) = appSpineArgCount e
appSpineArgCount (EDoOrigin _ e) = appSpineArgCount e
appSpineArgCount (EAnnot e _) = appSpineArgCount e
appSpineArgCount (EApp f _) = 1 + appSpineArgCount f
appSpineArgCount _ = 0

-- Chunk C messages (T-NOT-A-FUNCTION): a value of a ground non-function type is
-- being applied.  Base wording names the offending type; the context-aware
-- form names the over-applied callee and its arity.
notAFunctionMsg : String -> String
notAFunctionMsg tyName = "This expression has type \{tyName}, which is not a function, so it cannot be applied to an argument."

-- F-dimension actionable-fix hint appended to `notAFunctionMsg` (2026-07-04):
-- names the offending value when the spine head is a plain variable, else
-- falls back to a name-free phrasing.  No mechanical `fix` — the caller
-- decides whether to drop the argument or call a different function.
notAFunctionHint : Option String -> String
notAFunctionHint (Some name) = " '\{name}' is a value, not a function; remove the argument, or call a function here."
notAFunctionHint None = " This is a value, not a function; remove the argument, or call a function here."

overAppliedMsg : String -> Int -> Int -> String
overAppliedMsg name takes applied = "'\{name}' takes \{intToString takes} argument(s) but is applied to \{intToString applied}."

-- Batch B #6 reframe (2026-07-04): a container-consuming HOF whose next declared
-- parameter is a higher-kinded container `f a` (an unresolved tyvar head applied
-- to an element, i.e. `TApp (TVar …) elem`) is being handed a SCALAR argument —
-- a bare arity-0 type (`String`/`Int`/…) that has no element slot for `f` to
-- bind.  Left to the generic `unify (f a) String` sink it leaks a raw
-- tyvar-application `Type mismatch: a b vs String`.  This detector recognises the
-- shape UPSTREAM (in inferApp, where the callee name + arg loc are still in
-- scope) so the diagnostic can be reframed as container-vs-scalar.  SOUND: a
-- `TApp _ _ ~ TCon n` unification can only ever FAIL — a genuine container arg is
-- itself a `TApp` (`List a`, `Maybe a`, …) and would UNIFY the element, never
-- reaching a mismatch; the reframe therefore never hijacks a legitimate
-- higher-kinded mismatch.  Returns the scalar type's name when it fires.
containerParamScalarArg : Mono -> Mono -> Option String
containerParamScalarArg ft xt = match normalize ft
  TFun dom _ _ => match normalize dom
    TApp _ _ => match monoHeadCon dom
      None => match normalize xt
        TCon n => Some n
        _ => None
      Some _ => None
    _ => None
  _ => None

containerArgMsg : Option String -> String -> String
containerArgMsg fn argTy =
  let who = match fn
    Some n => "'\{n}'"
    None => "This function"
  "\{who} expects a container (like List or Array) here, but got \{argTy}\{containerArgHint argTy}"

-- Actionable-fix hint appended to `containerArgMsg`.  Special-cases `String`
-- (the most common scalar handed to `map`): a String's characters are reachable
-- via `string.toChars`, so name that conversion rather than a generic one.
containerArgHint : String -> String
containerArgHint "String" = ". Pass a List or Array; to work over a string's characters, convert it with `string.toChars` first."
containerArgHint argTy =
  ". Pass a List or Array, or convert the \{argTy} to one first."
-- P0-16 beginner call-syntax hint.  A newcomer writes `add(1, 2)` (C/Python/JS
-- call syntax) meaning `add 1 2`; in Medaka `(1, 2)` is a TUPLE, so `add(1, 2)`
-- applies `add` to ONE tuple argument → a partial application / no-impl error
-- whose message ("No impl of Display for ((Int, Int) -> (Int, Int))") is
-- inscrutable.  We DETECT the shape at the application site — a curried arity-N
-- function applied to a single arity-N tuple literal whose first param is NOT
-- itself an N-tuple — and record the residual result type as a candidate; the
-- no-impl + type-mismatch surface sites then append the tailored hint when the
-- offending type IS that residual.  Recording is generous (a legitimate partial
-- application like `map (const (1, 2)) xs` also records) but the hint is only
-- ever ATTACHED to a genuine downstream error, so a legit partial application —
-- whose residual is consumed WITHOUT error — never shows it.  (residualMono,
-- help, optional (span, replacement) fix); reset by resetState.
tupleCallCandidates : Ref (List (Mono, String, Option (Loc, String)))
tupleCallCandidates = Ref []

-- number of leading `->` arrows in a (normalized) type — its curried arity.
funArity : Mono -> Int
funArity m = match normalize m
  TFun _ _ r => 1 + funArity r
  _ => 0

-- the elements of a tuple-literal argument (through the transparent ELoc wrapper).
tupleArgElems : Expr -> Option (List Expr)
tupleArgElems (ELoc _ e) = tupleArgElems e
tupleArgElems (ETuple es) = Some es
tupleArgElems _ = None

-- True iff [ft]'s first parameter is itself an arity-[n] tuple type — the genuine
-- "the tuple IS the intended single argument" shape (e.g. `f (a, b) c = …`), which
-- must NOT be flagged.  Read on the PRISTINE ft (before inferApp unifies its domain
-- with the tuple arg), so the domain here is the declared param type.
domainIsTupleOfArity : Mono -> Int -> Bool
domainIsTupleOfArity ft n = match normalize ft
  TFun dom _ _ => match monoHeadCon dom
    Some c => c == "__tuple\{intToString n}__"
    None => False
  _ => False

-- render a tuple element to source-ish text for the `add 1 2` fix, for the simple
-- literal/var/negation cases; None for anything a full printer would be needed for.
renderCallArg : Expr -> Option String
renderCallArg (ELoc _ e) = renderCallArg e
renderCallArg (EAnnot e _) = renderCallArg e
renderCallArg (ENumLit n _ _) = Some (intToString n)
renderCallArg (ELit (LInt n)) = Some (intToString n)
renderCallArg (ELit (LBool True)) = Some "True"
renderCallArg (ELit (LBool False)) = Some "False"
renderCallArg (ELit (LString s)) = Some ("\"" ++ s ++ "\"")
renderCallArg (ELit (LChar c)) = Some ("'" ++ c ++ "'")
renderCallArg (EVar n) = Some n
renderCallArg (EUnOp "-" e _) = map ("-" ++ _) (renderCallArg e)
renderCallArg _ = None

-- all-or-nothing render of every tuple element.
renderCallArgs : List Expr -> Option (List String)
renderCallArgs [] = Some []
renderCallArgs (e::rest) = match renderCallArg e
  None => None
  Some s => map (s :: _) (renderCallArgs rest)

-- source span covering the whole `name(args)` call: callee start → tuple end.
tupleCallSpanLoc : Expr -> Expr -> Option Loc
tupleCallSpanLoc f x = match (exprLoc f, exprLoc x)
  (Some (Loc file sl sc _ _), Some (Loc _ _ _ el ec)) =>
    Some (Loc file sl sc el ec)
  _ => None

-- build the (help, optional fix) for a detected tuple-call.  The help names the
-- rule (Medaka calls with spaces, `(…)` is a tuple) and gives the exact rewrite;
-- when every element renders, a machine-applicable `fix` rewrites `add(1, 2)` →
-- `add 1 2` verbatim.  ERROR-QUALITY: located (caller sets loc), names the issue,
-- actionable fix, carries a code (T-NO-IMPL / T-TYPE-MISMATCH at the surface site).
buildTupleCallHint : String -> List Expr -> Expr -> Expr -> (String, Option (Loc, String))
buildTupleCallHint name es f x = match renderCallArgs es
  Some parts =>
    let argsCsv = joinWith ", " parts
    let argsSpaced = joinWith " " parts
    let spread = "\{name} \{argsSpaced}"
    let help = "Medaka calls functions with spaces, not parentheses: `\{name}(\{argsCsv})` applies `\{name}` to one tuple `(\{argsCsv})`. Did you mean `\{spread}`?"
    let fix = map (span => (span, spread)) (tupleCallSpanLoc f x)
    (help, fix)
  None =>
    let n = listLen es
    let help = "Medaka calls functions with spaces, not parentheses: `\{name}(…)` passes a single \{intToString n}-tuple, not \{intToString n} arguments. Separate them with spaces, e.g. `\{name} x y`."
    (help, None)

-- detect the tuple-call shape on the PRISTINE ft (before inferApp) and return the
-- (help, fix) to record against the residual result type, else None.  Fires only
-- when the argument is a tuple literal of arity N ≥ 2, the callee has curried arity
-- EXACTLY N (spreading the tuple would saturate it), and its first param is not
-- itself an N-tuple (the genuine tuple-arg case), and the callee is a named spine.
tupleCallShapeHint : Expr -> Expr -> Mono -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
tupleCallShapeHint f x ft = match tupleArgElems x
  None => None
  Some es =>
    let n = listLen es
    if n < 2 then
      None
    else if funArity ft != n then
      None
    else if domainIsTupleOfArity ft n then
      None
    else
      map (name => buildTupleCallHint name es f x) (appSpineName f)

-- surface-site lookup: does [m] (a type appearing in a no-impl / mismatch error)
-- equal a recorded tuple-call residual?  Compared by normalized rendering — the
-- residual IS the very type that flows into the error, so they render identically.
tupleCallHintFor : Mono -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
tupleCallHintFor m =
  findTupleCand (ppMono (normalize m)) tupleCallCandidates.value

findTupleCand : String -> List (Mono, String, Option (Loc, String)) -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
findTupleCand _ [] = None
findTupleCand key ((res, help, fix)::rest)
  | ppMono (normalize res) == key = Some (help, fix)
  | otherwise = findTupleCand key rest

-- first tuple-call hint matching any of the offending types in an error.
firstTupleCallHint : List Mono -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
firstTupleCallHint [] = None
firstTupleCallHint (m::rest) = match tupleCallHintFor m
  Some hf => Some hf
  None => firstTupleCallHint rest

inferApp : List (String, Expr) -> Expr -> Mono -> Mono -> Mono
inferApp lets appExpr ft xt =
  let r = freshVar ()
  -- Chunk C: if the callee normalizes to a concrete NON-arrow head (a known type
  -- constructor like `Int`/`List Int`, never a tyvar or an arrow), applying it
  -- is genuinely impossible.  Emit a context-aware `T-NOT-A-FUNCTION` instead of
  -- letting `unify ft (TFun …)` render a leaked `Int vs a -> b` mismatch, and
  -- poison the fresh result var so no secondary obligation cascade fires (reuses
  -- Chunk A's poisoned-var set).  Fires ONLY on a ground non-arrow head: a real
  -- function (`TFun`) or an as-yet-unresolved var (`TVar`) is unaffected.
  match monoHeadCon ft
    Some _ => inferAppNotFunction appExpr ft r
    None => match containerParamScalarArg ft xt
      Some scalarTy =>
        -- Batch B #6: container-consuming HOF handed a scalar.  Reframe here
        -- (callee name + arg loc in scope) instead of letting the generic sink
        -- leak `a b vs String`.  Skip the unify (it would push the leaked
        -- mismatch as a SECOND diagnostic) and poison `r` to suppress cascade.
        let hint = containerArgHint scalarTy
        let _ = pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-TYPE-MISMATCH" currentLoc.value (containerArgMsg (appSpineName appExpr) scalarTy) hint None
        -- Poison the container tyvar + element vars (in ft) exactly as the generic
        -- unify sink would (`monoUnboundIds a ++ b`), so the dangling `Mappable f`
        -- obligation sees a poisoned var and suppresses its own cascade.
        let _ = poisonMismatchVars ft xt
        let _ = poisonMismatchVars r r
        r
      None =>
        let eff = openRow ()
        -- Type-error span (Bite 3, fork 3 = ARGUMENT): point a wrong-arg-type
        -- mismatch at the argument expression rather than the callee.  The live
        -- `currentLoc` after inferring the arg is its last atom; refine it to the
        -- whole argument span so `len 5` (len : String -> Int) squiggles the `5`.
        let _ = match appArgLoc appExpr
          Some al => setRef currentLoc (Some al)
          None => ()
        let _ = unify ft (TFun xt eff r)
        -- v2 Stage 2b: if f resolves to a leaf extern carrying an inferred-hole
        -- Prefix atom (`<Net _>`), fill it by α of the application spine's FIRST
        -- argument before the row propagates / is checked against any bound.  `lets`
        -- seeds α with the enclosing body's let scope (EFFECTS WS-2).
        let eff2 = fillHolesInRow lets eff (spineFirstArg appExpr)
        let _ = performEffect curEffect eff2
        r

-- Chunk C: the applied head is a ground non-function.  If the spine over-applies
-- a named function (callee name known AND at least one arg was consumed before
-- the type went non-arrow) use the arity-aware wording; otherwise fall back to
-- naming the non-function type.  Poison `r` to suppress downstream cascade.
inferAppNotFunction : Expr -> Mono -> Mono -> Mono
inferAppNotFunction appExpr ft r =
  let applied = appSpineArgCount appExpr
  let takes = applied - 1
  let _ = match appSpineName appExpr
    Some name => if takes >= 1 then pushTypeError "T-NOT-A-FUNCTION" (overAppliedMsg name takes applied)
    else
      let hint = notAFunctionHint (Some name)
      pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt
        "T-NOT-A-FUNCTION"
        currentLoc.value
        (notAFunctionMsg (ppMono (normalize ft)) ++ hint)
        hint
        None
    None =>
      let hint = notAFunctionHint None
      pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt
        "T-NOT-A-FUNCTION"
        currentLoc.value
        (notAFunctionMsg (ppMono (normalize ft)) ++ hint)
        hint
        None
  let _ = poisonMismatchVars r r
  r

inferLam : TcEnv -> List Pat -> Expr -> Mono
inferLam env pats body =
  -- Patterns carry no source Loc, so a pattern-level type error (e.g. an
  -- over-applied nullary constructor `A _`) would otherwise be recorded at the
  -- STALE `currentLoc` left by the previous clause/function's body inference —
  -- mis-blaming an innocent sibling clause.  Anchor `currentLoc` to THIS clause's
  -- body loc before inferring the patterns, so the error lands on the right
  -- clause (the body re-sets `currentLoc` via its own ELoc arm afterwards).
  let _ = match exprLoc body
    Some l => setRef currentLoc (Some l)
    None => ()
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  let saved = curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef curEffect (openRow ())
  let bodyTy = infer (extendVars env (snd pr)) body
  let bodyEff = curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef curEffect saved
  arrowsWithLastEffect (fst pr) bodyEff bodyTy

-- The first Bool (`mut`) is always False — `let mut` is a parser error (beta
-- mutability model), so no inline mut-let path remains here.
inferLet : TcEnv -> Bool -> Bool -> Pat -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferLet env _ isFun pat e1 e2
  | isFun = match pat
    PVar x => inferRecLet env x e1 e2
    _ => inferLetSimple env pat e1 e2
  | otherwise = inferLetSimple env pat e1 e2

-- T2: a recursive inline function binding (`let go n = … in …`) must
-- pre-bind `go` with a monomorphic placeholder so the RHS can reference
-- itself, then generalize (a function is a value — oracle `:1668-1681`).

-- Self-recursive inline `let` binding (mirrors blockRecLet + the oracle is_fun
-- arm): placeholder pre-bind, infer body, unify, generalize through the body.
inferRecLet : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferRecLet env x e1 e2 =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let placeholder = freshVar ()
  let envSelf = extendVar env x (monoScheme placeholder)
  let t1 = infer envSelf e1
  let _ = unify placeholder t1
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  let addedObls = takeFirst (pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum addedObls t1  -- PLAN.md #11
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls [t1]  -- RETPOS soundness
  let _ = recordLocalBind x t1  -- LSP hover (recursive PVar let bypasses inferPat)
  infer (seedAlphaLets (extendVar env x (generalize t1)) x e1) e2  -- WS-2: α scope-seed

inferLetSimple : TcEnv -> Pat -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferLetSimple env pat e1 e2 =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let t1 = infer env e1
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  -- PLAN.md #11: ground an ambiguous Num-only var before this inner let
  -- generalizes, so `let z = 2 in y + z` (y:Float) is a clean type error and
  -- never leaks an unbound Num var.  Scoped to this binding's new obligations.
  let addedObls = takeFirst (pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum addedObls t1
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls [t1]   -- RETPOS soundness
  inferLetBody env pat t1 e1 e2

inferLetBody : TcEnv -> Pat -> Mono -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferLetBody env (PVar x) t1 e1 e2 =
  let _ = recordLocalBind x t1  -- LSP hover (PVar let bypasses inferPat)
  infer
    (seedAlphaLets
      (extendVar env x (genRestricted (isNonexpansive e1) t1))
      x
      e1)
    e2  -- WS-2: α scope-seed
inferLetBody env pat t1 _ e2 =
  let pr = inferPat env pat
  let _ = unify (fst pr) t1
  infer (extendVars env (snd pr)) e2

inferIf : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferIf env c t e =
  let _ = unify (infer env c) (TCon "Bool")
  let tt = infer env t
  let ee = infer env e
  let _ = noteNumlitCtx tt ee "if"  -- Chunk D: site-specific Num mis-framing wording
  let _ = unify tt ee
  tt

inferEach : TcEnv -> List Expr -> List Mono
inferEach _ [] = []
inferEach env (e::es) =
  let t = infer env e
  t :: inferEach env es

inferListLit : TcEnv -> List Expr -> Mono
inferListLit env es =
  let elem = freshVar ()
  let _ = unifyAllNumlit elem (inferEach env es)  -- Chunk D: tag list-element Num mismatch
  TApp (TCon "List") elem

-- Chunk D: like unifyAll but tags each element that is a still-unbound literal var
-- being forced against a concrete accumulator (or vice-versa) with the "list" ctx.
unifyAllNumlit : Mono -> List Mono -> Unit
unifyAllNumlit _ [] = ()
unifyAllNumlit t (x::xs) =
  let _ = noteNumlitCtx t x "list"
  let _ = unify t x
  unifyAllNumlit t xs

inferAnnot : TcEnv -> Expr -> Ty -> Mono
inferAnnot env e ty =
  let t = infer env e
  let pair = astTypeFreshTbl ty
  let annot = fst pair
  let _ = unify t annot
  let _ = checkAnnotTooGeneral ty (map snd (snd pair))
  annot

-- Phase 108 head-pin (`:~`, EHeadAnnot): like inferAnnot but WITHOUT the
-- checkAnnotTooGeneral skolemization guard — the pin's type variables are MEANT
-- to ground (the container literal's element types).  Fixes `e`'s head tycon to
-- the named container so `fromEntries`'s return-position dispatch resolves to
-- that container's impl.  Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's EHeadAnnot arm.
--
-- Phase 114 (was wrongly dropped here): ignore the *arity* the lowering happened
-- to supply and apply the head tycon to its *declared* arity of fresh vars.  The
-- parser can't tell empty `Map { }` from `Set { }` — both have no entries, so
-- classifyBrace falls through to `ESetLit name []` and the desugar pins a UNARY
-- `Map _a`, the wrong arity for `Map k v` (→ `Map vs Map …`).  Looking the arity
-- up by the head tycon makes empty literals work regardless of the lowering's
-- guess; element vars stay free and ground via inference as before.
inferHeadAnnot : TcEnv -> Expr -> Ty -> Mono
inferHeadAnnot env e ty =
  let t = infer env e
  let annot = headAnnotTy ty
  let _ = unify t annot
  t

-- The head-pin's target type: if the annotation's head is a TyCon with a known
-- declared arity (from `dataParamKindsRef`), rebuild it at that arity with fresh
-- vars (so an empty literal's wrong-arity pin is corrected); otherwise fall back
-- to the literal annotation type.
