headAnnotTy : Ty -> Mono
headAnnotTy ty = match tyAppSpine ty
  (TyCon n _, _) => match lookupAssoc n dataParamKindsRef.value
    Some kinds => applyParams (TCon n) (freshVars (listLen kinds))
    None => fst (astTypeFreshTbl ty)
  _ => fst (astTypeFreshTbl ty)

-- C3 (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml:1821-1842): each type variable named in the
-- annotation asserts polymorphism, so after unification it must still be a
-- DISTINCT, unbound variable.  If one ground to a concrete type, or two
-- annotation variables collapsed to the same variable, the annotation is more
-- polymorphic than the expression — reject.  `sigTvarIds` keeps only the entries
-- that normalize to a TVar, so a grounded var drops out (count shrinks) and a
-- collapse shows as a duplicate id.  EHeadAnnot is exempt (no call to this).
checkAnnotTooGeneral : Ty -> List Mono -> Unit
checkAnnotTooGeneral ty resolved =
  let ids = sigTvarIds resolved
  if listLen ids < listLen resolved || hasDupI ids then
    pushTypeError "T-TYPE-TOO-GENERAL" (annotTooGeneralMsg ty)
  else
    ()

inferMatch : TcEnv -> Mono -> List Arm -> Mono
inferMatch env scrutTy arms =
  let result = freshVar ()
  let _ = inferArms env scrutTy result arms
  let _ = checkMatchExhaustive scrutTy arms
  let _ = checkMatchRedundant scrutTy arms
  result
-- Type-aware exhaustiveness (`check_match`): run AFTER the arms have unified
-- the scrutinee type, so `scrutTy` is fully resolved when we read its head.

-- The non-exhaustive-`match` warning (note the em-dash).  The redundancy pass
-- is not ported — only the exhaustiveness warning.  When the Maranget witness
-- algorithm can name the uncovered case (`nonExhaustiveMsg`) the message is
-- specialised to name the scrutinee type + the missing constructor; this
-- generic form is the fallback if no witness is recoverable.
nonExhaustiveMatchWarning : String
nonExhaustiveMatchWarning =
  "Warning: non-exhaustive match. Not all cases are covered"

-- Warn if an all-wildcard query is still useful against the matrix of the
-- non-guarded arms — i.e. some scrutinee value escapes every arm.  Guarded arms
-- are excluded (their guard may fail at runtime), matching the reference.
checkMatchExhaustive : Mono -> List Arm -> Unit
checkMatchExhaustive scrutTy arms =
  let rows = nonGuardedRows arms
  if useful matchOracle.value (matchCol0Type scrutTy) rows [PWild] then
    let _ = setRef matchWarnings (nonExhaustiveMsg scrutTy rows :: matchWarnings.value)
    setRef matchWarningLocs (currentLoc.value::matchWarningLocs.value)
  else ()

-- ── unreachable / redundant match-arm warning (W-UNREACHABLE-ARM) ──────────
-- An arm is unreachable when its pattern is already fully covered by an EARLIER
-- *unguarded* arm: those arms fire unconditionally, so no value ever reaches
-- this one.  We fold arms left-to-right, accumulating a matrix of the preceding
-- UNGUARDED arm patterns, and flag any arm whose pattern is not `useful` against
-- it (`patUnreachable`).  Guarded predecessors are excluded (their guard may fail
-- at runtime, leaving a later same-shape arm reachable), which keeps the check
-- conservative — we flag only arms provably dead via unconditional predecessors.
-- The warning carries the ARM's own loc (its body span) so the caret points at
-- the dead arm, not the match head.
unreachableArmWarning : String
unreachableArmWarning = "Warning: unreachable match arm. This pattern is already covered by an earlier arm"

checkMatchRedundant : Mono -> List Arm -> Unit
checkMatchRedundant scrutTy arms =
  checkRedundantGo (matchCol0Type scrutTy) [] omEmpty False arms

-- PERF: `prec` accumulates the preceding arms' patterns and is PREPENDED to, so
-- it holds them in REVERSE arm order.  It used to be built with `prec ++ [[dp]]`,
-- which copies the whole accumulator once per arm — O(N²) allocation on a match
-- with many arms, and the dominant allocation source in the redundancy check.
--
-- Reversing the rows is sound because `useful` is a PREDICATE OVER THE SET of
-- matrix rows ("does some value match the query and no row?"): `specializeCon` /
-- `specializeLit` / `defaultMatrix` are row-wise filters, and the only decision
-- that reads rows in order is `inferCol0Type`, which just needs SOME column-0
-- constructor to name the column's type — and it is consulted only when `col0`
-- is `None`, i.e. when the scrutinee type has no concrete head, in which case no
-- arm carries a constructor pattern and there is no head constructor to find in
-- either order.
checkRedundantGo : Option String -> List (List Pat) -> OrdMap Unit -> Bool -> List Arm -> Unit
checkRedundantGo _ _ _ _ [] = ()
checkRedundantGo col0 prec heads offCol ((Arm pat gs body)::rest) =
  let dp = desugarPat matchOracle.value pat
  let _ = if armUnreachable col0 prec heads offCol dp then
    let _ = setRef matchWarnings (unreachableArmWarning :: matchWarnings.value)
    setRef matchWarningLocs (orElseLoc (exprLoc body) currentLoc.value :: matchWarningLocs.value)
  else ()
  -- Only UNGUARDED, range-free arms contribute to the covered set: a guarded arm
  -- may not fire, and a range collapses to a wildcard that over-states coverage
  -- (patHasRange) — either would produce a false "redundant" on a later arm.
  let contributes = armIsUnguarded gs && not (patHasRange pat)
  let prec2 = if contributes then [dp] :: prec else prec
  let heads2 = if contributes then noteHeadCtor dp heads else heads
  let offCol2 = offCol || (contributes && not (isConPat dp))
  checkRedundantGo col0 prec2 heads2 offCol2 rest

-- PERF fast path.  `patUnreachable` runs the full Maranget `useful` recursion, which
-- re-scans EVERY preceding row — so the fold as a whole is O(arms²) on a wide match.
-- But a constructor arm `C …` can only be unreachable if some preceding row could
-- match a `C` value, and the only rows that can are (a) rows headed by `C` itself and
-- (b) rows with a WILDCARD head, which match anything.  When neither exists,
-- `specializeCon C` yields the EMPTY matrix and `useful _ _ [] _` is `True` by
-- definition — the arm is reachable — so the recursion is guaranteed to say
-- "reachable" and we can skip it outright.
--
-- `heads` is the set of head constructors of the preceding contributing rows and
-- `offCol` records whether any of them has a NON-constructor head.  `offCol` is a
-- deliberate over-approximation of "has a wildcard head" (it also trips on a literal
-- head, which `specializeCon` would in fact discard): erring towards `True` only ever
-- costs us the fast path and falls back to the exact check, never an answer.
armUnreachable : Option String -> List (List Pat) -> OrdMap Unit -> Bool -> Pat -> Bool
armUnreachable col0 prec heads offCol (PCon c args)
  | not offCol && not (omHasKey c heads) = False
  | otherwise = patUnreachable matchOracle.value col0 prec (PCon c args)
armUnreachable col0 prec _ _ dp = patUnreachable matchOracle.value col0 prec dp

noteHeadCtor : Pat -> OrdMap Unit -> OrdMap Unit
noteHeadCtor (PCon c _) heads = omInsert c () heads
noteHeadCtor _ heads = heads

isConPat : Pat -> Bool
isConPat (PCon _ _) = True
isConPat _ = False

-- Only UNGUARDED arms contribute to the covered set (a guarded arm may not fire).
armIsUnguarded : List Guard -> Bool
armIsUnguarded [] = True
armIsUnguarded (_::_) = False

-- Build the specialised warning: recover a concrete uncovered pattern (the
-- Maranget witness) and name it, plus the scrutinee type when it has a real
-- head name.  Falls back to the generic wording if no witness is recoverable
-- (keeps the warning firing whenever `useful` should, never naming a wrong
-- constructor).  Actionable-fix hint (F dimension): when a witness IS
-- recoverable, append a concrete-edit suggestion using the witness string
-- VERBATIM (so `Triangle _` reads as `Triangle _`, not `Triangle`) and record
-- it in `matchWarningHelp` (keyed by the full message, mirroring
-- `typeErrorHelpFix`) so `--json`'s `help` field carries it too.
nonExhaustiveMsg : Mono -> List (List Pat) -> String
nonExhaustiveMsg scrutTy rows = match usefulWitness matchOracle.value (matchCol0Type scrutTy) rows 1
  Some (w::_) =>
    let witnessStr = renderWitness w
    let hint = "add a '\{witnessStr} => …' arm, or a '_' wildcard arm to catch the rest."
    let msg = "Warning: non-exhaustive match\{tyOfMsg (matchCol0Type scrutTy)}. Missing case: '\{witnessStr}'; \{hint}"
    let _ = setRef matchWarningHelp ((msg, hint)::matchWarningHelp.value)
    msg
  _ => nonExhaustiveMatchWarning

-- Name the scrutinee type (skip the synthetic tuple type names, which aren't
-- user-facing — the witness pattern already reads as a tuple).
tyOfMsg : Option String -> String
tyOfMsg None = ""
tyOfMsg (Some t) = if isTupleTyName t then "" else " of '\{t}'"

isTupleTyName : String -> Bool
isTupleTyName t = stringLength t >= 7 && stringSlice 0 7 t == "__tuple"

-- one single-column matrix row per non-guarded arm (its desugared pattern)
nonGuardedRows : List Arm -> List (List Pat)
nonGuardedRows [] = []
nonGuardedRows ((Arm pat [] _)::rest) =
  [desugarPat matchOracle.value pat] :: nonGuardedRows rest
nonGuardedRows ((Arm _ _ _)::rest) = nonGuardedRows rest

-- the head type name of the scrutinee, for the matrix's column-0 oracle: a
-- TCon/TApp head gives the type name; a tuple has no TCon head, so it maps to a
-- synthetic per-arity tuple type (mirrors the reference's col0_type).
matchCol0Type : Mono -> Option String
matchCol0Type ty = map (n => n) (headTyconMono ty)

inferArms : TcEnv -> Mono -> Mono -> List Arm -> Unit
inferArms _ _ _ [] = ()
inferArms env scrutTy result ((Arm pat gs body)::rest) =
  let _ = inferArm env scrutTy result pat gs body
  inferArms env scrutTy result rest

inferArm : TcEnv -> Mono -> Mono -> Pat -> List Guard -> Expr -> Unit
inferArm env scrutTy result pat gs body =
  let pr = inferPat env pat
  let _ = unify (fst pr) scrutTy
  let envBody = inferArmGuards (extendVars env (snd pr)) gs
  unify result (infer envBody body)

-- Thread an arm's guard qualifiers left-to-right (mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's
-- EMatch guard fold): a `GBool` must be `Bool` and leaves the env unchanged; a
-- `GBind p e` infers `e`, binds `p` against it, and extends the env so the bound
-- vars are in scope for LATER qualifiers and the arm body.
inferArmGuards : TcEnv -> List Guard -> TcEnv
inferArmGuards env [] = env
inferArmGuards env ((GBool g)::rest) =
  let _ = unify (infer env g) (TCon "Bool")
  inferArmGuards env rest
inferArmGuards env ((GBind p e)::rest) =
  let te = infer env e
  let pr = inferPat env p
  let _ = unify (fst pr) te
  inferArmGuards (extendVars env (snd pr)) rest

inferLetGroup : TcEnv -> List LetBind -> Expr -> Mono
inferLetGroup env binds body =
  let env2 = processLetGroup env binds
  infer env2 body

-- ── data declarations → constructor schemes ───────────────────────────────
registerData : TcEnv -> Decl -> TcEnv
registerData env (DData _ name params variants _) =
  registerVariants env name params variants
registerData env (DTypeAlias _ name params rhs) =
  let _ = setRef aliasTableRef ((name, (params, rhs))::aliasTableRef.value)
  env
registerData env (DNewtype _ name params con fty _) =
  registerVariants env name params [Variant con (ConPos [fty])]
registerData env _ = env

-- ── record metadata registration & instantiation ──────────────────────────
-- registerRecordInfoKeyed uses a distinct registry KEY and result TYPE name:
-- for a record they coincide, but a named-field data variant is keyed by its
-- constructor (DInterface) while its values have the data type (Decl).
registerRecordInfoKeyed : String -> String -> List String -> List Field -> Unit
registerRecordInfoKeyed key typeName params fields =
  let paramVars = freshVars (listLen params)
  let tvs = zipL params paramVars
  let result = applyParams (TCon typeName) paramVars
  let fieldMonos = map (recFieldMono tvs) fields
  let ri = RecordInfo (map monoTyvarId paramVars) result fieldMonos
  let _ = setRef recordsRef ((key, ri)::recordsRef.value)
  let _ = setRef recordByNameRef (omInsert key ri recordByNameRef.value)
  setRef
    fieldOwnersRef
    (addFieldOwners key (map fst fieldMonos) fieldOwnersRef.value)

-- Add `key` as an owner of each of its field names in the field→owners multimap.
-- Order/dedup is not maintained here — fieldOwnerNames `sortUniqS`es the value on
-- read — so this only needs to preserve the SET of owner keys per field.
addFieldOwners : String -> List String -> OrdMap (List String) -> OrdMap (List String)
addFieldOwners _ [] m = m
addFieldOwners key (fn::rest) m =
  addFieldOwners key rest (omInsert fn (key :: fromOption [] (omLookup fn m)) m)

recFieldMono : List (String, Mono) -> Field -> (String, Mono)
recFieldMono tvs (Field fn ty) = (fn, fromAstType tvs ty)

lookupRecordByName : String -> Option RecordInfo
lookupRecordByName name = omLookup name recordByNameRef.value

-- instantiate a record's quantified params with fresh vars
instantiateRecord : RecordInfo -> (Mono, List (String, Mono))
instantiateRecord (RecordInfo paramIds result fields) =
  let subst = freshSubst paramIds
  (substMono subst [] result, map (substField subst) fields)

substField : List (Int, Mono) -> (String, Mono) -> (String, Mono)
substField subst (fn, fm) = (fn, substMono subst [] fm)

registerVariants : TcEnv -> String -> List String -> List Variant -> TcEnv
registerVariants env name params variants =
  let kinds = inferParamKinds params variants
  let _ = setRef dataParamKindsRef ((name, kinds)::dataParamKindsRef.value)
  let paramReprs = mintParamReprs kinds
  let tvs = ktypeTvs params kinds paramReprs
  let etbl = krowEtbl params kinds paramReprs
  let paramTyIds = collectTyIds paramReprs
  let rowEffvarIds = collectRowEffvarIds paramReprs
  let result = applyParams (TCon name) paramReprs
  let _ = registerNamedFieldVariants name params variants
  addVariants env tvs etbl paramTyIds rowEffvarIds result variants

-- A data param is KRow iff its name appears in a field's effect-tail position
-- (`<e>`); else KType (the common case).  Inferred from usage — no syntax.
inferParamKinds : List String -> List Variant -> List Kind
inferParamKinds params variants =
  let tailNames = flatMap variantFieldTailNames variants
  map (p => if contains p tailNames then KRow else KType) params

variantFieldTailNames : Variant -> List String
variantFieldTailNames (Variant _ payload) =
  flatMap effTailNames (payloadAstTypes payload)

payloadAstTypes : ConPayload -> List Ty
payloadAstTypes (ConPos tys) = tys
payloadAstTypes (ConNamed fields _) = map fieldTyOf fields

fieldTyOf : Field -> Ty
fieldTyOf (Field _ ty) = ty

-- one representation per param shared across all ctors: KType → fresh TVar,
-- KRow → a TEff wrapping a fresh effvar (the row the param stands for).
mintParamReprs : List Kind -> List Mono
mintParamReprs [] = []
mintParamReprs (KType::ks) = freshVar () :: mintParamReprs ks
mintParamReprs (KRow::ks) =
  TEff (EffRow [] (Some (freshEffvar ()))) :: mintParamReprs ks

-- KType param names → their TVar (the tvs map fromAstTypeE consults for tyvars)
ktypeTvs : List String -> List Kind -> List Mono -> List (String, Mono)
ktypeTvs (p::ps) (KType::ks) (r::rs) = (p, r) :: ktypeTvs ps ks rs
ktypeTvs (p::ps) (_::ks) (_::rs) = ktypeTvs ps ks rs
ktypeTvs _ _ _ = []

-- KRow param names → the effvar cell inside their TEff repr, so a field's `<e>`
-- and a nested `Async e a` row-arg resolve to the SAME cell the result carries.
krowEtbl : List String -> List Kind -> List Mono -> List (String, Ref Effvar)
krowEtbl (p::ps) (KRow::ks) ((TEff (EffRow _ (Some cell)))::rs) =
  (p, cell) :: krowEtbl ps ks rs
krowEtbl (p::ps) (_::ks) (_::rs) = krowEtbl ps ks rs
krowEtbl _ _ _ = []

collectTyIds : List Mono -> List Int
collectTyIds ((TVar cell)::rs) = tyvarId cell :: collectTyIds rs
collectTyIds (_::rs) = collectTyIds rs
collectTyIds [] = []

collectRowEffvarIds : List Mono -> List Int
collectRowEffvarIds ((TEff (EffRow _ (Some cell)))::rs) =
  effvarId cell :: collectRowEffvarIds rs
collectRowEffvarIds (_::rs) = collectRowEffvarIds rs
collectRowEffvarIds [] = []

-- a named-field data variant (`C { f : T, … }`, e.g. ast's DInterface) carries a
-- record-shaped payload: register its field layout keyed by the CONSTRUCTOR name
-- but with the DATA TYPE as result type, so `C { f, … }` patterns / `C { … }`
-- literals resolve (the positional ctor scheme is still added by addVariants).
registerNamedFieldVariants : String -> List String -> List Variant -> Unit
registerNamedFieldVariants _ _ [] = ()
registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params ((Variant cname (ConNamed fields _))::rest) =
  let _ = registerRecordInfoKeyed cname tyName params fields
  registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params rest
registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params (_::rest) =
  registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params rest

freshVars : Int -> List Mono
freshVars n
  | n <= 0 = []
  | otherwise = freshVar () :: freshVars (n - 1)

monoTyvarId : Mono -> Int
monoTyvarId (TVar cell) = tyvarId cell
monoTyvarId _ = 0 - 1

applyParams : Mono -> List Mono -> Mono
applyParams head [] = head
applyParams head (p::ps) = applyParams (TApp head p) ps

addVariants : TcEnv -> List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Ref Effvar) -> List Int -> List Int -> Mono -> List Variant -> TcEnv
addVariants env _ _ _ _ _ [] = env
addVariants env tvs etbl paramTyIds rowEffvarIds result ((Variant cname payload)::rest) =
  let ctorType = foldrFun (payloadTypesE etbl tvs payload) result
  let env2 = addCtor env cname (Forall paramTyIds rowEffvarIds ctorType)
  addVariants env2 tvs etbl paramTyIds rowEffvarIds result rest

payloadTypesE : List (String, Ref Effvar) -> List (String, Mono) -> ConPayload -> List Mono
payloadTypesE etbl tvs (ConPos tys) = map (fromAstTypeE etbl tvs) tys
payloadTypesE etbl tvs (ConNamed fields _) = map (fieldTypeE etbl tvs) fields

fieldTypeE : List (String, Ref Effvar) -> List (String, Mono) -> Field -> Mono
fieldTypeE etbl tvs (Field _ ty) = fromAstTypeE etbl tvs ty

-- ── top-level driver ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
funClausePair : FunClause -> (List Pat, Expr)
funClausePair (FunClause pats body) = (pats, body)

funDefs : List Decl -> List (String, (List Pat, Expr))
funDefs [] = []
funDefs ((DFunDef _ n pats body)::rest) = (n, (pats, body)) :: funDefs rest
-- Top-level `let rec … with …` (DLetGroup): flatten each binding's clauses to
-- (name, (pats, body)) entries, exactly like a multi-clause DFunDef, so the SCC
-- builder groups the mutually-recursive members and assigns them schemes.  Mirrors
-- lib/typecheck.ml, which feeds DLetGroup bindings into the same letrec-group path.
funDefs ((DLetGroup _ binds)::rest) = letGroupDefs binds ++ funDefs rest
funDefs ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = funDefs (d::rest)
funDefs (_::rest) = funDefs rest

letGroupDefs : List LetBind -> List (String, (List Pat, Expr))
letGroupDefs [] = []
letGroupDefs ((LetBind n clauses)::rest) = map (tcClauseDef n) clauses
  ++ letGroupDefs rest

tcClauseDef : String -> FunClause -> (String, (List Pat, Expr))
tcClauseDef n c = (n, funClausePair c)

-- D2 fix: mirrors oracle is_syntactic_lambda (lib/typecheck.ml:2518-2521).
-- True iff the expression is an explicit lambda at the top level.
isSyntacticLambda : Expr -> Bool
isSyntacticLambda (ELam _ _) = True
isSyntacticLambda _ = False

-- D2 fix: check all top-level DLetGroup declarations for non-function recursive
-- bindings, mirroring the oracle's LetRecNonFunction guard
-- (lib/typecheck.ml:2596-2602, process_letrec_group).  A binding is illegal
-- when ALL its clauses have zero params AND none is a syntactic lambda.
-- Pushes the error into typeErrors for each violating member.
checkLetRecDecls : List Decl -> Unit
checkLetRecDecls [] = ()
checkLetRecDecls ((DLetGroup _ binds)::rest) =
  let _ = checkLetBinds binds
  checkLetRecDecls rest
checkLetRecDecls (_::rest) = checkLetRecDecls rest

checkLetBinds : List LetBind -> Unit
checkLetBinds [] = ()
checkLetBinds ((LetBind name clauses)::rest) =
  let _ = checkLetBind name clauses
  checkLetBinds rest

checkLetBind : String -> List FunClause -> Unit
checkLetBind name clauses
  | allList clauseIsNonFunction clauses =
    pushTypeError "T-NONREC-VALUE-LET" (letRecNonFunctionMsg name)
  | otherwise = ()

clauseIsNonFunction : FunClause -> Bool
clauseIsNonFunction (FunClause [] rhs) = not (isSyntacticLambda rhs)
clauseIsNonFunction (FunClause _ _) = False

letRecNonFunctionMsg : String -> String
letRecNonFunctionMsg name = "'"
  ++ name
  ++ "' is bound by 'let rec' but its right-hand side is not a function"

groupNames : List (String, (List Pat, Expr)) -> OrdMap Unit -> List String
groupNames [] _ = []
groupNames ((n, _)::rest) seen = match omLookup n seen
  Some _ => groupNames rest seen
  None => n :: groupNames rest (omInsert n () seen)

-- process a let-group (where clauses): bind names, infer, generalize
processLetGroup : TcEnv -> List LetBind -> TcEnv
processLetGroup env binds =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let placeholders = map letBindPlaceholder binds
  let env2 = extendVars env placeholders
  let _ = inferLetBinds env2 (zipL binds placeholders)
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  -- PLAN.md #11: ground ambiguous Num-only vars (not arg-reachable from any
  -- where-binding's type) before this group generalizes.  Scoped to this group's
  -- newly added obligations.
  let addedObls = takeFirst (pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultGroupNum addedObls (map (p => schemeMono (snd p)) placeholders)
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls (map (p => schemeMono (snd p)) placeholders)   -- RETPOS soundness
  generalizeGroup env (zipL binds placeholders)

letBindPlaceholder : LetBind -> (String, Scheme)
letBindPlaceholder (LetBind name _) = (name, monoScheme (freshVar ()))

inferLetBinds : TcEnv -> List (LetBind, (String, Scheme)) -> Unit
inferLetBinds _ [] = ()
inferLetBinds env ((LetBind _ clauses, (_, sch))::rest) =
  let _ = unify (schemeMono sch) (inferClauses env (map funClausePair clauses))
  inferLetBinds env rest

schemeMono : Scheme -> Mono
schemeMono (Forall _ _ t) = t

-- Phase / GAP-2 phase-a: the tyvar ids that some ARG-position method occurrence
-- discriminates on (the receiver monos queued in pendingArgStamps).  A where-helper
-- whose own type shares one of these is *method-constrained by the enclosing fn's
-- dispatch* — its receiver var unifies (at the helper's call site) with the
-- enclosing fn's constraint var that activeDictVars maps to a dict param.  We must
-- NOT generalize over such a var, or the helper instantiates a fresh (id != the
-- enclosing constraint var) so resolveArgStamp can't see the in-scope dict → the
-- in-body method route degrades to RNone → arg-tag → native panic on a primitive
-- receiver.  Keeping it monomorphic links the ids (normalize collapses them) so the
-- existing resolveArgStamp routes RDict and dict_pass threads the enclosing dict in.
-- Sound: an UNconstrained helper var (e.g. `pair x = (x,x)`) never appears in a
-- method-usage mono, so it still generalizes; only dispatch-constrained vars (which
-- compiler cannot dict-pass per-where-helper anyway) are pinned, matching their
-- single enclosing-typed use.
methodConstrainedIds : Unit -> List Int
methodConstrainedIds _ =
  flatMap (s => monoUnboundIds (argStampMono s)) pendingArgStamps.value

argStampMono : (String, Ref Route, Ref (List Route), Mono, String) -> Mono
argStampMono (_, _, _, am, _) = am

monoUnboundIds : Mono -> List Int
monoUnboundIds t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => [tyvarId cell]
  TCon _ => []
  TApp a b => monoUnboundIds a ++ monoUnboundIds b
  TFun a _ b => monoUnboundIds a ++ monoUnboundIds b
  TEff _ => []

anyIn : List Int -> List Int -> Bool
anyIn xs ys = anyList (x => containsI x ys) xs

-- PLAN.md #11 (locked §0.2): monomorphism-for-Num defaulting.  Before a type [t]
-- is generalized at a let-binding boundary, ground every *ambiguous* tyvar carrying
-- a `Num` constraint to `Int`.  "Ambiguous" = the var is NOT reachable from an
-- ARGUMENT position of [t] (a caller can't fix it).  This stops a bare `n = 1` (or
-- an inner `let z = 2`) from generalizing to `Num a => a` / leaking an unbound
-- `Num a` to eval, while leaving a genuinely polymorphic var live: `sum`'s element
-- var in `t a -> a` is reachable from the argument `t a`, so it stays polymorphic
-- (`sum [1.0,…]` still works); `x : Float; x = 0`'s var is already concrete Float
-- (not a generalizable var), so it is untouched.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
-- default_ambiguous_num: a Num-constrained var (alone OR with another class) that is
-- ambiguous is grounded to Int — the literal's `Num`/`fromInt` representation IS
-- Int, so grounding keeps impl selection deterministic.
monoArgUnboundIds : Mono -> List Int
monoArgUnboundIds t = match normalize t
  TFun a _ b => monoUnboundIds a ++ monoArgUnboundIds b
  _ => []

-- the unbound var ids that any pending `Num` obligation in [obls] constrains (the
-- operand monos recorded by recordIfaceObligation / the literal infer arm)
numConstrainedIds : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Int
numConstrainedIds obls = flatMap numObligIds obls

numObligIds : (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Int
numObligIds (iface, _, _, occ, _)
  | iface == "Num" = monoUnboundIds occ
  | otherwise = []

defaultAmbiguousNum : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Mono -> Unit
defaultAmbiguousNum obls t =
  let argIds = monoArgUnboundIds t
  let inT = monoUnboundIds t
  let candidates = dedupI (numConstrainedIds obls)
  groundNumVars
    obls
    (filterList
      (id => containsI id inT && not (containsI id argIds))
      candidates)

-- group-level defaulting: ground every Num-constrained var NOT reachable from an
-- ARGUMENT position of ANY member's type.  [monos] = the group's member placeholder
-- monos; [obls] = ONLY this group's newly-added obligations (an earlier group's
-- already-generalized var must not be touched).  Unlike defaultAmbiguousNum this
-- does NOT require the var to appear in a member's type (a var consumed inside a
-- body surfaces in no type but must still ground — `let n = tag [1,2,3]`).  Mirrors
-- process_letrec_group's group_arg_ids over added_obligs/added_methods.
defaultGroupNum : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Mono -> Unit
defaultGroupNum obls monos =
  let argIds = flatMap monoArgUnboundIds monos
  let candidates = dedupI (numConstrainedIds obls)
  groundNumVars obls (filterList (id => not (containsI id argIds)) candidates)

-- per-binding defaulting: run defaultAmbiguousNum on each member's placeholder mono
defaultEachMember : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
defaultEachMember _ [] = ()
defaultEachMember obls ((_, m)::rest) =
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum obls m
  defaultEachMember obls rest

-- RETPOS soundness — ambiguous interface constraint (run≠build hole).
-- A method obligation recorded inside a binding whose discriminating tyvar is
-- UNBOUND, UNANCHORED, and appears NOWHERE in the binding's generalized type is
-- genuinely ambiguous (`make`'s result var in `f x = reveal (make (reveal x))`,
-- `f : Thing a => a -> Int`: `make`'s var is tied to neither `a` nor the result).
-- No annotation determines the instance and, with ≥2 impls, eval vs emit default
-- to DIFFERENT impls — a silent run≠build miscompile (RETPOS-DISPATCH-DESIGN.md).
-- We REJECT it by handing the genuine ambiguous DISPATCH mono to the existing
-- undetermined-obligation path (registering it as a call obligation), which
-- rejects (ambiguousImplMsg) under the SAME `Num` / active-dict / impl-count≥2
-- guards an undetermined call site already uses (so a sole-impl interface is never
-- over-rejected and `Num` stays on its literal-defaulting path).  Three filters
-- keep this from firing on legitimately-deferred constraints:
--   • member-type membership (defaultGroupNum's `monoUnboundIds`) — a var IN the
--     binding's type is determined by a caller/annotation;
--   • group LEVEL — a DEEPER var belongs to an inner let/where binding that already
--     generalized + checked it (its obligation merely leaks into our addedObls
--     window, e.g. `maximum`'s `where step` comparing at step's own element var);
--   • ANCHORED vars — a var that also appears inside a CONCRETE-HEADED dispatch mono
--     of this binding (`Set _a` from a `Set {…}` literal's FromEntries/Display) is
--     ground at emit by head-pinning, so it is sound-but-deferred, NOT ambiguous.
-- The obligation's record-site loc rides along (currentLoc is stale post-HM).
registerAmbiguousConstraints : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Mono -> Unit
registerAmbiguousConstraints obls monos =
  -- groupLevel = the level THIS group generalizes at (we run post-exitLevel, so the
  -- outer currentLevel + 1).  anchoredIds = every unbound var that appears inside a
  -- concrete-headed dispatch mono across this group's obligations (head-pinnable).
  let anchoredIds = flatMap concreteHeadVarIds (flatMap oblDispatchMonos obls)
  registerAmbiguousGo obls (currentLevel.value + 1) (flatMap monoUnboundIds monos) anchoredIds

-- the dispatch monos this obligation discriminates on (empty for Num, whose
-- ambiguity is handled by literal defaulting).
oblDispatchMonos : (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Mono
oblDispatchMonos (iface, typarams, mty, occ, _)
  | iface == "Num" = []
  -- F0: dispatch/ambiguity keys off the RECEIVER (dispatch) typaram only, NOT every
  -- interface typaram.  A return-only typaram (`v` in `Get c v where get1 : c -> v`)
  -- is determined by the impl AFTER the receiver picks it, so it must never be
  -- registered as an independent impl-selection obligation — else the still-unbound
  -- `v`, grounded later by groundMultiParamObligations, is re-checked as a single-mono
  -- obligation with no matching impl (`No impl of Get for Int`).  dispatchTyparams
  -- = [first typaram], the same receiver classification argDispatchOfMethod uses.
  | otherwise = dispatchMonosOf (dispatchTyparams typarams) mty occ

-- the unbound var ids carried by a dispatch mono WITH a concrete head (`Set _a` →
-- [_a]); a bare-var dispatch (`t0`) anchors nothing.
concreteHeadVarIds : Mono -> List Int
concreteHeadVarIds dm = match headTyconMono dm
  Some _ => monoUnboundIds dm
  None => []

registerAmbiguousGo : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Int -> List Int -> List Int -> Unit
registerAmbiguousGo [] _ _ _ = ()
registerAmbiguousGo ((iface, typarams, mty, occ, loc)::rest) groupLevel memberIds anchoredIds =
  let _ = registerOneAmbiguous iface typarams mty occ loc groupLevel memberIds anchoredIds
  registerAmbiguousGo rest groupLevel memberIds anchoredIds

-- For each of this obligation's DISPATCH monos (the interface-param positions, via
-- dispatchMonosOf — NOT the whole method type), register it as a call obligation
-- when it carries an escaped, unanchored this-group var.  Pushing the dispatch mono
-- (not the method type) is essential: checkUndeterminedObligation's guards
-- (`headTyconMono`, `activeDictVarOf`, `allConcreteHeads`) inspect a bare dispatch
-- mono and do NOT traverse `TFun`, so a concrete-headed dispatch and a forwarded-
-- dict element var are correctly NOT flagged, while a bare-var dispatch with ≥2
-- impls and no anchor (`Thing t0` from `make`) IS rejected.
registerOneAmbiguous : String -> List String -> Ty -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Int -> List Int -> List Int -> Unit
registerOneAmbiguous iface typarams mty occ loc groupLevel memberIds anchoredIds
  | iface == "Num" = ()
  -- F0: register call obligations for the RECEIVER (dispatch) typaram only — a
  -- return-only typaram is impl-determined, not an impl-selection key (see
  -- oblDispatchMonos).
  | otherwise = registerDispatchMonos iface (dispatchMonosOf (dispatchTyparams typarams) mty occ) loc groupLevel memberIds anchoredIds

registerDispatchMonos : String -> List Mono -> Option Loc -> Int -> List Int -> List Int -> Unit
registerDispatchMonos _ [] _ _ _ _ = ()
registerDispatchMonos iface (dm::rest) loc groupLevel memberIds anchoredIds =
  let ambig = filterList (p => snd p <= groupLevel && not (containsI (fst p) memberIds) && not (containsI (fst p) anchoredIds)) (monoUnboundVarLevels dm)
  let _ = if isEmptyL ambig then () else pushCallObl (iface, dm, loc)
  registerDispatchMonos iface rest loc groupLevel memberIds anchoredIds

-- the (id, level) of every UNBOUND tyvar in [t] (sibling of monoUnboundIds, but
-- carrying the level so registerOneAmbiguous can tell a this-group constraint var
-- from a deeper inner-generalized one that merely leaked into the obligation list).
monoUnboundVarLevels : Mono -> List (Int, Int)
monoUnboundVarLevels t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => [(tyvarId cell, tyvarLevel cell)]
  TCon _ => []
  TApp a b => monoUnboundVarLevels a ++ monoUnboundVarLevels b
  TFun a _ b => monoUnboundVarLevels a ++ monoUnboundVarLevels b
  TEff _ => []

-- ground each id to Int by unifying the live var carrying it (found among [obls]'
-- Num operand monos — refs alias post-unify, grounding any grounds all)
groundNumVars : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Int -> Unit
groundNumVars _ [] = ()
groundNumVars obls (id::rest) =
  let _ = match liveNumVarGo id obls
    Some m => unify m (TCon "Int")
    None => ()
  groundNumVars obls rest

liveNumVarGo : Int -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Option Mono
liveNumVarGo _ [] = None
liveNumVarGo id ((iface, _, _, occ, _)::rest)
  | iface == "Num" = match findTvarInMono occ id
    Some m => Some m
    None => liveNumVarGo id rest
  | otherwise = liveNumVarGo id rest

-- the first [n] entries of a list (the newly-prepended obligations of a group)
takeFirst : Int -> List a -> List a
takeFirst n _
  | n <= 0 = []
takeFirst _ [] = []
takeFirst n (x::xs) = x :: takeFirst (n - 1) xs

generalizeGroup : TcEnv -> List (LetBind, (String, Scheme)) -> TcEnv
generalizeGroup env [] = env
generalizeGroup env (((LetBind name clauses), (_, sch))::rest) =
  let constrained = methodConstrainedIds ()
  let mono = schemeMono sch
  let isVal = clausesAreValue clauses && not (anyIn (freeGenVars currentLevel.value mono) constrained)
  -- WS-2: α scope-seed — single-clause zero-param bindings only (mirror α's
  -- alphaCollectBinds rule); a multi-clause / parameterized binding is not a
  -- known prefix.
  let env1 = extendVar env name (genRestricted isVal mono)
  let env2 = match clauses
    [FunClause [] rhs] => seedAlphaLets env1 name rhs
    _ => env1
  generalizeGroup env2 rest

-- A where-clause binding is a value (generalizable) iff every clause has params
-- (it's a function) or its zero-arg RHS is non-expansive.  Mirrors the oracle
-- `ELetGroup` value test (lib/typecheck.ml:1715-1716).
clausesAreValue : List FunClause -> Bool
clausesAreValue clauses = allList clauseIsValue clauses

clauseIsValue : FunClause -> Bool
clauseIsValue (FunClause [] rhs) = isNonexpansive rhs
clauseIsValue (FunClause _ _) = True

-- infer a clause group (multi-clause function), unifying all clauses to one type
inferClauses : TcEnv -> List (List Pat, Expr) -> Mono
inferClauses env clauses =
  let v = freshVar ()
  let _ = unifyClauses env v clauses
  v

unifyClauses : TcEnv -> Mono -> List (List Pat, Expr) -> Unit
unifyClauses _ _ [] = ()
unifyClauses env v ((pats, body)::rest) =
  let _ = unify v (inferLam env pats body)
  unifyClauses env v rest

-- ── elaboration: resolve return-position method dispatch (RKey) ────────────
-- Rewrite each return-position method occurrence to EMethodAt (with a fresh route
-- ref), type-check (recording the sites), then stamp each site's route from its
-- now-resolved result type.  Returns the SAME tree (refs filled) for eval.
-- DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 5: the flat `elaborate` wrapper is DELETED; callers use
-- the 1-module `elaborateOne` (the typed/dict tree of the degenerate 1-module
-- multi path).  `elaborateDict` itself is RETAINED — the EMIT path
-- (core_ir_dict_pp_main / llvm_emit_*_main, argStamp mode) still uses it.

-- ── Phase 0 (DRIVER-COLLAPSE): 1-module wrapper over the multi-module path ──
-- elaborateOne runs the SINGLE flat program [prog] (the user's decls, the live
-- prelude ALREADY shadow-dropped by the caller — same `combined` the flat
-- elaborateDict callers build, minus the joint core) through elaborateModules as
-- the degenerate 1-module list [(rootId, prog)], returning the FLAT shape the flat
-- callers consume: core2 (dict-passed core) ++ the dict-passed module's decls.
--
-- The 1-module case automatically satisfies the flat path's invariants — one
-- resetState (one module ⇒ one checkModuleFullImpl), zero imports ⇒ the full
-- constraint set, and (for the EVAL path, emitArgStampPasses OFF) elaborateModules
-- runs the eval-dict layer unconditionally (ARGSTAMP-UNIFY Phase 4), so the
-- return-position constrained-fn dict layer matches the flat elaborateDict.
--
-- NOTE (Phase 0 is DISCOVERY): the dict-name set differs by construction —
-- elaborateModules computes its own via `moduleDictNames` (return-position subset:
-- `preludeReturnPosDictNames coreDecls allDecls ++ returnPosConstrainedSigNames …`)
-- whereas the flat callers pass an externally-built `dictNames`
-- (`preludeReturnPosDictNames livePrelude combined ++ constrainedSigNames userDecls`,
-- the FULL constrained-sig set).  This wrapper takes `coreDecls` SEPARATE (not
-- pre-flattened into prog), exactly as elaborateModules expects, and lets the
-- module path own the dict-set.  diff_flat_vs_onemodule.sh exists to CHARACTERIZE
-- where that difference (and the eval install order) actually changes OUTPUT.
export elaborateOne : List Decl -> List Decl -> (String, List Decl) -> List Decl
elaborateOne runtimeDecls coreDecls (rootId, prog) =
  let (coreD, modules) = elaborateModules runtimeDecls coreDecls [(rootId, prog)]
  coreD ++ flatMap snd modules

-- Dict-passing elaboration (the `=>`-constraint layer).  [dictNames] are the
-- *signatured* constrained functions to dict-pass (the user program's — the
-- prelude keeps arg-tag / RKey dispatch, which already type-checks the goldens).
-- [eligibleNames] are the user fn names that may additionally be PROMOTED when an
-- INFERRED (unsignatured) constraint is discovered (see registerInferredConstraints).
--
-- Two-pass / fixpoint orchestration (mirrors the reference's mark → typecheck →
-- re-mark → typecheck → dict_pass): a mark+typecheck pass discovers each eligible
-- unsignatured fn's inferred constraints (promotedRef); we grow the dict-passed
-- set and re-run until it stabilizes (one layer of a call chain promotes per
-- pass), then mark + typecheck + route + dict_pass the final set.  In the RKey
-- rewrite, each occurrence of a name in the final set becomes an EDictAt (routes
-- filled after inference) and its def gets matching leading dict params.
export elaborateDict : List Decl -> List String -> List String -> List Decl -> List Decl
elaborateDict runtimeDecls dictNames eligibleNames prog =
  let _ = setRef superDeclsRef prog
  let _ = setRef dictEligibleRef eligibleNames
  let _ = setRef dictEligibleSetRef (namesToSet eligibleNames omEmpty)
  let _ = setRef implInferEnabled True
  let allNames = discoverAll runtimeDecls prog dictNames eligibleNames
  let _ = setRef argDispatchIdxRef (argDispatchIndices prog)
  let argNames = map fst argDispatchIdxRef.value
  -- P0-18: [] — the confirmed definer-shadow-emit path is elaborateModules; this
  -- single-file scratch elaborate stays shadow-map-free (byte-identical discovery).
  let prog2 = prePassDictArg (returnPosMethodNames prog ++ methodConstraintNames prog) allNames argNames [] prog
  let _ = checkProgramSeeded (externSchemes runtimeDecls) prog2
  let _ = expandSupersTable prog2
  let implTable = buildImplTable prog2
  let keyTable = buildKeyTable prog2
  let _ = resolveSites implTable keyTable (returnPosMethodNames prog2) pendingSites.value
  let _ = resolveOpSites True implTable keyTable pendingBinopSites.value
  let _ = resolveOpSites False implTable keyTable pendingUnopSites.value
  let _ = resolveArithSites pendingArithSites.value
  let _ = realizeRecDictApps pendingRecDictApps.value
  let _ = resolveDictApps prog2 implTable pendingDictApps.value
  let _ = resolveMethodDicts implTable pendingMethodDicts.value
  let _ = resolveArgStamps implTable keyTable pendingArgStamps.value
  dictPass allNames prog2

-- With no eligible names (the RKey-only / typed path), there is nothing to
-- promote — skip discovery entirely so that path stays a single typecheck pass.
discoverAll : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String -> List String -> List String
discoverAll _ _ dictNames [] = dictNames
discoverAll runtimeDecls prog dictNames _ =
  discoverPromoted runtimeDecls prog dictNames

-- Grow the dict-passed set to a fixpoint.  Each pass marks occurrences of the
-- current set as EDictAt and typechecks; registerInferredConstraints populates
-- promotedRef (reset each pass by checkProgramSeeded) with eligible unsignatured
-- fns that picked up a constraint — including a CALLER that inherited one through
-- a now-marked constrained call.  Stop when no new name appears.
discoverPromoted : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String -> List String
discoverPromoted runtimeDecls prog current =
  let prog1 = prePassDict (returnPosMethodNames prog ++ methodConstraintNames prog) current prog
  let _ = checkProgramSeeded (externSchemes runtimeDecls) prog1
  discoverNext runtimeDecls prog current (dedup (current ++ promotedRef.value))

discoverNext : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String -> List String -> List String
discoverNext runtimeDecls prog current next
  | listLen next == listLen current = current
  | otherwise = discoverPromoted runtimeDecls prog next

-- ── Cause A: module-path promotion fixpoint ───────────────────────────────
-- The multi-module analogue of discoverPromoted/discoverAll.  elaborateModules
-- typechecks each module in its own frame (checkModuleFullImpl), but PROMOTION
-- (deciding which unsignatured fns carry an inferred constraint) needs to see the
-- whole graph at once so a caller in module B that uses a constrained fn from A is
-- discovered.  Mirror the oracle's joint mark→typecheck: flatten runtime ++ core ++
-- all modules into ONE program and run discoverPromoted's fixpoint on it.  The seed
-- is dictEligibleRef (already set to the user modules' fn names).  Returns the
-- promoted names and, as a side effect, seeds crossModuleFunConstraintsRef with the
-- joint-discovered constraint arities (count per promoted fn) so they survive the
-- per-module resetState and dictArityOf can read them at the final dictPass —
-- mirroring how each defining module's own pass re-registers REAL var-ids into
-- activeDictVars (the count must agree; the body routes carry the surviving ids).
-- argNames marks ARG-position method occurrences EMethodAt so inferMethodAt's
-- recordArgSiteFn surfaces an inferred arg-position constraint to inferredConstraintIds
-- (the Cause-A `f s = display s` shape, where the dispatch is the bare method itself).
discoverPromotedModules : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> List String -> List String -> List String
discoverPromotedModules runtimeDecls coreDecls modules rpNames argNames =
  let joint = coreDecls ++ flatMap snd modules
  let dictSeed = moduleDictNames coreDecls joint modules
  let savedSticky = typeErrorsSticky.value
  let savedErrors = typeErrors.value
  -- Drop the prelude/core defs AND signatures shadowed by a same-named user-module
  -- fn before the SCRATCH joint typecheck.  The joint flatten is bare-name, so a
  -- core fn with a concrete signature (e.g. `isEven : Int -> Bool`) would otherwise
  -- PIN the user's same-named fn's inferred scheme to that concrete type — the
  -- user's genuinely-constrained `isEven x = x % 2 == 0` (inferred `Num a => …`)
  -- would NOT be discovered as promoted, so its define gets no leading dict param
  -- while its body still routes `$dict_isEven_0` (the real per-module pass, where the
  -- user fn shadows the prelude in its own scope, DOES infer the constraint) →
  -- `unbound identifier: $dict_isEven_0` on the run/loader path.  The real per-module
  -- pass already honours shadowing; mirror it here so discovery promotes the user fn.
  let discoveryJoint = dropShadowedCore (moduleUserFnNames modules) coreDecls ++ flatMap snd modules
  let grown = discoverPromotedJoint runtimeDecls discoveryJoint rpNames argNames dictSeed
  let promoted = filterList (n => not (contains n dictSeed)) grown
  let snapshot = promotedConstraints promoted funConstraintsRef.value
  let snapshotIfaces = promotedConstraintIfaces promoted funConstraintIfacesRef.value
  -- WS-1b: expand the cross-module snapshot's super slots too, so a cross-module
  -- constrained fn reached from another module under-fills exactly like clamp would.
  let expandedPair = expandSupersCross joint snapshot snapshotIfaces
  let snapshotE = fst expandedPair
  let snapshotIfacesE = snd expandedPair
  let _ = resetState ()
  let _ = setRef typeErrorsSticky savedSticky
  let _ = setRef typeErrors savedErrors
  let _ = setRef crossModuleFunConstraintsRef snapshotE
  let _ = setRef crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef snapshotIfacesE
  promoted
-- DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 2: discovery is a SCRATCH joint typecheck (see below); its
-- type-error side effects must NOT leak into the STICKY accumulator that gates the
-- build/run exit code.  The joint-flattened program can surface spurious errors
-- (cross-module names that resolve per-module but look ambiguous flattened) that
-- the REAL per-module pass (elabWorker) does not — so snapshot typeErrors +
-- typeErrorsSticky here and restore them after, leaving the gate to reflect only
-- the real per-module typecheck.  On the EMIT path discovery already ran, but
-- elaborateModules's caller (the emit driver) never reads hadTypeErrors, so this
-- was latent; the eval-path build/run gate (typecheckGate) DOES read it.

-- discoverPromotedJoint returns dictSeed GROWN by the newly-promoted user fns;
-- subtract the seed so [promoted] is ONLY the inferred-constraint user fns (the
-- prelude constrained fns in dictSeed are already handled by moduleDictNames +
-- constrainedSigArity — re-snapshotting THEIR joint-discovery arity into
-- crossModuleFunConstraintsRef would override each module's own sig-registered
-- arity and drop the prelude helpers' element-dict args → SIGSEGV).

-- Discovery is a SCRATCH typecheck purely to compute the promoted-name set; its
-- side effects on the shared inference refs (funConstraintsRef/activeDictVars/
-- pendingArgStamps/route refs left by the last checkProgramSeeded) must NOT leak
-- into the real per-module stamping that follows.  resetState wipes them; the
-- promoted arities survive in the snapshot, carried to the real pass via
-- crossModuleFunConstraintsRef.  dictEligibleRef + emitArgStampPasses are NOT touched
-- by resetState (set by the driver, constant).

-- Fixpoint body: prePass the joint program (return-position + constrained-fn names
-- → EMethodAt/EDictAt; arg-position method names → EMethodAt), typecheck it whole
-- (checkProgramSeeded resets promotedRef + funConstraintsRef each pass), grow the
-- set by the newly-promoted names, repeat until stable.  Uses prePassDictArg so
-- arg-position dispatch surfaces inferred constraints (recordArgSiteFn → methodSiteFns).
discoverPromotedJoint : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String -> List String -> List String -> List String
discoverPromotedJoint runtimeDecls joint rpNames argNames current =
  let prog1 = prePassDictArg (rpNames ++ methodConstraintNames joint) current argNames [] joint
  let _ = checkProgramSeeded (externSchemes runtimeDecls) prog1
  discoverNextJoint
    runtimeDecls
    joint
    rpNames
    argNames
    current
    (dedup (current ++ promotedRef.value))

discoverNextJoint : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String -> List String -> List String -> List String -> List String
discoverNextJoint runtimeDecls joint rpNames argNames current next
  | listLen next == listLen current = current
  | otherwise = discoverPromotedJoint runtimeDecls joint rpNames argNames next

-- the funConstraintsRef entries (name, ids) for the promoted user fns only —
-- snapshot of the joint discovery, carried across resetState by
-- crossModuleFunConstraintsRef so dictArityOf reads the right param count.
promotedConstraints : List String -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int)
promotedConstraints promoted entries =
  filterList (e => contains (fst e) promoted) entries

-- iface-list analog of promotedConstraints (parallel filter for funConstraintIfacesRef).
promotedConstraintIfaces : List String -> List (String, List String) -> List (String, List String)
promotedConstraintIfaces promoted entries =
  filterList (e => contains (fst e) promoted) entries

-- the USER modules' top-level fn names (NOT core/prelude) — the promotion-eligible
-- set on the build path.  Mirrors elaborateDict's eligibleNames = funNamesOf userDecls.
moduleUserFnNames : List (String, List Decl) -> List String
moduleUserFnNames modules =
  dedup (flatMap (p => map fst (funDefs (snd p))) modules)

-- Drop every core/prelude DEF and TYPE SIGNATURE whose name a user module
-- redefines, for the SCRATCH joint-discovery typecheck.  Unlike eval's
-- dropShadowed (DFunDef only), this ALSO drops the shadowed DTypeSig — the
-- signature is precisely what would pin the user fn's inferred scheme to the
-- prelude's concrete type and suppress its promotion.  Non-fn decls (impls,
-- interfaces, data) are kept (a user fn name can't shadow them).
dropShadowedCore : List String -> List Decl -> List Decl
dropShadowedCore _ [] = []
dropShadowedCore names ((DFunDef pub n pats body)::rest)
  | contains n names = dropShadowedCore names rest
  | otherwise = DFunDef pub n pats body :: dropShadowedCore names rest
dropShadowedCore names ((DTypeSig pub n ty)::rest)
  | contains n names = dropShadowedCore names rest
  | otherwise = DTypeSig pub n ty :: dropShadowedCore names rest
dropShadowedCore names (d::rest) = d :: dropShadowedCore names rest

-- the user program's top-level functions carrying a `=>` constraint in their
-- signature — the set elaborateDict dict-passes
export constrainedSigNames : List Decl -> List String
constrainedSigNames prog = flatMap constrainedSigName prog

constrainedSigName : Decl -> List String
constrainedSigName (DTypeSig _ n ty)
  | isConstrainedTy ty = [n]
  | otherwise = []
constrainedSigName _ = []

isConstrainedTy : Ty -> Bool
isConstrainedTy (TyConstrained _ _) = True
isConstrainedTy _ = False

-- The PRELUDE's own `=>`-constrained fns that must ALSO be dict-passed: those
-- whose body uses a return-position method (`when`/`unless` use `pure`), so
-- arg-tag dispatch can't resolve it at a constraint variable's type.  Every OTHER
-- constrained prelude fn (sum/any/elem/clamp/…) uses only arg-position methods —
-- arg-tag already resolves those, and the goldens type-check with them on RKey, so
-- they stay OUT.  [allDecls] supplies the interface set for returnPosMethodNames;
-- bodies come from [preludeDecls] (the shadow-dropped prelude).
export preludeReturnPosDictNames : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String
preludeReturnPosDictNames preludeDecls allDecls =
  let rpNames = returnPosMethodNames allDecls
  filterList
    (n => anyMember (declBodyVars n preludeDecls) rpNames)
    (constrainedSigNames preludeDecls)

-- D3b-2: the PRELUDE's `=>`-constrained fns that dispatch a method in ARGUMENT
-- position (the ~23 helpers: neq/elem/any/all/find/count/sum/product/maximum/
-- minimum/clamp/flatMap/flat/debugListItems/displayListItems/hashListItems/print/
-- println).  These stay OUT of the eval/golden dict set — arg-tag resolves them in
-- ceval (tagged values) and the goldens type-check with them on RKey — but the
-- NATIVE emit path MUST dict-pass them: arg-tag cannot dispatch on a PRIMITIVE
-- element (an `Int`/`String` immediate carries no cell tag).  The arg-position
-- analogue of preludeReturnPosDictNames: every constrained prelude fn that is NOT
-- already return-position.  Wired (alongside preludeReturnPosDictNames) into the
-- emit + dict_pp-oracle drivers ONLY (llvm_emit_typed_main / core_ir_dict_pp_main),
-- which no golden driver shares — so module/`=== TYPES ===`/eval goldens stay
-- byte-identical; the body sites route RDict $dict_<method>_<slot> through the same
-- activeDictVars layer registerConstraintRegs already builds for return-position.
export preludeArgPosDictNames : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String
preludeArgPosDictNames preludeDecls allDecls =
  let rpSet = preludeReturnPosDictNames preludeDecls allDecls
  filterList (n => not (contains n rpSet)) (constrainedSigNames preludeDecls)

-- the EVars referenced anywhere in [n]'s definition body (multi-clause: union)
declBodyVars : String -> List Decl -> List String
declBodyVars n decls = flatMap (declBodyVarsOf n) decls

declBodyVarsOf : String -> Decl -> List String
declBodyVarsOf n (DFunDef _ m _ body)
  | n == m = allEVars body
  | otherwise = []
declBodyVarsOf _ _ = []

-- methods whose interface type param appears ONLY in the result (no argument
-- mentions it) — pure/empty/minBound/maxBound/fromInt/… — are return-position
returnPosMethodNames : List Decl -> List String
returnPosMethodNames prog = flatMap returnPosOfDecl prog

returnPosOfDecl : Decl -> List String
returnPosOfDecl (DInterface { typarams, methods, ... }) =
  flatMap (returnPosMethod typarams) methods
returnPosOfDecl _ = []

returnPosMethod : List String -> IfaceMethod -> List String
returnPosMethod typarams (IfaceMethod mname mty _)
  | not (anyArgMentions (dispatchTyparams typarams) (methodArgs mty)) = [mname]
  | otherwise = []

-- argument types of a method (strip leading constraint/effect wrappers + arrows)
methodArgs : Ty -> List Ty
methodArgs (TyConstrained _ t) = methodArgs t
methodArgs (TyEffect _ _ t) = methodArgs t
methodArgs (TyFun a b) = a :: methodArgs b
methodArgs _ = []

anyArgMentions : List String -> List Ty -> Bool
anyArgMentions _ [] = False
anyArgMentions typarams (ty::rest) = argMentions typarams ty
  || anyArgMentions typarams rest

argMentions : List String -> Ty -> Bool
argMentions typarams ty = anyMember (tyVarNames ty) typarams

anyMember : List String -> List String -> Bool
anyMember [] _ = False
anyMember (x::xs) ys = contains x ys || anyMember xs ys

-- rewrite each return-position method occurrence to EMethodAt, and each
-- constrained-fn occurrence (a name in [dictNames]) to EDictAt — both carry a
-- fresh ref the typechecker fills.  The two name sets are disjoint (interface
-- methods vs top-level functions).
prePassDict : List String -> List String -> List Decl -> List Decl
prePassDict rpNames dictNames prog =
  mapProg (rewriteRPDict rpNames dictNames) prog

-- [rpNames] here is the union of return-position methods AND method-level-
-- constrained methods (foldMap): both must become EMethodAt so eval can narrow by
-- the t-route and/or fold on the method-level dicts.
rewriteRPDict : List String -> List String -> Expr -> Expr
rewriteRPDict rpNames dictNames (EVar n)
  | contains n rpNames = EMethodAt n (Ref RNone) (Ref []) (Ref [])
  | contains n dictNames = EDictAt n (Ref [])
  | otherwise = EVar n
rewriteRPDict _ _ e = e

-- D3a: like prePassDict but ALSO rewrite ARG-position method occurrences (names in
-- [argNames]) to EMethodAt, so inferMethodAt can stamp their route from the
-- discriminating-argument type.  [argNames] is empty unless emitArgStampPasses (the
-- emit path), so the golden/oracle paths get the same tree prePassDict produced.
-- The golden/oracle path (argNames == []) keeps the scope-BLIND mapProg rewrite,
-- byte-for-byte identical to prePassDict.  The emit path (argNames non-empty, set
-- only under emitArgStampPasses) instead uses the SCOPE-AWARE rewriteArgScoped below:
-- an arg-position interface-method name (in argNames) that is shadowed by a local
-- binder (a parameter, let, lambda arg, or match-pattern variable) stays a plain
-- EVar rather than becoming an EMethodAt.  This mirrors the reference typecheck's
-- Phase-95 `env.locals` guard (lib/typecheck.ml's EVar arm): the OCaml marker is
-- likewise scope-blind and the reference RECOVERS at inference by skipping the
-- scope-blind method table for locally-bound names.  Without it, a compiler
-- function whose parameter happens to share a method name (`arithOp lt rt`,
-- `composeOp gt ht`, `exprToPat … sub`) has that parameter mis-marked as a method
-- occurrence → arg-tag dispatch with no impl groups at emit time.  Gated on
-- argNames so the golden path is provably unchanged.  The rp/an/dn arms are ALL
-- scope-aware on this (emit + run `elaborateDict`) path: a local binder shadowing
-- a return-position method name (`let debug = 99` inside a fn body) keeps the
-- reference a plain EVar so inference resolves it to the local instead of
-- mis-stamping RKey<resultType> and dispatching to the prelude impl.  Mirrors
-- lib/typecheck.ml's `env.locals` guard, which skips the method_iface table for
-- any locally-bound name (the OCaml EVar-arm recovery).  Byte-identical for the
-- corpus (no corpus rp/arg/constrained name is shadowed by a local — the guard
-- only changes behaviour for a genuinely-shadowing local: an arg-method
-- `lt`/`gt`/`sub`, a constrained fn `elem`, or a return-position method `debug`).
-- The non-emit `mapProg` path (argNames == []) stays scope-blind.
-- [shadowMap] (P0-18) is the mangle definer-shadow map — non-empty only on the emit
-- path (argNames non-empty).  The scope-blind `mapProg` path (argNames == []) never
-- has mangled shadows, so it ignores it and stays byte-identical.
prePassDictArg : List String -> List String -> List String -> List (String, String) -> List Decl -> List Decl
prePassDictArg rpNames dictNames [] _ prog =
  mapProg (rewriteRPDictArg rpNames dictNames []) prog
prePassDictArg rpNames dictNames argNames shadowMap prog =
  map (prePassDeclScoped (ArgRw rpNames dictNames argNames shadowMap)) prog

rewriteRPDictArg : List String -> List String -> List String -> Expr -> Expr
rewriteRPDictArg rpNames dictNames argNames (EVar n)
  | contains n rpNames = EMethodAt n (Ref RNone) (Ref []) (Ref [])
  | contains n argNames = EMethodAt n (Ref RNone) (Ref []) (Ref [])
  | contains n dictNames = EDictAt n (Ref [])
  | otherwise = EVar n
rewriteRPDictArg _ _ _ e = e

-- ── scope-aware arg-position prepass (emit path; D3a shadow guard) ───────────
-- The three name sets carried through the scope-threaded rewrite (rp = return-
-- position methods, dn = constrained-fn dict names, an = arg-position methods).
-- rp = return-position methods, dn = constrained-fn dict names, an = arg-position
-- methods, sm = the P0-18 mangle definer-shadow map (mangledSymbol ↦ bareMethod).
data ArgRw =
  | ArgRw (List String) (List String) (List String) (List (String, String))

-- rewrite a decl body with its own parameters seeded into the bound set.  Mirrors
-- desugar's mapDecl coverage exactly (DFunDef / DInterface / DImpl / DProp /
-- DTest / DBench / DAttrib; everything else — incl. DLetGroup — left unchanged),
-- so with the emit path off this would degenerate to the same node set mapProg
-- visits.
prePassDeclScoped : ArgRw -> Decl -> Decl
prePassDeclScoped rw (DFunDef pub n ps e) =
  DFunDef pub n ps (rewriteArgScoped rw (patVarsListTc ps) e)
prePassDeclScoped rw (d@(DInterface { methods, ... })) =
  DInterface { d | methods = map (prePassIfaceMethodScoped rw) methods }
prePassDeclScoped rw (d@(DImpl { methods, ... })) =
  DImpl { d | methods = map (prePassImplMethodScoped rw) methods }
prePassDeclScoped rw (DProp pub name params body) =
  DProp pub name params (rewriteArgScoped rw (propParamNamesTc params) body)
prePassDeclScoped rw (DTest pub name body) =
  DTest pub name (rewriteArgScoped rw [] body)
prePassDeclScoped rw (DBench pub name body) =
  DBench pub name (rewriteArgScoped rw [] body)
prePassDeclScoped rw (DAttrib attrs d) = DAttrib attrs (prePassDeclScoped rw d)
-- Top-level `let rec … with …` (DLetGroup): mapDecl/the catch-all skip its bodies,
-- so a constrained-fn occurrence inside a group body would never be rewritten to
-- EDictAt/EMethodAt → its call site gets no dict route → the dict-passed callee is
-- under-applied at eval.  Rewrite each clause body with that clause's params scoped
-- in, exactly like the DFunDef arm.
prePassDeclScoped rw (DLetGroup pub binds) =
  DLetGroup pub (map (prePassLetBindScoped rw) binds)
prePassDeclScoped _ d = d

prePassLetBindScoped : ArgRw -> LetBind -> LetBind
prePassLetBindScoped rw (LetBind n clauses) =
  LetBind n (map (prePassFunClauseScoped rw) clauses)

prePassFunClauseScoped : ArgRw -> FunClause -> FunClause
prePassFunClauseScoped rw (FunClause ps e) =
  FunClause ps (rewriteArgScoped rw (patVarsListTc ps) e)

prePassIfaceMethodScoped : ArgRw -> IfaceMethod -> IfaceMethod
prePassIfaceMethodScoped _ (IfaceMethod n ty None) = IfaceMethod n ty None
prePassIfaceMethodScoped rw (IfaceMethod n ty (Some (MethodDefault ps e))) =
  IfaceMethod
    n
    ty
    (Some (MethodDefault ps (rewriteArgScoped rw (patVarsListTc ps) e)))

prePassImplMethodScoped : ArgRw -> ImplMethod -> ImplMethod
prePassImplMethodScoped rw (ImplMethod n ps e) =
  ImplMethod n ps (rewriteArgScoped rw (patVarsListTc ps) e)

propParamNamesTc : List PropParam -> List String
propParamNamesTc ps = map propParamName ps

propParamName : PropParam -> String
propParamName (PropParam n _) = n

-- the core scope-threaded rewrite.  `bound` is the set of names in scope as
-- locals at this node.  At an EVar: the rp arm is scope-blind (unchanged); the
-- arg-position (`an`) and constrained-fn (`dn`) arms fire only when the name is
-- NOT shadowed by a local.  Binders
-- (ELam/ELet/ELetGroup/EMatch arms/EBlock/EDo + the where/do/guard
-- binders) extend `bound`; every other node recurses into its children with the
-- same `bound`.
rewriteArgScoped : ArgRw -> List String -> Expr -> Expr
rewriteArgScoped (ArgRw rp dn an sm) bound (EVar n)
  -- P0-18: `n` is a MANGLED definer-shadow standalone (`<mid>__size`).  Mark it
  -- `EMethodAt` with the BARE dispatch name (so `implFor` finds the impl when the
  -- receiver has one) and seed the route's `RLocal <n>` fallback with the mangled
  -- symbol (so a no-impl receiver still reaches the standalone `@mdk_<mid>__size`).
  | not (contains n bound) && isSome (lookupAssoc n sm) =
    match lookupAssoc n sm
      Some bare => EMethodAt bare (Ref (RLocal n [])) (Ref []) (Ref [])
      None => EVar n
  | contains n rp && not (contains n bound) =
    EMethodAt n (Ref RNone) (Ref []) (Ref [])
  | contains n an && not (contains n bound) =
    EMethodAt n (Ref RNone) (Ref []) (Ref [])
  | contains n dn && not (contains n bound) = EDictAt n (Ref [])
  | otherwise = EVar n
-- binders
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ELam ps body) =
  ELam ps (rewriteArgScoped rw (patVarsListTc ps ++ bound) body)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ELet m r p e1 e2) =
  let pv = patVarsTc p
  let b1 = if r then pv ++ bound else bound
  ELet m r p (rewriteArgScoped rw b1 e1) (rewriteArgScoped rw (pv ++ bound) e2)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ELetGroup binds e2) =
  let bnd = letBindNamesTc binds ++ bound
  ELetGroup (map (rewriteArgLetBind rw bnd) binds) (rewriteArgScoped rw bnd e2)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EMatch e0 arms) =
  EMatch (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0) (map (rewriteArgArm rw bound) arms)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EBlock stmts) =
  EBlock (rewriteArgStmts rw bound stmts)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EDo stmts) = EDo (rewriteArgStmts rw bound stmts)
-- non-binder composites: recurse children with the same bound
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ELoc l e) = ELoc l (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EDoOrigin l e) =
  EDoOrigin l (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EApp f x) =
  EApp (rewriteArgScoped rw bound f) (rewriteArgScoped rw bound x)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EIf c t e) =
  EIf
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound c)
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound t)
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EBinOp op l r dr) =
  EBinOp op (rewriteArgScoped rw bound l) (rewriteArgScoped rw bound r) dr
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EUnOp op x dr) =
  EUnOp op (rewriteArgScoped rw bound x) dr
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EInfix op l r) =
  EInfix op (rewriteArgScoped rw bound l) (rewriteArgScoped rw bound r)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EFieldAccess e0 n r) =
  EFieldAccess (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0) n r
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ETuple es) =
  ETuple (map (rewriteArgScoped rw bound) es)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EListLit es) =
  EListLit (map (rewriteArgScoped rw bound) es)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EArrayLit es) =
  EArrayLit (map (rewriteArgScoped rw bound) es)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ERangeList lo hi i) =
  ERangeList (rewriteArgScoped rw bound lo) (rewriteArgScoped rw bound hi) i
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ERangeArray lo hi i) =
  ERangeArray (rewriteArgScoped rw bound lo) (rewriteArgScoped rw bound hi) i
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ESlice e0 lo hi i r) =
  ESlice
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0)
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound lo)
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound hi)
    i
    r
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EIndex e0 i r) =
  EIndex (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0) (rewriteArgScoped rw bound i) r
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EAnnot e0 t) =
  EAnnot (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0) t
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EHeadAnnot e0 t) =
  EHeadAnnot (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0) t
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ERecordCreate n fs) =
  ERecordCreate n (map (rewriteArgField rw bound) fs)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ERecordUpdate e0 fs r) =
  ERecordUpdate
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0)
    (map (rewriteArgField rw bound) fs)
    r
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EVariantUpdate c e0 fs) =
  EVariantUpdate
    c
    (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0)
    (map (rewriteArgField rw bound) fs)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EStringInterp parts) =
  EStringInterp (map (rewriteArgInterp rw bound) parts)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EGuards arms) =
  EGuards (map (rewriteArgGuardArm rw bound) arms)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ESection (SecRight op e0)) =
  ESection (SecRight op (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0))
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ESection (SecLeft e0 op)) =
  ESection (SecLeft (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e0) op)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EMapLit n kvs) =
  EMapLit n (map (rewriteArgKv rw bound) kvs)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (ESetLit n es) =
  ESetLit n (map (rewriteArgScoped rw bound) es)
rewriteArgScoped rw bound (EAsPat x sub) =
  EAsPat x (rewriteArgScoped rw bound sub)
-- leaves (ELit / EMethodRef / EDictApp / EVarAt / EMethodAt / EDictAt / SecBare)
rewriteArgScoped _ _ e = e

rewriteArgField : ArgRw -> List String -> FieldAssign -> FieldAssign
rewriteArgField rw bound (FieldAssign n e) =
  FieldAssign n (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e)

rewriteArgKv : ArgRw -> List String -> (Expr, Expr) -> (Expr, Expr)
rewriteArgKv rw bound (k, v) =
  (rewriteArgScoped rw bound k, rewriteArgScoped rw bound v)

rewriteArgInterp : ArgRw -> List String -> InterpPart -> InterpPart
rewriteArgInterp _ _ (InterpStr s) = InterpStr s
rewriteArgInterp rw bound (InterpExpr e) =
  InterpExpr (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e)

rewriteArgLetBind : ArgRw -> List String -> LetBind -> LetBind
rewriteArgLetBind rw bound (LetBind n clauses) =
  LetBind n (map (rewriteArgFunClause rw bound) clauses)

rewriteArgFunClause : ArgRw -> List String -> FunClause -> FunClause
rewriteArgFunClause rw bound (FunClause ps body) =
  FunClause ps (rewriteArgScoped rw (patVarsListTc ps ++ bound) body)

-- a match/function arm: the pattern binds for the guards + body; a `| p <- e`
-- guard binds for later guards + the body (threaded left-to-right).
rewriteArgArm : ArgRw -> List String -> Arm -> Arm
rewriteArgArm rw bound (Arm p gs body) =
  let b0 = patVarsTc p ++ bound
  let (gs2, bnd) = rewriteArgGuards rw b0 gs
  Arm p gs2 (rewriteArgScoped rw bnd body)

rewriteArgGuardArm : ArgRw -> List String -> GuardArm -> GuardArm
rewriteArgGuardArm rw bound (GuardArm gs body) =
  let (gs2, bnd) = rewriteArgGuards rw bound gs
  GuardArm gs2 (rewriteArgScoped rw bnd body)

rewriteArgGuards : ArgRw -> List String -> List Guard -> (List Guard, List String)
rewriteArgGuards _ bound [] = ([], bound)
rewriteArgGuards rw bound ((GBool e)::rest) =
  let e2 = rewriteArgScoped rw bound e
  let (rest2, bnd) = rewriteArgGuards rw bound rest
  (GBool e2 :: rest2, bnd)
rewriteArgGuards rw bound ((GBind p e)::rest) =
  let e2 = rewriteArgScoped rw bound e
  let (rest2, bnd) = rewriteArgGuards rw (patVarsTc p ++ bound) rest
  (GBind p e2 :: rest2, bnd)

-- do/block statements: a DoBind/DoLet pattern binds for SUBSEQUENT
-- statements (a recursive DoLet also for its own RHS).
rewriteArgStmts : ArgRw -> List String -> List DoStmt -> List DoStmt
rewriteArgStmts _ _ [] = []
rewriteArgStmts rw bound ((DoExpr e)::rest) =
  DoExpr (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e) :: rewriteArgStmts rw bound rest
rewriteArgStmts rw bound ((DoBind p e)::rest) =
  DoBind p (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e) ::
    rewriteArgStmts rw (patVarsTc p ++ bound) rest
rewriteArgStmts rw bound ((DoLet m r p e)::rest) =
  let b1 = if r then patVarsTc p ++ bound else bound
  DoLet m r p (rewriteArgScoped rw b1 e) :: rewriteArgStmts rw (patVarsTc p ++ bound) rest
rewriteArgStmts rw bound ((DoAssign x e)::rest) =
  DoAssign x (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e) :: rewriteArgStmts rw bound rest
rewriteArgStmts rw bound ((DoFieldAssign x fs e)::rest) =
  DoFieldAssign x fs (rewriteArgScoped rw bound e) ::
    rewriteArgStmts rw bound rest

letBindNamesTc : List LetBind -> List String
letBindNamesTc binds = map letBindNameTc binds

letBindNameTc : LetBind -> String
letBindNameTc (LetBind n _) = n

-- the variables a pattern binds (local copy; llvm_emit's patVars isn't importable
-- here, and this one also covers PRec field/pun binders).
patVarsTc : Pat -> List String
patVarsTc (PVar x) = [x]
patVarsTc (PCon _ args) = patVarsListTc args
patVarsTc (PCons h t) = patVarsTc h ++ patVarsTc t
patVarsTc (PTuple ps) = patVarsListTc ps
patVarsTc (PList ps) = patVarsListTc ps
patVarsTc (PAs x p) = x :: patVarsTc p
patVarsTc (PRec _ fields _) = flatMap recPatFieldVarsTc fields
patVarsTc _ = []

patVarsListTc : List Pat -> List String
patVarsListTc ps = flatMap patVarsTc ps

recPatFieldVarsTc : RecPatField -> List String
recPatFieldVarsTc (RecPatField label None) = [label]
recPatFieldVarsTc (RecPatField _ (Some p)) = patVarsTc p

-- interface methods carrying a method-level constraint (a `=>` over a tyvar that is
-- NOT the interface param, e.g. foldMap's `Monoid m`) — rewritten to EMethodAt
-- alongside the return-position methods so the method-level dict can be folded on.
-- Computed structurally (runs before checkProgramSeeded populates methodConstraintsRef).
methodConstraintNames : List Decl -> List String
methodConstraintNames prog = flatMap methodConstraintNamesOfDecl prog

methodConstraintNamesOfDecl : Decl -> List String
methodConstraintNamesOfDecl (DAttrib _ d) = methodConstraintNamesOfDecl d
methodConstraintNamesOfDecl (DInterface { typarams, methods, ... }) =
  flatMap (methodConstraintNameOf typarams) methods
methodConstraintNamesOfDecl _ = []

methodConstraintNameOf : List String -> IfaceMethod -> List String
methodConstraintNameOf typarams (IfaceMethod mname mty _)
  | hasMethodLevelConstraint typarams mty = [mname]
  | otherwise = []

-- does this method type carry a leading `=>` constraint whose tyvar is not an
-- interface param? (a method-LEVEL constraint, as opposed to one over the param)
hasMethodLevelConstraint : List String -> Ty -> Bool
hasMethodLevelConstraint typarams (TyConstrained cs t) = constraintsHaveMethodLevel typarams cs
  || hasMethodLevelConstraint typarams t
hasMethodLevelConstraint typarams (TyEffect _ _ t) =
  hasMethodLevelConstraint typarams t
hasMethodLevelConstraint _ _ = False

constraintsHaveMethodLevel : List String -> List Constraint -> Bool
constraintsHaveMethodLevel _ [] = False
constraintsHaveMethodLevel typarams (c::rest) = constraintIsMethodLevel typarams c
  || constraintsHaveMethodLevel typarams rest

-- a constraint is method-level iff it mentions at least one tyvar that is not an
-- interface param (so the dict is supplied per-call, not by the impl's dispatch)
constraintIsMethodLevel : List String -> Constraint -> Bool
constraintIsMethodLevel typarams c =
  isNonEmptyL (constraintNonParamVars typarams c)

constraintNonParamVars : List String -> Constraint -> List String
constraintNonParamVars typarams (Constraint _ args) =
  removeAllS typarams (flatMap tyVarNames args)

resolveSites : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List String -> List (String, Ref Route, Ref (List Route), Mono, Mono, String) -> Unit
resolveSites _ _ _ [] = ()
resolveSites implTable keyTable rpNames ((name, tagRef, implRef, resultMono, fullMono, encl)::rest) =
  let _ = resolveSite implTable keyTable rpNames name tagRef implRef resultMono fullMono encl
  resolveSites implTable keyTable rpNames rest

-- a method occurrence's route: if its discriminating type is a constrained
-- function's constraint variable, read that function's dict param (RDict);
-- otherwise the concrete impl head tag (RKey); an unresolved polymorphic type
-- stays RNone (arg-tag fallback).  When the route resolves to a concrete head
-- that names a parametric impl WITH a `requires`, also fill the impl-dicts ref so
-- eval threads the element dict(s) into the impl body (Phase 83/84 single-level).
-- Phase 83/84 #5: stamp the route for one pending method site.  At a concrete
-- result type (ground RKey case) the element dict routes are computed recursively
-- by implDictRoutesFor.  At a polymorphic result type (RDict case) the route is
-- RDictFwd for return-position methods — eval will extract and forward the
-- enclosing VDict's nested requires at runtime.  Arg-position methods (display/==)
-- stay RDict; their VMulti dispatch is by arg-tag, not by the forwarded reqs.
-- TYPECHECK-AUDIT C7: the ground RKey case prefers the canonical impl KEY (via
-- keyForSite) over the bare head tycon, so two non-overlapping impls sharing a
-- head tycon (Pair Int Bool vs Pair Bool Int) route to distinct keys; eval's
-- narrowMethod then matches the right VTypedImpl by its key field.  No matching
-- key entry (args-free / unknown) → fall back to the head tag, behaviour unchanged.
-- C5 NOTE: standalone-shadow RLocal routing is NOT done here — a return-position
-- site keys off the RESULT mono (`isEmpty`'s `Bool`), which never names the receiver's
-- impl, so this gate would mis-route every shadow method.  RLocal is stamped by the
-- arg-position resolveRLocalSites (keyed on the RECEIVER mono) AFTER resolveSites.
resolveSite : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List String -> String -> Ref Route -> Ref (List Route) -> Mono -> Mono -> String -> Unit
resolveSite implTable keyTable rpNames name tagRef implRef resultMono fullMono encl = match activeDictVarOfEncl resultMono encl
  Some dname => setRef tagRef (if contains name rpNames then RDictFwd dname else RDict dname)
  None => match headTyconMono resultMono
    Some tag =>
      let routeKey = fromOption tag (keyForSite keyTable name resultMono)
      let _ = setRef tagRef (RKey routeKey [])
      let paramMonos = fromOption [resultMono] (ifaceParamMonos name fullMono)
      setRef
        implRef
        (implDictRoutesForFull implTable name tag resultMono paramMonos)
    None => ()

-- Recover the FULL list of interface-type-parameter monos for a method occurrence,
-- ordered by the interface's typaram declaration.  Look up the method's interface
-- (methodIfaceParamsRef: iface, typarams, declared method Ty), match the declared Ty
-- against the occurrence's full mono to bind each typaram → its inferred mono, then
-- read them out in typaram order.  For `fromEntries : List e -> c` matched against the
-- occurrence `List Int -> Set _a`, this yields [Set _a, Int] (c then e) — the element
-- grounding (`e ↦ Int`) the result-only `resultMono` (= c = Set _a) had lost.  None
-- when the method has no registered interface (a plain `=>`-constrained fn) or the
-- declared/occurrence shapes do not match — resolveSite falls back to [resultMono].
ifaceParamMonos : String -> Mono -> Option (List Mono)
ifaceParamMonos name fullMono = match omLookup name methodIfaceParamsRef.value
  None => None
  Some (_, typarams, mty) =>
    map (subst => map (paramMonoOf subst) typarams) (matchTyMono mty fullMono)

paramMonoOf : List (String, Mono) -> String -> Mono
paramMonoOf subst tp = fromOption (TCon tp) (lookupAssoc tp subst)

-- C5: does some impl in [keyTable] define method [name] at head tycon [tag]?  The
-- static analogue of eval's narrowMethod finding a VTypedImpl tagged [tag] in the
-- method's VMulti — keyTable carries one entry per user+prelude impl with the methods
-- it defines and its head tycon.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's impl_exists_for_head.
implExistsForHead : KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Bool
implExistsForHead buckets name tag =
  implExistsForHeadGo (bucketOf tag buckets) name tag

implExistsForHeadGo : List KeyEntry -> String -> String -> Bool
implExistsForHeadGo [] _ _ = False
implExistsForHeadGo ((KeyEntry methods tag2 _ _)::rest) name tag
  | tag2 == tag && contains name methods = True
  | otherwise = implExistsForHeadGo rest name tag

-- C5: stamp RLocal on each recorded standalone-shadow arg-position site whose
-- receiver mono now grounds to a concrete head tycon with NO impl of the method.
-- The receiver HAS an impl (the genuine-method case, e.g. `toList (Some 7)`) → leave
-- the route untouched (RNone → eval arg-tag-dispatches to the real impl).  An
-- ungrounded receiver (still a tyvar) → leave it (polymorphic, deferred).  Mirrors
-- lib/typecheck.ml:1603-1640: RLocal only when impl_exists is false.
-- S-1: [prog]/[implTable] are here so the stamp can call routesOfMonosTop — a PURE
-- function of the (union-find-solved) constraint monos — and fill the standalone's own
-- dict routes IN PLACE.  Deliberately NOT routed through pendingDictApps: that list is
-- resolved by resolveDictApps, which runs AFTER this in elabModuleStamp, so the stamp
-- would read `[]`.  Resolving inside the stamp is ordering-immune.  An unconstrained
-- standalone has no monos ⇒ routesOfMonosTop returns [] immediately (it cannot even
-- reach its AmbiguousImpl arm) ⇒ `RLocal sym []` ⇒ byte-identical to the pre-S1 stamp.
resolveRLocalSites : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List RLocalSite -> Unit
resolveRLocalSites _ _ _ [] = ()
resolveRLocalSites prog implTable keyTable ((RLocalSite name tagRef am sym forceLocal monos ifaces)::rest) =
  let _ = if forceLocal then
    setRef tagRef (RLocal sym (routesOfMonosTop prog implTable monos ifaces))
  else
    resolveRLocalSite prog implTable keyTable name tagRef am sym monos ifaces
  resolveRLocalSites prog implTable keyTable rest

-- C5: stamp RLocal on each recorded standalone-shadow arg-position site whose
-- receiver mono now grounds to a concrete head tycon with NO impl of the method.
-- The receiver HAS an impl (the genuine-method case, e.g. `toList (Some 7)`):
-- leave the route untouched — eval arg-tag-dispatches on RNone; emit already
-- stamped the correct RKey<receiverHead> via resolveArgStamps.
-- An ungrounded receiver (still a tyvar) → leave it.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
-- RLocal-vs-ordinary-dispatch split (the oracle keys the genuine case off the receiver).
resolveRLocalSite : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> String -> List Mono -> List String -> Unit
resolveRLocalSite prog implTable keyTable name tagRef am sym monos ifaces = match headTyconMono am
  -- S2 (THE INVERSION): route by SHADOW KIND first, receiver second.
  --   * DEFINER shadow (the name is this module's own top-level fn) → the standalone,
