  --     UNCONDITIONALLY.  The impl universe is not consulted; a grounded head with a live
  --     impl no longer steals the call.  (The APP path already forces this via the
  --     RLocalSite `forceLocal` flag; this arm is what carries the other recorders —
  --     notably the VALUE-position sites pushed by the generic recordRLocalSite, which is
  --     gated on standaloneValuesRef and that set holds definer AND importer shadows.)
  --   * IMPORTER shadow (Fork 1) → the OLD per-receiver rule, unchanged: a live impl at the
  --     receiver head dispatches, else the standalone.  An `import` is a SIBLING scope, not
  --     an inner one, so it does not shadow; inverting it too would break the everyday
  --     `import map` pattern (`isEmpty [1,2]` must still reach `Foldable.isEmpty`).
  -- am is the ACTUAL argument type (inferDefinerShadowApp records it), not the standalone's
  -- declared domain, so the importer arm's impl query is still meaningful.
  --
  -- ⚠️ isDefinerShadow — NOT a bare definerShadowNamesRef membership test.  That ref is
  -- populated for a multi-TYPARAM interface's method too, but EVERY typing entry point is
  -- gated on singleParamIfaceMethod and so leaves such an occurrence to ordinary dispatch
  -- (the open S-3 / row-26 bug).  Forcing RLocal here without the same gate would route a
  -- site whose TYPE came from the dispatch path — route and type disagreeing is exactly the
  -- P0-20 bug class.  Keep the two gates identical.
  Some tag => stampRLocalOrFallback
    (not (isDefinerShadow name) && implExistsForHead keyTable name tag)
    tagRef sym
    (routesOfMonosTop prog implTable monos ifaces)
  -- An UNGROUNDED definer-shadow receiver keeps the old default (RLocal → standalone);
  -- other (importer) ungrounded sites stay untouched (deferred/polymorphic).
  None => if contains name definerShadowNamesRef.value then
    setRef tagRef (RLocal sym (routesOfMonosTop prog implTable monos ifaces))
  else ()

-- C5: hasImpl ⇒ genuine method; route already correct (RNone for arg-tag eval,
-- or RKey stamped by resolveArgStamps on emit) — leave untouched.
-- no impl ⇒ standalone shadow ⇒ RLocal, carrying the standalone's own constraint
-- dicts (S-1; [] for an unconstrained standalone ⇒ the pre-S1 stamp exactly).
stampRLocalOrFallback : Bool -> Ref Route -> String -> List Route -> Unit
stampRLocalOrFallback True _ _ _ = ()
stampRLocalOrFallback False tagRef sym dicts = setRef tagRef (RLocal sym dicts)

-- if [m] normalizes to a registered constraint variable, the dict parameter name
-- a method at that type reads.  A return-position method at a HIGHER-KINDED
-- constraint var (`pure : a -> m a`, so the result mono is `m Unit` = TApp (TVar
-- m) Unit) carries the dict var in the application HEAD, not at top level — recurse
-- through TApp to find it.  (A concrete head, e.g. `Option Unit` after unification,
-- is a TCon and falls through to None → RKey, unchanged.)
activeDictVarOf : Mono -> Option String
activeDictVarOf m = match normalize m
  TVar cell => lookupAssocI (tyvarId cell) activeDictVars.value
  TApp a _ => activeDictVarOf a
  _ => None

-- activeDictVarOf, but PREFER the activeDictVars entry whose dict-param name belongs
-- to the enclosing method [encl] (`$dict_<encl>_<slot>`).  registerImplRequires
-- registers EVERY method of an impl under the SAME impl tyvar id (`impl Ord (List a)`
-- registers `a`'s id once per compare/lt/gt/min/max/… each to `$dict_<method>_<slot>`),
-- so the global first-match `activeDictVarOf` returns whichever method registered
-- LAST — inside `compare`'s body that wrongly yields `$dict_max_0` (out of compare's
-- scope → emitter "unbound dict witness").  Picking the [encl]-owned entry keeps each
-- method body's element-dict route bound to ITS OWN leading param.  encl == "" or no
-- match ⇒ fall back to the global lookup (genuine element dict from another impl).
activeDictVarForEncl : Mono -> String -> Option String
activeDictVarForEncl m encl
  | encl == "" = activeDictVarOf m
  | otherwise = match normalize m
    TVar cell => match firstDictForEncl (tyvarId cell) ("$dict_" ++ encl ++ "_") activeDictVars.value
      Some dname => Some dname
      None => activeDictVarOf m
    TApp a _ => activeDictVarForEncl a encl
    _ => None

firstDictForEncl : Int -> String -> List (Int, String) -> Option String
firstDictForEncl _ _ [] = None
firstDictForEncl id pfx ((eid, ename)::rest)
  | eid == id && startsWith pfx ename = Some ename
  | otherwise = firstDictForEncl id pfx rest

-- activeDictVarOf, but for a constraint var that is the ENCLOSING fn's OWN constraint
-- var, return [encl]'s in-scope dict param (the slot it sits at) rather than the
-- GLOBAL activeDictVars entry.  In a mutually-recursive group the constraint vars of
-- the siblings unify to one id; activeDictVars (global, uncleared) maps that id to
-- whichever sibling registered it LAST — so an arg-stamp inside `evenCat` would
-- forward `$dict_oddCat_0` (out of scope in evenCat's body).  Preferring encl's own
-- slot keeps the forwarded dict the one actually bound in the body.  Falls back to
-- the global lookup when the var is not one of encl's (a genuine element dict from a
-- different in-scope impl).
activeDictVarOfEncl : Mono -> String -> Option String
activeDictVarOfEncl m encl = match enclDictVarOf m encl
  Some dname => Some dname
  None => activeDictVarForEncl m encl

enclDictVarOf : Mono -> String -> Option String
enclDictVarOf m encl
  | encl == "" = None
  | otherwise = match normalize m
    TVar cell => map (dictParamName encl) (indexOfId (tyvarId cell) (fromOption [] (lookupAssocSL2 encl funConstraintsRef.value)))
    TApp a _ => enclDictVarOf a encl
    _ => None

-- Phase 151 / Gap G: stamp each comparison/equality OPERATOR site's EBinOp route
-- ref with the resolved Eq/Ord impl key (RKey) — but ONLY when the operand type
-- grounds to a NON-primitive head tycon.  Primitive (Int/Float/String/Bool/Char)
-- and ungroundable operands stay RNone → the structural-builtin EBinOp eval path
-- (so `eq a b = a == b` on Int never recurses).  Mirrors resolveSite's concrete
-- branch (head tycon → canonical key) minus the impl-dicts: the rewritten `eq`/`lt`
-- is arg-position, so it narrows by arg tag and needs no forwarded element dict.
binopPrimitiveHead : String -> Bool
binopPrimitiveHead h = contains h ["Int", "Float", "String", "Bool", "Char"]

-- Roadmap #18a: the heads a binop lowers to a BUILTIN for, and must therefore never
-- route to a typeclass method.  For the comparison operators that is exactly the
-- scalar primitives.  For `++` (→ Semigroup's `append`) it is those PLUS `List`:
-- `++` on a String/List is lowered structurally (eval's `appendVal`; the emitter's
-- `mdk_string_append`/`mdk_list_append`), and core.mdk's own builtin impls are
-- written in terms of the operator —
--     impl Semigroup (List a) where append xs ys = xs ++ ys
--     impl Semigroup String   where append s1 s2 = s1 ++ s2
-- — so routing a List/String `++` to `append` would make those bodies call
-- themselves unboundedly.  Every OTHER head still routes, which is precisely what
-- makes a user `impl Semigroup MyRope` reachable from `a ++ b`.
binopBuiltinHead : String -> String -> Bool
binopBuiltinHead "append" tag = binopPrimitiveHead tag || tag == "List"
binopBuiltinHead _ tag = binopPrimitiveHead tag

-- SHARED-FLOAT-RESIDUAL §3(C): after the module is inferred, zonk each recorded
-- arithmetic operand mono; when it grounds to concrete Float, stamp RScalar
-- "Float" on the node's route so lowering carries it into CBinPrim and the native
-- emitter picks the Float primitive (bypassing the type-lost structural
-- staticIsFloat).  Grounded-only, exactly like resolveBinopSite: a still-
-- polymorphic operand (headTyconMono None) is left untouched (RNone → dict path),
-- so polymorphic-Num arithmetic keeps routing through the runtime Num dict.  Only
-- Float is stamped (Int is the primitive default already; leaving Int operands
-- RNone avoids perturbing every integer binop's emitted IR).  Never overwrites a
-- route another pass already set (a comparison op has a different node/ref; an
-- arithmetic route starts RNone) — the guard is: stamp only if the site is still
-- RNone AND grounds to Float.
resolveArithSites : List (Ref Route, Mono) -> Unit
resolveArithSites [] = ()
resolveArithSites ((dref, m)::rest) =
  let _ = resolveArithSite dref m
  resolveArithSites rest

resolveArithSite : Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
resolveArithSite dref m = match dref.value
  RNone => match headTyconMono m
    Some "Float" => setRef dref (RScalar "Float")
    _ => ()
  _ => ()

-- EBinOp and EUnOp share ONE dict-route resolution family
-- (resolveOpSites/resolveOpSite/stampOpRoute/opDictVarOf), selected by an `isBinop`
-- flag (Roadmap #18c collapsed the binop/unop mirror pair — unopMethodApp already
-- reused binopRouteSplit, proving the shapes unify).  The two paths differ in exactly
-- two flag-keyed places: the dict-var-of policy (opDictVarOf) and the RKey stamp
-- (stampOpRoute — its builtin-head guard and Ord-style innerDefaultMethod reduction).
resolveOpSites : Bool -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List (String, Ref Route, Mono, Bool, String) -> Unit
resolveOpSites _ _ _ [] = ()
resolveOpSites isBinop implTable keyTable ((method, tagRef, operandMono, inImpl, encl)::rest) =
  let _ = resolveOpSite isBinop implTable keyTable method tagRef operandMono inImpl encl
  resolveOpSites isBinop implTable keyTable rest

-- G4: an `==`/`<`/… INSIDE an impl body ([inImpl]) whose operand is the enclosing
-- impl's abstract `requires`-constrained tyvar routes RDict that impl's element dict
-- (`activeDictVarOf` maps the operand's surviving tyvar id → `$dict_<method>_<slot>`,
-- registered by registerImplRequires after body inference; this deferred batch sees
-- the populated table).  binopMethodApp then rewrites it to a dict-dispatched
-- `eq x y` and `usesImplDict` prepends the matching param.  Without this the abstract
-- operand never grounds to a head tycon → the route stayed RNone → the SHALLOW
-- structural builtin (`mdk_value_eq` / pointer-eq), silently mis-comparing any non-Int
-- element (List → False, nested Box → False) and crashing once the dicts were threaded.
-- The [inImpl] gate is REQUIRED for the GLOBAL activeDictVars route: activeDictVars
-- is global + uncleared, so a TOP-LEVEL comparison's operand tyvar id can collide
-- with a stale impl element-var entry → a spurious RDict (this flipped the repl's
-- String-keyed `:browse` sort).  But a legitimately-constrained TOP-LEVEL operator
-- (`f x y = x == y` under `Eq a =>`) must STILL forward the enclosing fn's own class
-- dict, exactly as the direct `eq x y` arg-stamp path does.  enclDictVarOf draws that
-- line PRECISELY: it consults `funConstraintsRef[encl]` (the enclosing fn's OWN
-- declared constraint slots, keyed by fn name) — NOT the global activeDictVars — so
-- it fires ONLY when the operand var is genuinely one of encl's constraint vars, and
-- can never pick up a stale cross-impl element-var id.  A concrete operand (no active
-- dict var / not a constraint var, grounds to Some tag) keeps the existing RKey path.
--
-- Roadmap #18a RESIDUAL — `++`/`append` does NOT take the IN-IMPL element-dict route.
-- `binopDictVarOf` sends `append` to `enclDictVarOf` even when inImpl, so an operand
-- that is an impl's abstract `requires` tyvar stays RNone (the structural builtin)
-- instead of routing the impl's threaded element dict.
--
-- WHY (measured, not assumed).  Taking that route for `append` regresses
-- `test/eval_typed_modules_fixtures/impl_requires_nonfunctor_sibling`:
--     impl Applicative (VT e) requires Semigroup e where
--       pure a = VS a
--       ap (VF e1) (VF e2) = VF (e1 ++ e2)
-- Routing `e1 ++ e2` to `$dict_ap_0` makes `usesImplDict "ap"` true for the FIRST
-- time, so `implDictPassMethods` PREPENDS an impl-`requires` dict param to `ap` —
-- which shifts `ap`'s arg-position dispatch index by one.  The VTypedImpl's recorded
-- discriminating indices are NOT re-offset to match, so the impl then tag-checks its
-- leading DICT argument instead of its receiver, rejects it, and every candidate
-- falls through → `no matching impl for dispatch`.  (Before #18a that
-- `requires Semigroup e` was effectively DEAD — nothing in the body consumed it —
-- which is why the latent hole never fired.)  Fixing it properly means re-offsetting
-- arg-dispatch indices when impl-dict params are prepended: Phase 83/84 territory,
-- across ast/typecheck/dict_pass/eval, and deliberately NOT attempted here.
--
-- What still works (all gated): a GROUND non-builtin operand (`Rope "a" ++ Rope "b"`)
-- routes RKey to the user impl — the whole point of #18a, and the fix for the `build`
-- SIGSEGV; a TOP-LEVEL declared `Semigroup a => a -> a` routes RDict through
-- `enclDictVarOf` (the enclosing fn's OWN declared constraint slot, a different and
-- already-sound dict source); and String/List stay on the structural builtin.
-- What does NOT: `++` on an impl's abstract `requires`-bound tyvar — it keeps the
-- pre-#18a builtin behaviour exactly (correct for List/String, which is every such
-- site in the tree today), so this is a residual, not a regression.
-- BINOP path (`isBinop`): `append` → enclDictVarOf even when inImpl (Roadmap #18a
-- residual, above); any other method → activeDictVarOf when inImpl, else enclDictVarOf.
-- UNOP path (not `isBinop`): ALWAYS enclDictVarOf.  Roadmap #18a/#45 landmine (same
-- shape as the `append` residual): routing `-x` through activeDictVarOf can make
-- usesImplDict true for a method for the FIRST time, prepending a dict param that
-- shifts the impl's arg-dispatch index without re-offsetting the VTypedImpl's
-- discriminating indices — dispatch then tag-checks the dict instead of the receiver.
-- So `-x` on an impl's own abstract `requires`-bound tyvar stays on the structural
-- builtin (RNone) via a top-level fn's own declared constraint slot, never
-- activeDictVarOf — a residual, not a regression (mirrors the `++` residual exactly).
opDictVarOf : Bool -> String -> Mono -> Bool -> String -> Option String
opDictVarOf True "append" m _ encl = enclDictVarOf m encl
opDictVarOf True _ m inImpl encl
  | inImpl = activeDictVarOf m
  | otherwise = enclDictVarOf m encl
opDictVarOf False _ m _ encl = enclDictVarOf m encl

resolveOpSite : Bool -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Bool -> String -> Unit
resolveOpSite isBinop implTable keyTable method tagRef operandMono inImpl encl = match opDictVarOf isBinop method operandMono inImpl encl
  Some dname => setRef tagRef (RDict dname)
  None => match headTyconMono operandMono
    Some tag =>
      stampOpRoute isBinop implTable keyTable method tagRef operandMono tag
    None => ()

-- Native Gap C: a tuple (or other parametric-with-requires) Eq/Ord impl carries
-- element `requires` dicts (Eq a, Eq b) that the rewritten `eq`/`compare` method body
-- reads.  Stamp them into the RKey route's reqs so binopMethodApp can forward them
-- as the EMethodAt impl-dict routes — exactly as resolveArgStamp does for a direct
-- `eq`/`compare` call.  A concrete non-parametric impl (user `Eq MyType`) has no
-- requires → empty reqs → the plain direct call, behaviour unchanged.
--
-- PARAMETRIC DEFAULT CASE (now routed RKey, gap closed 2026-06-12): a comparison
-- OPERATOR (`<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=` → `lt`/`gt`/`lte`/`gte`) whose method is NOT itself
-- impl'd at this head (an Ord interface DEFAULT reducing to inner `compare`) AND the
-- head's Ord impl HAS `requires` (parametric, e.g. tuple / `List a` / `Box a`).  This
-- stamps the RKey route with the SAME element-dict reqs as the `==`/`eq` path, but
-- keyed on the INNER same-interface `compare` (the relational method has no own impl
-- at this head, so `argImplDictRoutesFor` keyed on it would find nothing).
-- binopMethodApp forwards them as the EMethodAt impl-dicts; emitDefaultRKey passes
-- them as leading words to `@mdk_default_<op>_<tag>`, whose emitDefaultDefine
-- eta-PREPENDS the matching dict params and threads them into the inner
-- `@mdk_impl_<tag>_compare(dict.., a, b)` call.  Previously this case BACKED OFF to
-- RNone, which the backend lowered as a raw pointer `icmp` (silently WRONG result) /
-- SIGSEGV on the by-name `lt` path.  Concrete Ord (no requires) keeps empty reqs →
-- the plain `@mdk_default_<op>_<tag>(a, b)` direct call, unchanged.
-- Flag-keyed differences between the two paths:
--   * builtin-head guard — BINOP: `binopBuiltinHead method tag` (append also treats
--     List as builtin); UNOP: `binopPrimitiveHead tag` (a ground Int/Float stays on
--     evalUnop's VInt/VFloat fast path, the unop analogue of the comparison guard).
--   * dictMethod — BINOP reduces an Ord-style interface DEFAULT to its inner
--     same-interface `compare` (innerDefaultMethod) when the head does not impl the
--     method directly; UNOP has no interface-level default to reduce through (every
--     `impl Num`, including Int/Float's own `negate a = 0 - a`, defines `negate`
--     directly), so dictMethod is just `method`.
stampOpRoute : Bool -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> String -> Unit
stampOpRoute isBinop implTable keyTable method tagRef operandMono tag =
  let isBuiltin = if isBinop then
    binopBuiltinHead method tag
  else
    binopPrimitiveHead tag
  if isBuiltin then ()
  else
    let key = fromOption tag (keyForSite keyTable method operandMono)
    let dictMethod = if isBinop then
      if implDefinesMethodAt implTable method tag then method else innerDefaultMethod method
    else
      method
    let reqs = argImplDictRoutesFor implTable dictMethod tag operandMono
    setRef tagRef (RKey key reqs)
-- Gap #21 (CLOSED): element reqs are now forwarded unconditionally on both eval and
-- emit paths.  The impl method `eq`/`compare` carries leading `$dict_*` params
-- (resolveArgStamps runs on both paths); emitter applies them; eval's methodAtNarrow
-- forwards dict reqs (like RDictFwd) truncated to the matched impl's reqCount.

-- The inner same-interface method an interface DEFAULT reduces to, supplying the
-- element `requires` dicts.  Ord's `lt`/`gt`/`lte`/`gte`/`min`/`max` defaults all
-- reduce to `compare`; any other relational/equality method has its own impl so the
-- `implDefinesMethodAt` guard keeps it on `method`, never reaching here.
innerDefaultMethod : String -> String
innerDefaultMethod m =
  if contains m ["lt", "gt", "lte", "gte", "min", "max"] then
    "compare"
  else
    m

implDefinesMethodAt : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Bool
implDefinesMethodAt buckets method tag =
  implDefinesMethodAtGo (bucketOf tag buckets) method tag

implDefinesMethodAtGo : List ImplEntry -> String -> String -> Bool
implDefinesMethodAtGo [] _ _ = False
implDefinesMethodAtGo ((ImplEntry tag2 methodNames _ _ _ _)::rest) method tag
  | tag2 == tag && contains method methodNames = True
  | otherwise = implDefinesMethodAtGo rest method tag

-- ── coherence check (TYPECHECK-AUDIT S3) ───────────────────────────────────
-- Mirror lib/typecheck.ml's check_coherence (Phase 68): reject incoherent USER
-- impl sets at declaration time.  Two impls of the SAME interface whose head
-- types can match the same concrete type (impls_overlap) are unresolvable unless
-- one strictly specializes the other (most-specific-wins).  Genuinely incoherent:
--   • two overlapping impls that are NOT a strict specialization → OverlappingImpls.
-- (Named impls + `@Name` selection + `default impl` were removed 2026-07-11; the
-- newtype-wrapper approach is the answer for a second instance.)
-- Prelude/seeded impls are excluded entirely (the driver passes USER decls only):
-- user impls are meant to override prelude impls (e.g. single-file `impl Ord Int`),
-- so they must not pair with a prelude `impl Ord Int`.  At most ONE error is
-- emitted (mirrors the oracle raising Type_error on the first conflict).

-- a user impl, reduced to what coherence needs: iface + head-arg monos (fresh
-- vars).
-- CohImpl iface head-tys ownerModuleId.  The 3rd field is the id of the module
-- that PHYSICALLY declares this impl ("" for the single-file / entry path, so its
-- goldens stay byte-identical — only the global cross-module check threads a real
-- module id and uses it to name BOTH modules in a conflict).
public export data CohImpl = CohImpl String (List Mono) String

cohImplsOf : Decl -> List CohImpl
cohImplsOf d = cohImplsOfMid "" d

-- module-tagged variant: stamp every harvested CohImpl with [mid].
cohImplsOfMid : String -> Decl -> List CohImpl
cohImplsOfMid mid (DAttrib _ d) = cohImplsOfMid mid d
cohImplsOfMid mid (DImpl { iface, tys, ... }) =
  [CohImpl iface (cohFreshTys tys) mid]
cohImplsOfMid _ _ = []

-- the impl's head args → monos under ONE shared fresh-var map (so a var reused
-- across args, e.g. `Conv a a`, is the SAME fresh tyvar — the oracle shares one
-- tbl across the whole impl head; distinct impls get distinct ids).
cohFreshTys : List Ty -> List Mono
cohFreshTys tys =
  let names = dedup (flatMap tyVarNames tys)
  let tvs = freshTvMap names
  map (fromAstType tvs) tys

-- impls_overlap: do two impl head-arg lists share a common instance?  Every
-- TVar (fresh per impl, so distinct ids) is a unification wildcard with a shared
-- subst — `(List Int)` vs `(List a)` overlap via a:=Int; `a a` vs `Int Bool`
-- does NOT (resolve binds a:=Int, then Int vs Bool fails), mirroring the oracle.
cohOverlap : List Mono -> List Mono -> Bool
cohOverlap xs ys
  | listLen xs == listLen ys = cohOverlapGo (Ref []) (zipL xs ys)
  | otherwise = False

cohOverlapGo : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> List (Mono, Mono) -> Bool
cohOverlapGo _ [] = True
cohOverlapGo subst ((a, b)::rest)
  | cohGo subst a b = cohOverlapGo subst rest
  | otherwise = False

-- resolve a mono through the wildcard subst (chasing bound vars)
cohResolve : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono
cohResolve subst m = match normalize m
  TVar cell => match lookupAssocI (tyvarId cell) subst.value
    Some m2 => cohResolve subst m2
    None => TVar cell
  other => other

cohGo : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohGo subst t1 t2 = cohGoR subst (cohResolve subst t1) (cohResolve subst t2)

cohGoR : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohGoR subst (TVar c1) (TVar c2)
  | tyvarId c1 == tyvarId c2 = True
  | otherwise = cohBind subst (tyvarId c1) (TVar c2)
cohGoR subst (TVar c1) t = cohBind subst (tyvarId c1) t
cohGoR subst t (TVar c2) = cohBind subst (tyvarId c2) t
cohGoR _ (TCon a) (TCon b) = a == b
cohGoR subst (TApp f1 a1) (TApp f2 a2) = cohGo subst f1 f2 && cohGo subst a1 a2
cohGoR subst (TFun a1 _ b1) (TFun a2 _ b2) = cohGo subst a1 a2
  && cohGo subst b1 b2
cohGoR _ (TEff _) (TEff _) = True
cohGoR _ _ _ = False

-- bind id := m in the wildcard subst and return True
cohBind : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Int -> Mono -> Bool
cohBind subst id m =
  let _ = setRef subst ((id, m)::subst.value)
  True

-- subsumes ~general ~specific: is [specific] an instance of [general]?  Only the
-- general side's vars bind (consistently: a var bound twice must see structurally
-- equal types); the specific side's vars are rigid (match only the same id).
cohSubsumes : List Mono -> List Mono -> Bool
cohSubsumes general specific
  | listLen general == listLen specific =
    cohSubGo (Ref []) (zipL general specific)
  | otherwise = False

cohSubGo : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> List (Mono, Mono) -> Bool
cohSubGo _ [] = True
cohSubGo subst ((g, s)::rest)
  | cohSub subst g s = cohSubGo subst rest
  | otherwise = False

cohSub : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohSub subst g s = cohSubR subst (normalize g) (normalize s)

cohSubR : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohSubR subst (TVar c) s = match lookupAssocI (tyvarId c) subst.value
  Some prev => cohEqMono prev s
  None => cohBind subst (tyvarId c) s
cohSubR _ (TCon a) (TCon b) = a == b
cohSubR subst (TApp f1 a1) (TApp f2 a2) = cohSub subst f1 f2
  && cohSub subst a1 a2
cohSubR subst (TFun a1 _ b1) (TFun a2 _ b2) = cohSub subst a1 a2
  && cohSub subst b1 b2
cohSubR _ (TEff _) (TEff _) = True
cohSubR _ _ _ = False

-- rigid structural equality (the specific side's vars match only the same id)
cohEqMono : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohEqMono a b = cohEqR (normalize a) (normalize b)

cohEqR : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohEqR (TVar c1) (TVar c2) = tyvarId c1 == tyvarId c2
cohEqR (TCon a) (TCon b) = a == b
cohEqR (TApp f1 a1) (TApp f2 a2) = cohEqMono f1 f2 && cohEqMono a1 a2
cohEqR (TFun a1 _ b1) (TFun a2 _ b2) = cohEqMono a1 a2 && cohEqMono b1 b2
cohEqR (TEff _) (TEff _) = True
cohEqR _ _ = False

-- [a] strictly more specific than [b]: b subsumes a but not a subsumes b
cohStrictlyMoreSpecific : List Mono -> List Mono -> Bool
cohStrictlyMoreSpecific a b = cohSubsumes b a && not (cohSubsumes a b)

-- render two impl head-arg lists against ONE shared naming ctx (so a var shared
-- across both prints identically and distinct vars never collide) — mirrors the
-- oracle's pp_monos_pair.
cohPpPair : List Mono -> List Mono -> (String, String)
cohPpPair xs ys =
  let ctx = Ref []
  let cnt = Ref 0
  let s1 = joinWith " " (cohPpEach ctx cnt xs)
  let s2 = joinWith " " (cohPpEach ctx cnt ys)
  (s1, s2)

cohPpEach : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> List Mono -> List String
cohPpEach _ _ [] = []
cohPpEach ctx cnt (m::ms) = ppGo ctx cnt 0 m :: cohPpEach ctx cnt ms

-- the coherence error string for one conflicting pair (OverlappingImpls).
cohOverlapMsg : String -> List Mono -> List Mono -> String
cohOverlapMsg iface xs ys = match cohPpPair xs ys
  (s1, s2) => "Overlapping impls of \{iface}: \{s1} and \{s2} can match the same type. Make them disjoint, or wrap one type in a newtype"

-- scan all ordered pairs; return the FIRST conflict message (mirrors the oracle
-- raising on the first incoherent pair).  None ⇒ coherent.
cohFirstConflict : List CohImpl -> Option String
cohFirstConflict [] = None
cohFirstConflict (e::rest) = match cohConflictWith e rest
  Some msg => Some msg
  None => cohFirstConflict rest

cohConflictWith : CohImpl -> List CohImpl -> Option String
cohConflictWith _ [] = None
cohConflictWith (e1@(CohImpl if1 xs mid1)) ((CohImpl if2 ys mid2)::rest)
  | if1 == if2 && cohOverlap xs ys = cohAnonConflict e1 xs ys mid1 mid2 rest
  | otherwise = cohConflictWith e1 rest

-- two overlapping impls: a strict specialization (most-specific-wins) is coherent
-- — it falls through to the rest of the scan.  Otherwise it is an unresolvable
-- overlap (mirrors the oracle's OverlappingImpls).  When the two impls come from
-- DIFFERENT modules (mid1 /= mid2, both non-empty), the message names BOTH owning
-- modules (the cross-module global-coherence case).
cohAnonConflict : CohImpl -> List Mono -> List Mono -> String -> String -> List CohImpl -> Option String
cohAnonConflict e1 xs ys mid1 mid2 rest
  | cohStrictlyMoreSpecific xs ys = cohConflictWith e1 rest
  | cohStrictlyMoreSpecific ys xs = cohConflictWith e1 rest
  | mid1 != "" && mid2 != "" && mid1 != mid2 =
    Some (cohCrossModuleMsg (cohImplIface e1) mid1 mid2)
  | otherwise = Some (cohOverlapMsg (cohImplIface e1) xs ys)

-- cross-module conflict wording: names the interface and BOTH owning modules.
cohCrossModuleMsg : String -> String -> String -> String
cohCrossModuleMsg iface mid1 mid2 =
  "Conflicting `impl \{iface}`. Defined in \{mid2} and \{mid1}"

cohImplIface : CohImpl -> String
cohImplIface (CohImpl i _ _) = i

-- run coherence over USER decls; push the first conflict (if any) into typeErrors.
-- The impls are scanned in REVERSE declaration order to mirror the oracle, whose
-- env.impls is built by prepending each entry (`entry :: !(env.impls)`) — so the
-- conflict message names the later-declared impl as the first head, byte-identical.
checkCoherence : List Decl -> Unit
checkCoherence userDecls = match cohFirstConflict (reverseL (cohCollectImpls userDecls))
  Some msg => pushTypeError "T-CONFLICTING-IMPL" msg
  None => ()

-- ── D1 WS-1a: superinterface-existence gate ────────────────────────────────
-- Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's `check_superinterface_obligations`.  An
-- `impl C T` whose interface declares `interface C a requires D a` is unsound
-- unless a matching `impl D T` also exists.  This post-pass iterates the USER
-- impls (so a user impl that overrides a prelude impl isn't double-flagged) and,
-- for each, looks up its interface's supers in the FULL decl universe
-- ([allDecls], prelude included — the SUPER impl it needs may be a prelude impl).
-- It fires ONLY when the impl head's type args are all CONCRETE/ground (mirrors
-- the oracle's `is_concrete` gate); a parametric head like `impl Monoid (Bag a)`
-- (free tyvar) is deferred exactly as the oracle defers it.  Error wording is
-- byte-identical to the oracle's MissingSuperImpl message.
-- [userDecls] = the impls to check; [allDecls] = prelude + user, for both the
-- interface-supers lookup AND super-impl existence.
checkSuperImpls : List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
checkSuperImpls userDecls allDecls =
  foreachUnit
    (pushTypeError "T-MISSING-SUPER-IMPL")
    (flatMap (superImplMsgsOf allDecls) userDecls)

foreachUnit : (a -> Unit) -> List a -> Unit
foreachUnit _ [] = ()
foreachUnit f (x::xs) =
  let _ = f x
  foreachUnit f xs

-- ── P0-17: impl-completeness check ─────────────────────────────────────────
-- Beta finding P0-17 (qa-beta-2026-07-07): an `impl Iface Ty…` that defines only
-- SOME of the interface's methods used to `check` clean, then PANIC at runtime
-- (`E-PANIC: unbound identifier: <method>`) when a missing method is dispatched —
-- a run≠check hole.  For each impl, verify every REQUIRED interface method (one
-- declared WITHOUT a default body) is defined by the impl body; each missing one
-- is a located `T-INCOMPLETE-IMPL` error, so check/run/build all reject instead of
-- deferring to a runtime panic.
--
-- Runs POST-desugar (from the typecheck cores), which is the ONLY stage where both
-- sets are cleanly visible with defaults distinguishable:
--   • desugar's mergeIfaceDefaults has coalesced each interface method's split
--     signature-entry (`lt : …`, default None) and body-entry (`lt x y = …`,
--     default Some) into ONE `IfaceMethod` carrying the merged Option MethodDefault.
--     PRE-merge the sig-only `None` entry would make every defaulted method (Ord's
--     lt/gt/…) look REQUIRED → hundreds of false positives across core/stdlib.
--   • desugar's fillImplDefaults has synthesized same-module default clauses INTO
--     the impl — which only ADDS to the defined set, never removes a required one,
--     so it cannot mask a genuinely-missing required method.
-- [lookupDecls] (prelude + accumulated modules + this module) resolves the
-- interface decl even when it is imported; [iterDecls] (this module's own decls)
-- are the impls to check.  Unknown interface (not in scope) → skip (a distinct
-- error is reported elsewhere), mirroring superImplMsgsOf.
checkImplCompleteness : List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
checkImplCompleteness lookupDecls iterDecls =
  foreachUnit
    pushIncompleteImpl
    (flatMap (implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls) iterDecls)

pushIncompleteImpl : (Option Loc, String) -> Unit
pushIncompleteImpl (loc, msg) = pushTypeErrorOnceAt "T-INCOMPLETE-IMPL" loc msg

-- the missing-required-method errors for one decl (a DImpl, possibly DAttrib-
-- wrapped); [] for a non-impl decl, an unknown interface, or a complete impl.
implCompletenessMsgsOf : List Decl -> Decl -> List (Option Loc, String)
implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls (DAttrib _ d) =
  implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls d
implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls DImpl { iface, tys, methods, ... } =
  match ifaceRequiredMethods lookupDecls iface
    None => []   -- unknown interface (reported elsewhere) → skip
    Some required =>
      let defined = map implMethodName methods
      let loc = firstImplMethodLoc methods
      map
        (m => (loc, missingImplMethodMsg iface tys m))
        (filter (m => not (contains m defined)) required)
implCompletenessMsgsOf _ _ = []

implMethodName : ImplMethod -> String
implMethodName (ImplMethod n _ _) = n

-- a loc to attribute the error to: the first present impl-method body's span (the
-- impl head carries no Loc of its own).  None for a wholly-empty impl body.
firstImplMethodLoc : List ImplMethod -> Option Loc
firstImplMethodLoc [] = None
firstImplMethodLoc ((ImplMethod _ _ body)::_) = exprLoc body

-- the names of interface [target]'s REQUIRED methods (those whose merged
-- IfaceMethod carries NO default body); None if no such interface is in scope.
ifaceRequiredMethods : List Decl -> String -> Option (List String)
ifaceRequiredMethods [] _ = None
ifaceRequiredMethods ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) target = match ifaceRequiredMethods [d] target
  Some r => Some r
  None => ifaceRequiredMethods rest target
ifaceRequiredMethods ((DInterface { name, methods, ... })::rest) target
  | name == target = Some (requiredMethodNames methods)
  | otherwise = ifaceRequiredMethods rest target
ifaceRequiredMethods (_::rest) target = ifaceRequiredMethods rest target

requiredMethodNames : List IfaceMethod -> List String
requiredMethodNames [] = []
requiredMethodNames ((IfaceMethod n _ None)::rest) =
  n :: requiredMethodNames rest
requiredMethodNames ((IfaceMethod _ _ (Some _))::rest) =
  requiredMethodNames rest

missingImplMethodMsg : String -> List Ty -> String -> String
missingImplMethodMsg iface tys method = "'impl \{iface} \{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom tys)}' is missing method '\{method}'. Every interface method without a default body must be implemented — define '\{method}' in the impl (or check for a typo in a method name)"

-- ── WS-5: phantom interface method detection ──────────────────────────────
-- A method whose declared type mentions NONE of the interface's type parameters
-- (neither in argument position nor in the result) cannot be dispatched: there
-- is no argument or return type to select an impl from.  Reject at check time
-- so the silent mis-dispatch at runtime is caught early.
--
-- Examples:
--   interface Phantom a where
--     name : String          -- PHANTOM: `a` appears nowhere in the type
--   interface Show a where
--     show : a -> String     -- OK: `a` appears in argument position
--   interface Pure f where
--     pure : a -> f a        -- OK: `f` appears in result position
--
-- The check uses the full method type (args AND result via tyVarNames), so
-- return-position methods like `pure : a -> f a` or `minBound : a` are NOT
-- flagged.  Multi-param interfaces: phantom iff ALL params absent.

checkPhantomMethods : List Decl -> Unit
checkPhantomMethods decls =
  foreachUnit
    (pushTypeError "T-PHANTOM-METHOD")
    (flatMap phantomMethodMsgs decls)

phantomMethodMsgs : Decl -> List String
phantomMethodMsgs (DAttrib _ d) = phantomMethodMsgs d
phantomMethodMsgs (DInterface { name, typarams, methods, ... }) =
  flatMap (phantomMethodMsg name typarams) methods
phantomMethodMsgs _ = []

phantomMethodMsg : String -> List String -> IfaceMethod -> List String
phantomMethodMsg ifaceName typarams (IfaceMethod mname mty _) =
  if isEmptyL typarams then
    []
  else if anyMember (tyVarNames mty) typarams then
    []
  else
    [
      "Method '\{mname}' in interface '\{ifaceName}' does not mention interface parameter(s) \{intercalateComma typarams}; cannot dispatch"
    ]

intercalateComma : List String -> String
intercalateComma [] = ""
intercalateComma [x] = "'" ++ x ++ "'"
intercalateComma (x::rest) = "'\{x}', \{intercalateComma rest}"

-- ── WS-4a: cyclic superinterface detection ────────────────────────────────
-- A cyclic `interface A requires B; interface B requires A` is ill-formed: no
-- impl can ever be constructed (each demands the other) and the transitive
-- evidence resolver would diverge.  DFS over the `requires` graph; if any node
-- is re-encountered on the current DFS stack, report the cycle.
-- Runs over ALL interfaces in scope (prelude + user) — a prelude cycle would
-- be equally ill-formed.  The `done` ref tracks fully-explored nodes to avoid
-- redundant traversal and re-reporting multi-root cycles.
checkInterfaceCycles : List Decl -> Unit
checkInterfaceCycles allDecls =
  let ifaceNames = collectIfaceNames allDecls
  let done = Ref []
  foreachUnit (checkOneIfaceCycle allDecls done) ifaceNames

-- #152: the whole-program final-checks tail, shared by all five entry points
-- (checkToLines / checkToLinesWithRuntime / checkErrorsWithRuntime /
-- checkProgramDiags / checkModuleFullDiags).  [cohDecls] feeds coherence AND
-- phantom-methods; [cycDecls] feeds interface-cycles AND checkSuperImpls's second
-- (whole-universe) argument.  ⚠️ trap #5: coherence must see USER decls only, so
-- this helper has NO access to `prog` — the caller decides [cohDecls] explicitly
-- (never a `prog` fallback).  Call ORDER is load-bearing (all push into typeErrors).
runFinalChecks : List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
runFinalChecks cohDecls cycDecls =
  let _ = checkCoherence cohDecls
  let _ = checkInterfaceCycles cycDecls
  let _ = checkPhantomMethods cohDecls
  checkSuperImpls cohDecls cycDecls

checkOneIfaceCycle : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> String -> Unit
checkOneIfaceCycle allDecls done ifaceName =
  if not (contains ifaceName done.value) then
    foreachUnit (pushTypeError "T-CYCLIC-SUPERINTERFACE") (ifaceDfsCycle allDecls done [ifaceName] ifaceName)
  else
    ()

collectIfaceNames : List Decl -> List String
collectIfaceNames [] = []
collectIfaceNames ((DInterface { name, ... })::rest) =
  name :: collectIfaceNames rest
collectIfaceNames ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = collectIfaceNames (d::rest)
collectIfaceNames (_::rest) = collectIfaceNames rest

-- DFS from [current]; [path] is the stack of names visited so far (including
-- [current] as its last element, used to reconstruct the cycle message).
-- Marks [current] as done after all its supers are explored.
ifaceDfsCycle : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> List String -> String -> List String
ifaceDfsCycle allDecls done path current = match ifaceSupersOf allDecls current
  None =>
    let _ = setRef done (current::done.value)
    []
  Some (_, supers) =>
    let msgs = ifaceDfsCycleSupers allDecls done path supers
    let _ = setRef done (current::done.value)
    msgs

ifaceDfsCycleSupers : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> List String -> List Super -> List String
ifaceDfsCycleSupers _ _ _ [] = []
ifaceDfsCycleSupers allDecls done path ((Super superName _)::rest) =
  let msgs1 = ifaceDfsCycleOneSuper allDecls done path superName
  msgs1 ++ ifaceDfsCycleSupers allDecls done path rest

-- check one super edge: cycle if superName is on the DFS path; skip if done; else recurse.
ifaceDfsCycleOneSuper : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> List String -> String -> List String
ifaceDfsCycleOneSuper allDecls done path superName =
  if contains superName path then
    [cyclicSuperIfaceMsg (path ++ [superName])]
  else if contains superName done.value then
    []
  else
    ifaceDfsCycle allDecls done (path ++ [superName]) superName

-- e.g. ["A", "B", "A"] → "cyclic superinterface: A requires B requires A"
cyclicSuperIfaceMsg : List String -> String
cyclicSuperIfaceMsg path = "Cyclic superinterface: "
  ++ joinWith " requires " path

-- the missing-superinterface error messages for one user decl (a DImpl, possibly
-- wrapped in DAttrib); [] for any non-impl decl or when every super impl exists.
superImplMsgsOf : List Decl -> Decl -> List String
superImplMsgsOf allDecls (DAttrib _ d) = superImplMsgsOf allDecls d
superImplMsgsOf allDecls DImpl { iface, tys, ... } =
  match ifaceSupersOf allDecls iface
    None => []   -- unknown interface (already reported elsewhere) → skip
    Some (typarams, supers) =>
      if listLen typarams == listLen tys && allList tyIsConcrete tys then
        flatMap (superMsgFor allDecls iface tys (zipL typarams tys)) supers
      else
        []   -- arity mismatch, or a non-concrete head (deferred, like the oracle)
superImplMsgsOf _ _ = []

-- check one `Super superName params` against the substitution: map each super
-- param name through [subst] to its concrete Ty; if every param mapped, all are
-- concrete, AND no matching `impl superName <concrete>` exists, emit the error.
superMsgFor : List Decl -> String -> List Ty -> List (String, Ty) -> Super -> List String
superMsgFor allDecls iface implTys subst (Super superName params) =
  let concrete = substParams params subst
  if listLen concrete == listLen params && allList tyIsConcrete concrete && not (superImplExists allDecls superName concrete) then
    [missingSuperImplMsg iface implTys superName concrete]
  else
    []

-- map each super-param NAME through [subst] to its Ty, dropping any unmapped (so a
-- short result vs [params] signals an incomplete substitution → caller skips).
substParams : List String -> List (String, Ty) -> List Ty
substParams [] _ = []
substParams (p::rest) subst = match lookupAssoc p subst
  Some t => t :: substParams rest subst
  None => substParams rest subst

-- byte-identical to lib/typecheck.ml's MissingSuperImpl rendering: both the impl
-- head and the super are printed with the same space-joined atom-position args.
missingSuperImplMsg : String -> List Ty -> String -> List Ty -> String
missingSuperImplMsg iface implTys superName superTys = "'impl \{iface} \{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom implTys)}' requires a superinterface 'impl \{superName} \{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom superTys)}', which is missing"

-- the (typarams, supers) of interface [target], scanning DInterface decls; None if
-- no such interface is in scope.
ifaceSupersOf : List Decl -> String -> Option (List String, List Super)
ifaceSupersOf [] _ = None
ifaceSupersOf ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) target = match ifaceSupersOf [d] target
  Some r => Some r
  None => ifaceSupersOf rest target
ifaceSupersOf ((DInterface { pub, def, name, typarams, supers, methods })::rest) target
  | name == target = Some (typarams, supers)
  | otherwise = ifaceSupersOf rest target
ifaceSupersOf (_::rest) target = ifaceSupersOf rest target

-- does any `impl superName <head>` with a head matching [concrete] exist? (head
-- may be parametric — a free tyvar in the impl head matches any concrete arg,
-- mirroring the oracle's mono_matches: pattern=impl head, concrete=super args.)
superImplExists : List Decl -> String -> List Ty -> Bool
superImplExists allDecls superName concrete =
  anyList (implMatchesSuper superName concrete) allDecls

implMatchesSuper : String -> List Ty -> Decl -> Bool
implMatchesSuper superName concrete (DAttrib _ d) =
  implMatchesSuper superName concrete d
implMatchesSuper superName concrete (DImpl { iface, tys, ... }) = iface == superName
  && listLen tys == listLen concrete
  && allList (p => tyMatchesAst (fst p) (snd p)) (zipL tys concrete)
implMatchesSuper _ _ _ = False

-- a Ty is concrete iff it contains no TyVar (mirror of the oracle's is_concrete,
-- which treats effect rows as transparent — TyEffect ignores its row).
tyIsConcrete : Ty -> Bool
tyIsConcrete (TyVar _) = False
tyIsConcrete (TyCon _ _) = True
tyIsConcrete (TyApp a b) = tyIsConcrete a && tyIsConcrete b
tyIsConcrete (TyFun a b) = tyIsConcrete a && tyIsConcrete b
tyIsConcrete (TyTuple ts) = allList tyIsConcrete ts
tyIsConcrete (TyEffect _ _ t) = tyIsConcrete t
tyIsConcrete (TyConstrained _ t) = tyIsConcrete t

-- one-directional structural match (pattern may carry free TyVar wildcards;
-- concrete is fully ground).  Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's mono_matches.
tyMatchesAst : Ty -> Ty -> Bool
tyMatchesAst (TyVar _) _ = True
tyMatchesAst (TyCon a _) (TyCon b _) = a == b
tyMatchesAst (TyApp f1 a1) (TyApp f2 a2) = tyMatchesAst f1 f2
  && tyMatchesAst a1 a2
tyMatchesAst (TyFun a1 b1) (TyFun a2 b2) = tyMatchesAst a1 a2
  && tyMatchesAst b1 b2
tyMatchesAst (TyTuple ts1) (TyTuple ts2) = listLen ts1 == listLen ts2
  && allList (p => tyMatchesAst (fst p) (snd p)) (zipL ts1 ts2)
tyMatchesAst (TyEffect _ _ t1) t2 = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst t1 (TyEffect _ _ t2) = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst (TyConstrained _ t1) t2 = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst t1 (TyConstrained _ t2) = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst _ _ = False

-- effectful concatMap of cohImplsOf (astTypeFresh allocates fresh tyvars → <Mut>)
cohCollectImpls : List Decl -> List CohImpl
cohCollectImpls [] = []
cohCollectImpls (d::rest) = cohImplsOf d ++ cohCollectImpls rest

-- ── D3 / WS-2 part-2: GLOBAL (cross-module) coherence ──────────────────────
-- Per-module coherence (checkCoherence prog) accepts two DIFFERENT modules that
-- each define `impl C T` for the SAME instance — each module is locally coherent,
-- but jointly they are incoherent (silent dispatch ambiguity).  This pass scans
-- the joint impl set over ALL USER modules (prelude EXCLUDED — a user `impl Eq Int`
-- legitimately overrides the prelude one, so prelude impls must NOT be in the set).
-- Each CohImpl carries the id of the module that PHYSICALLY declares it; imports do
-- NOT copy impl decls, so the joint set has exactly one entry per definition and a
-- structural (iface, head-tys) overlap already distinguishes distinct definitions —
-- no occurrence-dedup needed.  A conflict between two distinct modules is reported
-- naming BOTH (cohCrossModuleMsg via cohAnonConflict's mid1/=mid2 branch).
cohCollectModuleImpls : List (String, List Decl) -> List CohImpl
cohCollectModuleImpls [] = []
cohCollectModuleImpls ((mid, prog)::rest) = flatMap (cohImplsOfMid mid) prog
  ++ cohCollectModuleImpls rest

-- the first cross-module coherence conflict (if any) as a bare message — drivers
-- that thread errs as a VALUE (cmEntryCollect) prepend this themselves,
-- rather than relying on the typeErrors ref (cleared per-module by resetState).
globalCoherenceConflict : List (String, List Decl) -> Option String
globalCoherenceConflict modules =
  cohFirstConflict (reverseL (cohCollectModuleImpls modules))

-- the USER decls a driver wants coherence-checked.  Set by single-file drivers
-- (check.mdk / typecheck_main.mdk / check_batch.mdk) to the desugared USER program
-- (NO prelude), so a user impl that overrides a prelude impl is not flagged.  []
-- (the golden/profile drivers and the no-runtime path) ⇒ checkToLines uses its own
-- prelude-free arg; checkToLinesWithRuntime runs no coherence (byte-identical).
coherenceUserDecls : Ref (List Decl)
coherenceUserDecls = Ref []

-- exported so the single-file front-end drivers set the user boundary before check
export setCoherenceUserDecls : List Decl -> Unit
setCoherenceUserDecls ds = setRef coherenceUserDecls ds

-- ── instance-`requires` impl-dict routing (Phase 83/84 single-level) ────────
-- One parametric impl that carries a `requires`: its head tag (the tycon eval
-- narrows by), the methods it defines (to disambiguate which impl a method site
-- picked), the head type pattern (matched against the call's concrete result mono
-- to recover the element type), and its requires constraints.
-- Fields: head tycon tag, defined method names, head type pattern, requires,
-- full impl type-args, and the INTERFACE this impl is for.  The iface field
-- disambiguates same-head impls: one type can carry several `requires`-bearing
-- impls at the same head tycon for DIFFERENT interfaces whose requires differ in
-- shape (e.g. `Applicative (VT e) requires Semigroup e` alongside
-- `Debug (VT e a) requires Debug e, Debug a`).  RKey element-dict routing must
-- pick the entry for the interface being dispatched, not merely the first by tag.
public export data ImplEntry =
  | ImplEntry String (List String) Ty (List Require) (List Ty) String

-- #148: the impl/key tables are keyed by head tycon TAG into a bucket of entries
-- kept in FORWARD declaration order (append; never re-sorted).  Every per-site
-- lookup filters `tag2 == tag` first, so it scans ONE bucket instead of the whole
-- impl universe — turning findImplEntry's O(impls) scan into O(bucket).  The
-- forward intra-bucket order is load-bearing: findImplEntry / implDictRoutesIn /
-- argImplDictRoutesForGo are FIRST-MATCH, so the earlier-declared of two coherent
-- overlapping requires-bearing siblings at the same head must still win.  (This is
-- NOT the coherence structure — cohFirstConflict scans a separate reverseL-ed list;
-- these buckets keep forward order and coherence is not routed through them.)
export type ImplBuckets = OrdMap (List ImplEntry)
export type KeyBuckets = OrdMap (List KeyEntry)

-- the entries at head tycon [tag] (empty when no impl heads there), in declaration
-- order.  Shared by both bucket flavours (List ImplEntry / List KeyEntry).
bucketOf : String -> OrdMap (List a) -> List a
bucketOf tag buckets = fromOption [] (omLookup tag buckets)

-- build the table from the program's impls-with-requires (others need no dicts),
-- bucketed by head tycon tag with each bucket in forward declaration order.
buildImplTable : List Decl -> ImplBuckets
buildImplTable prog = bucketImplEntries (flatMap implEntryOf prog) omEmpty

-- fold the flat, forward-ordered entry list into tag buckets, APPENDING so each
-- bucket preserves declaration order (first-match semantics).
bucketImplEntries : List ImplEntry -> ImplBuckets -> ImplBuckets
bucketImplEntries [] acc = acc
bucketImplEntries ((ImplEntry tag ms hty reqs itys ifn)::rest) acc =
  let e = ImplEntry tag ms hty reqs itys ifn
  bucketImplEntries rest (omInsert tag (bucketOf tag acc ++ [e]) acc)

implEntryOf : Decl -> List ImplEntry
implEntryOf (DAttrib _ d) = implEntryOf d
implEntryOf (DImpl { iface, tys, reqs, methods, ... }) = match reqs
  [] => []
  _ => implEntryFromTys iface tys reqs methods
implEntryOf _ = []

implEntryFromTys : String -> List Ty -> List Require -> List ImplMethod -> List ImplEntry
implEntryFromTys _ [] _ _ = []
implEntryFromTys iface (headTy::rest) reqs methods = match headTyconTy headTy
  Some tag => [
    ImplEntry tag (map implMethodNameTc methods) headTy reqs (headTy::rest) iface
  ]
  None => []

implMethodNameTc : ImplMethod -> String
implMethodNameTc (ImplMethod n _ _) = n

-- ── TYPECHECK-AUDIT C7: canonical-impl-key registry ────────────────────────
-- One entry per USER impl (regardless of `requires`): the methods it defines (to
-- match a method site), its head tycon (to detect a head-tag collision), its
-- head-arg type pattern (matched against the site's concrete result mono), and its
-- canonical impl key.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's `matching_impls` + `entry.impl_key`.
-- The route normally keeps the bare head tycon (the established head-tag dispatch
-- the native Core-IR/LLVM backend keys symbols by); it is UPGRADED to the full
-- canonical key ONLY when two impls share that head tycon for the method (the C7
-- case, e.g. Pair Int Bool vs Pair Bool Int).  This keeps every single-impl-per-head
-- site — i.e. all existing code and the native backend — byte-identical, while
-- letting eval's narrowMethod pick the right VTypedImpl by its key field when the
-- heads genuinely collide.
public export data KeyEntry = KeyEntry (List String) String Ty String

buildKeyTable : List Decl -> KeyBuckets
buildKeyTable prog = bucketKeyEntries (flatMap keyEntryOf prog) omEmpty

-- fold the flat, forward-ordered entry list into tag buckets (declaration order).
bucketKeyEntries : List KeyEntry -> KeyBuckets -> KeyBuckets
bucketKeyEntries [] acc = acc
bucketKeyEntries ((KeyEntry ms tag hty key)::rest) acc =
  let e = KeyEntry ms tag hty key
  bucketKeyEntries rest (omInsert tag (bucketOf tag acc ++ [e]) acc)

keyEntryOf : Decl -> List KeyEntry
keyEntryOf (DAttrib _ d) = keyEntryOf d
keyEntryOf (DImpl { iface, tys, methods, ... }) = match tys
  headTy::_ => match headTyconTy headTy
    Some tag => [KeyEntry (map implMethodNameTc methods) tag headTy (implKeyTc iface tys)]
    None => []
  [] => []
keyEntryOf _ = []

-- the canonical impl key, byte-identical to eval.mdk's implKeyOf: `iface ++ "|" ++
-- <prec-2 args> ++ "|"` (the trailing empty name-component is retained so keys stay
-- byte-identical to the pre-removal format).
implKeyTc : String -> List Ty -> String
implKeyTc iface tys = "\{iface}|\{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom tys)}|"

-- the canonical key to stamp for the impl of [name] matching the ground result
-- mono — but ONLY when another impl of the same method shares its head tycon (so
-- bare-head routing is ambiguous).  No collision (the common case) or no match →
-- None → the caller keeps the head tag, behaviour unchanged.
keyForSite : KeyBuckets -> String -> Mono -> Option String
keyForSite table name resultMono = match matchedEntry table name resultMono
  Some (KeyEntry _ tag _ key) =>
    if headCollides table name tag then
      Some key
    else
      None
  None => None

-- the entry whose method set contains [name] and whose head pattern matches the
-- concrete result mono (the impl the checker dispatches into at this site).  When
-- SEVERAL entries' head patterns match (overlapping instances, e.g. `Def (List a)`
-- and `Def (List Int)` both matching the ground result `List Int`), pick the
-- MOST-SPECIFIC one — the entry that is strictly more specific than (or equal to)
-- every other matching entry (WS-3/D4 return-position most-specific-wins).  No
-- unique most-specific (equal heads or incomparable partial overlap) → keep the
-- FIRST match, behaviour unchanged.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
-- `strictly_more_specific` selection at a call site.
matchedEntry : KeyBuckets -> String -> Mono -> Option KeyEntry
matchedEntry table name resultMono =
  pickMostSpecificEntry (matchingEntries table name resultMono)

-- all entries defining [name] whose head pattern matches [resultMono], in
-- declaration order.  #148: matchTyMono can only succeed when the entry's head
-- tycon equals [resultMono]'s spine head (a KeyEntry head is never a bare TyVar —
-- keyEntryOf only emits one when headTyconTy is Some), so scanning ONLY the bucket
-- at `monoHeadCon resultMono` collects exactly the same entries in the same order
-- as the old whole-table scan.  A headless mono (TVar/TFun) matched no entry then
-- and keys no bucket now → [].
matchingEntries : KeyBuckets -> String -> Mono -> List KeyEntry
matchingEntries buckets name resultMono = match monoHeadCon resultMono
  Some tag => matchingEntriesGo (bucketOf tag buckets) name resultMono
  None => []

matchingEntriesGo : List KeyEntry -> String -> Mono -> List KeyEntry
matchingEntriesGo [] _ _ = []
matchingEntriesGo ((KeyEntry methodNames tag headTy key)::rest) name resultMono
  | contains name methodNames = match matchTyMono headTy resultMono
    Some _ => KeyEntry methodNames tag headTy key :: matchingEntriesGo rest name resultMono
    None => matchingEntriesGo rest name resultMono
  | otherwise = matchingEntriesGo rest name resultMono

-- pick the entry strictly more specific than every OTHER matching entry; if no
-- such unique entry exists (≤1 match, equal heads, or incomparable overlap) keep
-- the head of the list (the pre-existing first-match behaviour).
pickMostSpecificEntry : List KeyEntry -> Option KeyEntry
pickMostSpecificEntry [] = None
pickMostSpecificEntry (e::rest) = match findMostSpecificEntry (e::rest) (e::rest)
  Some best => Some best
  None => Some e

findMostSpecificEntry : List KeyEntry -> List KeyEntry -> Option KeyEntry
findMostSpecificEntry [] _ = None
findMostSpecificEntry (c::cs) all
  | entryCoversAllOthers c all = Some c
  | otherwise = findMostSpecificEntry cs all

-- is [cand]'s head pattern more specific than (or structurally equal to) every
-- entry in [all]?  Equal-head entries are covered trivially; a genuinely more-
-- general entry is NOT covered, so [cand] is rejected for it.
entryCoversAllOthers : KeyEntry -> List KeyEntry -> Bool
entryCoversAllOthers cand all = allList (entryCovers cand) all

entryCovers : KeyEntry -> KeyEntry -> Bool
entryCovers (KeyEntry _ _ candTy _) (KeyEntry _ _ otherTy _) = tyHeadEq candTy otherTy
  || tyStrictlyMoreSpecific candTy otherTy

-- the two entries are the SAME head pattern (structurally equal up to var
-- renaming) — no ordering needed, the candidate trivially covers it.
tyHeadEq : Ty -> Ty -> Bool
tyHeadEq a b = tySubsumes a b && tySubsumes b a

-- [a] is STRICTLY more specific than [b]: [b] subsumes [a] but not vice versa.
tyStrictlyMoreSpecific : Ty -> Ty -> Bool
tyStrictlyMoreSpecific a b = tySubsumes b a && not (tySubsumes a b)

-- is [specific] an instance of [general]?  Only the GENERAL side's TyVars act as
-- wildcards (bound consistently — bound twice must see structurally equal types);
-- the SPECIFIC side's TyVars are rigid and match only the same name.  Mirrors
-- lib/typecheck.ml's `subsumes`.
tySubsumes : Ty -> Ty -> Bool
tySubsumes general specific = match tySubsumeGo general specific (Some [])
  Some _ => True
  None => False

tySubsumeGo : Ty -> Ty -> Option (List (String, Ty)) -> Option (List (String, Ty))
tySubsumeGo _ _ None = None
tySubsumeGo (TyVar gn) s (Some sub) = match lookupAssoc gn sub
  Some prev => if tyStructEq prev s then Some sub else None
  None => Some ((gn, s)::sub)
tySubsumeGo (TyCon a _) (TyCon b _) acc = if a == b then acc else None
tySubsumeGo (TyCon a _) _ acc = None
tySubsumeGo (TyApp f1 a1) (TyApp f2 a2) acc =
  tySubsumeGo a1 a2 (tySubsumeGo f1 f2 acc)
tySubsumeGo (TyApp f1 a1) _ acc = None
tySubsumeGo (TyFun a1 b1) (TyFun a2 b2) acc =
  tySubsumeGo b1 b2 (tySubsumeGo a1 a2 acc)
tySubsumeGo (TyFun a1 b1) _ acc = None
tySubsumeGo (TyTuple ts1) (TyTuple ts2) acc =
  if listLen ts1 == listLen ts2 then
    tySubsumeGoList ts1 ts2 acc
  else
    None
tySubsumeGo (TyTuple ts1) _ acc = None
tySubsumeGo (TyEffect _ _ t1) s acc = tySubsumeGo t1 (stripTyWrap s) acc
tySubsumeGo (TyConstrained _ t1) s acc = tySubsumeGo t1 (stripTyWrap s) acc

tySubsumeGoList : List Ty -> List Ty -> Option (List (String, Ty)) -> Option (List (String, Ty))
tySubsumeGoList [] [] acc = acc
tySubsumeGoList (t1::ts1) (t2::ts2) acc =
  tySubsumeGoList ts1 ts2 (tySubsumeGo t1 t2 acc)
tySubsumeGoList _ _ _ = None

stripTyWrap : Ty -> Ty
stripTyWrap (TyEffect _ _ t) = stripTyWrap t
stripTyWrap (TyConstrained _ t) = stripTyWrap t
stripTyWrap t = t

-- structural equality of two AST type patterns (rigid; TyVar names must match).
tyStructEq : Ty -> Ty -> Bool
tyStructEq (TyCon a _) (TyCon b _) = a == b
tyStructEq (TyVar a) (TyVar b) = a == b
tyStructEq (TyApp f1 a1) (TyApp f2 a2) = tyStructEq f1 f2 && tyStructEq a1 a2
tyStructEq (TyFun a1 b1) (TyFun a2 b2) = tyStructEq a1 a2 && tyStructEq b1 b2
tyStructEq (TyTuple ts1) (TyTuple ts2) = tyStructEqList ts1 ts2
tyStructEq (TyEffect _ _ t1) (TyEffect _ _ t2) = tyStructEq t1 t2
tyStructEq (TyConstrained _ t1) (TyConstrained _ t2) = tyStructEq t1 t2
tyStructEq _ _ = False

tyStructEqList : List Ty -> List Ty -> Bool
tyStructEqList [] [] = True
tyStructEqList (t1::ts1) (t2::ts2) = tyStructEq t1 t2 && tyStructEqList ts1 ts2
tyStructEqList _ _ = False

-- do two distinct impls of [name] share head tycon [tag]?  (≥2 entries that
-- define [name] at the same head tycon.)
headCollides : KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Bool
headCollides table name tag = countHead table name tag > 1

countHead : KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Int
countHead buckets name tag = countHeadGo (bucketOf tag buckets) name tag

countHeadGo : List KeyEntry -> String -> String -> Int
countHeadGo [] _ _ = 0
countHeadGo ((KeyEntry methodNames tag2 _ _)::rest) name tag
  | contains name methodNames && tag2 == tag = 1 + countHeadGo rest name tag
  | otherwise = countHeadGo rest name tag

-- the head type constructor of an (unelaborated) AST type
headTyconTy : Ty -> Option String
headTyconTy (TyCon n _) = Some n
headTyconTy (TyApp a _) = headTyconTy a
headTyconTy (TyTuple ts) = Some (tupleHeadTagTc (listLen ts))
headTyconTy _ = None

-- Multi-param-interface variant: [paramMonos] is the FULL list of the interface's
-- type-parameter monos at this occurrence (recovered by ifaceParamMonos), e.g. for
-- `Set { 3,1,2 } : Set _a` the `FromEntries c e` occurrence has c↦Set _a, e↦Int.
-- The single-param result-only path lost the element grounding (`e`): matching the
-- impl head `Set a` against the result `Set _a` only binds a↦_a (a free var), so the
-- `requires Ord a` route fell to RNone → a null Ord dict → SIGSEGV.  Matching the
-- impl's FULL `implTys` (`[Set a, a]`) against ALL the param monos recovers a↦Int from
-- the second param, exactly as the OCaml oracle's impl_head_subst keys off the full
-- concrete_args (the interface's param_vars), not just the result.
implDictRoutesForFull : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Mono -> List Route
implDictRoutesForFull implTable name tag resultMono paramMonos =
  implDictRoutesIn
    implTable
    (bucketOf tag implTable)
    name
    tag
    resultMono
    paramMonos

-- [fullTable] is the whole bucketed table (for the nested implReqRoutes recursion,
-- which re-buckets by the requires' own head); the scanned second arg is the [tag]
-- bucket only.
implDictRoutesIn : ImplBuckets -> List ImplEntry -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Mono -> List Route
implDictRoutesIn _ [] _ _ _ _ = []
implDictRoutesIn fullTable ((ImplEntry tag2 methodNames headTy reqs implTys _)::rest) name tag resultMono paramMonos
  | tag2 == tag && contains name methodNames = match headSubstWithParams headTy implTys resultMono paramMonos
    Some subst => implReqRoutes fullTable subst reqs
    None => implDictRoutesIn fullTable rest name tag resultMono paramMonos
  | otherwise = implDictRoutesIn fullTable rest name tag resultMono paramMonos

-- Build the impl head substitution.  Always match the impl HEAD (`Set a`) against
-- the result mono (the historical single-param behaviour, byte-identical when no
-- extra param info is supplied).  THEN, when the caller has the interface's FULL
-- param monos (`[Set _a, Int]` for a multi-param interface) and they line up with
-- the impl's full type-arg patterns (`[Set a, a]`), additionally match the trailing
-- patterns against the trailing param monos and merge — preferring a concrete head
-- (Int) over a free var (_a) on a key collision.  This recovers the element grounding
-- (`a ↦ Int`) the head-only match lost.  When the param monos don't line up (a plain
-- result-only call, or a shape mismatch) the head match alone stands.
headSubstWithParams : Ty -> List Ty -> Mono -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
headSubstWithParams headTy implTys resultMono paramMonos = map
  (headSub => augmentWithParams headSub implTys paramMonos)
  (matchTyMono headTy resultMono)

-- When the impl's full type-arg patterns line up with the interface's full param
-- monos (a multi-param interface), fold the trailing element groundings into the
-- head subst (concrete-wins); otherwise return the head subst unchanged.
augmentWithParams : List (String, Mono) -> List Ty -> List Mono -> List (String, Mono)
augmentWithParams headSub implTys paramMonos
  | listLen implTys == listLen paramMonos && listLen paramMonos > 1 = match implHeadSubst implTys paramMonos
    Some full => mergeSubstPreferConcrete headSub full
    None => headSub
  | otherwise = headSub

-- Match the impl's full type-arg patterns [pats] (`[Set a, a]`) against the
-- interface's concrete param monos [concretes] (`[Set _a, Int]`), accumulating a
-- name→mono subst.  When the SAME impl var appears in several positions (here `a`
-- is both the head element and the standalone element param), prefer the binding
-- whose mono has a CONCRETE head tycon (Int) over a still-free var (_a) — that is
-- what recovers the element grounding the result-only match missed.  Mirrors the
-- oracle's impl_head_subst (Hashtbl.replace last-wins, with concrete winning here).
-- A length mismatch / structural failure → None (no match, try the next impl), so a
-- single-param interface ([resultMono] only) behaves exactly as the old matchTyMono.
implHeadSubst : List Ty -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
implHeadSubst pats concretes
  | listLen pats != listLen concretes = None
  | otherwise = implHeadSubstGo pats concretes (Some [])

implHeadSubstGo : List Ty -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono)) -> Option (List (String, Mono))
implHeadSubstGo [] [] acc = acc
implHeadSubstGo (p::ps) (c::cs) None = None
implHeadSubstGo (p::ps) (c::cs) (Some sofar) = match matchTyMono p c
  None => None
  Some s => implHeadSubstGo ps cs (Some (mergeSubstPreferConcrete sofar s))
implHeadSubstGo _ _ _ = None

-- Merge two name→mono substs; on a key collision keep whichever value has a
-- concrete head tycon (so the grounded element wins over a free result var).
mergeSubstPreferConcrete : List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Mono)
mergeSubstPreferConcrete acc [] = acc
mergeSubstPreferConcrete acc ((k, v)::rest) =
  mergeSubstPreferConcrete (mergeOne acc k v) rest

mergeOne : List (String, Mono) -> String -> Mono -> List (String, Mono)
mergeOne [] k v = [(k, v)]
mergeOne ((k2, v2)::rest) k v
  | k2 == k = (k2, preferConcrete v2 v)::rest
  | otherwise = (k2, v2) :: mergeOne rest k v

preferConcrete : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
preferConcrete a b = match headTyconMono a
  Some _ => a
  None => b

-- match an impl-head type PATTERN against a concrete mono, binding the impl's
-- type-variable names to monos (single-level: the pattern's args are bare vars).
matchTyMono : Ty -> Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
matchTyMono (TyVar n) m = Some [(n, m)]
matchTyMono (TyCon n _) m = match normalize m
  TCon n2 => if n == n2 then Some [] else None
  _ => None
matchTyMono (TyApp a b) m = match normalize m
  TApp ma mb => combineSubst (matchTyMono a ma) (matchTyMono b mb)
  _ => None
-- Native Gap C: a tuple impl head (`(a, b)`) matches a same-arity tuple mono,
-- recovering one element-var subst per position — so a tuple impl's per-element
-- `requires` (Eq a, Eq b, …) recover their element-dict routes, exactly as the
-- TyApp arm does for parametric ADTs.  Mismatched arity → None (distinct groups).
matchTyMono (TyTuple ts) m = match tupleSpine m
  Some ms => if listLen ts == listLen ms then matchTyMonoList ts ms else None
  _ => None
-- Function-type arm: recover typaram bindings from BOTH a method's argument and
-- result positions (used by ifaceParamMonos to ground a multi-param interface's
-- element param `e` from the argument `List e`).  Effects are ignored.  Wrapper
-- nodes (effect/constrained annotations on the declared Ty) are stripped first.
matchTyMono (TyFun a b) m = match normalize m
  TFun ma _ mb => combineSubst (matchTyMono a ma) (matchTyMono (stripEff b) mb)
  _ => None
matchTyMono (TyEffect _ _ t) m = matchTyMono t m
matchTyMono (TyConstrained _ t) m = matchTyMono t m
matchTyMono _ _ = None

-- zip-match element-type patterns against element monos, combining the substs.
matchTyMonoList : List Ty -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
matchTyMonoList [] [] = Some []
matchTyMonoList (t::ts) (m::ms) =
  combineSubst (matchTyMono t m) (matchTyMonoList ts ms)
matchTyMonoList _ _ = None

combineSubst : Option (List (String, Mono)) -> Option (List (String, Mono)) -> Option (List (String, Mono))
combineSubst (Some xs) (Some ys) = Some (xs ++ ys)
combineSubst _ _ = None

-- one route per requires: substitute the recovered element subst into the
-- requires' dispatch arg, take its head tycon (the dict tag eval narrows by),
-- and recursively compute that impl's own requires routes (Phase 83/84 #5).
implReqRoutes : ImplBuckets -> List (String, Mono) -> List Require -> List Route
implReqRoutes _ _ [] = []
implReqRoutes implTable subst ((Require rIface rargs)::rest) =
  reqRoute implTable rIface subst rargs :: implReqRoutes implTable subst rest

-- depth-limited variants used by implRequiresRoutesRec (WS-4b).
implReqRoutesD : ImplBuckets -> List (String, Mono) -> List Require -> Int -> List Route
implReqRoutesD _ _ [] _ = []
implReqRoutesD implTable subst ((Require rIface rargs)::rest) depth =
  reqRouteD implTable rIface subst rargs depth ::
    implReqRoutesD implTable subst rest depth

reqRouteD : ImplBuckets -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List Ty -> Int -> Route
reqRouteD _ _ _ [] _ = RNone
reqRouteD implTable iface subst (arg::_) depth =
  let m = fromAstType subst arg
  match headTyconMono m
    Some tag => RKey tag (implRequiresRoutesRecD implTable iface tag m depth)
    None => RNone

reqRoute : ImplBuckets -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List Ty -> Route
reqRoute _ _ _ [] = RNone
reqRoute implTable iface subst (arg::_) =
  let m = fromAstType subst arg
  match headTyconMono m
    Some tag => RKey tag (implRequiresRoutesRec implTable iface tag m)
    None => RNone

-- Phase 83/84 #5: given the concrete mono [m] whose head tycon is [tag], find the
-- matching parametric impl in [implTable] and compute its requires routes
-- recursively — so RKey(tag, [RKey(subTag, [...])]) encodes the full element-dict
-- nesting.  Terminates because each step structurally shrinks the requirement type.
-- WS-4b: depth fuse (max 32 levels) guards against non-shrinking impl contexts
-- like `impl C (T a) requires C (T (T a))` that would otherwise diverge here at
-- route-resolution time.  Depth 32 is ample for valid programs (real nesting rarely
-- exceeds 3–4 levels); a pathological impl is ill-formed and checkInterfaceCycles
-- won't catch it (it's an impl-requires cycle, not a superinterface cycle), so the
-- fuse is the primary guard.  On limit-hit we return [] (no routes), which means
-- the nested element dict resolves to RNone → the emit path emits a null cell;
-- this is safe because the impl itself is ill-formed and will never produce a
-- valid concrete instantiation in a well-typed program.
implRequiresRoutesRec : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Route
implRequiresRoutesRec implTable iface tag m =
  implRequiresRoutesRecD implTable iface tag m 0

-- depth-limited core of implRequiresRoutesRec (WS-4b: max 32 levels).
implRequiresRoutesRecD : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> Int -> List Route
implRequiresRoutesRecD implTable iface tag m depth =
  if depth >= 32 then []
  else match findImplEntry implTable iface tag m
    Some (ImplEntry _ _ headTy reqs _ _) => match matchTyMono headTy m
      Some subst => implReqRoutesD implTable subst reqs (depth + 1)
      None => []
    None => []

-- find the ImplEntry in [implTable] for interface [iface] whose head tycon is
-- [tag] and whose head pattern matches the concrete mono [m].  A type may carry
-- SEVERAL `requires`-bearing impls at one head tycon for different interfaces
-- whose requires differ in shape (arity/parameters); keying on the tag alone
-- picked the first such impl and produced the WRONG element-dict routes (e.g.
-- `Applicative (VT e) requires Semigroup e` stealing dispatch from
-- `Debug (VT e a) requires Debug e, Debug a`).  So we filter by [iface] too.
-- [iface] == "" means "no interface known" (the method-level path) → fall back to
-- the historical first-by-tag behaviour.  The head-pattern match against [m] is
-- also required so a same-iface but wrong-arity head (e.g. `VT e` vs `VT e a`) is
-- skipped rather than matched-then-dropped.
findImplEntry : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> Option ImplEntry
findImplEntry buckets iface tag m =
  findImplEntryGo (bucketOf tag buckets) iface tag m

findImplEntryGo : List ImplEntry -> String -> String -> Mono -> Option ImplEntry
findImplEntryGo [] _ _ _ = None
findImplEntryGo ((ImplEntry tag2 ms hty reqs itys ifn)::rest) iface tag m
  | tag2 == tag && (iface == "" || ifn == iface) = match matchTyMono hty m
    Some _ => Some (ImplEntry tag2 ms hty reqs itys ifn)
    None => findImplEntryGo rest iface tag m
  | otherwise = findImplEntryGo rest iface tag m

-- fill each constrained-fn call site's routes: one route per constraint mono.  A
-- mono pinned to a concrete head → RKey; still a constraint variable (a nested
-- constrained call) → RDict; otherwise RNone.
resolveDictApps : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> List (Ref (List Route), List Mono, List String) -> Unit
resolveDictApps _ _ [] = ()
resolveDictApps prog implTable ((routesRef, monos, ifaces)::rest) =
  let routes = routesOfMonosTop prog implTable monos ifaces
  let _ = setRef routesRef routes
  resolveDictApps prog implTable rest

-- TOP-LEVEL constraint routes (a constrained-fn CALL site, e.g. `roundtrip 10`).
-- Identical to routesOfMonos except for the UNDETERMINED case (the constraint var is
-- pinned by neither an argument nor the result): instead of the nested-recursion
-- RNone (which yields an empty dict → runtime `intToString: not an Int` / a null
-- build dict), look up how many impls of the slot's interface exist:
--   • exactly ONE  → stamp a concrete RKey to that impl's head tag (deterministic
--                    dispatch on both run and build);
--   • two or more  → genuinely ambiguous → pushTypeError AmbiguousImpl (DELIBERATELY
--                    diverges from the frozen oracle's silent first-wins);
--   • zero         → keep RNone (existing no-impl behaviour/error).
-- Kept SEPARATE from routesOfMonos/routeOfMono so the nested element-dict recursion
-- (argReqRoute → routeOfMono) is untouched — a blanket RNone→RKey there would corrupt
-- element-dict routing.
routesOfMonosTop : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> List Mono -> List String -> List Route
routesOfMonosTop _ _ [] _ = []
routesOfMonosTop prog implTable (m::rest) [] =
  routeOfMonoTop prog implTable "" m :: routesOfMonosTop prog implTable rest []
routesOfMonosTop prog implTable (m::rest) (iface::ifacesRest) =
  routeOfMonoTop prog implTable iface m ::
    routesOfMonosTop prog implTable rest ifacesRest

routeOfMonoTop : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> String -> Mono -> Route
routeOfMonoTop prog implTable iface m = match activeDictVarOf m
  Some dname => RDict dname
  None => match headTyconMono m
    Some tag => RKey tag (argImplRequiresRoutesRec implTable iface tag m)
    None => routeUndeterminedTop prog implTable iface

-- the undetermined-constraint resolution: count impls of [iface] in [prog].
routeUndeterminedTop : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> String -> Route
routeUndeterminedTop prog implTable iface
  | iface == "" = RNone
  | otherwise = match implHeadTagsForIface prog iface
    [] => RNone
    [tag] => RKey tag (argImplRequiresRoutesRec implTable iface tag (TCon tag))
    _ => reportAmbiguousImpl iface

reportAmbiguousImpl : String -> Route
reportAmbiguousImpl iface =
  let _ = pushTypeError "T-AMBIGUOUS-INSTANCE" (ambiguousImplMsg iface)
  RNone

-- every impl head tycon for [iface] declared in [prog] (a single-arg interface has
-- one head ty per impl; we read its head tycon).  Dedups so a prelude impl imported
-- twice via multi-module loading doesn't read as ambiguous.
implHeadTagsForIface : List Decl -> String -> List String
implHeadTagsForIface prog iface =
  dedupStr (flatMap (implHeadTagForIface iface) prog)

implHeadTagForIface : String -> Decl -> List String
implHeadTagForIface want (DAttrib _ d) = implHeadTagForIface want d
implHeadTagForIface want (DImpl { iface, tys, ... })
  | iface == want = match tys
    headTy::_ => match headTyconTy headTy
      Some tag => [tag]
      None => []
    [] => []
  | otherwise = []
implHeadTagForIface _ _ = []

dedupStr : List String -> List String
dedupStr xs = dedupStrGo [] xs

dedupStrGo : List String -> List String -> List String
dedupStrGo _ [] = []
dedupStrGo seen (x::rest)
  | contains x seen = dedupStrGo seen rest
  | otherwise = x :: dedupStrGo (x::seen) rest

ambiguousImplMsg : String -> String
ambiguousImplMsg iface = "Ambiguous instance for `"
  ++ iface
  ++ "`. Cannot determine which impl; add a type annotation"

routesOfMonos : ImplBuckets -> List Mono -> List Route
routesOfMonos _ [] = []
routesOfMonos implTable (m::rest) =
  routeOfMono implTable m :: routesOfMonos implTable rest

routeOfMono : ImplBuckets -> Mono -> Route
routeOfMono implTable m = match activeDictVarOf m
  Some dname => RDict dname
  None => match headTyconMono m
    Some tag => RKey tag (argImplRequiresRoutesRec implTable "" tag m)
    None => RNone

-- encl-aware analogue of routeOfMono: when the element mono is one of the enclosing
-- method [encl]'s own constraint vars, route to [encl]'s `$dict_<encl>_<slot>` param
-- (activeDictVarForEncl) rather than the global last-registered name.  Recurses
-- encl-aware so nested element dicts (`List (k, v)` under a `requires Ord k, Ord v`)
-- forward [encl]'s params at every level.  encl == "" ⇒ identical to routeOfMono.
routeOfMonoEncl : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> Route
routeOfMonoEncl implTable iface encl m = match activeDictVarForEncl m encl
  Some dname => RDict dname
  None => match headTyconMono m
    Some tag =>
      RKey tag (argImplRequiresRoutesRecEncl implTable iface encl tag m)
    None => RNone

-- Native #54/#21 residual: the activeDictVar-AWARE analogue of
-- implRequiresRoutesRec, used ONLY by the arg-position routeOfMono recursion.
-- The return-position implRequiresRoutesRec recurses through reqRoute, which is
-- activeDictVar-BLIND (RNone for an abstract var) — correct for the golden return
-- path, but WRONG one level down on the arg path: e.g. a `Debug (Map k v)` impl
-- body's `debug (toList m)` arg-stamps `List (k, v)` → RKey "List" recursing into
-- the tuple `(k, v)` whose element vars `k`/`v` ARE the enclosing impl's
-- `requires Debug k, Debug v` dict params (registered in activeDictVars), so they
-- must route RDict $dict_debug_<slot>, NOT RNone.  Recursing via routeOfMono
-- (not reqRoute) keeps every nesting level activeDictVar-aware, so the emitted
-- element-dict cell carries the live impl dicts instead of a null (0) field — the
-- null field was the SIGSEGV (the inner per-element debug/eq/compare dispatched
-- through a 0 dict).  Kept SEPARATE from implRequiresRoutesRec so the golden
-- return-position routing stays byte-identical.
argImplRequiresRoutesRec : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Route
argImplRequiresRoutesRec implTable iface tag m =
  argImplRequiresRoutesRecEncl implTable iface "" tag m

argImplRequiresRoutesRecEncl : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Route
argImplRequiresRoutesRecEncl implTable iface encl tag m = match findImplEntry implTable iface tag m
  Some (ImplEntry _ _ headTy reqs _ _) => match matchTyMono headTy m
    Some subst => argImplReqRoutes implTable encl subst reqs
    None => []
  None => []

-- ── D3b-1: arg-position element-dict routing ────────────────────────────────
-- The argument-position analogue of implDictRoutesFor.  Same impl-table match +
-- head-pattern unification against the discriminating-argument mono, but each
-- requires route is computed by routeOfMono (activeDictVar-aware): a recursive
-- element of the SAME parametric type (the impl's own element tyvar `a`, still a
-- registered constraint var) forwards the in-scope `$dict_<method>_<slot>` param
-- (RDict); a concrete element (`MyList Int`) materializes a static RKey.  Kept
-- SEPARATE from the return-position reqRoute/implDictRoutesFor chain so that
-- golden path's routing is untouched (this runs only for emit-path arg stamps).
argImplDictRoutesFor : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Route
argImplDictRoutesFor implTable name tag mono =
  argImplDictRoutesForEncl implTable "" name tag mono

-- encl-aware variant: thread the ENCLOSING method [encl] so the recursively-routed
-- element dicts forward [encl]'s own `$dict_<encl>_<slot>` params (not whichever
-- method registered the shared impl tyvar id LAST — see activeDictVarForEncl).  encl
-- == "" recovers the original global routing (binop path, single-method impls).
argImplDictRoutesForEncl : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Route
argImplDictRoutesForEncl implTable encl name tag mono =
  argImplDictRoutesForGo implTable (bucketOf tag implTable) encl name tag mono

-- #21 (G4): element routes (argImplReqRoutes) must search the FULL impl table, not
-- the suffix from the matched outer entry.  The original recursion passed
