    [] => joinNl (schemeLines schemes ++ warns)
    _ => joinNl (typeErrorLines (map teErrMsg errs))
-- B5: seed the exhaustiveness oracle and clear any stale match warnings so the
-- composed check.mdk path surfaces non-exhaustive-match warnings, mirroring the
-- OCaml oracle's `medaka check` output.

-- coherence over the USER decls the driver staged (NO prelude) — empty when the
-- driver staged nothing (golden/profile paths) ⇒ no check, byte-identical.

-- G1 (SOUNDNESS): does the runtime-seeded typecheck pass produce any TYPE ERROR?
-- Mirrors checkToLinesWithRuntime's side-effects EXACTLY (same seeding + coherence
-- over the same staged user decls) but returns a Bool keyed on the typeErrors
-- accumulator, ignoring the success-path scheme dump and the match WARNINGS (which
-- are not errors).  Used by check.mdk's checkHasErrors to gate the CLI exit code
-- WITHOUT altering runCheck's reported stdout.
export checkErrorsWithRuntime : List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> Bool
checkErrorsWithRuntime runtimeDecls coreProg userProg =
  let prog = coreProg ++ userProg
  let _ = setRef implInferEnabled False
  let _ = setRef matchWarnings []
  let _ = setRef matchOracle (buildOracle prog)
  let _ = checkProgramSeededSplit (externSchemes runtimeDecls) coreProg userProg
  let _ = runFinalChecks coherenceUserDecls.value prog
  match typeErrors.value
    [] => False
    _ => True

-- ── type-aware non-exhaustive-match check (the `check_match` driver) ────────
-- Run the full typecheck pass over [prog] (runtime externs seeded, NO prelude —
-- mirrors the reference's check_program_no_prelude oracle) and return one
-- `Warning: non-exhaustive match …` line per `match` that isn't exhaustive.
-- The oracle is built from the program's own data decls + the syntactic builtins
-- (same set check_match's env-backed oracle has in the no-prelude path).  Diffs
-- against `dev/diagdump.exe --check-match` (the harness sorts).
export checkMatchToLines : List Decl -> List Decl -> String
checkMatchToLines runtimeDecls prog =
  let _ = setRef matchWarnings []
  let _ = setRef matchOracle (buildOracle prog)
  let _ = checkProgramSeeded (externSchemes runtimeDecls) prog
  joinNl (reverseL matchWarnings.value)

-- ── diagnostics entry point (compiler/diagnostics.mdk bridge) ────────────
-- Run the full typecheck pass (runtime-seeded) over [prog] and return the raw
-- error strings — each paired with its captured `Option Loc` (B.10.2b) — plus
-- the warning strings (each paired with its captured `Option Loc`, S4), so
-- diagnostics.mdk can wrap them in structured Diag values carrying the span.
export checkProgramDiags : List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> (List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc))
checkProgramDiags runtimeDecls coreProg userProg =
  let prog = coreProg ++ userProg
  let _ = setRef implInferEnabled False
  let _ = setRef matchWarnings []
  let _ = setRef matchWarningLocs []
  let _ = setRef matchOracle (buildOracle prog)
  let _ = checkProgramSeededSplit (externSchemes runtimeDecls) coreProg userProg
  let _ = runFinalChecks coherenceUserDecls.value prog
  (
    reverseL typeErrors.value,
    zipL (reverseL matchWarnings.value) (reverseL matchWarningLocs.value),
  )

-- ── multi-module typecheck (bootstrap front-end) ───────────────────────────
-- Threads per-module exports in dependency order.  The prelude (core) is checked
-- ONCE and ALL its schemes + ctors seed every module (it's implicitly visible);
-- each compiler module then contributes only its PUBLIC value schemes — private
-- helpers stay module-local, so same-named helpers across modules never collide
-- (the per-module-frame property, without a frame) — plus its public ctors.
-- Returns each module's OWN value schemes, in load order.

-- typecheck one program with seeded value schemes + accumulated data decls.
-- Like checkProgramSeeded but ALSO re-registers prior modules' public data decls
-- (`accData ++ prog`) so imported constructors AND named-field-variant record
-- info (e.g. ast's DInterface) resolve.  Returns the module's own value schemes.
checkModuleFull : List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkModuleFull seedVars accData prog =
  checkModuleFullImpl "" seedVars accData [] prog

-- E5: like checkModuleFull but takes [implDecls] (core + every EARLIER module +
-- this module) as the impl-body inference universe.  Under implInferEnabled (the
-- emitArgStampPasses emit path ONLY) it ALSO infers THIS module's parametric impl
-- bodies — mirroring checkProgramSeeded's inferImplBodiesIfEnabled — so their
-- ARG-position ELEMENT dispatch (`eq`/`compare`/… on the impl head tyvar) reaches
-- inferMethodAt and lands in pendingArgStamps, which elabModuleStamp's
-- resolveArgStamps then stamps RDict (via the requires dict registered by
-- registerImplRequires).  [implDecls] gives ifaceMethodTy the interface decls
-- (core's Eq/Ord/…) AND lets a body dispatch against an earlier module's impl.
-- OFF (every golden module driver), this is exactly checkModuleFull pre-E5:
-- implInferEnabled is False → no inference, no extra stamping, byte-identical.
checkModuleFullImpl : String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkModuleFullImpl mid seedVars accData implDecls prog =
  checkBodyImpl seedVars (Module mid accData implDecls) prog
-- C5: compute this module's standalone-shadow set BEFORE inference, so the
-- inferMethodAt arg-position record can gate on it (resetState cleared it via
-- pendingRLocalSites, but standaloneValuesRef is set fresh here per module).
--
-- Two flavours of standalone-shadow, BOTH needing RLocal recording so a method
-- call on a no-impl receiver routes to the standalone instead of mis-dispatching:
--   • IMPORTER shadow (`importerShadows`): a name imported from a sibling that is
--     also an interface method (box importing map's `toList`).  Its scheme must be
--     REBOUND to the interface-method scheme (`shadowMethodSchemes`) so the call
--     site types polymorphically; RLocal then redirects the no-impl receiver back.
--   • DEFINER shadow (`definerShadows`): a name THIS module defines as a top-level
--     funDef that ALSO names an interface method (map-as-root's own `toList` /
--     `isEmpty`).  The MARK pass (`buildStandaloneShadowsGraph`) rewrites these to
--     `EMethodAt`, so without a matching RLocal record the route falls back to the
--     result-type `RKey` (e.g. `toList`'s `List` result), dispatching the method on
--     the WRONG impl (List's `toList = identity`) → `debug`/`display` recurse on the
--     unchanged receiver forever (Map Debug/Eq/Ord impl bodies call standalone
--     `toList`).  These must be RECORDED for RLocal but must NOT be rebound to the
--     method scheme — their local standalone scheme (`Map k v -> …`) is what the
--     impl body types against.

-- E6: re-install the cross-module constraint accumulator that resetState just
-- wiped, so this module's `=>`-constrained call sites into EARLIER modules resolve.
-- Post-ARGSTAMP-UNIFY (Phase 4): eval and emit both thread dicts, so this runs
-- UNCONDITIONALLY on both paths (was `emitArgStampPasses || evalDictLayerActive`, an
-- always-True tautology since evalDictLayerActive = not emitArgStampPasses).

-- resetState wiped methodIfaceParamsRef; re-register it from the FULL impl
-- universe [implDecls] (core/earlier modules ++ this prog) so resolveSite's
-- ifaceParamMonos can recover a multi-param interface's full param monos (the
-- element grounding `e` for `FromEntries c e`).  The flat checkProgramSeeded does
-- the same against its whole-program [prog].  Without it the module path leaves the
-- table empty → ifaceParamMonos returns None → a Set/Map-literal return-position
-- `fromEntries` route drops its `Ord` element dict → null dict → SIGSEGV.

-- C5: a standalone-shadow method name (box's `toList`/`isEmpty`) is bound in
-- seedVars to its imported MONOMORPHIC standalone scheme (`Box k v -> …`), which
-- would shadow the polymorphic interface-method scheme and mis-type a genuine
-- method call (`isEmpty [1,2,3]` unifying List with Box).  Re-bind those names to
-- the graph-wide METHOD scheme so the call site types against the method (oracle
-- behaviour); RLocal then redirects only the no-impl receiver back to the standalone
-- at eval.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml: the EApp method-head arm types against the
-- method scheme even when standalone_values holds the name.
-- DEFINER shadows are EXCLUDED here: the module's OWN funDef (map-root's `toList`)
-- must type against its local standalone scheme (`Map k v -> List (k, v)`), not the
-- imported method scheme — the impl bodies call it ON a Map.  Only IMPORTER shadows
-- (imported-from-sibling method names) get the method-scheme rebind.

-- E5: infer this module's parametric impl bodies (gated, emit path only), seeing
-- [implDecls] for interface method types + cross-module impl dispatch.  The sites
-- it records stay in pendingArgStamps/pendingSites for elabModuleStamp to resolve.

-- C8(b): also infer THIS module's interface DEFAULT bodies (gated identically to
-- the single-file path's inferDefaultBodiesIfEnabled) so a default body's type
-- error is caught on the module path too — mirrors checkProgramSeeded.

-- Phase 4.55 (mirror checkProgramSeeded + lib/typecheck.ml:4254-4262): infer this
-- module's prop bodies so their prepass-marked EDictAt/EMethodAt routes resolve.
-- The multi-module test path (runPropsMulti) evaluates the ELABORATED prop body
-- against the dict-passed env, so the body's calls into the file's own promoted
-- `=>`-constrained fns (set's `wellFormed`/`fromList`) need their dict-arg routes
-- filled here — otherwise the call under-applies the dict-passed callee → not VBool
-- → every prop "fails".  funDefs ignores DProp, so without this the module path
-- never typed props at all.

-- E6: fold THIS module's freshly-registered fn-constraints into the cross-module
-- accumulator so a LATER module's call site finds them after its own reset.
-- Post-ARGSTAMP-UNIFY (Phase 4): unconditional on both paths (was the always-True
-- `emitArgStampPasses || evalDictLayerActive`).

-- Shape A (gap #44): ground a container literal's free result-element var THROUGH
-- the unique impl's method type, so this module's pending sites/arg-stamps resolve
-- against the GROUNDED element (Set Int, not Set _a).  [implDecls] is the full impl
-- universe (core ++ earlier modules ++ prog), so the Set/Map impls are visible.

-- gate: per-module impl-body inference runs only on the emit path (implInferEnabled).
-- allProg = [implDecls] (interfaces + accumulated impls); rpNames computed over it.
inferModuleImplBodiesIfEnabled : TcEnv -> List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
inferModuleImplBodiesIfEnabled env implDecls prog
  | implInferEnabled.value =
    inferImplBodies env implDecls (returnPosMethodNames implDecls) prog
  | otherwise = ()

-- public top-level value names (the pub flag may sit on the sig, def, or extern).
-- C8(a): a PUBLIC interface ALSO exports its method names so an importer can see
-- their schemes (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's pub_iface_schemes — methods of an
-- `is_pub = true` interface are appended to the exported value schemes).
publicValNames : List Decl -> List String
publicValNames [] = []
publicValNames ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = publicValNames [d] ++ publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DFunDef True n _ _)::rest) = n :: publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DTypeSig True n _)::rest) = n :: publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DExtern True n _)::rest) = n :: publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DInterface { pub = True, methods, ... })::rest) = ifaceMethodNames methods
  ++ publicValNames rest
publicValNames (_::rest) = publicValNames rest

ifaceMethodNames : List IfaceMethod -> List String
ifaceMethodNames [] = []
ifaceMethodNames ((IfaceMethod n _ _)::rest) = n :: ifaceMethodNames rest

-- C5: every interface method name declared anywhere in [decls] (unwrapping DAttrib).
allIfaceMethodNames : List Decl -> List String
allIfaceMethodNames [] = []
allIfaceMethodNames ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = allIfaceMethodNames [d]
  ++ allIfaceMethodNames rest
allIfaceMethodNames ((DInterface { methods, ... })::rest) = ifaceMethodNames methods
  ++ allIfaceMethodNames rest
allIfaceMethodNames (_::rest) = allIfaceMethodNames rest

-- C5: the standalone-shadows-method set for THIS module.  A name qualifies when it
-- is (a) a top-level standalone funDef in the FULL graph [implDecls], (b) an interface
-- method name, and (c) NOT defined in the current module [prog] (a same-module def
-- shadows the method through ordinary scoping, taking the non-RLocal EVar path — only
-- the CROSS-module import is the RLocal case).  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
-- standalone_values ∩ method_iface, minus env.locals.
buildStandaloneShadows : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String
buildStandaloneShadows implDecls prog =
  -- P0-18 (importer residual, check path): scan interface methods across BOTH the
  -- imported universe [implDecls] AND this module [prog] — the shadowed interface may
  -- be defined LOCALLY in the importing module (e.g. `import prov.{size}` where THIS
  -- module declares `interface Sizeable a where size : a -> Int`).  With only
  -- [implDecls] the local interface's `size` was invisible, so the imported standalone
  -- was not recognised as an importer shadow → recordImplObligation fired a spurious
  -- `No impl of Sizeable for Int` on a no-impl receiver (`size 3`) that `run`
  -- correctly resolved to the standalone.  EMIT path is unaffected: there the method
  -- names are bare but the imported funDefs are mangled (`<mid>__size`), so the
  -- intersection stays empty (the emit-side importer routing is recovered separately
  -- via the mark-seeded RLocal route in definerShadowArgHead).
  let methodNames = allIfaceMethodNames (implDecls ++ prog)
  let localNames = map fst (funDefs prog)
  let importedFns = map fst (funDefs implDecls)
  filter
    (n => contains n methodNames && contains n importedFns && not (contains n localNames))
    (dedup importedFns)

-- C5 (DEFINER shadow): the names THIS module declares as a top-level funDef that
-- ALSO name an interface method visible in [implDecls] (the imported prelude/core).
-- E.g. map-as-root defines `toList`/`isEmpty`, which shadow Foldable's methods.  The
-- MARK pass (`buildStandaloneShadowsGraph`) rewrites occurrences of these to
-- `EMethodAt`, so they MUST be in `standaloneValuesRef` for `recordRLocalSite` to
-- record an RLocal candidate — otherwise the route falls back to the method's
-- RESULT-type RKey (`toList`'s `List`) and the method dispatches on the WRONG impl.
-- Unlike importer shadows these keep their LOCAL standalone scheme (NOT rebound to the
-- method scheme), since the impl bodies apply them to the concrete receiver.
buildDefinerShadows : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String
buildDefinerShadows implDecls prog =
  -- A method name that is ALSO bound by a LOCAL pattern anywhere in the module (a
  -- clause/lambda param, a let/match binder) is NOT a definer shadow at that
  -- occurrence: lexical scope resolves it to the local.  Without this guard the
  -- definer-shadow application path (inferDefinerShadowVarApp / inferDefinerShadowApp)
  -- types `applyEq eq x y = eq x y` — where `eq` is a PARAMETER — against the TOP-LEVEL
  -- standalone `eq : Int -> Int -> Int`, ignoring the binder entirely.  Mirrors the
  -- MARK pass's identical exclusion (marker.mdk's shadowRenames `keepNotIn
  -- (localBoundNames prog)`), so check/run/build agree on which occurrences are shadows.
  let methodNames = allIfaceMethodNames implDecls
  let localNames = dedup (map fst (funDefs prog))
  let direct = filter (n => contains n methodNames) localNames
  -- P0-18: on the EMIT path this module's funDefs are MANGLED, so `direct` finds no
  -- shadow.  For each mangled funDef that the mangle map flags as an iface-method
  -- shadow, register the BARE method name — that is the name the mark pass stamped
  -- on the `EMethodAt`, which `definerShadowArgHead`/`resolveRLocalSite` key off.
  let bares = flatMap (n => match lookupAssoc n mangledShadowMapRef.value
    Some bare => [bare]
    None => []) localNames
  removeAllS (localBoundNames prog) (dedup (direct ++ bares))

-- C5: graph-wide standalone-shadow names — every top-level funDef name in [userDecls]
-- (the user modules, NOT core) that is also an interface method name anywhere in
-- [allDecls].  Used by the MARK pass to rewrite such occurrences to EMethodAt (so the
-- route ref exists for resolveRLocalSites to stamp).  Excludes core's own decls as the
-- funDef source so core's elaboration (no standalone shadows there) is untouched.
-- P0-18: recover, per (module, iface-method) pair, the mangled standalone symbol the
-- mangler produced for a definer-shadow funDef.  Forward-constructs `mangledName mid
-- method` (using the KNOWN mid — exact, not lossy prefix-stripping) and checks it
-- against the module's actual funDef names; a hit is a `(mangledSymbol ↦ bareMethod)`
-- entry.  On the un-mangled path no funDef matches → [] → every consumer unchanged.
computeMangledShadowMap : List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, String)
computeMangledShadowMap allDecls units =
  let methodNames = allIfaceMethodNames allDecls
  flatMap (unitMangledShadows methodNames) units

unitMangledShadows : List String -> (String, List Decl) -> List (String, String)
unitMangledShadows methodNames (mid, decls) =
  let fns = dedup (map fst (funDefs decls))
  flatMap
    (m => if contains (mangledName mid m) fns then [(mangledName mid m, m)] else [])
    methodNames

buildStandaloneShadowsGraph : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String
buildStandaloneShadowsGraph allDecls userDecls =
  let methodNames = allIfaceMethodNames allDecls
  let fns = dedup (map fst (funDefs userDecls))
  let direct = filter (n => contains n methodNames) fns
  -- P0-18: on the EMIT path the funDef names are MANGLED (`<mid>__toList`), so the
  -- `direct` name-intersection misses them; recover them via the mangle map so the
  -- prePass marks their (mangled) occurrences `EMethodAt`.  Empty on the un-mangled
  -- path (the map is empty there) → `direct` alone → byte-identical.
  let mangled = filter (n => isSome (lookupAssoc n mangledShadowMapRef.value)) fns
  dedup (direct ++ mangled)

-- `public export data` / `public export record` decls — re-registered in every
-- importing module so their constructors + field layout are in scope.
-- ALSO carries public DInterface (so the multi-module path can register the
-- interface's method→iface-param map for #11 obligation checking — a numeric
-- literal's `fromInt`/`Num` obligation, recorded via recordImplObligation, needs
-- `Num`'s entry in methodIfaceParamsRef), AND public DImpl (so checkImplObligations'
-- implHeadsOf sees core's `impl Num Int` etc. — without it a legitimately Int-typed
-- literal's Num obligation finds no matching impl head and is spuriously rejected).
-- registerAllData and buildOracle read only DData, so the extra DInterface/
-- DImpl entries are inert for them.
publicDataDecls : List Decl -> List Decl
publicDataDecls [] = []
publicDataDecls ((DData VisPublic n ps vs ds)::rest) =
  DData VisPublic n ps vs ds :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls ((d@(DInterface { pub = True, ... }))::rest) =
  d :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls ((d@(DImpl { pub = True, ... }))::rest) =
  d :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls ((d@(DTypeAlias True _ _ _))::rest) = d :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls (_::rest) = publicDataDecls rest

-- WS-1a fix: every impl in scope, EXPORT OR NOT.  Instances are GLOBAL (coherence
-- + dispatch leak across module boundaries regardless of `export`), so the
-- superinterface existence query (checkSuperImpls/superImplExists) must see an
-- imported module's `impl Semigroup T` even when it isn't `export`-marked — else
-- `impl Monoid T` in an importer is falsely rejected for a "missing" super impl.
pickSchemes : List String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
pickSchemes [] _ = []
pickSchemes (n::ns) table = match lookupAssoc n table
  Some s => (n, s) :: pickSchemes ns table
  None => pickSchemes ns table

-- Bug C: for each importer-shadow name, pick from [table] the scheme that is the
-- imported STANDALONE — the one whose receiver (first arrow domain) heads in a
-- CONCRETE type constructor (`Map k v`), NOT the polymorphic interface-method scheme
-- (`t a`) that shares the same name in the seed.  A name with no concrete-domain
-- entry contributes nothing (its only binding is the method scheme; the ordinary
-- dispatch path handles it).
pickStandaloneSchemes : List String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
pickStandaloneSchemes [] _ = []
pickStandaloneSchemes (n::ns) table = match concreteDomainScheme n table
  Some s => (n, s) :: pickStandaloneSchemes ns table
  None => pickStandaloneSchemes ns table

-- the FIRST scheme bound to [n] in [table] whose first arrow domain has a concrete
-- (TCon-headed) receiver — i.e. the standalone rather than the `t a` method scheme.
concreteDomainScheme : String -> List (String, Scheme) -> Option Scheme
concreteDomainScheme _ [] = None
concreteDomainScheme n ((n2, s)::rest) =
  if n == n2 && schemeHasConcreteDomain s then
    Some s
  else
    concreteDomainScheme n rest

-- True iff the scheme's type is `dom -> _` and [dom]'s spine head is a TCon (a
-- concrete receiver), distinguishing the standalone `Map k v -> …` from the method
-- `t a -> …` whose domain head is a quantified tyvar.
schemeHasConcreteDomain : Scheme -> Bool
schemeHasConcreteDomain (Forall _ _ t) = match normalize t
  TFun dom _ _ => monoSpineHeadIsCon dom
  _ => False

monoSpineHeadIsCon : Mono -> Bool
monoSpineHeadIsCon m = match normalize m
  TApp a _ => monoSpineHeadIsCon a
  TCon _ => True
  _ => False

-- ── IMPORT-SCOPED per-module seeding (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml:4513-4543) ────
-- The multi-module drivers must NOT flat-seed the cumulative UNION of every
-- dependency's public schemes into each module's env: omInsert is last-write-wins,
-- so a later-loaded dependency's same-named binding (e.g. `string.reverse :
-- String -> String`) silently overwrites an earlier one (`list.reverse :
-- List a -> List a`) — mis-typing a module that explicitly `import list.{reverse}`.
-- Instead each module's seed is built from ONLY the names its own `DUse` decls
-- import, looked up in the SPECIFIC dependency they name.  The implicit prelude
-- (runtime + core) is always fully in scope and is threaded separately as baseSeed.

-- the bare member names a single `import` form names from its dependency —
-- mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's imported_names match (BEFORE mangle-key resolution):
--   UseGroup (_, ms) → the member names         (import dep.{a, b, c})
--   UseWild _        → ALL of the dep's schemes  (import dep.*)
--   UseName ns       → [last ns] if dotted (import a.b.name), else [] (bare name)
--   UseAlias _       → [] (alias only — no auto-imported value names)
-- `None` ⇒ wildcard (every scheme); `Some names` ⇒ exactly those member names.
-- (ORIGIN, LOCAL) per imported member.  They differ only under a member alias
-- (`import m.{a as b}`): the SCHEME is looked up under the origin `a` in the
-- dependency, and bound into this module's env under the local `b`.
importedBindings : UsePath -> Option (List (String, String))
importedBindings (UseGroup _ ms) = Some (map memberBinding ms)
importedBindings (UseWild _) = None
importedBindings (UseName ns)
  | listLen ns > 1 = Some [selfBinding (lastComponent ns)]
  | otherwise = Some []
-- A module alias enumerates the dependency's schemes, so it cannot be expressed as a
-- fixed member list — importFormSchemes handles it directly.
importedBindings (UseAlias _ _) = Some []

memberBinding : UseMember -> (String, String)
memberBinding m = (useMemberOrigin m, useMemberLocal m)

selfBinding : String -> (String, String)
selfBinding n = (n, n)

-- resolve one imported bare member name to its actual key in a dependency's public
-- schemes.  The golden path keys schemes by the bare name; the EMIT path runs after
-- universal mangling (backend.private_mangle), which rewrites a definition `member`
-- to `<dep>__member` but leaves the importer's `DUse` member name BARE — so the key
-- is the SUFFIX-matching scheme `…__member` (uniquely identified within THIS dep's
-- own schemes; distinct members never share an `__<name>` suffix).  Try the bare
-- key first (golden), then the `__<name>` suffix (mangled emit path).
memberSuffix : String -> String
memberSuffix n = "__" ++ n

-- local suffix test (string.endsWith is not imported here): `s` ends with `suf`.
endsWithStr : String -> String -> Bool
endsWithStr suf s =
  let ls = stringLength s
  let lsuf = stringLength suf
  lsuf <= ls && stringSlice (ls - lsuf) ls s == suf

-- Look the member up by its ORIGIN name, bind it under its LOCAL name.  Without an
-- alias the two coincide and this is the historical behaviour.
--
-- On the MANGLED emit path the bare origin is absent (the dep's keys are `<dep>__name`)
-- AND the reference in this module's body has itself been mangled to `<dep>__name` by
-- backend/private_mangle — so the suffix-matched entries are passed through under their
-- MANGLED keys, which is exactly the key the mangled reference will look up.  (A member
-- alias is erased by the same mangling, so it needs no rebinding there.)
resolveMemberSchemes : (String, String) -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
resolveMemberSchemes (origin, local) depSchemes = match lookupAssoc origin depSchemes
  Some s => [(local, s)]
  None => filter (kv => endsWithStr (memberSuffix origin) (fst kv)) depSchemes

-- `import m as A` binds EVERY value m exports under `A.name` — so it enumerates the
-- dependency's schemes rather than a fixed member list.
--
-- Same golden/mangled split as above, made exact by testing the dep's own mangle prefix
-- (`<sanitized mid>__`) instead of guessing: on the emit path the reference is already
-- `<mid>__name`, so the key must stay mangled; on the golden path the reference is the
-- flat `A.name` that desugar produced, so bind it under that.
aliasSchemes : String -> String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
aliasSchemes mid alias depSchemes = map (aliasSchemeEntry mid alias) depSchemes

aliasSchemeEntry : String -> String -> (String, Scheme) -> (String, Scheme)
aliasSchemeEntry mid alias (k, s) =
  if isMangledFor mid k then
    (k, s)
  else
    (qualifiedLocal alias k, s)

-- does `k` carry the `<sanitized mid>__` prefix the mangler would have given it?
isMangledFor : String -> String -> Bool
isMangledFor mid k =
  let pre = mangledName mid ""
  let lp = stringLength pre
  lp <= stringLength k && stringSlice 0 lp k == pre

-- the (key, scheme) pairs a single `import` form brings into scope, resolving each
-- member to its (possibly mangled) key in the dependency's schemes.
importFormSchemes : String -> UsePath -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
importFormSchemes mid (UseAlias _ alias) depSchemes =
  aliasSchemes mid alias depSchemes
importFormSchemes _ path depSchemes = match importedBindings path
  None => depSchemes
  Some bindings => flatMap (b => resolveMemberSchemes b depSchemes) bindings

-- ── import aliasing: re-key the dict-arity / dict-iface tables ───────────────────
-- Both tables (funConstraintsRef, funConstraintIfacesRef) are keyed by the name a CALL
-- SITE writes.  An alias makes that name differ from the definition's, so without this
-- an aliased constrained callee gets zero dict routes and under-applies.  Generic over
-- the payload so the arity table (List Int) and the iface table (List String) share it.
--
-- [qual] is the (definerMid, name)-keyed table, which is what makes this PRECISE: an
-- entry is copied only when its DEFINER is the very module the alias names.
aliasConstraintEntries : List Decl -> List ((String, String), List a) -> List (String, List a)
aliasConstraintEntries [] _ = []
aliasConstraintEntries ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) qual = aliasConstraintEntries [d] qual
  ++ aliasConstraintEntries rest qual
aliasConstraintEntries ((DUse _ path _)::rest) qual = aliasEntriesFor (usePathModuleId path) path qual
  ++ aliasConstraintEntries rest qual
aliasConstraintEntries (_::rest) qual = aliasConstraintEntries rest qual

aliasEntriesFor : String -> UsePath -> List ((String, String), List a) -> List (String, List a)
-- `import m as A` → every constrained fn m defines is callable as `A.<name>`.
aliasEntriesFor mid (UseAlias _ a) qual = flatMap (moduleAliasEntry mid a) qual
-- `import m.{x as y}` → `y` carries `x`'s dict arity.
aliasEntriesFor mid (UseGroup _ ms) qual =
  flatMap (memberAliasEntry mid qual) ms
aliasEntriesFor _ _ _ = []

-- ── import aliasing of an interface/impl METHOD ─────────────────────────────────
-- A method is GLOBAL-BY-NAME.  Impl coalescing, dispatch routing, the emitter's method
-- tables and the METHOD-side dict-arity tables are every one of them keyed by the
-- method's own name, and there is exactly ONE cell per name — a method is deliberately
-- NOT a per-module binding (eval.mdk's globalCells / coalesceImpls).  So an alias of a
-- method is not a new binding at all: it is a second SPELLING of the same method.
--
-- Resolve it back to the origin name here, once, right after resolve has validated the
-- alias and before ANY marking / route-stamping / dict-arity machinery runs.  Every
-- downstream stage — marker, typecheck route stamping, private_mangle, the emitter, both
-- eval drivers — then sees an ordinary method reference and needs no alias awareness.
--
-- That is also precisely why the METHOD-side dict-arity tables (methodConstraintsRef /
-- crossModuleMethodConstraintsQualRef / scopeMethodArities) need no alias re-keying the
-- way funConstraintsRef did: by the time they are built, no aliased method name survives.
-- The standalone `=>`-constrained functions are the opposite case — they ARE per-module
-- bindings that keep their local name, which is why they needed the re-keying.
--
-- Caveat, shared with the marker's own shadowRename: the rewrite is scope-blind, so a
-- LOCAL binder whose name equals an alias local would also be rewritten.  An alias local
-- is chosen by the importing file, so this is a self-inflicted collision.
-- The rewrite has TWO halves, and they must agree: rewriting only the references would
-- leave them bound to nothing.
--   (a) every reference to an aliased method  → the origin method name
--   (b) the module's own `DUse` decls          → import that method under its ORIGIN name
-- With (b) in place, the import seed (typecheck), the import frame (eval) and the
-- rename map (private_mangle) all bind the origin name by their ORDINARY un-aliased
-- paths — none of them needs to learn anything about aliases.
renameAliasedMethods : List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, List Decl)
renameAliasedMethods coreDecls modules =
  let coreEntry = ("core", declMethodNames coreDecls)
  let methodsPerMid = coreEntry :: methodsPerMidGo [coreEntry] modules
  map (renameAliasedMethodsIn methodsPerMid) modules

-- A module's method set is what it DECLARES *plus what it RE-EXPORTS*, folded in
-- dependency-first (loader) order so a re-export resolves against its source's
-- already-computed set (and a >1-hop chain still resolves).
--
-- The `++ reexportedMethodNames` half closes a run≠build divergence that predates
-- core re-exports and is NOT core-specific: with a module's set limited to its own
-- `declMethodNames`, a module that re-exports a method did not list it, so at an
-- importer's `import reExporter.{method as alias}` neither half of the rewrite fired
-- — the alias was not dropped from the DUse (b) and references were not renamed to the
-- origin (a).  `run` still worked (eval's import frame binds the alias to the origin's
-- cell), but a method is global-by-name with no per-module symbol, so EMIT had nothing
-- to bind `alias` to and died with `unbound variable '<alias>'`.  Observed on a pure
-- user-module chain (b defines `area`; c re-exports it; main imports `area as surface`
-- → run prints 16, build fails), and on `import list.{filter as f}` once
-- `export import core.{filter}` began re-exporting at all.
methodsPerMidGo : List (String, List String) -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, List String)
methodsPerMidGo _ [] = []
methodsPerMidGo acc ((mid, decls)::rest) =
  let entry = (mid, dedup (declMethodNames decls ++ reexportedMethodNames acc decls))
  entry :: methodsPerMidGo (entry::acc) rest

-- method names a module re-exports via `export import src.{…}` (`DUse True`), taken
-- from `acc` — the dependency's already-folded method set.
reexportedMethodNames : List (String, List String) -> List Decl -> List String
reexportedMethodNames acc decls = flatMap (reexportedMethodsOf acc) decls

reexportedMethodsOf : List (String, List String) -> Decl -> List String
reexportedMethodsOf acc (DAttrib _ d) = reexportedMethodsOf acc d
reexportedMethodsOf acc (DUse True path _) = match lookupAssoc (usePathModuleId path) acc
  None => []
  Some srcMethods => match importedBindings path
    -- `export import src.*` re-exports every one of src's methods
    None => srcMethods
    Some bs => filterList (n => contains n srcMethods) (map fst bs)
reexportedMethodsOf _ _ = []

renameAliasedMethodsIn : List (String, List String) -> (String, List Decl) -> (String, List Decl)
renameAliasedMethodsIn methodsPerMid (mid, prog) = match aliasMethodRenames methodsPerMid prog
  [] => (mid, prog)
  renames =>
    let prog2 = flatMap (deAliasMethodImports methodsPerMid) prog
    (mid, mapProg (renameMethodVar renames) prog2)

-- (b): make each import bind the METHODS it names under their ORIGIN name.
--   `import m.{area as computeArea}` → `import m.{area}`  (the alias is dropped: a method
--                                      is global-by-name, so the rename already points
--                                      every reference at `area`)
--   `import m as A`                  → keep it (its non-method values are still `A.f`)
--                                      and ADD `import m.{…m's methods…}`, which is what
--                                      `A.area` was rewritten to refer to.
deAliasMethodImports : List (String, List String) -> Decl -> List Decl
deAliasMethodImports mm (DAttrib attrs d) =
  map (DAttrib attrs) (deAliasMethodImports mm d)
deAliasMethodImports mm (d@(DUse pub path loc)) = match lookupAssoc (usePathModuleId path) mm
  None => [d]
  Some methods => match path
    UseGroup quals ms =>
      [DUse pub (UseGroup quals (map (deAliasMethodMember methods) ms)) loc]
    UseAlias quals _ => match methodMembersOf methods loc
      [] => [d]
      mems => [d, DUse pub (UseGroup quals mems) loc]
    _ => [d]
deAliasMethodImports _ d = [d]

deAliasMethodMember : List String -> UseMember -> UseMember
deAliasMethodMember methods (m@(UseMember n allCtors loc _))
  | contains n methods = UseMember n allCtors loc None
  | otherwise = m

methodMembersOf : List String -> Loc -> List UseMember
methodMembersOf methods loc = map (n => UseMember n False loc None) methods

renameMethodVar : List (String, String) -> Expr -> Expr
renameMethodVar renames (e@(EVar x)) = match lookupAssoc x renames
  Some origin => EVar origin
  None => e
renameMethodVar _ e = e

-- (localName, originMethodName) for every import in [prog] whose ORIGIN is a method of
-- the module it comes from.  Only an alias yields an entry — an un-aliased import already
-- carries the origin name, which is why this gap stayed invisible for so long.
aliasMethodRenames : List (String, List String) -> List Decl -> List (String, String)
aliasMethodRenames methodsPerMid prog =
  flatMap (aliasMethodRenamesOf methodsPerMid) prog

aliasMethodRenamesOf : List (String, List String) -> Decl -> List (String, String)
aliasMethodRenamesOf mm (DAttrib _ d) = aliasMethodRenamesOf mm d
aliasMethodRenamesOf mm (DUse _ path _) = match lookupAssoc (usePathModuleId path) mm
  None => []
  Some methods => aliasMethodRenamesOfPath methods path
aliasMethodRenamesOf _ _ = []

aliasMethodRenamesOfPath : List String -> UsePath -> List (String, String)
-- `import m as A` → `A.area` is method `area`
aliasMethodRenamesOfPath methods (UseAlias _ a) =
  map (n => (qualifiedLocal a n, n)) methods
-- `import m.{area as computeArea}` → `computeArea` is method `area`
aliasMethodRenamesOfPath methods (UseGroup _ ms) =
  flatMap (memberMethodRename methods) ms
aliasMethodRenamesOfPath _ _ = []

memberMethodRename : List String -> UseMember -> List (String, String)
memberMethodRename methods m = match useMemberAlias m
  Some local =>
    let origin = useMemberOrigin m
    if contains origin methods then [(local, origin)] else []
  None => []

-- every interface/impl method name a decl list declares (interface methods with or
-- without a default, plus impl method names).
declMethodNames : List Decl -> List String
declMethodNames decls = dedup (flatMap declMethodNamesOf decls)

declMethodNamesOf : Decl -> List String
declMethodNamesOf (DAttrib _ d) = declMethodNamesOf d
declMethodNamesOf (DInterface { methods, ... }) = ifaceMethodNames methods
declMethodNamesOf (DImpl { methods, ... }) = map implMethodName methods
declMethodNamesOf _ = []

-- IMPORT ALIASING: the LOCAL names, across every module, under which a CONSTRAINED
-- function is reachable through an alias.  These must join dictNames or the aliased
-- call site is never marked as a dict application (see the call site of this fn).
--
-- [bare] is the un-aliased dictNames; [unitDecls] is (mid, decls) for core + every
-- module, used to ask which fns a given module actually DEFINES — so `import m as A`
-- contributes `A.<n>` only for m's own constrained fns, never for a same-named
-- constrained fn belonging to some other module.
aliasDictNames : List String -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, List Decl) -> List String
aliasDictNames bare unitDecls modules =
  flatMap (u => aliasDictNamesOfUnit bare unitDecls (snd u)) modules

aliasDictNamesOfUnit : List String -> List (String, List Decl) -> List Decl -> List String
aliasDictNamesOfUnit bare unitDecls decls =
  flatMap (aliasDictNamesOfDecl bare unitDecls) decls

aliasDictNamesOfDecl : List String -> List (String, List Decl) -> Decl -> List String
aliasDictNamesOfDecl bare unitDecls (DAttrib _ d) =
  aliasDictNamesOfDecl bare unitDecls d
aliasDictNamesOfDecl bare unitDecls (DUse _ path _) =
  aliasDictNamesOfPath bare unitDecls (usePathModuleId path) path
aliasDictNamesOfDecl _ _ _ = []

aliasDictNamesOfPath : List String -> List (String, List Decl) -> String -> UsePath -> List String
aliasDictNamesOfPath bare unitDecls mid (UseAlias _ a) = match lookupAssoc mid unitDecls
  Some ds =>
    map (qualifiedLocal a) (filter (n => contains n bare) (declTopFnNames ds))
  None => []
aliasDictNamesOfPath bare _ _ (UseGroup _ ms) = flatMap (memberDictName bare) ms
aliasDictNamesOfPath _ _ _ _ = []

memberDictName : List String -> UseMember -> List String
memberDictName bare m = match useMemberAlias m
  Some local => if contains (useMemberOrigin m) bare then [local] else []
  None => []

moduleAliasEntry : String -> String -> ((String, String), List a) -> List (String, List a)
moduleAliasEntry mid a ((defMid, n), ids)
  | defMid == mid = [(qualifiedLocal a n, ids)]
  | otherwise = []

memberAliasEntry : String -> List ((String, String), List a) -> UseMember -> List (String, List a)
memberAliasEntry mid qual m = match useMemberAlias m
  None => []
  Some local =>
    let origin = useMemberOrigin m
    map
      (e => (local, snd e))
      (filter (e => fst (fst e) == mid && snd (fst e) == origin) qual)

-- build THIS module's import-scoped seed: walk its DUse decls, and for each one
-- pick the imported names out of the named dependency's public schemes (held in
-- depEnv, keyed by the dotted module id that usePathModuleId yields == the loader's
-- mid).  `core` DUse decls are skipped — core is always seeded via baseSeed.
importSeed : List Decl -> List (String, List (String, Scheme)) -> List (String, Scheme)
importSeed [] _ = []
importSeed ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) depEnv = importSeed [d] depEnv
  ++ importSeed rest depEnv
importSeed ((DUse _ path _)::rest) depEnv =
  let depId = usePathModuleId path
  match lookupAssoc depId depEnv
    None => importSeed rest depEnv
    Some depSchemes => importFormSchemes depId path depSchemes
      ++ importSeed rest depEnv
importSeed (_::rest) depEnv = importSeed rest depEnv

-- WS-2/D2: THIS module's imported value name → import-source module id, from its DUse
-- decls.  Mirrors importSeed's walk but records (localName, depId) for EXPLICIT imports
-- only (importedBindings = Some bindings); a wildcard `import mod.*` (None) is skipped —
-- it can't enumerate names here, so those names fall back to the bare arity table at the
-- call site.  `core` is skipped (core fns route via the prelude dict machinery, never the
-- cross-module qual tables).  depId == usePathModuleId == the loader mid == the qual
-- tables' definer key for a DIRECT import; a re-exported fn's depId is the intermediate
-- (≠ definer), so its qual lookup misses and it too falls back to bare (correct).
--
-- Keyed by the LOCAL name, because that is the name the CALL SITE writes: under a member
-- alias (`import m.{a as b}`) the call site says `b`, so `b` is what must map to m.
--
-- A whole-module alias (`import m as A`) contributes nothing, for the same reason a
-- wildcard doesn't: its locals are one-per-export and this walk has no depEnv to
-- enumerate them.  Those call sites take the same documented bare-table fallback as
-- every wildcard import does today.  The dict ARGS themselves are unaffected — they are
-- stamped from the callee's SCHEME, which importSeed binds under the alias local — so
-- this costs only the cross-module arity DISAMBIGUATION, and only when two modules
-- export same-named constrained fns of differing dict arity.
importDefinersOf : List Decl -> List (String, String)
importDefinersOf [] = []
importDefinersOf ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = importDefinersOf [d]
  ++ importDefinersOf rest
importDefinersOf ((DUse _ path _)::rest) =
  let depId = usePathModuleId path
  if depId == "core" then importDefinersOf rest
  else match importedBindings path
    None => importDefinersOf rest
    Some bindings => map (b => (snd b, depId)) bindings ++ importDefinersOf rest
importDefinersOf (_::rest) = importDefinersOf rest

-- the (key, scheme) pairs a module RE-EXPORTS via `export import` (`DUse True`).
-- Mirrors resolve's re-export set (resolve.pubUsePaths filters the same `DUse True`)
-- into the typecheck seed: walk the export-flagged DUse decls and, for each, pick the
-- re-exported member names out of the SOURCE dependency's public schemes already in
-- depEnv — the exact member resolution importSeed uses (importFormSchemes /
-- resolveMemberSchemes), so it works on both golden and mangled emit paths.  Schemes
-- come from depEnv by IDENTITY (the definer's original schemes, never re-generalized),
-- which preserves the original-definer constraint ids (D2 diamond-safety).  ONLY
-- `DUse True` is threaded, so a plain `import` never leaks the importer's names
-- downstream.  Transitivity is automatic: pubV accumulates these and is carried
-- forward as depEnv, so a second `export import` hop re-resolves against the enriched
-- pubV.  Methods need no special case — the source module's pubV already holds the
-- method scheme (publicValNames includes DInterface method names).
--
-- `core` is the one source that is NOT in depEnv: it is the implicit prelude, so its
-- schemes go into `baseSeed`, not into the per-module dep chain.  It is therefore
-- passed separately as `coreV` — without it, `lookupAssoc "core" depEnv` missed and
-- `export import core.{filter}` re-exported nothing (in silence, and forever).
-- `coreV` is consulted ONLY here, on `DUse True`: `importSeed` keeps taking the plain
-- depEnv, so a plain `import core.{…}` still seeds nothing (its names are already in
-- baseSeed — seeding them twice would only add duplicate assoc entries).
reexportSeed : List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List (String, List (String, Scheme)) -> List (String, Scheme)
reexportSeed _ [] _ = []
reexportSeed coreV ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) depEnv = reexportSeed coreV [d] depEnv
  ++ reexportSeed coreV rest depEnv
reexportSeed coreV ((DUse True path _)::rest) depEnv =
  let depId = usePathModuleId path
  let src = if depId == "core" then Some coreV else lookupAssoc depId depEnv
  match src
    None => reexportSeed coreV rest depEnv
    Some depSchemes => importFormSchemes depId path depSchemes
      ++ reexportSeed coreV rest depEnv
reexportSeed coreV (_::rest) depEnv = reexportSeed coreV rest depEnv

-- ── UNIFIED per-module fold (#151) ─────────────────────────────────────────
-- The single home for the identical per-module skeleton the four module drivers
-- (checkModules / checkModulesDiags / checkModulesEntryFull / elaborateModules)
-- share, so the next import-scoped-seed-style fix touches ONE place instead of
-- four (the miss would otherwise be path-specific — LSP-only or emit-only).  It
-- owns: the IMPORT-SCOPED seed (`baseSeed ++ importSeed prog depEnv` — a flat
-- union let `string.reverse` shadow the `list.reverse` a module imports), the
-- per-module PUBLIC accumulator (`pubV = pickSchemes (publicValNames prog) schemes
-- ++ reexportSeed coreV prog depEnv`), and the DEPENDENCY-FIRST threading of
-- depEnv (PREPEND `(mid,pubV)::depEnv`, so importSeed's first-match picks the
-- nearest dependency), accData (`++ publicDataDecls prog`) and accAll (`++ prog`,
-- the cumulative impl-body universe).  Each driver supplies:
--   * worker  : mid seed accData accAll prog -> (schemes, extra) — runs the
--               per-module pass; MUST surface `schemes` (pubV needs it for the
--               next depEnv) plus its driver-specific payload `extra`.
--   * collect : isLast mid extra restResult -> result — a foldr combiner, where
--               restResult is the fold over the REMAINING modules (`base` for []).
--               `isLast` is True only for the terminal (entry) module — the one
--               place checkModulesEntryFull keeps the module's OWN schemes/warns
--               rather than the recursion's.
-- foldModules owns ONLY the loop and performs NO resetCrossModuleState: the reset
-- boundary stays in each driver's preamble (a reset leaking in here would hand the
-- `check*` drivers a reset they must not have).
foldModules : (String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> (List (String, Scheme), e)) -> (Bool -> String -> e -> acc -> acc) -> acc -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, List (String, Scheme)) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> acc
foldModules _ _ base _ _ _ _ _ [] = base
foldModules worker collect base baseSeed _coreV depEnv accData accAll [(mid, prog)] =
  let seed = baseSeed ++ importSeed prog depEnv
  let (_, extra) = worker mid seed accData accAll prog
  collect True mid extra base
foldModules worker collect base baseSeed coreV depEnv accData accAll ((mid, prog)::rest) =
  let seed = baseSeed ++ importSeed prog depEnv
  let (schemes, extra) = worker mid seed accData accAll prog
  let pubV = pickSchemes (publicValNames prog) schemes ++ reexportSeed coreV prog depEnv
  collect
    False
    mid
    extra
    (foldModules
      worker
      collect
      base
      baseSeed
      coreV
      ((mid, pubV)::depEnv)
      (accData ++ publicDataDecls prog)
      (accAll ++ prog)
      rest)

-- Shared preamble of the three `check*` module drivers (the "three verbatim entry
-- wrappers" of #151): reset the cross-module accumulators, register abstract
-- record types, and seed runtime + core schemes.  Returns (runtimeSeed,
-- coreSchemes) for the caller to thread into foldModules.  elaborateModules does
-- NOT use this — its preamble additionally marks/prePasses the graph and stamps
-- core via elabModuleStamp.
checkModulesPreamble : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> (List (String, Scheme), List (String, Scheme))
checkModulesPreamble runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  let _ = resetCrossModuleState ()
  -- #80/#201: populate effect domains ONCE over the WHOLE import graph (builtins +
  -- every `effect …` decl in core or any module), BEFORE the per-module foldModules
  -- loop, so a module using an effect declared in ANOTHER module sees its domain.
  -- resetState no longer re-seeds, so this survives every per-module reset below.
  -- Must precede the core checkModuleFull (it runs checkEffectParams, reading these).
  let _ = populateEffectDomains (coreDecls ++ flatMap snd modules)
  let runtimeSeed = externSchemes runtimeDecls
  let _ = seedAbstractRecordTypes coreDecls modules
  let coreSchemes = checkModuleFull runtimeSeed [] coreDecls
  (runtimeSeed, coreSchemes)

-- worker: plain per-module typecheck (no diagnostics harvest, no accAll).  Surfaces
-- `schemes` twice — once as the pubV source foldModules needs, once as the payload
-- checkModules collects.
cmCheckWorker : String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> (List (String, Scheme), List (String, Scheme))
cmCheckWorker _mid seed accData _accAll prog =
  let schemes = checkModuleFull seed accData prog
  (schemes, schemes)

-- check every module in dependency order against a shared prelude + runtime.
-- modules = (moduleId, desugared program) in dependency-first order.
export checkModules : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, List (String, Scheme))
checkModules runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  let (runtimeSeed, coreSchemes) = checkModulesPreamble runtimeDecls coreDecls modules
  foldModules
    cmCheckWorker
    (_last mid schemes rest => (mid, schemes)::rest)
    []
    (runtimeSeed ++ coreSchemes)
    coreSchemes
    []
    (publicDataDecls coreDecls)
    coreDecls
    modules

-- ── per-module ISOLATED diagnostics (the analyzeProject typecheck stage) ────
-- Like checkModuleFull but ALSO harvests THIS module's type diagnostics: it
-- mirrors checkProgramDiags's side-effects (matchOracle/matchWarnings seeded,
-- coherence over the module's OWN decls) while keeping the module ISOLATED — only
-- PUBLIC schemes/data of earlier modules are seeded (via seedVars/accData), so a
-- private same-named helper in another module never collides (the bug the old
-- flat prefix-concat diagnostics path had).  checkModuleFull calls resetState (),
-- which clears typeErrors at module start, so the post-call typeErrors snapshot is
-- exactly THIS module's contribution; matchWarnings is cleared here explicitly
-- (resetState does NOT clear it).  Returns (own value schemes, errs, warns) so the
-- driver can thread accV/accData exactly as foldModules does.
export checkModuleFullDiags : List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> (List (String, Scheme), List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc))
checkModuleFullDiags seedVars accData accAll prog =
  let _ = setRef implInferEnabled False
  let _ = setRef matchWarnings []
  let _ = setRef matchWarningLocs []
  -- P0-9: THIS module's own decls (`prog`) come FIRST so its constructors win
  -- the oracle's first-wins bare-name tables.  Another loaded module (in accData)
  -- can define a same-named ctor at a different arity (`map`'s arity-5 `Bin` vs
  -- `set`'s arity-4 `Bin`); with accData first, the type-aware `check_match` reads
  -- the WRONG arity/type for this module's own `Bin` in a match and false-warns
  -- W-NONEXHAUSTIVE.  buildOracle is duplicate-tolerant, and accData still
  -- supplies prelude Option/Result ctors (this module never redefines them).
  let _ = setRef matchOracle (buildOracle (prog ++ accData))
  -- Bug C: pass the cumulative full impl universe (core + earlier modules) as
  -- implDecls (was checkModuleFull, i.e. implDecls=[]) so buildStandaloneShadows sees
  -- an imported standalone funDef that shadows an interface method (`map`'s `toList`
  -- vs Foldable's): without it the importer-shadow set is EMPTY on the CHECK path and
  -- `toList m` routes to method dispatch → spurious `No impl of Foldable for Map`.
  -- implInferEnabled stays False, so this does NOT trigger impl-body inference — it
  -- only feeds the shadow/dispatch-index/key tables, matching the eval path's
  -- elabModuleStamp (which passes accAll ++ prog too).
  let schemes = checkModuleFullImpl "" seedVars accData accAll prog
  let _ = runFinalChecks prog (accAll ++ accData ++ prog)
  (
    schemes,
    reverseL typeErrors.value,
    zipL (reverseL matchWarnings.value) (reverseL matchWarningLocs.value),
  )
-- Seed the ctor oracle over ALL data decls in scope (prelude + every imported
-- module via accData, plus this module's own prog), NOT just `prog`.  The
-- single-file path (checkProgramDiags:5826) sees a flat prelude+user dump, so
-- its `buildOracle prog` already includes Option/Result; the multi-module path
-- keeps modules separate, so prelude ADTs live in accData.  Seeding with only
-- `prog` left the oracle missing Some/None|Ok/Err → exhaustive matches on
-- Option/Result false-warned "non-exhaustive".  buildOracle reads only DData
-- and is duplicate-tolerant, so accData ++ prog is safe.

-- checkModuleFull → checkModuleFullImpl runs resetState () first (clears
-- typeErrors + currentLevel), so the snapshot below is this module's own errors.

-- coherence over THIS module's own decls (mirrors checkProgramDiags's
-- checkCoherence coherenceUserDecls.value, which moduleTypeDiags set to the
-- module decls).  Pushes any conflict into typeErrors AFTER the resetState above.

-- multi-module diagnostics driver: threads the runtime+core seed and per-module
-- PUBLIC accumulators exactly as foldModules threads, but at each module harvests its
-- ISOLATED diagnostics via checkModuleFullDiags.  Returns one
-- (mid, (errs, warns)) per module in load order.  Core is checked ONCE (its
-- diagnostics are not surfaced — it is the trusted prelude, as in checkModules).
-- IMPORT-SCOPED seed (baseSeed ++ importSeed prog depEnv), exactly like
-- foldModules — NOT a flat union of every earlier module's publics, which let
-- a same-named function from a non-imported module win by load order (e.g.
-- string.reverse shadowing the list.reverse a module imports → spurious errors).
-- worker: ISOLATED per-module diagnostics harvest (checkModuleFullDiags — see its
-- doc above).  Surfaces this module's own `schemes` (for pubV) plus its (errs, warns)
-- payload.  IMPORT-SCOPED seed exactly like cmCheckWorker; NOT a flat union.
cmDiagsWorker : String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> (List (String, Scheme), (List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc)))
cmDiagsWorker _mid seed accData accAll prog =
  let (schemes, errs, warns) = checkModuleFullDiags seed accData accAll prog
  (schemes, (errs, warns))

export checkModulesDiags : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, (List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc)))
checkModulesDiags runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  let (runtimeSeed, coreSchemes) = checkModulesPreamble runtimeDecls coreDecls modules
  let perMod = foldModules
    cmDiagsWorker
    (_last mid ew rest => (mid, ew) :: rest)
    []
    (runtimeSeed ++ coreSchemes)
    coreSchemes
    []
    (publicDataDecls coreDecls)
    coreDecls
    modules
  -- D3 / WS-2 part-2: attach any cross-module coherence conflict to the ENTRY
  -- module's diagnostics (last in dependency-first order), so LSP/analyzeProject
  -- surfaces it too.  User modules only (prelude excluded).
  match globalCoherenceConflict modules
    Some msg => attachEntryDiag ("T-CONFLICTING-IMPL", msg, None) perMod
    None => perMod

-- prepend an error to the LAST module's (errs, warns) in a per-module diag list.
attachEntryDiag : (String, String, Option Loc) -> List (String, (List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc))) -> List (String, (List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc)))
attachEntryDiag _ [] = []
attachEntryDiag e [(mid, (errs, warns))] = [(mid, (e::errs, warns))]
attachEntryDiag e (m::rest) = m :: attachEntryDiag e rest

-- the entry module is last in dependency-first order
export entryOwnSchemes : List (String, List (String, Scheme)) -> List (String, Scheme)
entryOwnSchemes [] = []
entryOwnSchemes [(_, ss)] = ss
entryOwnSchemes (_::rest) = entryOwnSchemes rest

-- ── multi-module `check` CLI report (DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 4, OPTION A) ─────
-- The unified `medaka check` path for IMPORT-BEARING files: the caller loads the
-- entry + its transitive imports (loadProgram), then this typechecks the whole
-- graph in dependency order (checkModuleFullDiags per module: per-module-frame,
-- prelude seeded once) and harvests the ENTRY module's OWN (schemes, errs, warns).
-- Imports are RESOLVED (their public schemes seed the entry), so a valid cross-
-- module reference no longer falls through to `UnknownModule` — the known "native
-- check is single-file" limitation, fixed.  The entry is last in dependency-first
-- order.  No-import (1-module) files still route through runCheck (full prelude+
-- user dump, byte-identical); this path is reached ONLY when the load returned
-- more than one module.
checkModulesEntryFull : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> (List (String, Scheme), List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc))
checkModulesEntryFull runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  let (runtimeSeed, coreSchemes) = checkModulesPreamble runtimeDecls coreDecls modules
  let (schemes, errs, warns) = foldModules
    cmEntryWorker
    cmEntryCollect
    ([], [], [])
    (runtimeSeed ++ coreSchemes)
    coreSchemes
    []
    (publicDataDecls coreDecls)
    coreDecls
    modules
  -- D3 / WS-2 part-2: cross-module coherence over the USER modules only (prelude
  -- excluded — it is checked separately and a user impl may override a prelude one).
  -- Prepend any conflict (dependency-first, ahead of the entry's own errors).
  match globalCoherenceConflict modules
    Some msg => (schemes, ("T-CONFLICTING-IMPL", msg, None) :: errs, warns)
    None => (schemes, errs, warns)

-- worker: same ISOLATED per-module diagnostics as cmDiagsWorker, but surfaces the
-- FULL triple (schemes, errs, warns) as its payload so cmEntryCollect can keep the
-- terminal module's OWN schemes/warns.  IMPORT-SCOPED seed, exactly like the others.
cmEntryWorker : String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> (List (String, Scheme), (List (String, Scheme), List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc)))
cmEntryWorker _mid seed accData accAll prog =
  let (schemes, errs, warns) = checkModuleFullDiags seed accData accAll prog
  (schemes, (schemes, errs, warns))

-- collect: the ENTRY report keeps the ENTRY (last/terminal) module's SCHEMES and
-- WARNINGS (those belong to the entry's report), but ACCUMULATES type errors from
-- EVERY module dependency-first: a type error in an IMPORTED module is still a hard
-- error of `check`ing the entry graph and must surface (else it silently reaches
-- codegen — the G1-class soundness hole).  Earlier-module errors are PREPENDED
-- (`e ++ eE`) to the recursion's; only isLast (the terminal module) uses its OWN
-- schemes/warns, every earlier module inherits eS/eW from the recursion.
cmEntryCollect : Bool -> String -> (List (String, Scheme), List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc)) -> (List (String, Scheme), List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc)) -> (List (String, Scheme), List (String, String, Option Loc), List (String, Option Loc))
cmEntryCollect isLast _mid extra rest =
  let (s, e, w) = extra
  let (eS, eE, eW) = rest
  if isLast then (s, e ++ eE, w) else (eS, e ++ eE, eW)

-- render the entry report: type errors win (each `TYPE ERROR: …`), else the
-- entry's `name : scheme` lines followed by its non-exhaustive-match warnings.
-- The harness sorts, so scheme/warn relative order is not load-bearing.  Mirrors
-- runCheck's single-file shape, but over the entry module of a resolved graph.
export checkModulesEntryReport : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> String
checkModulesEntryReport runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  let (schemes, errs, warns) = checkModulesEntryFull runtimeDecls coreDecls modules
  match errs
    [] => joinNl (schemeLines schemes ++ map fst warns)
    _ => joinNl (typeErrorLines (map teErrMsg errs))

-- exit-code predicate analog of checkHasErrors: any type error in ANY module of
-- the resolved graph (entry or imported — see cmEntryCollect / foldModules).
export checkModulesEntryHasErrors : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> Bool
checkModulesEntryHasErrors runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  let (_, errs, _) = checkModulesEntryFull runtimeDecls coreDecls modules
  match errs
    [] => False
    _ => True

-- run the multi-module front-end and render the ENTRY module's own bindings as
-- `name : scheme` per line (the harness sorts) — diffs vs dev/tc_module_probe.
export checkModulesEntryLines : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> String
checkModulesEntryLines runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  joinNl (schemeLines (entryOwnSchemes (checkModules
    runtimeDecls
    coreDecls
    modules)))

-- Emit EVERY module's bindings in one pass, each section preceded by a
-- `## MODULE <mid>` marker line.  A single full-closure run can then cover many
-- modules at once (the batch harness splits on the marker and diffs each
-- section vs its per-module oracle).  Scheme of a module depends only on its
-- dependency-closure (which precedes it in topo order), so a module's section
-- is identical whether checked alone or inside a larger union closure.
export checkModulesAllLines : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> String
checkModulesAllLines runtimeDecls coreDecls modules =
  joinNl (allModuleLines (checkModules runtimeDecls coreDecls modules))

allModuleLines : List (String, List (String, Scheme)) -> List String
allModuleLines [] = []
allModuleLines ((mid, ss)::rest) =
  "## MODULE " ++ mid :: schemeLines ss ++ allModuleLines rest

-- ── TYPED multi-module elaboration (bootstrap eval front-end) ──────────────
-- The multi-module analog of `elaborate`: the composition of `checkModules`
-- (per-module-frame typecheck, dependency-first) and the EMethodAt route-
-- stamping `elaborate` does single-module.  prePasses the prelude + every module
-- (rewriting return-position method occurrences to EMethodAt over the WHOLE
-- program's RP method set), typechecks each module in dependency order (seeded
-- like checkModules), and STAMPS each module's route refs from its now-resolved
-- result types.  Returns the SAME trees (refs filled) so evalModules can route
-- return-position dispatch (`pure`/`andThen` — the Parser monad) by RKey instead
-- of the untyped arg-tag fallback.  RKey-only (dictNames = []): the bootstrap
-- source uses no `=>`-constrained user polymorphism, so no EDictAt / dict params
-- and no pendingDictApps / pendingRecDictApps to resolve.
export elaborateModules : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> (List Decl, List (String, List Decl))
-- E4: under emitArgStampPasses (the LLVM emit drivers ONLY) apply D3b's ARG-position
-- dict-passing per module, mirroring elaborateDict.
--
-- implInferEnabled is now ON unconditionally (was `emitArgStampPasses.value`): the EVAL
-- path needs impl-body inference too, so a standalone-shadow method call INSIDE an
-- impl body (map's `Debug`/`Eq`/`Ord`/`Display (Map k v)` bodies all call standalone
-- `toList m`) RECORDS its RLocal site (recordRLocalSite → pendingRLocalSites) for
-- resolveRLocalSites to stamp.  Without it the impl-body `toList` route stayed at its
-- default RNone → eval arg-tag-dispatched a Map through the no-matching-tag `keepOrAll`
-- fallback to Foldable List's `toList = identity` → returned the Map unchanged →
-- `debug`/`display` recursed forever (SIGBUS on `medaka test stdlib/map.mdk`).  The
-- TYPECHECK golden probes use checkModulesEntryLines/checkModulesDiags (NOT
-- elaborateModules), so their goldens are unaffected; the emit-path diff gates run
-- with emitArgStampPasses True, where impl inference was already ON — also unchanged.
elaborateModules runtimeDecls coreDecls modulesIn =
  let _ = setRef implInferEnabled True
  -- IMPORT ALIASING of an interface/impl METHOD — resolve it back to the origin name
  -- before ANY of the marking / stamping / dict-arity machinery below runs.  See
  -- renameAliasedMethods: a method is global-by-name, so an alias of one is a second
  -- SPELLING, not a second binding.
  let modules = renameAliasedMethods coreDecls modulesIn
  let _ = setRef mainSchemeRef None
  let _ = resetCrossModuleState ()
  let allDecls = coreDecls ++ flatMap snd modules
  -- #80/#201: populate effect domains ONCE over the WHOLE import graph (builtins +
  -- every `effect …` decl), BEFORE any per-module resetState (discovery scratch pass
  -- + the elabModuleStamp loop both call it) can read them — resetState no longer
  -- re-seeds, and effectDomains has a single writer (populateEffectDomains), so this
  -- persists untouched through the whole emit pass.
  let _ = populateEffectDomains allDecls
  -- P0-18: recover the definer-shadow map from the (already-mangled on the emit path)
  -- decls BEFORE marking/typecheck reads it.  Empty on the un-mangled path.  Set once
  -- here; per-module resetState (in checkModuleFullImpl) does NOT clear it.
  let _ = setRef mangledShadowMapRef (computeMangledShadowMap allDecls (("core", coreDecls)::modules))
  let _ = setRef superDeclsRef allDecls  -- WS-1b: interfaces in scope for the discovery scratch typecheck
  let _ = setRef userIfaceNamesRef (collectIfaceNames (flatMap snd modules))  -- WS-1b: gate super-expansion to user interfaces
  let rpNames = returnPosMethodNames allDecls
  let _ = setRef argDispatchIdxRef (argDispatchIndices allDecls)
  let argNames = map fst argDispatchIdxRef.value
  let _ = setRef dictEligibleRef (moduleUserFnNames modules)
  let _ = setRef dictEligibleSetRef (namesToSet (moduleUserFnNames modules) omEmpty)
  let promoted = discoverPromotedModules runtimeDecls coreDecls modules rpNames argNames
  let bareDictNames = dedup (moduleDictNames coreDecls allDecls modules ++ promoted)
  -- IMPORT ALIASING: prePassDict* marks a call site `EVar n` as a dict application only
  -- when `n` is in dictNames — a set of DEFINITION names.  An aliased call site writes
  -- the LOCAL name, so unless the alias locals are added here the call is never marked,
  -- receives no dict arguments, and silently UNDER-APPLIES: `check` stays green and
  -- `run` dies downstream with a type-confused value (the Phase-134 shape).
  let dictNames = dedup (bareDictNames
    ++ aliasDictNames bareDictNames (("core", coreDecls)::modules) modules)
  let markRpNames = dedup (rpNames ++ methodConstraintNames allDecls ++ buildStandaloneShadowsGraph allDecls (flatMap snd modules))
  let core2 = prePassDictArg markRpNames dictNames argNames mangledShadowMapRef.value coreDecls
  let modules2 = map (prePassModulePairArg markRpNames dictNames argNames mangledShadowMapRef.value) modules
  let runtimeSeed = externSchemes runtimeDecls
  let coreSchemes = elabModuleStamp "" runtimeSeed [] core2 core2
  let _ = foldModules
    elabWorker
    (_last _mid _extra _rest => ())
    ()
    (runtimeSeed ++ coreSchemes)
    coreSchemes
    []
    (publicDataDecls core2)
    core2
    modules2
  dictPassModulesIfEnabled dictNames core2 modules2
-- arg-position interface-method names whose route resolveArgStamps may stamp
-- (empty when OFF → prePassDictArg degenerates to prePassDict, goldens unchanged)

-- DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 2 (global): seed the promotion-eligible set with the USER
-- modules' top-level fn names (NOT core/prelude — those keep arg-tag / RKey
-- dispatch, which type-checks the goldens).  Previously gated on emitArgStampPasses
-- (build path only); now ALSO seeded on the eval path (emitArgStampPasses OFF) so
-- elaborateModules promotes INFERRED-constraint user fns exactly as flat
-- elaborateDict does (discoverAll runs discoverPromoted whenever eligibleNames is
-- non-empty, independent of emitArgStampPasses).  The TYPECHECK golden module drivers
-- (checkModulesEntryLines/checkModulesDiags) never reach elaborateModules, so their
-- goldens are unaffected; the eval goldens are captured from the OCaml oracle's
-- run, which dict-passes prelude+modules unconditionally — so promotion-on-eval
-- matches them.  Mirrors elaborateDict's dictEligibleRef seed.

-- Cause A / Phase 2: run a JOINT-flattened promotion fixpoint over runtime ++ core
-- ++ all modules (mirror discoverPromoted) so an unsignatured fn whose body
-- dispatches a method in ARG position (`f s = println s` → inferred `Display a => …`)
-- OR uses a return-position method (`doubleEmpty x = append (append x empty) empty`
-- → inferred `Monoid a => …`) is discovered + registered in
-- promotedRef/funConstraintsRef/activeDictVars.  The promoted names then join the
-- dict-name set so their call sites are marked EDictAt (caller supplies the dict
-- ARGUMENT) and their defs get the leading dict PARAM.  Now runs on BOTH paths
-- (eval too): with argNames=[] on eval it surfaces only RETURN-position inferred
-- constraints (no arg-position dict-passing), matching flat elaborateDict's eval
-- behaviour (emitArgStampPasses OFF ⇒ argNames=[] there too).

-- E6: dict-name set for the `=>`-constrained-function layer, mirroring the
-- single-file emit driver's assembly (llvm_emit_typed_main.runEmit): prelude
-- return- AND arg-position `=>`-constrained fns (clamp/debugListItems/…) plus the
-- modules' own constrained signatures.  [] when OFF (emitArgStampPasses gates every
-- consumer), so the golden module drivers compute nothing extra → byte-identical.
-- Used to MARK constrained-fn call sites EDictAt in the prePass (so the caller
-- supplies the dict ARGUMENT) AND to add the leading dict PARAM in the final
-- dictPass (so the callee body's witness binds).  BOTH halves are required: marking
-- alone under-defines the param; param alone under-applies the call (ABI mismatch).
-- Cause A: promoted (inferred-constraint) user fns join the set.

-- ARGSTAMP-UNIFY Phase 4: the eval-dict layer is now PERMANENTLY active on both
-- paths (flag retired).  Flat elaborateDict runs its resolve chain + dictPass
-- UNCONDITIONALLY; the 1-module / multi-module eval path matches, so method-level
-- constraints (`build : Num e =>`) and parametric-impl `requires` (`impl Default
-- (List a) requires Default a`) — which leave dictNames EMPTY (no top-level
-- constrained FUNCTION) but still need resolveMethodDicts / the DImpl dict-pass —
-- get serviced.  The dictPass/resolve passes are no-ops when nothing was marked, so
-- this leaves the golden eval trees byte-identical where there is nothing to
-- dict-pass.
-- C5: ALSO mark cross-module standalone-shadow method occurrences (box's
-- `toList`/`isEmpty`) as EMethodAt, so they carry a route ref for elabModuleStamp's
-- resolveRLocalSites to stamp RLocal.  Without this they stay EVar (arg-position,
-- emitArgStampPasses OFF on the eval path) → no route ref → arg-tag panic on a no-impl
-- receiver.  Only added to the MARK set (not rpNames proper, which discoverPromoted
-- etc. consume) so other passes are unchanged.
-- DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 2: ALSO include methodConstraintNames (methods with their OWN
-- method-level `=>` constraint, e.g. `build : Num e => e -> t`), mirroring flat
-- elaborateDict's prePass mark set (`returnPosMethodNames prog ++ methodConstraintNames
-- prog`).  Without it the method-constraint occurrence stays unmarked → its impl
-- clause's leading method-dict param is dropped/mis-bound → `non-exhaustive match`
-- (method_constraint_impl_offset).

-- impl table accumulates across modules (dependency order): a module's
-- arg-position dispatch can thread an EARLIER module's / core's parametric
-- `requires` element dict.  core2 seeds it.

-- E5: on the emit path (emitArgStampPasses), dict-pass each module's now-stamped
-- trees, mirroring elaborateDict's final `dictPass`.  A parametric `requires`
-- impl whose body element-dispatch E5 routed RDict needs its leading
-- `$dict_<m>_<slot>` PARAM (dictPassDecl's DImpl arm) so the emitter can bind the
-- witness the route references; the call site's impl-dict route (filled by
-- resolveArgStamp) supplies the matching ARGUMENT.  E6: dictNames now carries the
-- prelude/module `=>`-constrained fns (clamp/debugListItems/displayListItems/…),
-- so dictPassDecl's DFunDef arm ALSO prepends their `$dict_<fn>_<slot>` params —
-- closing the "unbound dict witness" residual.  OFF (oracle eval_typed_modules +
-- every golden module driver), dictPass is skipped → core2/modules2 returned
-- verbatim, byte-identical with the pre-E5 trees.

-- E5/E6: gated final dict-pass of the stamped module trees (emit path only).
-- [dictNames] is the prelude + module `=>`-constrained fn set; [] when OFF.
-- E6: each module's typecheck calls resetState, which clears funConstraintsRef,
-- so by this final pass only the LAST module's constraint arities survive — a
-- core-level constrained fn like `clamp` would get `dictArityOf` = 0 and NO leading
-- param.  Re-seed funConstraintsRef from every constrained signature (core +
-- modules) so dictPassDecl's DFunDef arm prepends the right number of
-- `$dict_<fn>_<slot>` params.  Only the COUNT is read here (the body's EDictAt/RDict
-- routes were already stamped, with real ids, during each module's own pass), so a
-- placeholder id list of the correct length is sufficient.  Prepended → wins the
-- lookupAssocSL2 first-match over any stale last-module entry.  Gated by the
-- emitArgStampPasses guard below (golden drivers never reach it).
-- Step 4 (Phase-134 fix): per-module importer-scoped arity, mirroring the oracle
-- eval.ml:2104-2164 collect_arities-per-scope.  The pre-fix JOINT
-- seedDictAritiesFromSigs keyed arity by BARE NAME, so a constrained fn in module A
-- forced spurious leading dict params onto an unconstrained same-named fn in module
-- B (whose call sites then under-applied → silent partial closure).  Core
-- (≈ the oracle's prelude) is imported by every module, so it keeps the FULL joint
-- scope.  Each module's scope = its own decls ∪ the decls of its transitive
-- importers ∪ core (the importer part covers a PUBLIC constrained fn referenced only
-- by its importers; mangleUnits already de-collided PRIVATES).  promotedC = the
-- joint-discovered promoted-fn arities (unsignatured, so constrainedSigArity misses
-- them) carried across resetState in crossModuleFunConstraintsRef.
dictPassModulesIfEnabled : List String -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> (List Decl, List (String, List Decl))
dictPassModulesIfEnabled dictNames core2 modules2 =
  let allModDecls = flatMap snd modules2
  let promotedQ = crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef.value
  let allMods = namesToSet (map fst modules2) omEmpty
  let _ = setRef funConstraintsRef (scopeArities promotedQ allMods (core2 ++ allModDecls))
  let _ = setRef methodConstraintsRef (scopeMethodArities crossModuleMethodConstraintsQualRef.value allMods)
  let core2' = dictPass dictNames core2
  let modules2' = dictPassModulesScoped dictNames promotedQ core2 modules2 modules2
  (core2', modules2')
-- Post-ARGSTAMP-UNIFY (Phase 4): ALWAYS run on BOTH paths.  Emit (emitArgStampPasses):
-- the DImpl arm prepends parametric impl-body element-dict params even when no
-- top-level `=>`-constrained FUNCTION is in [dictNames] (e.g. the empty-prelude
-- nested_dict_dispatch fixture: a parametric `Cmp (Box a) requires Cmp a` with no
-- constrained fn).  Eval: same — a parametric-impl `requires` whose DImpl body needs
-- its leading element-dict param (nested_instance_dicts: `impl Default (List a)
-- requires Default a`) gets dict-passed even with no top-level constrained FUNCTION.
-- Mirrors flat elaborateDict's unconditional final dictPass.  No-op when nothing was
-- marked → byte-identical golden trees.  scopeArities sizes each define's leading
-- dict params to its surviving-constraint count.  (Was the always-True
-- `emitArgStampPasses || evalDictLayerActive` clause guard.)

-- per-module: set funConstraintsRef to the module's importer-scoped arities, then
-- dict-pass that module.  [allModules] is the full set (for the transitive-importer
-- walk); [modules2] is the remaining suffix to process.  core2 is always in scope.
dictPassModulesScoped : List String -> List ((String, String), List Int) -> List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, List Decl)
dictPassModulesScoped _ _ _ _ [] = []
dictPassModulesScoped dictNames promotedQ core2 allModules ((mid, prog)::rest) =
  let importerDecls = transitiveImporterDecls mid allModules
  let scope = core2 ++ prog ++ importerDecls
  let scopeMods = namesToSet (mid :: transitiveImporterIds mid allModules) omEmpty
  -- IMPORT ALIASING: scopeArities keys arity by the DEFINITION's name, but an aliased
  -- call site in THIS module writes the LOCAL name.  Re-key this module's own aliases
  -- (from its DUse decls) onto the origin's arity, else the aliased call under-applies
  -- its dicts.  This is the RUN/eval path's counterpart to the same fix in
  -- checkModuleFullImpl — the EMIT path never needs it, because private_mangle has
  -- already rewritten `A.name` to the origin's real symbol before this point.
  let _ = setRef funConstraintsRef (aliasConstraintEntries prog promotedQ
    ++ scopeArities promotedQ scopeMods scope)
  let _ = setRef methodConstraintsRef (scopeMethodArities crossModuleMethodConstraintsQualRef.value scopeMods)
  (mid, dictPass dictNames prog) :: dictPassModulesScoped dictNames promotedQ core2 allModules rest

-- the arity table for a scope: every `=>`-constrained signature in [scope]
-- (constrainedSigArity) PLUS the joint-discovered promoted-fn arities whose fn is
-- DEFINED in [scope] (so a promoted fn out of scope doesn't leak a param count in).
-- #55: the signature count (`constrainedSigArity`) is the SYNTACTIC `=>`-constraint
-- count; the INFERRED count in [promotedC] (crossModuleFunConstraintsRef, the
-- surviving generalized constraint vars from each body's inference) is what every
-- CALL site computes (constraintMonosOf over the call's instantiated scheme).  These
-- diverge for a point-free fn whose body MONOMORPHIZES a constraint var — e.g.
-- `sum = fold (+) 0`: the `0` literal is typed `Int` (litType), pinning `Num a`'s `a`
-- to `Int`, so `a` is NOT generalized → the scheme keeps only `Foldable t` (1
-- constraint) while the signature still lists 2.  If the define were sized off the
-- 2-count sig it would take 2 leading dict params while call sites pass 1 (dict +
-- container) → the container arg lands in an unpassed register → garbage deref /
-- SIGSEGV at -O2.  So for a fn WITH an inferred entry, the inferred arity WINS (drop
-- its sig entry); the sig count is the fallback for fns the body-inference path never
-- registered (unreached/never-called constrained fns).  `elem` is unaffected: both
-- its `Eq a` and `Foldable t` vars survive (no literal pins them) → inferred == sig == 2.
-- WS-2/D2: [promotedQ] is the module-attributed inferred arities
-- (crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef); [scopeMods] is the set of module ids in this
-- dict-pass scope (this module ∪ its transitive importers ∪ "" for core, which is in
-- every scope).  An inferred entry survives when its fn NAME is in scope (as before).
